name: 1-2-3 the First
originalDescription: he is the midwayer from primary order that was the eldest of all midwayer order in Urantia since Caligastia betrayal (77:9.4-5) he stood loyal to Gabriel in Van and Amadon times and managed to reduce rebel casualties in his order now he is member of twenty-four counselors of Jerusem and has been governor general on Urantia once (45:4.18) (77:9.5)
improvedDescription: The eldest midwayer of the primary order on Urantia who stood loyal to Gabriel during the Caligastia rebellion and led efforts to reduce rebel casualties among his order. He currently serves on Jerusem as a member of the twenty-four counselors and has previously functioned as governor general of Urantia.

originalDescription: The domain of life is characterized by number three. There are three primal life plans after the order  of the three Paradise Sources and Centers and in Nebadon there are three types of planets (36:2.11)
improvedDescription: A fundamental number in the domain of life, representing one of three primal life plans after the order of the three Paradise Sources and Centers. In Nebadon, these three basic forms of life are segregated on three different types of planets.

originalDescription: the threefold Deity personalization has seven associative possibilities (16:0.1) (16:1.1) (17:3.1) (18:1.3) number seven is basic in the universe to transmission of character (42:9.2) there are seven colors in natural spectrum (42:9.3) the domain of life is characterized by number seven. There are seven architectural types of life design (36:2.11) number seven is fundamental to universe organization. Universe is operated in seven grand divisions (10:2.8) (16:0.1) (42:9.2) in natural philosophy number seven was considered fundamental to nature (42:9.1) in periodic table, physical and chemical properties recur by periods of seven (42:9.2-3) secoraphic types recur serially by seven (28:4.3-4) (28:5.6) prematter has a sevenfold electronic organization (42:9.1,3)
improvedDescription: A number that is basic to the central universe and the spiritual system of inherent transmissions of character. The number seven reflects the sevenfold associative possibilities of the three persons of Deity and is fundamental to universe organization, appearing in the chemical domains, life design patterns, and throughout the structure of reality.

originalDescription: the number ten, the decimal system, is inherent in energy and matter but not in spirit (36:2.11) (42:9.2)
improvedDescription: The decimal system that is inherent in energy, matter, and the physical universe but not in the spiritual realm.

originalDescription: the domain of life is characterized by the number twelve. There were, originally twelve divine concepts of life and number twelve runs throughout all basic life patterns in the universe (36:2.11)
improvedDescription: A fundamental number in the domain of life, with twelve distinct and divine concepts of transmissible life originally existing. This number, with its subdivisions and multiples, runs throughout all basic life patterns of the seven superuniverses.

originalDescription: in antiquity number thirteen was been regarded as evil (88:1.6)
improvedDescription: A number that has been regarded as evil or unlucky since antiquity, contrasting with the lucky numbers three and seven that came from later revelations.

name: 606 of Satania
originalDescription: 606 is the number of our planet in the planetary group of Satania, that contains 619 inhabited worlds. It is another way to refer to Urantia, our world (15:14.5) (31:10.22) (43:0.1) (49:0.3) (58:1.1) (62:7.7) (63:0.1) (74:1.5) (74:2.8) (93:1.2) (93:10.1)
improvedDescription: The numerical designation of Urantia in the planetary system of Satania, being the 606th inhabited world in this local system. Satania currently contains 619 inhabited worlds with more than two hundred additional planets evolving toward habitation.

name: A-B-C the First
originalDescription: it is the name of the first secondary midwayer of the first subgroup of eight subgroups that exists. Each subgroup are 248 brothers and sisters, childs of the same parents (77:6.4)
improvedDescription: The designation for the first secondary midwayer of the first subgroup among eight subgroups of Adamic midwayers. Each subgroup consists of 248 members who are children of the same parents.

name: a priori assumptions
originalDescription: causation, duty, and worship constitute the a priori assumptions which make it possible for man to function as a rational and self-conscious personality (16:6.10)
improvedDescription: The three fundamental cosmic mind insights of causation, duty, and worship that constitute the basic assumptions enabling humans to function as rational and self-conscious personalities in the realms of science, philosophy, and religion.

name: Aaron (Old Testament)
originalDescription: Aaron was the older brother of Moses and the one who starred in the event of the golden calf, an idol with which the Israelites decided to represent Yahweh. Peter talk about him in a preach (146:1.1)
improvedDescription: The older brother of Moses who was involved in the golden calf incident, where the Israelites created an idol to represent Yahweh. Peter preached about Aaron and the Golden Calf during his early ministry in Rimmon.

name: Aaron the stone mason
originalDescription: Aaron was healed of withered hand by Jesus (148:7) he was who told to paralytic lowered from roof about Jesus (148:9.2)
improvedDescription: Aaron was a stone mason healed by Jesus of his withered hand, who later told a paralytic about Jesus' healing powers, leading to the dramatic roof incident where the paralytic was lowered through the ceiling to reach Jesus.

name: Abaddon
originalDescription: Abaddon was the chief of staff of Caligastia; then he was chief of Urantia rebels (53:1.5)
improvedDescription: Abaddon was the chief of staff of Caligastia who followed his master into rebellion and has served as chief executive of the Urantia rebels ever since.

name: abandonters
originalDescription: they are the permanent citizens of Uversa headquarters spheres, the same as midwayers for Urantia or Material Sons and Daughters for Jerusem (37:9.12) abandonters are a group of beings highly artistically skilled and socially adapted (43:7.4) they were created by agents of Ancients of Days and Reflective Spirits (37:9.12) they appear in personality registers (30:1.44) (30:2.122) they are supervised by Son-fused mortals (37:9.12)
improvedDescription: Abandonters are the permanent citizens of Uversa headquarters spheres, created by unrevealed agents of the Ancients of Days and the seven Reflective Spirits. They are highly skilled artistically and socially adapted beings who administer routine affairs under the supervision of Son-fused mortals.

name: Abel
originalDescription: he was first child of Adam born in second garden. he elected to be a herder (76:2.1,4) he lived greatly influenced by his unfavorable surroundings (76:2.6) Abel taunted older brother Cain and ended slain by him (76:2.2-5)
improvedDescription: Abel was the first child of Adam and Eve born in the second garden. He chose to become a herder and was slain by his older brother Cain after taunting him about their different parentage and the preference shown for Abel's animal sacrifices.

name: Abila
originalDescription: It was one of the cities in Decapolis It was one of the places where the twelve apostles preached during the first labor of the twelve between mid-August 26 and the end of that year (138:9.3) Jesus, the twelve apostles and the twelve of John preached in this city during the first preaching tour in the Decapolis, between the months of November and December 27 (144:7.1) Nathanael was here preaching during the second tour of the Decapolis between August 18 and September 16, 29 (159:4.1)
improvedDescription: Abila was one of the cities in the Decapolis where Jesus and his apostles preached during multiple evangelistic tours, including the first labor of the twelve and both preaching tours through the region.

name: ability
originalDescription: a created capacity for higher celestial ministry doesn’t necessarily imply ability to function on lower levels (39:0.10) inherent capacities cannot be exceeded (48:7.5) the three sources of special human ability are inherent aptitude, Adjuster leading and help of spirit artisans (44:8.2) (160:4.11)
improvedDescription: Ability is an inherent gift or capacity that cannot be exceeded, distinguished from skill which is acquired through practice. The three sources of special human ability are natural aptitude, Thought Adjuster guidance, and assistance from spirit artisans.

name: Abimelech
originalDescription: Abraham made converts among the people of Abimelek, a people who had many superstitions and practiced the sacrifice of first-born sons (93:9.4)
improvedDescription: Abimelech was a leader whose people Abraham converted to his teachings, though they retained many superstitions including the practice of sacrificing first-born sons.

name: Abiram
originalDescription: He was the son of a king who was sacrificed in the construction of walls of Jericho with his brother (89:6.6)
improvedDescription: Abiram was the first-born son of a petty king in Palestine who was sacrificed alive in the foundation of Jericho's walls, along with his younger brother Segub who was placed in the gate foundations.

name: Abner
originalDescription: Abner was the chief of John’s the Baptist loyal supporters (144:6.1-3) he had been the head of Engedi Nazarite colony (135:2.4) (142:8.1) (165:0.1) he had his home at Sebaste (144:9.1) he became the head of Philadelphia church (166:5) (168:5.3) during Jesus life after Jesus death
improvedDescription: Abner was the chief of John the Baptist's loyal supporters and former head of the Engedi Nazarite colony. After John's death, he became head of seventy gospel messengers and later led the Philadelphia church, remaining faithful to Jesus' original teachings throughout his 89-year life.

name: abodes
originalDescription: Jesus repeated several times to his followers that there are many other abodes or residences in Father’s universe, referring without clarification to other inhabited planets and architectural worlds (133:4.9) (178:3.4) (180:3.4) (180:6.3) (181:1.2)
improvedDescription: Abodes refer to the many dwelling places or stopping-places in the Father's universe that Jesus promised his followers, including other inhabited planets and architectural worlds where ascending mortals continue their eternal progression toward Paradise.

name: aborigines (Australian)
originalDescription: Some of these aborigines, we are told, have not made much progress since the ancient days of hunting (68:5.4) These Aboriginal Australians of today perpetuate fear of ghosts, fear of the dark, and ancestor veneration, with a clan-centered religion (92:6.1) (103:3.1) In them you can see today the remains of ancient antisocial peoples (68:1.6) they never developed a tribal government (70:4.9) they never had slaves (69:8.7) in some tribes of Australia early religious prayers preceded belief in spirits (91:0.3) In these tribes, their superstitions prevent women from hunting and men from stooping to pull out a root, as it is considered bad luck. (84:3.6)
improvedDescription: Indigenous peoples of Australia who remain at primitive stages of human development. They have made limited progress since ancient hunting days, perpetuating fear of ghosts, ancestor veneration, and clan-centered religion. These Aboriginal Australians represent remains of ancient antisocial peoples who never developed tribal government or used slaves.

name: aborigines (Indian)
originalDescription: They are not representative of the primitive peoples who populated India, but form the fringe of southeastern India that was not absorbed by Andites or Aryans (79:2.2)
improvedDescription: Indigenous peoples of India who are not representative of the primitive peoples who originally populated India, but rather form the most inferior southern and eastern fringe that was never fully absorbed by either the early Andites or their later appearing Aryan cousins.

name: abortion
originalDescription: A child has been in existence about nine months before it experiences *birth*. This is a statement that seems to indicate the existence of a child at conception (103:2.1) abortion has been a very common practice after the taboo on childbirth among unmarried (68:6.9) the human embryo is a transient parasitic stage of human life (112:5.4)
improvedDescription: A practice that became very common after the establishment of taboos on childbirth among the unmarried. Many races learned techniques of abortion, and among primitive clans, this practice along with infanticide threatened group survival, though maternal affection typically prevented destruction of children after they had been suckled.

name: Abraham (Old Testament)
originalDescription: His original name was Abram and he changed it to Abraham when his covenant with Melchizedek took on a formal aspect. (93:6.5) Indeed, three celestial beings appeared to him (93:6.7) Abraham was an ancestor of Joseph, the father of Jesus (122:1.1) he was born at Ur, but left because of sun worship settled there (133:9.2) (142:3.4) In Egipt In Canaan His wifes The covenant with Melquisedek Jews as Abraham descendants Named by Jesus and John
improvedDescription: The Hebrew patriarch originally named Abram who changed his name to Abraham when his covenant with Melchizedek took on a formal aspect. He was born at Ur but left due to sun worship, became a chosen individual responsible for keeping alive the truth of one God, and was an ancestor of Joseph, the father of Jesus. Abraham was a wealthy and efficient businessman who attended the Salem school and became one of Melchizedek's most brilliant pupils.

name: Abraham the Pharisee
originalDescription: Abraham was baptized by Abner (148:8.1) he gave all worldly goods to apostolic treasury (148:8.5) he was an influential Sanhedrist; but espoused teachings of Jesus (148:8.1) religious leaders frantic over conversion of Abraham (149:3.2)
improvedDescription: A young and influential member of the Sanhedrin who publicly espoused the teachings of Jesus and was baptized in the pool of Siloam by Abner. His conversion caused great excitement in Jerusalem and he gave all his worldly goods to the apostolic treasury, which enabled the immediate sending forth of newly trained evangelists.

name: Abram
improvedDescription: The original name of the Hebrew patriarch who later changed his name to Abraham when his covenant with Melchizedek took on a formal aspect.

name: Absalom
originalDescription: Absalom was one of the sons of King David. We are informed that his mother was Canaanite and that he would today be called a demagogue. Absalom was an aspirant to the throne that Solomon finally occupied (97:9.15)
improvedDescription: One of the sons of King David whose mother was Canaanite. He was an aspirant to the throne that Solomon finally occupied and would today be called a demagogue. Absalom was among the half dozen contenders for David's throne.

name: absent landlord (parable)
originalDescription: this is one of the parables of Jesus, Mk 12:1-12; Mt 21:33-46; Lk 20:9-19 (173:4)
improvedDescription: One of the parables told by Jesus to the chief Pharisees, scribes, and listening multitude, following the story of the two sons. This parable appears in the Gospel accounts of Mark, Matthew, and Luke.

name: absoluta
originalDescription: also known as cosmic force; space potency; space-force; space energy; mother force of space; universal force-charge of space (0:6.5) (11:5.8) (11:8.5) (15:4.1) (42:2.1-6) (42:4.5) (42:5.14-16) (42:8.2) (42:10.1) definitions origin its transformations
improvedDescription: The term used on Uversa for space potency, which is the unquestioned free space presence of the Unqualified Absolute. This represents the universe force-space potential that is prereality and responsive only to the personal grasp of the Universal Father, being the domain from which all cosmic force and energy manifestations originate.

name: Absolute
originalDescription: this person is an evolution of Buddhist concept of Buddha Eternal, the best aproximation of buddists to a divine concept (94:11.7-11) he is shoen as an impersonal and co-ordinate reaction of the Trinity to all spatial and universal situations, but it is not valid see him as an ancestor of the Trinity because the Trinity is infinite and eternal (56:9.4) in infinity and existentially, there is one the Absolute, although in subinfnite levels there are three Absolutes (56:9.1) (106:8.17) science suggests existence of Absolute when affirms the existence of a First Cause (101:2.7) it is a term never used in The Urantia Book as negation of aught or as impersonal Deity (0:11.16)
improvedDescription: A concept that evolved in Buddhist philosophy as the Absolute Buddha or Eternal One of infinite existence, representing the highest Buddhist approximation of divine reality. In universe philosophy, the Absolute is understood as the impersonal and coordinate reaction of the Trinity to all basic space situations, though in infinity there is ultimately one Absolute while on subinfinite levels there are three Absolutes.

name: Absolute Buddha
improvedDescription: A Buddhist concept referring to the ultimate, unconditioned state of enlightenment or awakening beyond all relative existence.

name: Absolute Deity
improvedDescription: The absolute aspect of Deity, representing the unlimited and unconditioned nature of divine reality.

name: absolute level
improvedDescription: The highest level of reality characterized by unlimited, unconditioned existence beyond time and space.

name: absolute mind (mind)
improvedDescription: The unconditioned, unlimited aspect of mind that transcends all finite and experiential forms of consciousness.

name: Absolute Mind (person)
improvedDescription: The Infinite Spirit as the personification of absolute mind, representing the unlimited mental aspect of deity.

name: Absolute Person
improvedDescription: The Eternal Son as the absolute expression of divine personality, representing perfect and unlimited personal reality.

name: Absolutes
originalDescription: of actuality of potentiality the seven Absolutes (105:3) the three Absolutes (0:11)
improvedDescription: The seven eternal, coordinate realities that constitute the foundation of all existence: the Universal Father, Eternal Son, Infinite Spirit, Isle of Paradise, Deity Absolute, Universal Absolute, and Unqualified Absolute. These seven Absolutes are unqualifiedly eternal and represent the premise of all reality, functioning as the basic foundations for all universe reality.

name: absolutism
originalDescription: Son, Spirit, and Trinity liberate Father from personality absolutism (9:0.1) (10:0.1) (10:2.1) (10:3.7)
improvedDescription: The state of being bound by unlimited, unconditioned perfection, from which the Universal Father is liberated through the personalization of the Son, Spirit, and Trinity, allowing for diverse expressions of divine will and personality.

name: absolutum
originalDescription: absolutum is the substance of Paradise; it is the original nonspiritual expression of Father, and is without duplicate (11:2.9) it is a stationary homogeneous organization of space potency not found outside Paradise (11:2.9)
improvedDescription: The unique material substance of Paradise, described as a homogeneous organization of space potency that is neither dead nor alive. It represents the original nonspiritual expression of the First Source and Center and exists nowhere else in the universe of universes.

name: absonite level
improvedDescription: A transcendent level of reality that exists beyond the finite but below the absolute, characterized by supertime and transcended space.

name: absoniters
improvedDescription: An order of transcendental beings with absonite nature, related to the Transcendentalers.

name: absonites
improvedDescription: Beings or entities characterized by absonite nature, existing at a transcendental level of reality between the finite and absolute levels.

name: absonity
originalDescription: absonitized beings transcend time and space (30:1.114) (31:2.1) God the Ultimate may endow midsoniters with the transcendental and eternal spirit of absonity (36:4.8) the limit of absonity was reached with the 28,012th Master Architect (31:9.10) these beings are neither infinity nor absoluteness (30:1.92) Absonity and divinity are the progressive course that Paradise Arrivals must study (30:4.31)
improvedDescription: A transcendental level of reality that exists between the finite and absolute levels, characterized by the ability to transcend time and space. Absonite beings are neither finite nor infinite, neither infinity nor absoluteness, representing a progressive course toward divinity that Paradise arrivals must study.

name: abstraction
originalDescription: philosophic abstraction purely subjective is devoid of love (16:9.8) when trying to invent a religion it would be considered that one cannot serve an abstraction (102:7.3) Philosophers commit their gravest error when they are misled into the fallacy of abstraction (2:7.5) science fights for deliverance from bondage of abstraction (12:9.5)
improvedDescription: A philosophical concept or practice that can become problematic when divorced from spiritual reality. Philosophers commit grave error when misled by the fallacy of abstraction, focusing on one isolated aspect as the whole truth, while purely subjective philosophical abstraction is devoid of love.

name: accidents
originalDescription: the belief in so-called accidents is substituted by a law wherein all effects are preceded by definite causes (86:2.5) accidents, as time passes, always work out for welfare and progress of the universes (10:7.5-6) believers are not immune to accidents (159:3.13) Blind and unforeseen accidents do not occur in the cosmos (48:7.9) if psychic circles not attained due to unavoidable accident, a probation time is granted in mansion world (112:5.6) Material accidents are not arbitrarily interfered with by celestial personalities (123:4.7) primitive man did not believe in accidents (86:2.6) spirit-born individuals know that catastropghes are but redirecting cataclysms which wreck temporal creations preliminary to the rearing of more enduring realities (100:2.8) to God-knowing believer, does not matter if all things earthly crash (100:2.7) Jesus teachings how to end them
improvedDescription: Seemingly random or chance occurrences that primitive peoples viewed as intentional spiritual interventions. While believers are not immune to the accidents of time or natural catastrophes, blind and unforeseen accidents do not occur in the cosmos, and all such events eventually work out for the welfare and progress of the universes.

name: Achaia
originalDescription: Andrew the apostle was crucified in Achaia (139:1.12) Corinth was the capital of Achaia. Here Jesus passed during his travel through the Mediterranean (133:2.5) Luke write his gospel in Achaia (121:8.8)
improvedDescription: A Roman province in ancient Greece where Corinth served as the capital. The apostle Andrew was crucified in Patrae in Achaia, and Luke wrote his gospel there in 82 A.D.

name: achievement
originalDescription: all beings seek new achievement; there is always time for new achievements (25:7.2) (38:7.5) ambition, judgment, and wisdom are essentials of achievement (156:5.7) artistic achievement is effected by personal efforts (44:8.3) attained goals are simply milestones, but none are denied transient satisfaction from achievements(39:4.7) advancement grows out of the divine capacity for action, and achievement is the child of imaginative adventure (28:6.18) Father works to improve our earthly achievement, but especially our heavenly estate (147:3.3) greatness lies not in possessing strength, but in making wise and divine use of one’s strength (48:7.27) humor helps in earthly careers (48:4.13-14) we increase our success by not taking self too seriously (48:6.37) life is not real to those who cannot do one thing expertly (160:4.11) we need to learn to maintain a well-balanced poise in success (48:6.35) We will never be held responsible for the accomplishment of that which is beyond your abilities (171:8.10) the Father has predicated finite progress upon effort, creature achievement upon perseverance (115:7.2) psychic circle attainment augments success potential (110:6.19) purposeful service produces highest satisfaction (28:6.17) achievement is the real test of Havona (26:5.5) the recognition indicative of achievement is granted to all (20:8.3) it is the self element that exhausts, not the effort to achieve (48:6.37) sometimes ignorance is essential to achievement (13:1.6) (39:5.9) (138:7.1) Lucifer rebellion threatened the thrill of adding something personal and unique to experiential wisdom (54:2.3) Your power and achievement is “according to his mercy, through the renewing of the Spirit.”  (34:6.5) Van’s achievement resulted from clear thinking, unselfish purpose, discipline, dedication to Father (67:3.6) when man and God enter into partnership, no limitation can be placed upon future possibilities (118:5.2) Adjusters material success teamwork | groups:value of groups
improvedDescription: The realization of goals through personal effort, imagination, and divine partnership. True achievement is the child of imaginative adventure, grows out of divine capacity for action, and requires wisdom, judgment, and perseverance, with recognition that all beings are held responsible only for accomplishments within their abilities.

name: acknowledgments
originalDescription: acknowledgments are done by authors of Urantia Papers in some papers (0:12.13-14) (121:0.1) (121:8.12-14)
improvedDescription: Formal recognition of sources and assistance used by authors of The Urantia Papers. The midwayer author of Jesus' life narrative extensively acknowledges drawing from human sources, concepts, and expressions from over two thousand individuals who lived from Jesus' time to the inditing of the revelations.

name: Acropolis
originalDescription: under this hill the apostle Paul preached about “the Unknown God.” (195:1.1)
improvedDescription: The ancient hilltop citadel of Athens, Greece, under whose shadow the Apostle Paul preached to the Athenians about the Unknown God at the council of the Areopagus.

name: Act of God
originalDescription: children of Spirit are truly the “Act of God.” (10:3.3)
improvedDescription: A divine designation referring specifically to the children of the Infinite Spirit, who are truly the Act of God, while the divine Sons are the Word of God.

name: action
originalDescription: defined as Benefits from action evils of inaction pray requires act we must act correctly
improvedDescription: A concept fundamental to spiritual and evolutionary progress, representing the essential link between faith and experience. Action is required to transform spiritual potentials into actuals, make decisions meaningful, and achieve personal growth and cosmic advancement.

name: Actium
originalDescription: Actium is the scene of a resounding victory for Emperor Augustus in his battle against Marco Antonio. To commemorate this fact, Augustus founded Nicopolis, which means “city of victory.” (133:2.5)
improvedDescription: The site of a decisive naval battle where Emperor Augustus defeated Mark Antony, commemorated by Augustus founding the nearby city of Nicopolis, meaning 'city of victory.'

name: actors
originalDescription: heavenly reproducers and celestial artisans are scenic actors (44:2.6-10)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who serve as dramatic performers in heavenly pageants and spectacular presentations, often collaborating with reversion directors in morontia and spirit assemblies.

name: Acts, Book of
originalDescription: book written by Luke that he left unfinished because he died in A.D. 90 (121:8.8-9)
improvedDescription: The second book planned by Luke, the physician of Antioch, as part of his three-volume work on Christ and Christianity. Luke died in A.D. 90 before completing this work about the apostles' activities.

name: acts of God
originalDescription: inexplicable things sometimes termed as “acts of God” (85:0.4)
improvedDescription: A term used to describe inexplicable natural phenomena and powerful forces that primitive humans could not comprehend, such as storms, earthquakes, and other mysterious manifestations.

name: actual
originalDescription: in phases of reality we have actual, potential, and undisclosed (0:4.7)
improvedDescription: One of the three phases of reality, representing realities existing in fullness of expression, in contrast to potential realities which carry undisclosed capacity for growth.

name: Actual, the
originalDescription: The Actual is the triodity of actuality, the union of Son, Spirit and Paradise (104:5.2) (115:3.7-11) (115:3.16) (115:7.4)
improvedDescription: The triodity of actuality consisting of the Eternal Son, Infinite Spirit, and Paradise Isle - the union of the three Absolutes of actuality that constitutes the actual revelation of the First Source and Center's originality.

name: Adam
originalDescription: Adam, along with his consort, Eve, are the Material Sons of Jerusem in charge of acting as the initiators of the adamic race on Urantia, following a plan that is thus carried out on all inhabited planets. on Jerusem (74:1) warned in Jerusem arrival of Adam and Eve children default of Adam(75) after default inheritance of Adam and Eve physical nature of Adam and Eve teachings about Adam and Eve Urantia problem for Adam and Eve (75:1)
improvedDescription: The Planetary Adam of Urantia, a Material Son from Jerusem who, along with his consort Eve, was tasked with biologically uplifting the human races. Despite their default and subsequent mortality, they made significant contributions to human civilization and racial improvement.

name: Adam (original of Satania)
originalDescription: is the overseeing chief of the Material Sons of Satania (45:3.16) this Adam and his consort Eve his are the only two in Satania who are immortal by inherent endowment. His offspring require mental synchronism with the Spirit in order to be immortal (51:1.5)
improvedDescription: The supervising head and original Material Son of Satania, after whom all subsequent Material Sons are named. Unlike their progeny, this original Adam and his consort Eve possess inherent immortality.

name: Adam (village)
originalDescription: In this town east of the Jordan, also called Adam, is where John the Baptist spoke his words of attack against Herod Antipas (135:10.2) John the Baptist passed through this town as he traveled north along the Jordan River (135:7.2)
improvedDescription: A village east of the Jordan River where John the Baptist made his memorable attack on Herod Antipas and first spoke of 'another one who is to come after me,' referring to Jesus.

name: Adamic dispensation
improvedDescription: A planetary epoch that begins with the arrival of a Material Son and Daughter on an evolutionary world, marking a new phase in planetary spiritual and biological development.

name: Adamic Sons
improvedDescription: Material Sons who serve as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds, working alongside Material Daughters to improve the physical and genetic heritage of planetary races.

name: Adamites
originalDescription: the violet race in the Garden migrations benefits of Adam bestowal
improvedDescription: The violet race founded by Adam and Eve on Urantia, characterized by fair complexions, light hair, and blue eyes. They were the ninth human race to appear on Urantia and served as biologic uplifters, contributing their superior life plasm to the evolutionary races through careful interbreeding programs.

name: Adamson
originalDescription: Adanson was the first child of Adam and Eve on Urantia (74:6.2) (76:3.3) (80:7.5) along with a company of 27 relatives Adanson migrated north from the second Garden (77:5.5) his first wife and his 32 children went to Edentia after the absence of Adam and Eve (77:5.3) Adanson founded a new civilization around 35,000 BC. (77:4.13) he grew wheat and barley (81:1.7) lived 396 years (77:5.7) traditions about Adanson survived in Greece (98:1.1) Adanson and Ratta
improvedDescription: The first-born child of Adam and Eve on Urantia who founded a secondary center of violet race civilization north of the second Eden. He lived 396 years and, together with his wife Ratta, became the progenitor of the secondary midwayers and established a high culture that influenced later civilizations.

name: Adamsonites
originalDescription: the Adansonites were the discoverers of bronze (81:3.5) the Adansonite seat was east of the southern shore of the Caspian Sea, near the Kopet Mountains (78:1.3) the Adansonites, led by Sato, a direct descendant of Adamson and Ratta, settled in Greece in 10,000 BC, and formed the ancestors of the Greeks (80:7.3-4) The Adansonites are the descendants of Adamson, son of Adam and Eve, and Ratta, a surviving Nodite. They maintained a high culture for 7,000 years. They mixed with Nodites and Andonites but their culture survived into later European civilization (77:5.9) Together with the Adamites they formed the violet race, one of the nine pure races that have existed on Earth (78:1.3)
improvedDescription: The descendants of Adamson and Ratta who maintained a high culture for almost seven thousand years in their headquarters east of the Caspian Sea. They were the discoverers of bronze and eventually migrated to Greece, becoming the ancestors of the Greeks and contributing to European civilization.

name: Aden
originalDescription: Aden is a strange preacher of the gospel who was converted by Amos of Jeresa, the madman whom Jesus had supposedly cured of a possession. The apostle John forbade him to preach, but he ignored it and managed to gather a considerable group of believers, continuing his preaching in Mesopotamia (159:2)
improvedDescription: A preacher who taught in Jesus' name and claimed to cast out devils, despite never having been with the apostles. When John forbade his activities, Jesus instructed that he should not be hindered, teaching the principle that "he who is not against us is for us."

name: Adirondack Mountains
originalDescription: The Adirondack Mountains are part of an ancient east-west ridge of fossil-bearing rock that stretches from Pennsylvania through Michigan, Wisconsin, and Minnesota. (58:7.4)
improvedDescription: An ancient mountain range in North America that forms part of a primitive fossil-bearing rock ridge extending from Pennsylvania through the Great Lakes region to Minnesota.

name: adjudication
originalDescription: Adjudication is the highest function of any government, and those who are intrusted with verdict rendering should be chosen from the highest and most noble types of the most experienced and understanding individuals. (22:4.3)
improvedDescription: The highest function of any government involving the rendering of verdicts and judgments. Those entrusted with adjudication should be chosen from the most experienced, understanding, and noble individuals available.

name: Adjuster-expression
improvedDescription: The manifestation or communication of divine guidance through the indwelling Thought Adjuster's influence on mortal consciousness and decision-making.

name: Adjuster-fused mortals
improvedDescription: Ascending mortals who have achieved eternal fusion with their indwelling Thought Adjusters, representing one of the three orders of fusion that characterize the survival careers of evolutionary beings.

name: Adjuster fusion
improvedDescription: The process by which a mortal's soul permanently unites with their indwelling Thought Adjuster, achieving spiritual immortality and becoming an agondonter of the finaliter corps.

name: adjutant mind-spirits
originalDescription: defined as Spirit #1 – Intuition Spirit #2 – Understanding Spirit #3 – Courage Spirit #4 – Knowledge (62:6.3-6) Spirit #5 – Counsel Spirit #6 – Worship (62:6.3-6) (65:7.7) (85:7) Spirit #7 – Wisdom (65:7.7) (85:7) their ministry headquarters function
improvedDescription: Seven mind-spirits created by the Divine Minister of each local universe that minister to material minds from animal to human levels. Named intuition, understanding, courage, knowledge, counsel, worship, and wisdom, they guide evolutionary development and prepare minds for spiritual ministry, with the first five functioning in animals and all seven in human intellect.

name: adjutants of Havona circuits
originalDescription: each Circuit Spirit has twelve of these helpers. We mortals of Urantia will one day be brought before the seventh Circuit Spirit, that of Orvonton, and before that his twelve adjutants of circuits. There we will have to prove our compliance with the requirements to advance towards Havona (26:5.5) In the classification of beings they are found within the so-called Supreme Spirits that are part of the beings of a single origin, descendants of a single Deity (30:1.59)
improvedDescription: Twelve helper beings who serve each of the seven Circuit Spirits of Havona. Ascending mortals must pass tests before these twelve adjutants of their superuniverse's Circuit Spirit to advance from one Havona circle to the next.

name: administrative assistants
originalDescription: They are part of the fourth order of Seraphim, the administrator Seraphim, which are destined for the administrative tasks of the local systems. They serve System Sovereigns and Planetary Princes. Currently a thousand of them serve on Urantia (39:4.2-3)
improvedDescription: Able seraphim of the fourth order who serve as immediate assistants to System Sovereigns and Planetary Princes. They execute administrative details, serve as personal agents for system rulers, and travel between worlds on missions for system welfare. One thousand of this order currently assist Urantia's acting ruler.

name: administrative enactors
originalDescription: They are one of the seven groups of beings called Heavenly Reproducers, who are dedicated to reproducing things of supreme value on the morontia level. Administrative enactors are those who describe the importance of governmental philosophy and administrative technique, the heavenly dramatists of sovereignty. (44:2.9)
improvedDescription: One of seven groups of Heavenly Reproducers who specialize in depicting the significance of governmental philosophy and administrative technique. They are celestial dramatists of sovereignty who reproduce supreme governmental values on the morontia level.

name: administrator seraphim
originalDescription: the acting ruler of Urantia is assisted by a thousand of them (39:4.3) they form the fourth order of the Seraphim and are assigned to the administrative tasks of the local systems. They are native to systems capitals but are also found in architectural spheres and inhabited worlds (39:4.1) many of this order were lost in the Lucifer rebellion. It was the most damaged order. Manotia was a loyal angel who managed to save two-thirds of them, but a third were lost. On Urantia the situation was more disastrous as about half of the Seraphim administrators supported the cause of Lucifer. (53:6.2,4) (53:7.6) (67:3.2) Their residence is in the Jerusem circles, specifically in the fourth circle, and they constitute a very large group (46:5.22) types (39:4.2)
improvedDescription: The fourth order of seraphim assigned to administrative duties in local systems. Native to system capitals but stationed throughout mansion worlds and inhabited planets, they possess unusual administrative ability and assist in universe government affairs. Many were lost during the Lucifer rebellion, with about half of Urantia's administrator seraphim joining the revolt.

name: Adonai
originalDescription: It was one of the appellation with which the Semites avoided the use of the name of their Deity, a name that they considered taboo (96:1.9)
improvedDescription: One of the appellations used by the Semites to avoid speaking the name of their Deity, which they considered taboo. Along with other titles like The Lord, The Almighty, and The Most High, it allowed reverent reference to God without pronouncing the sacred name.

name: Adonia
originalDescription: It was the commercial metropolis of central Asia near Ashkhabad. This trade was promoted by the Andite tribes, who preferred manufacturing to herding (79:1.4)
improvedDescription: The commercial metropolis of central Asia, located near present-day Ashkhabad. It served as a major trade center where migrating Andites established commerce in stone, metal, wood, and pottery, preferring urban manufacturing to herding as climatic conditions made hunting unprofitable.

name: adoption
originalDescription: in antiquity it was the custom to kill all strangers; to gain friendship you need to obtain adoption into the clan (70:3.6) ancient ceremonies of adoption mean blood drinking or saliva exchange (70:3.6-8) this custom made sure that someone be available after death for doing offerings (87:3.3) Prince’s staff kept five hundred adopted children in homes (66:7.5) To sojourn in the Garden a Urantian had to be “adopted.” (74:7.11)
improvedDescription: An ancient practice involving ceremonial blood drinking or saliva exchange to gain clan membership and friendship. Originally used to avoid killing strangers, adoption ensured someone would provide post-death offerings for the soul and was practiced in various forms including the adoption of children into superior households.

name: adultery
originalDescription: ancient puberty ceremonies taught boys to avoid adultery (70:7.11) Adultery was recognized as a form of stealing, an infringement of the husband’s property rights. The reason for holding the wife to stricter sex account than the husband was because her marital infidelity involved descent and inheritance (82:4.3-4) many ancient laws expressed commandments against adultery (66:7.14) (93:4.12) (94:8.12) (131:2.12) (140:6.5) (163:2.4) there existed an infidelity test from Old Testament that required women to drink a nasty potion (70:10.6) primitive wives took pride in husbands’ affairs with other women (82:3.13) primitives believed multiple births were caused by magic or infidelity, so they usually killed one of the twins after birth (68:6.8) in the past it was believed that a woman who died in childbirth, specially with twins, was guilty of adultery (84:4.7) in John gospel is the tale of a woman taken in adultery brought to Jesus by scribes and Pharisees. Scholars discuss if this event actually took place and The Urantia Book states that really happened, detailing the name of the adulteress, Hildana (162:3)
improvedDescription: Recognized in ancient times as a form of stealing and infringement of the husband's property rights. Women were held to stricter sexual accountability than men because marital infidelity affected descent and inheritance. Various cultures developed tests and punishments for adultery, and it was prohibited in numerous ancient legal codes.

name: adventure
originalDescription: Andites contributed to this trait; they were adventurous; never stopped until they had circumnavigated the globe (78:4.6) (78:5.8) we should be patient and and be not tempted by cheap and sordid adventure (195:5.10) God has embarked on eternal adventure with man (5:1.12) love of adventure suggests that with death begins eternal discovery (14:5.10) High spirit personalities are not given to the gratification of purposeless curiosity, purely useless adventure. (13:3.3) supreme adventure is man seeking and finding God (1:0.6) (155:5.10)
improvedDescription: Adventure represents the quest for new experience and discovery, both material and spiritual. The Andites contributed this trait to human evolution through their roving dispositions and extensive explorations. Adventure encompasses the supreme human experience of seeking and finding God, as well as the eternal progression that begins with death and continues through endless spiritual discovery and growth.

name: advice
originalDescription: celestial beings never proffer advice unless asked (33:5.2) (43:4.2) (55:4.16) it is said in morontial mota that it must be given advice only when asked (48:7.16) in The Urantia Book is told Jesus’ counsel to a rich man when he asked what he would do with wealth (132:5) Jesus seldom offered advice unless asked (129:1.9) (132:5.2,14) Prince’s staff never sent emissaries except upon request (66:6.5)
improvedDescription: Advice should only be given when requested, according to both celestial protocol and human wisdom. High celestial beings, including Trinity Sons and other Paradise personalities, never proffer counsel unless asked. Similarly, Jesus seldom offered advice unless specifically requested, following the principle that wisdom should be shared only when the recipient genuinely desires guidance.

name: advisers regarding conquest of predatory animals
improvedDescription: Members of the Caligastia one hundred who provided guidance on controlling and managing dangerous animals during the early development of human civilization.

name: Advisory Commission on Ethics and Self-government
originalDescription: reserves of Faithfuls of Days function in Paradise as Advisory Commission on Ethics and Self-government (18:7.1)
improvedDescription: A Paradise organization composed of reserve Faithfuls of Days who serve as the Advisory Commission of Interuniverse Ethics and Self-government. These high Trinity-origin personalities provide guidance on ethical matters and governmental principles across the universes.

name: aesthetics
originalDescription: a sound religious philosophy does not recognize that aesthetic cult of pure wonder is a substitute for religion (101:7.5) The attainment of cosmologic levels of thought includes aesthetic appreciation, love of the beautiful (56:10.7)
improvedDescription: The appreciation of beauty and artistic expression, which represents one of the cosmologic levels of thought. While aesthetic appreciation and love of the beautiful are important aspects of spiritual development, sound religious philosophy recognizes that the aesthetic cult of pure wonder cannot serve as a substitute for genuine religion.

name: affectation
originalDescription: Jesus free from affectation; he was always refreshingly genuine (100:7.2) Affectation is the ridiculous effort of the ignorant to appear wise, the attempt of the barren soul to appear rich. (48:7.17)
improvedDescription: The ridiculous effort of the ignorant to appear wise or the attempt of the barren soul to appear rich. Jesus was completely free from affectation, being always refreshingly genuine and sincere without any pretense or shamming.

name: affection
originalDescription: enduring marriage never has been founded on biologic affection alone (84:7.10) personal affection is the spiritual bond which holds families together (160:2.4)
improvedDescription: Personal affection serves as the spiritual bond that holds families together and creates effective human relationships. While enduring marriages cannot be founded on biological affection alone, genuine personal affection between individuals, whether of opposite or same sex, forms the basis for lasting human associations and friendships.

name: Afghanistan
originalDescription: It is in the mountainous regions of Afghanistan that for more than half a million years the most important center of primitive culture survived, founded by Badonan, a descendant of the first human beings. (79:0.1)
improvedDescription: A mountainous region in Asia where Badonan, a descendant of the first human beings Andon and Fonta, founded a primitive center of culture that persisted for over half a million years. This area served as an important focal point for early human development and cultural evolution.

name: Africa
originalDescription: geology evolution evolution and race migrations costums and believes gospel in Africa
improvedDescription: The continent where early human races, particularly the secondary Sangik peoples including the green, orange, and indigo races, migrated and established various civilizations. Africa played a significant role in geological evolution, race migrations, cultural development, and later became a field for gospel teaching through various missionaries and teachers.

name: Agaman
originalDescription: Agaman was a widow from Damascus who was part of the women’s corps that Jesus instructed in parallel to that of the twelve apostles (150:1.1) she was present at the sixteenth morontia appearance of Jesus (193:0.1)
improvedDescription: A widow from Damascus who was among the ten women commissioned by Jesus for the ministering work of the kingdom. She served in the women's corps that provided parallel ministry to the twelve apostles and was present at Jesus' sixteenth morontia appearance in Jerusalem.

name: Agni
originalDescription: Agni, the god of fire, was an ancient Aryan deity who was often exalted as the head of the Vedic pantheon (94:1.4) (94:4.6) Around 16,000 BC India was almost conquered to the idea of the Trinity but then in five thousand years it degenerated into the triune symbol of the fire god, Agni. In the early second millennium BC the Aryan gods organized themselves under the leadership of Dyaus pitar, of the sky, Indra, of the air, and Agni, of the fire. Agni, the three-headed god of fire, was long one of the few remnants of the ideas about the Trinity that Adam and Eve and later Melchizedek began to spread in ancient times (79:3.4) (94:1.3) (104:1.2)
improvedDescription: Agni was the ancient Aryan fire god, often exalted as the head of the Vedic pantheon. He was depicted as a three-headed deity who served as lord of the earth and represented a vestigial symbol of earlier Trinity concepts that had degenerated from the original monotheistic teachings brought to India by Sethite priests around 16,000 BC.

name: agondonters
originalDescription: ascendant beings, like humans, living in planetary isolation are known by the name agondonters, which means evolutionary will creatures that can believe without seeing and persevere when isolated. This is the case with us, the inhabitants of Urantia. Along with the other isolated worlds, we are assigned our own residential sector on Jerusem and are soon entrusted with special tasks involving great trust. Tabamantia is an agondonter with finaliter status who survived in one of the quarantined spheres involved in the first rebellion that has ever taken place. (50:7.2)
improvedDescription: Agondonters are ascendant evolutionary will creatures who can believe without seeing, persevere when isolated, and triumph over insuperable difficulties even when alone. These beings from quarantined worlds occupy their own residential sector on Jerusem and maintain this functional grouping throughout their ascension until reaching Paradise, where they join the Corps of the Mortal Finality.

name: agriculture
originalDescription: ancient peoples, notably the Chinese, discover agriculture accidentally (81:1.6) Andites were expert farmers (78:5.8) agriculture appeared in age of Planetary Prince (52:2.8) (68:5.9) the growing of plants exerts an ennobling influence; association with plants instills patience, quiet, and peace (68:5.9,11) (79:8.2) Cain, the son of Eve and Cano, was farmer (76:2.2) farmer is victim of drought, floods, hail, storms, pests, diseases, heat, and cold. Thar brought ideas about chance and luck (86:1.5) farmers are conservative (79:8.3) the coming of agriculture has enhanced woman’s prestige and social standing up to the time man himself turned agriculturist and not only warrior. When men displaced women and applied war techniques learned at war, they greatly improved agriculture (84:3.10) agriculture is the highest blessing; most human of all activities (66:7.19) (68:5.9,13) (81:1.4-5) hunters and herders looked down upon farmers (68:5.11) (70:8.7) (81:1.4) institutions of slavery and private land came with agriculture (69:9.14) (81:1.4) (81:2.13) agriculture is universal on all atmospheric worlds (49:4.5) weakness of agriculture and industry is lack of excitement and adventure (68:5.11)
improvedDescription: Agriculture is the cultivation of plants and soil, representing the highest type of material civilization and the most human of all activities. It appeared during the age of the Planetary Prince and was taught by both Caligastia and Adam, with the growing of plants exerting an ennobling influence that instills patience, quiet, and peace in all races of mankind.

name: Ahab
originalDescription: King of Ephraim; was reproved by the prophet Elijah (123:5.12) killed the Naboths to get the land from him (97:3.6) (97:9.19) It was his Phoenician wife who forged the papers ordering the confiscation of the Naboth lands on charges of blasphemy (97:9.19) resisted against King Shalmanasar III at Karkar in a battle that ended in a draw (97:9.18)
improvedDescription: Ahab was a king of Ephraim who was reproved by the prophet Elijah for his moral transgressions. He murdered Naboth to seize his land, with his Phoenician wife forging papers to confiscate Naboth's property on charges of blasphemy, and later resisted King Shalmaneser III at the battle of Karkar.

name: Ahura-Mazda
originalDescription: is the god at the head of the constellation of the seven gods of Zoroaster (95:6.2) the Sun God Invictus was a degeneration of Ahura-Mazda (98:5.3)
improvedDescription: Ahura-Mazda was the supreme god at the head of Zoroaster's constellation of seven deities. This concept later degenerated into Sol Invictus, the sun god of Mithraism, representing the evolution and corruption of Zoroastrian religious ideas.

name: Ai
originalDescription: The attack on Ai, the city that Joshua completely annihilated with all its inhabitants and its king, is a faithful example of the barbaric massacres of ancient times, before the enslavement of the prisoners of war made these practices some progress (69:8.3)
improvedDescription: Ai was an ancient city that Joshua completely destroyed in a barbaric massacre, killing all inhabitants including men, women, and children, with only the king spared to gratify the conqueror's vanity. This attack exemplified the brutal warfare practices before the more merciful custom of enslaving prisoners of war developed.

name: Akkad
originalDescription: It is one of the city-states of Mesopotamia that once had sovereignty over the region (78:8.9-10)
improvedDescription: Akkad was one of the prominent city-states of ancient Mesopotamia that once held sovereignty over the region. It was part of the Kish confederation and later fell to northern invaders around 2500 BC, after which the Mesopotamian Andites passed from history.

name: Alabama
originalDescription: In Alabama, coal layers reveal a history of land subsidence and uplift, with thirty-five distinct layers, more than Illinois and Pennsylvania but fewer than Canada. (58:7.4) (59:5.17)
improvedDescription: Alabama is a North American state whose geological record contains thirty-five distinct coal layers, indicating multiple cycles of land subsidence and uplift throughout Earth's history. These coal beds contain both fresh-water and salt-water fossils.

name: Alaska
originalDescription: we are told that red race arrived to Alaska (64:7.18)
improvedDescription: Alaska is the northwesternmost region of North America where the red race first arrived over 80,000 years ago. It forms part of the geological ridge system extending from Alaska to Mexico and was volcanically active during ancient mountain-forming periods.

name: alchemy
originalDescription: the honest search that gave birth to science turned alchemy into chemistry (81:2.9)
improvedDescription: Alchemy was an early proto-scientific practice that sought to transform base materials. Through honest and fearless search for true causes, alchemy evolved into the modern science of chemistry, just as astrology became astronomy and magic became medicine.

name: Alexander (brother of Philo)
originalDescription: He was a Jewish banker from Alexandria, brother of the famous philosopher Philo, with whom Gonod, the merchant who hired Jesus during his trip to the Mediterranean, had business (130:3.9)
improvedDescription: A Jewish banker from Alexandria and brother of the famous philosopher Philo, with whom Gonod conducted business during Jesus' Mediterranean journey.

name: Alexander of Cyrene
originalDescription: was an effective teacher of the gospel in Africa, son of Simon of Cyrene (187:1.10)
improvedDescription: Son of Simon of Cyrene who became an effective teacher of the gospel in Africa after his father carried Jesus' crossbeam to the crucifixion.

name: Alexander the Great
originalDescription: Facts about Alexander the Great mentioned in The Urantia Book
improvedDescription: Macedonian king who expanded Greece into an empire stretching from the Adriatic to the Indus, spreading Hellenistic civilization throughout the near-Eastern world. His cultural influence helped prepare the way for Christianity's later expansion into Greek territories.

name: Alexandria
originalDescription: in Alexandria was the world’s largest synagogue, ruled by seventy elders (130:3.8) in Jesus times this city of one million people had a good number of attractions (130:3.4,7-8) Hebrew scriptures were translated into Greek at Alexandria (121:6.2) Ptolemy’s library had a million manuscripts (74:8.12) (130:3.4) Jesus and Alexandria christianism and Alexandria
improvedDescription: Major Egyptian city and center of blended Occidental culture, home to the world's largest library containing nearly a million manuscripts and the largest Jewish synagogue governed by seventy elders. Jesus and his family lived there for two years during his infancy, and he later visited during his Mediterranean journey.

name: algae, seaweed
originalDescription: algae are the ancestors of corals (59:2.11) there are fossils of algae (58:7.2) a big part of limestone was laid down by lime-secreting algae (59:2.3,11) there were virtually no free oxygen in the atmosphere until it was generated by the seaweeds and other vegetables (57:7.9) man’s ascends evolutively from algae (65:2.1) (65:6.8)
improvedDescription: Marine plant life that played crucial roles in early Earth history, generating oxygen in the atmosphere and laying down limestone deposits. Algae represent part of the evolutionary chain from which higher life forms, including humans, eventually developed.

name: Allah
originalDescription: Allah is the one and only Deity of Islam, a clear and well-defined concept in which the strength of Islam has resided. (95:7.6)
improvedDescription: The one and only Deity of Islam, representing a clear-cut and well-defined concept of universal divinity that has been the source of Islam's strength.

name: allegories
originalDescription: Jesus advised against use of allegories when using his parables (149:3.1) (151:2.1,7) (151:3.3)
improvedDescription: Extended metaphorical narratives that Jesus advised against using when teaching, preferring instead simple parables that illustrated single truths rather than complex symbolic stories requiring detailed interpretation.

name: alligators
originalDescription: alligators are descended from nonprogressive reptiles (65:2.10)
improvedDescription: Reptiles descended from nonprogressive reptilian lines, representing one of the four surviving divisions of the ancient reptile kingdom along with snakes, lizards, and turtles.

name: Almighty
improvedDescription: A term referring to the supreme power aspect of deity, often used in connection with the Supreme Being's evolutionary sovereignty over the grand universe.

name: Almighty Supreme
improvedDescription: The power-sovereignty aspect of the Supreme Being, representing the evolving almighty power of God the Supreme as it emerges through the experiences of time and space.

name: aloes
originalDescription: Jesus was embalmed with aloes (188:1.4)
improvedDescription: A fragrant spice used along with myrrh to embalm Jesus' body for burial, applied with bandages saturated in these solutions.

name: Alpha and Omega
originalDescription: God is the first and last (10:7.1) Jesus is Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last (128:1.10)
improvedDescription: A divine title meaning "the first and the last" or "the beginning and the end," used by God the Father and claimed by Jesus to signify eternal divine nature.

name: Alpheus
originalDescription: Alpheus is the father of apostles James and Judas Alpheus (138:4.1)
improvedDescription: The father of apostles James and Judas Alpheus, who were twin fishermen from Kheresa called by Jesus to be among the twelve apostles.

name: Alpheus twins
originalDescription: James and Judas Alpheus, also called the Alpheus twins, were two of Jesus' apostles his mother was named Mary and his father Alpheus (138:4.1) (189:4.4) (190:3.1) James or Jacob Alpheus or Thaddeus Judas Alpheus or Lebbeus Period with Jesus After the death of Jesus
improvedDescription: James and Judas Alpheus, twin brothers and fishermen from Kheresa who became two of Jesus' twelve apostles. They were 26 years old, married with children, and served as chief ushers managing crowds during Jesus' ministry. Though considered simple and mediocre by their fellow apostles, they were faithful, good-natured helpers who loved Jesus for his humility and simplicity.

name: Alpine race
improvedDescription: One of the three major divisions of the white race, representing a distinct ethnic group within the broader Caucasian racial classification.

name: Alps
originalDescription: The Alps is one of the places where the Heidelberg race spread (64:2.3) many plants and animals displaced by glaciers are found high in mountains, as in the case of the Alps (61:7.16) Large volcanic activities occurred south of the Alps during the Cretaceous (60:3.6)
improvedDescription: A major European mountain range where the Heidelberg race spread and where many plants and animals displaced by glaciers found refuge at high elevations. The Alps experienced significant geological activity, with limestone deposits forming the characteristic dolomite walls and peaks of the region.

name: altars
originalDescription: early Christian and Mithraic cults had altars (98:6.3) family hearths were altars for Andites, and Vedic peoples continued this practices in India (94:1.1) in ancient times it was beleived that altars were polluted if built of hewn stone (92:2.2)
improvedDescription: Sacred structures used for worship and sacrifice. Ancient peoples believed altars were polluted if built of hewn stone, preferring natural materials. Early Christian and Mithraic cults both used altars, and Andite families used their hearths as altars for religious purposes.

name: alter ego
originalDescription: childs when learn language etnd to relate with a fictitious alter ego (91:3.1) an effective prayer technique is to return to alter ego concept and then recognize that the idea of this alter ego has evolved from a fiction to the truth of God’s indwelling us in the Adjuster (91:3.7)
improvedDescription: A concept used in prayer technique where one relates to an imaginary companion or second self. Children naturally develop this when learning language, and mature prayer involves recognizing this alter ego concept has evolved from fiction to the truth of God's indwelling presence through the Thought Adjuster.

name: altruism
originalDescription: Adjuster gives origin to unselfish interest in welfare of others; leads to happiness (103:1.1) (103:2.7) (103:5.1,3) differences in status of races and groups are essential to development of tolerance and altruismo (64:6.34) Every human being very early experiences something of a conflict between his self-seeking and his altruistic impulses (103:2.4,7-10) Fire was a great civilizer, providing man with his first means of being altruistic without loss by enabling him to give live coals to a neighbor without depriving himself (69:6.3) altruism is learned in one’s family (84:7.28) it is more blessed to give than to receive (28:6.18) (100:7.10) (103:2.9) (140:8.18) noble soul is generous among the grasping (131:3.6) only religion lastingly increases altruism (99:1.5) only a well-unified personality can arbitrate multiform contentions regarding the ego cravings and altruism (103:5.4) overdeveloped altruism may seriously injure the self (103:2.10) reason, morality, and religion necessary for altruism (16:9.7-8) selflessness is inherent in parental love (2:6.4) Is altruism—service of one’s fellows—desirable? Then must life experience provide for encountering situations of social inequality. Is unselfishness—the spirit of self-forgetfulness—desirable? Then must mortal man live face to face with the incessant clamoring of an inescapable self for recognition and honor (3:5.7,13) It is neither tenderness nor altruism to bestow futile sympathy upon degenerated human beings, unsalvable abnormal and inferior mortals (52:2.12) unselfishness
improvedDescription: Unselfish concern for the welfare of others, originating from the divine spirit indwelling human minds through the Thought Adjuster. It represents the impulse to serve one's fellows rather than seeking personal gain, and is essential for social progress and spiritual development. True altruism requires the enlightenment of reason, morality, and religious consciousness.

name: Alvoring
originalDescription: Alvoring is a neighboring universe to Nebadon (32:2.12)
improvedDescription: One of the seven neighboring universes closest to Nebadon, located in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton.

name: Amadon
originalDescription: Outstanding human hero of the Lucifer rebellion; was Andonite and associate of Van, of Caligastia's entourage (67:3.8-9) (67:8) supported by 1-2-3 the first, the dean of Urantia's primary order of midwayers (77:9.5) Amadon is a clear example of Thought Adjuster-enhanced loyalty (67:3.7) remained on Urantia 150,000 years after the rebellion, until the arrival of Adam (67:6.4,8) welcomed Adam and Eve on their arrival (74:2.1,5) he was transferred to Jerusem seven years after the arrival of Adam and Eve (67:6.8) (74:5.2)
improvedDescription: Outstanding human hero of the Lucifer rebellion who was an Andonite and associate of Van from Caligastia's entourage. Amadon remained steadfast in loyalty to the universe government throughout the seven-year struggle, supported by the primary midwayer 1-2-3 the first, and remained on Urantia for 150,000 years after the rebellion until Adam and Eve arrived, when he was translated to Jerusem.

name: Amadonites
originalDescription: Amadonites were derived from the group of 144 loyal Andonites to which Amadon belonged, and who have become known by his name (67:6.3) (67:8.4) Fifty-six of this number were of immortality status, and all, except Amadon, were translated along with the loyal members of the staff. The remainder of this noble band continued on earth to the end of their mortal days under the leadership of Van and Amadon. (67:6.3) they were the descendants of Andonites who remained loyal with Van and Amadon; it is more cultural than racial term. Amadonites were esentially Andonites (73:1) they joined Nodites to become the Vanites (77:4.10) Nodites and Amadonites had a traditional enmity between them and they found exceedingly difficult to work together (73:1.4)
improvedDescription: A group derived from 144 loyal Andonites who remained faithful with Van and Amadon during the Lucifer rebellion and became known by Amadon's name. This group comprised 39 men and 105 women, with 56 of immortality status who were translated along with loyal staff members, while the remainder continued on earth under Van and Amadon's leadership as biological leaven for future generations.

name: Amatha
originalDescription: Amata is the mother of Perpetua, Simon Peter’s wife. She suffered from malaria, a disease that she usually presented with cyclical fevers, an erroneous reason why it was considered that Jesus cured her immediately while he comforted her in her bed. However, Jesus did miraculously heal her hours later in the context of the massive healing that occurred at sunset. (145:2.15-16)
improvedDescription: Simon Peter's mother-in-law who suffered from malarial fever. She was not miraculously healed when Jesus first comforted her at her bedside, but was cured several hours later during the extraordinary healing event that occurred at sunset in the Zebedee home's front yard.

name: Amathus
originalDescription: population of Perea, west of Gerasa and south of Pella, near Ragaba. It was one of the towns visited during the first public works trip of Jesus and the twelve, between January 19 and April 6, 27. They stayed in Amathus for three weeks (141:3.1) (141:4.1) (141:5.1) (141:6.1,5) It was one of the towns visited during the Perea mission of the seventy, between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1) There Jesus healed ten lepers to show his apostles how wrong they were in regarding some peoples as incapable of accepting the gospel. (166:2.1,8)
improvedDescription: A town in Perea, west of Gerasa and south of Pella, near Ragaba, where Jesus and the twelve stayed for three weeks during their first public ministry tour. It was later visited during the Perean mission of the seventy, and was the location where Jesus healed ten lepers to demonstrate that all peoples were capable of accepting the gospel.

name: Amaziah
originalDescription: King of Judah who was assassinated after military defeat in the attack on his northern neighbors (97:9.22)
improvedDescription: King of Judah who achieved victory over the revolting Edomites but was defeated when he attacked his northern neighbors. He was subsequently assassinated by revolting rural folk who then placed his sixteen-year-old son Azariah on the throne.

name: ambassadors
originalDescription: human ambassadors universe ambassadors
improvedDescription: Representatives appointed to maintain diplomatic relations between different governmental levels in the universe administration. Universe ambassadors are appointed by judicial decree to represent universes to each other, while human ambassadors developed from tribal peace negotiations and represent one group to another in diplomatic relations.

name: amber
originalDescription: Nordics traded in (80:9.4)
improvedDescription: A fossilized resin traded by the Nordic peoples from the Baltic coast, who built extensive commerce with the Danubian broadheads through the Brenner Pass, leading to cultural exchanges including the adoption of mother worship and cremation practices.

name: ambitions
originalDescription: Abraham’s surrender of personal ambitions in behalf of Melchizedek is why three celestial beings appeared to him (93:6.7) man many times bows down before the idols of his selfish ambitions (4:3.1) thousands of men allow ambition to enslave them to debt (69:8.10) Few mortals ever dare to draw anything like the sum of personality credits established by the combined ministries of nature and grace. The majority of impoverished souls are truly rich, but they refuse to believe it. (48:7.6) Forceful ambition, intelligent judgment, and seasoned wisdom are the essentials of material success (156:5.7) ambition is futile without an audience to parade before (68:2.10) Jesus repeatedly dashed his apostles’ ambitions (139:0.1) every human being should remember: Many ambitions to excel which tantalize mortals in the flesh will not persist with these same mortals in the morontia and spirit careers, but before ascending mortals leave the local universe to embark upon their spirit careers, they will be satiated respecting every intellectual, artistic, and social longing or true ambition (44:8.4-5) ambitions may thwart maternal instinct (84:1.7) ambition is opposed by animal indolence (111:7.5) spirit-born individuals are so remotivated in life that they can calmly stand by while their fondest ambitions perish and their keenest hopes crash (100:2.8) it is forever true: What we begin in time we will assuredly finish in eternity—if it is worth finishing. (111:3.7) ambition in religiones dangers of ambition
improvedDescription: Personal desires and aspirations for achievement or advancement that can either motivate spiritual growth or become enslaving when focused on selfish aims. True spiritual progress requires the surrender of purely selfish ambitions while maintaining wholehearted dedication to doing God's will, as exemplified by Abraham's surrender of personal ambitions in favor of Melchizedek's larger plans.

name: Amdon
originalDescription: He is the Chaldean shepherd of Sumerian origin who was the first witness to see Melchizedek when he incarnated on Earth (93:2.1-3)
improvedDescription: A Chaldean shepherd of Sumerian origin who was the first mortal to witness Melchizedek's materialization on Urantia. Melchizedek entered Amdon's tent and proclaimed his mission, sharing the first meal of his material existence with this shepherd under the stars while beginning his revelation of the truth about God.

name: amends
originalDescription: When we find ourselves in the wrong, do not hesitate to confess our error and be quick to make amends. (131:9.3) By our efforts to make amends for past sins we acquire strength to resist future tendencies thereto. (131:3.3) all fraudulently obtained wealth should be restored (132:5.18,22)
improvedDescription: The act of making restitution or correcting wrongs committed, particularly through confession of error and restoration of ill-gotten gains. Making amends for past sins provides strength to resist future wrongdoing and is essential for spiritual progress, as taught across various religious traditions.

name: Amenemope
originalDescription: Amenemope was a great Egyptian prophet (92:5.11) (95:3.5) he influenced the concept of El Shaddai, a complex concept of God inspired by El Elyon (96:1.5) he greatly influenced the religious faith of the Hebrews and the philosophy of the Greeks (95:4.4) he was known as “son of man” (95:4.1) He exalted the conscience into the supreme arbiter between good and evil, taught that sins would be punished, and proclaimed that salvation was obtained through recourse to the solar deity (95:4) Mesopotamian religion entered Hebrew literature through the work of Amenemope (95:1.11)
improvedDescription: Amenemope was a great Egyptian prophet and religious teacher who greatly influenced both Hebrew religious faith and Greek philosophy. Known as the "son of man," he exalted conscience as the supreme arbiter between right and wrong, taught that sins would be punished, and proclaimed salvation through the solar deity. His teachings contributed to the Hebrew concept of El Shaddai and helped preserve Mesopotamian religious culture in Hebrew literature.

name: Amenhotep III
originalDescription: On the walls of a temple in Luxor, the birth of Amenhotep is shown represented together with another identical child who symbolizes the Egyptian belief in the ka, the protective spirit that accompanies distinguished people throughout their lives. (111:0.5)
improvedDescription: Amenhotep III was an Egyptian pharaoh whose birth is depicted on temple walls at Luxor alongside another identical child representing the ka, the Egyptian protective spirit believed to accompany distinguished individuals throughout their lives.

name: Amerinds
improvedDescription: American Indians, the indigenous peoples of the Americas who are part of the red race.

name: Amida Buddha
improvedDescription: A divine figure in Buddhist theology, specifically the God of Paradise in the west, believed by Amidists to show infinite mercy and loving care to all who call upon his name in true faith.

name: Amidist
originalDescription: believer in Amida Buddha (94:12.3)
improvedDescription: A believer in Amida Buddha who holds that souls may choose to sojourn in Paradise before entering Nirvana, attaining salvation through faith in Amida's divine mercies and loving care.

name: Ammonites
originalDescription: King David imposed a great tribute on this people (97:9.14) Saul defeated the Ammonites when they wanted to attack the Gileadites. The victory was later extolled and gave rise to the pretentious Hebrew history (97:9.3-5)
improvedDescription: An ancient people neighboring the Hebrews who were defeated by Saul when they attacked the Gileadites, and later had heavy tribute imposed on them by King David.

name: amoeba
originalDescription: Amebas are typical survivors of the initial stage of animal life (59:1.1) ameba has come on down but little changed since ancient times (65:2.4)
improvedDescription: Single-celled animal organisms that are typical survivors of the initial stage of animal life, having remained largely unchanged since ancient times as representatives of early evolutionary development.

name: Amorites
originalDescription: They were a confederation of Semitic clans, but they were far behind and were committing suicide as a nation because of their foolish practices. Melchizedek advised Abraham against fight them because he revealed to him that in the future they would be a weak people easy to defeat by their descendants (93:6.2) Quemos was the god of the Amorites, and the Hebrews also believed for a time in his existence, although he was subordinate to Yahweh (96:1.14)
improvedDescription: A confederation of Semitic clans who were committing cultural suicide through foolish practices, making them vulnerable to future defeat by Abraham's descendants. Chemosh was their god, whom the Hebrews acknowledged as real but subordinate to Yahweh.

name: Amos (brother of Jesus)
originalDescription: Amos was the seventh brother of Jesus. He was born on the night of Sunday, January 9, A.D. 7, and he died on the afternoon of Saturday, December 3, AD 12, after a week sick with a high fever. This death was even more painful for the family because father Joseph had recently passed away (124:5.2) (127:3.13) Jesus got along especially well with his little siblings, Amos and Ruth (124:4.3)
improvedDescription: The seventh child of Joseph and Mary, born January 9, A.D. 7, who died at age five on December 3, A.D. 12, after a week-long illness with high fever. Jesus had an especially good relationship with little Amos and his sister Ruth.

name: Amos (John Mark’s friend)
originalDescription: Amos is a friend of John Mark the evangelist, who was overprotected by his parents, denying him the possibility of joining the Jesus movement unless he ran away from home and hurt his parents’ feelings. (177:2.2-4)
improvedDescription: A young friend of John Mark who believed in the gospel but was prevented by overprotective parents from joining Jesus' movement. Jesus considered him less dependable than John Mark due to his problematic home upbringing that lacked the normal, loving, and wise training necessary for developing strong character.

name: Amos (Kheresa lunatic)
originalDescription: Amos led Aden to believe in Jesus and Aden later dedicated himself to preaching (159:2.4) He was a lunatic who lived in the caves and the cemetery near Kheresa. He used to be in chains but he had managed to untie himself and was wandering around when he found Jesus. Amos did not have any demonic possession as he believed nor was he healed by Jesus but he had a mental affliction and abruptly changed his behavior when listening to Jesus.
