The story that he later believed, that the demons that tormented him happened to dwell in a swine, helped to consolidate his recovery. All the apostles except Thomas believed in the story of the swine (151:6.6)
improvedDescription: Amos was a mentally afflicted man who lived in caves and cemeteries near Kheresa. When he encountered Jesus, his troubled mind was calmed, and he became convinced that evil spirits had left him and entered a nearby herd of swine that subsequently tumbled over a cliff. This belief in his miraculous healing helped consolidate his recovery, and he later led Aden to believe in Jesus, who dedicated himself to preaching.

name: Amos (Prophet)
originalDescription: Amos was a prophet who denounced the drunkenness and immorality of the northern tribes (97:4.1-4) Jesus mentioned that the Father certainly spoke through prophets like Amos (155:6.2) John the Baptist lived as a shepherd in the style of Amos (135:6.3) he proclaimed Yahweh as God of all nations, attacking the concept of the chosen people (97:4.2,4) threatened that God would abandon Israel unless justice was restored (97:8.3) Amos and other prophets were the ones who started the secret scriptures that gave rise to the Old Testament (97:9.20) was stoned to death (97:4.4)
improvedDescription: Amos was a Hebrew prophet who emerged from the southern hills to denounce the criminality, drunkenness, and immorality of the northern tribes. He proclaimed Yahweh as the "God of all nations" and attacked the concept of the chosen people, threatening that God would abandon Israel unless justice was restored. He was eventually stoned to death for his courageous teachings that challenged the double standard of national justice and morality.

name: Amosad
originalDescription: Amosad was a leader who around 15,000 B.C. brought about a regeneration of the decadent Sethite clergy, a rebirth that sustained the survival of that faith longer than any other (78:5.1) (92:5.9)
improvedDescription: Amosad was a leader who around 15,000 B.C. brought about a regeneration of the decadent Sethite clergy. Under his leadership, the Sethite priesthood experienced a brilliant renaissance that inaugurated a new era and sustained the survival of their faith longer than any other religious movement of that time.

name: amphibians
originalDescription: During the Devonian period, amphibians emerged as the first land animals, evolving from arthropods whose swim bladders had developed into lungs. They could live both on land and in water. (59:5.5)
improvedDescription: During the Devonian period, amphibians emerged as the first land animals, evolving from arthropods whose swim bladders had developed into lungs. These air-breathing creatures were able to live both on land and in water, representing a sudden and significant evolutionary development.

name: amphitheater
originalDescription: It is a typical public place of the Roman Empire where shows and games were offered. Some amphitheaters mentioned in The Urantia Book are: * at Lake Urmia, a building for lectures dedicated to the “spirit of religion”, where Jesus spent some time (134:3.1) * in Caesarea, with capacity for 20,000 sat spectators. Here Jesus attended a performance (130:2.1) * in the Decapolis, in Scythopolis, where Jesus and his father attended the annual athletic games (123:5.12) (124:3.7) * after attending the Scythopolis anphiteater, young Jesus suggested his father Joseph building a similar amphitheater in Nazareth, causing the anger of his father (124:3.8-9) in architectural spheres there are constructions of the same type: * on Edentia, the amphiteater encircling the sea of glass, the receiving area of Edentia (43:1.7) * on Jerusem, the amphiteater encircling the broadcast-receiving station, with seats for five billion (46:3.1-3) (46:5.30) (53:5.6)
improvedDescription: Amphitheaters are circular or oval structures designed for public gatherings, performances, and lectures. Notable examples include the amphitheater at Lake Urmia dedicated to the "spirit of religion," the massive amphitheater in Caesarea with capacity for 20,000 spectators, and the enormous broadcast-receiving amphitheaters on Jerusem and Edentia that accommodate billions of beings for universe communications.

name: anarchy
originalDescription: anarchy augments misery (70:0.3) Jesus declared that in the universe, where two or more creatures act in co-operation, there is always provided the authority of leadership (181:2.16)
improvedDescription: Anarchy augments misery and represents the absence of organized government or leadership. Jesus taught that in the universe, where two or more creatures act in cooperation, there is always provided the authority of leadership, making anarchy incompatible with divine order and effective group functioning.

name: Anaxagoras
originalDescription: Anaxagoras was a greek mechanist who recognized a First Cause (98:2.6)
improvedDescription: Anaxagoras was a Greek philosopher and mechanist who recognized a First Cause or Initial Mind, distinguishing him from pure materialists. Though primarily mechanistic in his thinking, he acknowledged the necessity of an originating intelligence behind the universe.

name: Anaxand
originalDescription: Anaxando is a young Greek with whom Jesus spoke in Caesarea when he was traveling in the Mediterranean (130:2.4-5) he became quartermaster of the Roman centurion named Cornelius, who was made a believer by Peter (130:2.5) converted to Christianity by the Apostle Philip (130:2.5) he accidentally perished while helping wounded in the massacre of 20,000 Jews, which occurred in Caesarea in 66 AD. (130:2.5)
improvedDescription: Anaxand was a young Greek shipyard worker in Caesarea who became deeply moved by Jesus' teachings about overcoming evil with goodness. He later became quartermaster to the Roman centurion Cornelius, converted to Christianity through Philip's preaching, and perished accidentally while ministering to the wounded during the massacre of 20,000 Jews in Caesarea in 66 AD.

name: ancestor worship
originalDescription: The advancing ghost cult made ancestor worship inevitable (87:3.1) The great weakness of ancestor veneration is that it promotes a backward-looking philosophy. (79:8.8) ancestor worship is based on the belief that the dead punished those who did not follow the rules that they had followed when they they were in the flesh (68:4.3) Ancestor worshipers view the failure to have sons as the supreme calamity for all time and eternity. (84:7.19) The great strength in a veneration of ancestry is the value that such an attitude places upon the family. (79:8.9-10) Later this ritual developed into a pseudo seance at which ghosts were reputed to have appeared. (70:7.15) During a certain period, solar veneration became a species of ancestor worship. (95:2.7) in Israel in Europe in China, Japan and India (79:8.3) (94:5.7)
improvedDescription: Ancestor worship evolved from the advancing ghost cult and became the connecting link between common spirits and higher gods. While it promoted family stability and social cohesion, especially in Chinese culture, its great weakness was fostering a backward-looking philosophy that regarded the past as the exclusive source of truth, based on the belief that the dead would punish those who failed to follow ancestral customs.

name: Ancients of Days
originalDescription: who they are their personality Abodes Relationship with mortals supreme judges in the Bible
improvedDescription: The Ancients of Days are the triune rulers of the seven superuniverses, with three governing each superuniverse from its headquarters world. They are Trinity-origin beings who represent the beginning of personality records in the universe of universes and serve as supreme executive judges with the power to decree the eternal extinction of will creatures. They are the most powerful direct rulers of time-space creations and provide uniform administration across the diverse superuniverses.

name: Ancients of Days of Uversa
originalDescription: they communicated with Michael during Michael's time in the tomb (188:3.12) they certified the mortal transit of Jesus (189:1.10) The Urantia Papers were produced according to a mandate from the Uversa Ancients of Days (31:10.22) Ancients and Lucifer's rebellion
improvedDescription: The Ancients of Days of Uversa are the supreme rulers of the superuniverse of Orvonton who serve as the highest administrative and judicial authority for all local universes within their domain. They communicated with Michael during his bestowal on Urantia, provided mandates for The Urantia Papers, and made crucial decisions regarding the Lucifer rebellion including the imprisonment of Lucifer and certification of Jesus' mortal transit.

name: Andes mountains
originalDescription: During the Mesozoic era, the Andes Mountains began to rise, marking their emergence while most of South America was submerged under the polar sea. This uplift shaped the continent's geology and prevented complete inundation. (59:5.18) (60:2.6) (60:3.4)
improvedDescription: The Andes Mountains are a major mountain range in South America that began rising during the Mesozoic era, marking their emergence while most of South America was submerged under polar seas. This uplift shaped the continent's geology and prevented complete inundation of the landmass.

name: Andites
originalDescription: The Andites were the best all-round human stock to appear on Urantia since the days of Adamites; most skillful and sagacious militarists ever to live on Urantia (78:4.3-6) (80:4.3) The Andites were a great people, but the crucial factor in determining the course of their civilization was the increasing aridity of Iran, Turkestan, and Sinkiang, which forced them to invent and adopt new and advanced methods of wresting a livelihood from their decreasingly fertile lands. (81:6.4) they drove Andonites out of Europe (80:9.6-7) It was the violet race and their Andite successors who first attempted to divorce the mathematical from the volitional. (103:6.10) These early Andites were not Aryan; they were pre-Aryan. They were not white; they were pre-white. They were neither an Occidental nor an Oriental people. But it is Andite inheritance that gives to the polyglot mixture of the so-called white races that generalized homogeneity which has been called Caucasoid. (78:4.4) migrations of Andites (79) (80) (78:5-6) influence in the races origin traditions Renown Andites
improvedDescription: The Andites were the best all-round human stock to appear on Urantia since the days of the pure-line violet peoples, representing a blend of Adamite, Nodite, and superior evolutionary races. They were skilled militarists and adventurers who made extensive migrations across Eurasia, significantly influencing the development of civilization through their contributions to art, agriculture, animal domestication, and cultural advancement before eventually being absorbed into the populations they encountered.

name: Andon and Fonta
originalDescription: Andón and Fonta are the true parents of all mankind (61:6.2) (63:1.1) they lived at the beginning of the third ice age, in a difficult and invigorating environment (61:6.3) Both were born in what is now India (79:0.1) family in which they were born their great decission their descendants achievements death and current status
improvedDescription: Andon and Fonta were the first two human beings on Urantia and the true parents of all mankind, born about one million years ago during the third glacial advance in what is now India. This remarkable pair made the first human decision of moral significance, discovered fire, developed early language, and lived to age forty-two before being killed in an earthquake, later achieving fusion with their Thought Adjusters and serving on Jerusem as counselors to ascending mortals from Urantia.

name: Andonites
originalDescription: Andonites are the descendants of Andon and Fonta, the parents of humankind, and therefore the first race on Earth The appearance of the earlier Andonites on Urantia was also something new in Satania. On no other world in the local system has such a race of will creatures evolved in advance of the evolutionary races of color. (64:6.2) migrations physical characteristics (63:4.1) practices Religion Language Renown Andonites
improvedDescription: The Andonites were the descendants of Andon and Fonta, representing the first human race on Urantia and the only race of will creatures to evolve in advance of the evolutionary races of color in the local system. They possessed swarthy complexions similar to modern Eskimos, developed basic tools and hunting techniques, and were eventually dispersed by climate changes and pressure from later races, with their purest surviving descendants being the Eskimos and other northern peoples.

name: Andovontia
originalDescription: He is the Tertiary circuit supervisor of Universal Circuits stationed in our local universe. He is the one who quarantined Urantia after the Caligastia betrayal (37:8.3) Andovontia is the name of the Tertiary circuit supervisor of the Circuits with the number 572,842 for Nebadon and his assignment is eternal. He is a member of the personal staff of Michael of Nebadon (24:1.11)
improvedDescription: Andovontia is the tertiary Universe Circuit Supervisor stationed in the local universe of Nebadon, responsible for spirit and morontia circuits. He isolated Urantia at the time of Caligastia's betrayal during the Lucifer rebellion and serves as a permanent member of Michael of Nebadon's personal staff with the designation number 572,842.

name: Andrew
originalDescription: Andrew was the first apostle of Jesus and chief of the apostolic group while Jesus lived he was 33 years old in A.D. 26, when he was accepted by Jesus (139:1.2) he had a certain tendency to suspicion and over-individualism (193:4.3) his family his work as an apostle the words of Jesus after the death of Jesus the gospel of Andrew
improvedDescription: Andrew was the first chosen apostle and chairman of the apostolic corps, a 33-year-old fisherman from Capernaum who served as the administrative leader of the twelve. Though lacking oratorical ability, he was an understanding executive and efficient administrator who faithfully managed the practical affairs of the apostolic group and later traveled through Armenia, Asia Minor, and Macedonia before being crucified in Patrae in Achaia.

name: Andromeda
originalDescription: Andromeda is a sun-forming nebula that is outside the inhabited superuniverse and is very active. It is a nebula visible to the naked eye and its light takes a million years to reach us (15:4.7) in Andromeda a giant nova collapsed in 40 minutes (41:8.3)
improvedDescription: Andromeda is a sun-forming nebula outside the inhabited superuniverse of Orvonton that is visible to the naked eye from Urantia. The light from this distant nebula takes nearly one million years to reach Earth, and it was the site of a giant nova that collapsed in just forty minutes about fifty years prior to the time of The Urantia Book's revelation.

name: Andronover
originalDescription: Andronover es la nebulosa que dio origen a nuestro sol. It is only mentioned in The Urantia Book; current science has failed to discover the origin of our sun. (15:3.6) (57:1.1) The total number of suns and sun systems having origin in the Andronover nebula was 1,013,628. (57:2.2) (57:4.3,8) The final nuclear remnant of this magnificent nebula still burns with a reddish glow and continues to give forth moderate light and heat to its remnant planetary family of one hundred and sixty-five worlds, which now revolve about this venerable mother of two mighty generations of the monarchs of light. (57:4.9) 875,000,000,000 years ago the enormous Andronover nebula number 876,926 was duly initiated. (57:1.6) It was scarcely a million years subsequent to this epoch that Michael of Nebadon, a Creator Son of Paradise, selected this disintegrating nebula as the site of his adventure in universe building. (57:3.8-9) Monmatia, our solar system, revolves around the nucleus of this former nebula (15:3.9) Nebadon, our local universe, was physically assembled out of the stellar and planetary progeny of Andronover and other nebulae (41:0.3) this nebula is registered in the universe with number 876,926 (57:1.6) the Andronover stellar family and its associated clusters revolves about the composite rotation-gravity center of the star cloud of Nebadon. (15:3.10)
improvedDescription: Andronover was the enormous nebula that gave origin to our sun and solar system, designated as number 876,926 in universe records. This former spiral nebula produced 1,013,628 suns and sun systems over its evolutionary cycles before its final nuclear remnant settled into a stable configuration that continues to provide light and heat to its remaining family of 165 worlds.

name: Ang
originalDescription: Ang was one of the corporeal members of the staff of the Planetary Prince, who presided over the council of food and material well-being, and who taught irrigation, furriers, and weaving to primitive men (66:5.2) Ang and three members of his council remained loyal and did not join the rebellion (67:4.1)
improvedDescription: Ang was one of the corporeal members of Caligastia's staff who presided over the council on food and material welfare, teaching primitive humans irrigation, furriers, and weaving techniques. He remained loyal during the Lucifer rebellion along with three other members of his food council and was later transferred to Jerusem to resume his Paradise journey.

name: Angamon
originalDescription: Angamon was the leader of the church of the Stoics in Rome. Jesus had a talk with him that we are given a summary of in The Urantia Book. He was a great friend of Paul of Tarsus and an ardent follower of the Christian church in Rome (132:1) Angamon introduced Jesus to a wealthy man from Rome who was greatly influenced by his teachings (132:5.1)
improvedDescription: Angamon was the leader of the Stoics in Rome who had an all-night talk with Jesus during his sojourn in Rome. He subsequently became a great friend of Paul of Tarsus and one of the strong supporters of the Christian church at Rome, and he introduced Jesus to a wealthy Roman citizen who was greatly influenced by Jesus' teachings.

name: Angelic World
originalDescription: this world is the third transitional cultural world of Jerusem, the capital of Satania (45:1.5) upon reaching the third mansonia, the third satellite of transition world number one, permission is granted to visit the world of angels (47:5.2)
improvedDescription: The Angelic World is the third transitional cultural world of Jerusem, serving as the headquarters of all seraphic hosts engaged in system activities and surrounded by seven worlds of angelic training and instruction. Mortals who reach the third mansion world receive permission to visit this sphere, which houses the various system training schools for angelic orders.

name: angels
originalDescription: all groups of angels have own transport corps (39:2.8) in The Urantia Book the term angel is restricted to seraphim serving mortals (38:3.1) chief supernaphim is original pattern angel (27:2.1) Angels of certain orders may become Paradise Companions for a season; some become Celestial Recorders; others ascend to the ranks of the Technical Advisers. (26:1.10) Urantia mortals must obtain light-energy through the vegetative incarnation, but the angelic hosts are encircuited; they “have food that you know not.” They also partake of the circulating teachings of the marvelous Trinity Teacher Sons; they have a reception of knowledge and an intake of wisdom much resembling their technique of assimilating the life energies. (26:1.17) they are registered as personalities of the Infinite Spirit (30:2.82) These brilliant creatures of light are sustained directly by the intake of the spiritual energy of the primary circuits of the universe. (26:1.17) Together with the Messenger Hosts of Space, the ministering spirits enjoy seasons of rest and change; they have a large capacity for appreciation of the ministrations of the reversion directors; they possess very social natures and have an associative capacity far transcending that of human beings. (26:1.1) orders (26:1.2) (30:2.82-89)
improvedDescription: Angels are the ministering-spirit associates of evolutionary and ascending will creatures throughout space, serving as colleagues and working associates of higher divine personalities. They are distinct personalities registered under the Infinite Spirit, sustained directly by spiritual energy, and possess very social natures with associative capacities far transcending those of human beings.

name: anger
originalDescription: anger must be conquer with mercy (131:4.6) (141:3.8) every one who is angry with his brother is in danger of condemnation (140:6.4) anger is a mean and despicable sentiment (4:3.2) All physical poisons greatly retard the efforts of the Adjuster to exalt the material mind, while the mental poisons of fear, anger, envy, jealousy, suspicion, and intolerance likewise tremendously interfere with the spiritual progress of the evolving soul. (110:1.5) rage can induce mystic state, but mystical experiences should not be confused with direct communion with one’s Thought Adjuster (100:5.10) Anger is a material manifestation which represents, in a general way, the measure of the failure of the spiritual nature to gain control of the combined intellectual and physical natures. (149:4.2) Jesus’ disciples learned that a soft answer turns away wrath (128:6.7) (131:3.6) (139:4.13) (149:4.2) (150:9.3) (181:2.15) Spirit of Truth endowed mortal man with power to challenge the evils of hate and anger by the fearless acts of love and forbearance (194:3.12) anger is like a stone hurled into a hornet’s nest (48:7.20) Fear and anger weaken character and destroy happiness. This momentous sermon started out upon the note of happiness. (140:5.6) the wrath of God
improvedDescription: Anger is a material manifestation representing the failure of spiritual nature to gain control over the combined intellectual and physical natures. It is considered mean and despicable, weakens character, destroys happiness, and greatly interferes with spiritual progress and the work of the Thought Adjuster.

name: angiosperms
originalDescription: During the Cretaceous period, angiosperms emerged and quickly spread across the continents, dominating plant life. They provided a primary food source for evolving mammals and included many modern trees, such as oak, walnut, and maple, as well as fruits, grasses, and cereals. (61:2.2) (60:3.7,19)
improvedDescription: Angiosperms are flowering plants that emerged during the Cretaceous period and quickly spread across the continents, becoming the dominant plant life. They provided the primary food source for evolving mammals and included many modern trees such as oak, walnut, and maple, as well as fruits, grasses, and cereals.

name: Anglo-Saxons
originalDescription: Anglo-Saxons are a people descendants of Andite and blue races (80:4.5)
improvedDescription: Anglo-Saxons are descendants of the blended Andite and blue races who became the ancestors of the Nordic peoples. They are among the forefathers of the Scandinavian, German, and Anglo-Saxon peoples who emerged from the westward movement of Andite horsemen across the Russian plains.

name: Angona
originalDescription: Angona is the name of a massive solar system made up of a dark giant from space, perhaps a black hole, which 4.5 billion years ago approached our solar system and gave rise to twelve planets and a cloud of meteors by disgorging material from the sun (41:10.1) (57:5.4-14) it is possible that Angona was part of a large nebula neighboring Andronover whose approach distorted Andronover as we are told that this distortion occurred at the time of the birth of our solar system (15:3.6)
improvedDescription: Angona was a massive dark giant solar system that approached our sun 4.5 billion years ago, causing the birth of the twelve planets of our solar system through gravitational disruption. This enormous system drew off vast columns of solar material that eventually condensed into the planets, asteroids, and meteors that comprise our present solar system.

name: animals
originalDescription: differences with respect to man Animal evolution Andonites use of animals domestication of animals Animals in heavens Animals in other planets Relationship wih man Relationship with Jesus Animals mentioned
improvedDescription: Animals are material creatures that possess mind and can communicate in limited ways but lack spiritual nature, reflective thinking, and the capacity for spiritual experience. They differ from humans in their inability to know God, use symbols for meaningful communication, or experience personality survival, though they serve important roles in planetary ecosystems and human civilization.

name: animism
originalDescription: in Jesus’ time, every rock and tree believed to be possessed by spirits (121:7.12) Prayer is little associated with animism, but such beliefs may exist alongside emerging religious sentiments. (91:1.5) The savage looked upon all nature as alive, as possessed by something. (86:2.6-7) slaves brought a crude animism to Greece, with bloody rites where criminals where executed in a ceremony (98:1.1)
improvedDescription: Animism is the primitive belief that all nature is alive and possessed by spirits, viewing every rock, tree, and natural object as spirit-inhabited. This crude religious concept was prevalent in Jesus' time and represents one of the earliest forms of religious thinking, though it often existed alongside other emerging religious sentiments.

name: anise
originalDescription: Jesus set an example of how important spiritual things are neglected by mentioning how the Pharisees were overly concerned with paying the anise tithe (175:1.17)
improvedDescription: Anise is a plant mentioned by Jesus when criticizing the Pharisees for their meticulous attention to tithing minor herbs like mint, anise, and cumin while neglecting the weightier matters of the law such as faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: Anna
originalDescription: Anna was the poetess who declaimed a poem in the presentation in the temple of Jesus (122:9.2-4)
improvedDescription: Anna was a Galilean poetess who, along with Simeon, frequently lingered in the temple courts and was an intimate of the priest Zacharias. She wrote a poem that was sung during Jesus' presentation in the temple as part of their hymn celebrating the redemption of the first-born son.

name: Annan
originalDescription: Annan was the spokesperson and daughter for the Nodite race who was part of the Adam and Eve reception committee (74:2.5)
improvedDescription: Annan was a loyal daughter and spokesperson for the Nodite race who served on the twelve-member Urantia reception committee that welcomed Adam and Eve upon their arrival in the Garden of Eden.

name: Annas
originalDescription: Annas was the former high priest, the most powerful individual of all the Jews, and father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest during the preaching of Jesus (129:1.5) (183:3.10) (183:5.1) (184:0.1-2) (184:1.1) Annas was a high priest and was deposed from office in A.D. 13. (129:1.5) he was distantly related to Salome (142:0.2) (184:0.3) Annas spent a lot of cordial time with Jesus in A.D. 22. showing him the temple (129:2.7-8) When Jesus began preaching in Jerusalem he visited Annas, but was received very coldly (142:0.2) (184:1.2) The “sons of Annas”, that is, priests of the family of the high priest, had bazaars in the temple with them doing a succulent business; but this business was overthrown some time later (173:1.2) enmity with Jesus arose when he threatened his revenues by driving the money changers out of the temple (184:1.3) interrogation and judgement of Jesus
improvedDescription: Annas was the former high priest of the Jews, deposed in A.D. 13, who remained the most powerful individual in all Jewry through his temple revenues and Roman connections. He was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the acting high priest, and conducted the preliminary examination of Jesus before his trial, having previously known Jesus during his earlier visits to Jerusalem.

name: Annon
originalDescription: Mary, the mother of Jesus, was a descendant of Annon, an illustrious and unknown woman whose name does not appear in the Bible (122:1.2)
improvedDescription: Annon was one of the illustrious women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, representing part of the remarkable heritage that characterized Mary's diverse racial background.

name: Anova
originalDescription: The oldest inhabited world of Satania, world number one, is Anova, one of the forty-four satellites revolving around an enormous dark planet but exposed to the differential light of three neighboring suns. Anova is in an advanced stage of progressive civilization. (49:0.5)
improvedDescription: Anova is the oldest inhabited world in the Satania system, designated as world number one. It is one of forty-four satellites revolving around an enormous dark planet and is exposed to the differential light of three neighboring suns, having reached an advanced stage of progressive civilization.

name: Ansie
originalDescription: Mary, the mother of Jesus, was descended from Ansie, an illustrious and unknown woman whose name does not appear in the Bible (122:1.2)
improvedDescription: Ansie was one of the illustrious women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, representing part of the remarkable heritage that characterized Mary's diverse racial background.

name: Antarctica
originalDescription: Antarctica, Australia, and the land indicated by the islands of the Pacific broke away on the south and east and have drifted far away since that day. (58:4.3) rose out of Pacific depths. Europe and Africa began to rise out of the Pacific depths along with those masses now called Australia, North and South America, and the continent of Antarctica, while the bed of the Pacific Ocean engaged in a further compensatory sinking adjustment. (57:8.21) the vast polar ice sheets like the one in Antartica began to form 100,000 years ago and as long as the polar regions continue to be covered with ice, it is hardly possible for another glacial age to occur (61:7.11)
improvedDescription: Antarctica is a continent that broke away from the ancient land masses along with Australia and Pacific islands, drifting far from its original position. It was covered by extensive ice sheets during glacial periods and continues to influence global climate patterns through its polar ice coverage.

name: Antares
originalDescription: Antares is the largest star in local universe of Nebadon, and is four hundred and fifty times the diameter of your sun and is sixty million times its volume (41:3.2)
improvedDescription: Antares is the largest star in the local universe of Nebadon, measuring four hundred and fifty times the diameter of our sun and sixty million times its volume.

name: anteaters
originalDescription: Ten million years ago, anteaters evolved in South America alongside sloths, armadillos, and primitive South American monkeys, contributing to the continent's unique fauna. (61:4.4)
improvedDescription: Anteaters evolved in South America ten million years ago alongside sloths, armadillos, and primitive South American monkeys, contributing to the continent's unique fauna during the Cenozoic era.

name: antelopes
originalDescription: Ten million years ago, antelopes migrated from Asia to North America during a period of global land connection, playing a role in the exchange of animal species between continents. (61:4.3)
improvedDescription: Antelopes migrated from Asia to North America ten million years ago during a period when all continents except Australia were connected, participating in the last great worldwide animal migration.

name: anthracite
originalDescription: Anthracite has been subjected to more pressure and heat than other coal. (59:5.16)
improvedDescription: A type of coal that has been subjected to more pressure and heat than other coal types, making it a hard, high-grade form of coal.

name: anthropomorphism
originalDescription: An exalted anthropomorphism is the highest attainment level of purely evolutionary religion. (5:4.9)
improvedDescription: The attribution of human characteristics to God or divine beings. An exalted anthropomorphism represents the highest attainment level of purely evolutionary religion, with Christianity elevating this concept through the glorified Christ.

name: antigravity
originalDescription: alternate flow of space levels exerts antigravity influence (11:7.9) Antigravity can annul gravity within a local frame; it does so by the exercise of equal force presence. (9:3.3) Antigravity is also employed in Havona in the organization of the material functions and the spiritual activities of these enormous worlds. (14:3.6) It operates only with reference to material gravity, and it is not the action of mind. (9:3.3) Pervaded space also exerts an antigravity influence upon physical or linear gravity; space can actually neutralize such gravity action even though it cannot delay it. (11:8.3) prevents gravity from converting all energy into matter. Given a sufficient duration of retarding influence, gravity would eventually convert all energy into matter were it not for two factors: First, because of the antigravity influences of the energy controllers, and second, because organized matter tends to disintegrate under certain conditions. (15:8.5-6,10) ultimatonic energies sometimes exhibit antigravity behaviour (42:4.10) (42:6.3) Evolving energy has substance; it has weight, although weight is always relative, depending on revolutionary velocity, mass, and antigravity. (15:8.3) beings endowed with antigravity
improvedDescription: A force that can neutralize or resist the effects of material gravity within a local frame by exercising equal force presence. It operates only with reference to material gravity and is possessed by certain beings and employed in various cosmic functions, including the organization of Havona worlds and the work of energy controllers.

name: Antioch
originalDescription: Antioch was the capital of the Roman province of Syria, and here the imperial governor had his residence. Antioch had half a million inhabitants; it was the third city of the empire in size and the first in wickedness and flagrant immorality. (133:8.1) Jesus and Antioch Early christians and Antioch
improvedDescription: The capital of the Roman province of Syria and third largest city in the Roman Empire, with half a million inhabitants. Known for its wickedness and immorality, Antioch was where Jesus lived for over two months during his Mediterranean journey and later became the headquarters of Pauline Christianity.

name: Antipatris
originalDescription: During the time of Jesus, Antipatris was a site where people traveled to hear his teachings. In A.D. 27, residents of Antipatris were among those who came to listen as Jesus and his apostles preached the good news of the kingdom in the surrounding regions. (127:6.4) (143:0.1)
improvedDescription: A city in Palestine that Jesus passed through on his way to Jerusalem and from which people came to hear his teachings. Residents of Antipatris were among those who traveled to listen to Jesus and his apostles preach the good news of the kingdom.

name: antithesis
originalDescription: Jesus effectively employed the antithesis, comparing the minute to the infinite and so on (159:5.17)
improvedDescription: A rhetorical device effectively employed by Jesus in his teaching, comparing contrasting elements such as the minute to the infinite to illustrate spiritual truths and convey large meanings through small expressions.

name: Antonia (fortress)
originalDescription: The fortress of Roman was a castle in Jerusalem located in a corner of the Temple (162:4.2) (189:2.4) Jesus’ trial take place in praetorium, an addition to the fortress of Antonia, where Pilate and his wife made their headquarters when stopping in Jerusalem. (185:0.2)
improvedDescription: A Roman fortress in Jerusalem that housed the praetorium where Pilate and his wife stayed during visits to the city. It was the location where Jesus' trial took place and served as headquarters for Roman forces, standing as a grim reminder of Roman rule over the Jews.

name: Anu
originalDescription: Mesopotamian god of sky (95:1.4)
improvedDescription: A Mesopotamian god of the sky who was part of the Babylonian triad of supreme deities, along with Bel and Ea, representing the gods of sky, earth, and sea respectively.

name: anxiety
originalDescription: During the psychologically unsettled times of the twentieth century, amid the economic upheavals, the moral crosscurrents, and the sociologic rip tides of the cyclonic transitions of a scientific era, thousands upon thousands of men and women have become humanly dislocated; they are anxious, restless, fearful, uncertain, and unsettled; as never before in the world’s history they need the consolation and stabilization of sound religion. (99:4.6) anxiety can induce mystic state confused with Thought Adjuster communion, but it is not the case (100:5.10) many times we are demoralized by thoughtless panic of dread and anxiety (113:2.5) Too often, all too often, we mar our minds by insincerity and sear them with unrighteousness; we subject them to animal fear and distort them by useless anxiety. (9:5.7) Our anxieties and sorrows, our trials and disappointments, are just as much a part of the divine plan on our sphere as are the exquisite perfection and infinite adaptation of all things to their supreme purpose on the worlds of the central and perfect universe. (23:2.12) much that causes us serious concern often are senselessness (48:4.6) many of man’s foolish animal fears and childish anxieties thwarts work of Adjuster (108:6.2) Jesus Humor, the cure for anxiety
improvedDescription: A state of worry, fear, or unease that can dominate human thinking and interfere with spiritual growth. Jesus taught against anxiety, emphasizing trust in the Father's care and the importance of seeking first the kingdom of God rather than being troubled by material concerns.

name: Apennines
originalDescription: Forty million years ago, the Apennines were highlands rising above the water as islands, while much of Europe was submerged under lakes and bays, connected to the expanded Mediterranean Sea. (61:1.12)
improvedDescription: A mountain range in Italy that, forty million years ago, existed as highlands rising above water as islands when much of Europe was submerged under the expanded Mediterranean Sea.

name: Aphrodite
originalDescription: goddess of beauty, sensuality and love Aphrodite is the name given to the goddess Ishtar in Greece (95:1.7) when the cult of Aphrodite was brought to Rome, Aphrodite became Venus (98:3.3)
improvedDescription: Greek goddess of beauty, sensuality and love. Aphrodite is the name given to the goddess Ishtar in Greece, and when this cult was brought to Rome, Aphrodite became known as Venus.

name: apocalyptists
originalDescription: About one hundred years before the days of Jesus and John a new school of religious teachers arose in Palestine, the apocalyptists. These new teachers evolved a system of belief that accounted for the sufferings and humiliation of the Jews on the ground that they were paying the penalty for the nation’s sins. (135:5.2)
improvedDescription: A school of religious teachers that arose in Palestine about one hundred years before Jesus and John the Baptist. These teachers developed a belief system that attributed Jewish sufferings to the nation's sins and taught that Israel's period of discipline was nearly finished, with God's patience toward gentile foreigners almost exhausted.

name: apocrypha
originalDescription: The Urantia Book mentions two apocryphal biblical books, which are not in canon:
improvedDescription: Non-canonical biblical books mentioned in The Urantia Book, including the Book of Enoch and the Book of Wisdom. Jesus found the Book of Enoch particularly influential in his adoption of the term 'Son of Man' as a designation for his bestowal mission.

name: Apollo
originalDescription: Apollo, the god of music and the arts, had a temple located on the Palatine Hill in Rome (132:0.2)
improvedDescription: Greek and Roman god of music and the arts who had a temple located on the Palatine Hill in Rome, along with the emperor's residence and Greek and Latin libraries.

name: apostles of Jesus
originalDescription: The apostles were the group of twelve followers with whom Jesus began his preaching their names: * Andrew | Andrew * Simon Peter | Simon Peter * James Zebedee | James Zebedee * John Zebedee | John Zebedee * Philip | Philip (Apostle) * Nathaniel | Nathaniel (Apostle) * Matthew Levi | Matthew Levi * Thomas Didymus | Thomas Didymus * James Alpheus | Alpheus twins * Judas Alpheus * Simon Zelotes | Simon Zelotes * Judas Iscariot | Judas Iscariot * Other known were Matthias and Paul, although the latest never met Jesus | Matthias; Paul The Urantia Book gives detailed personal descriptions of each (139) most were married, and some had several children (138:9.3) all except the Alphaeus twins were graduates of synagogue schools (139:0.3-4) most were rough fishermen (140:8.20) they were not all the same in terms of schooling (139:0.3-4) many carried swords (171:4.1) (172:0.3) (172:2.1) (172:5.2) many were martyred | martyrs organization experience with Jesus beliefs of apostles Jesus ordination resurrection after Pentescost
improvedDescription: The twelve chosen followers of Jesus: Andrew, Simon Peter, James and John Zebedee, Philip, Nathaniel, Matthew Levi, Thomas Didymus, James and Judas Alpheus, Simon Zelotes, and Judas Iscariot. Most were married fishermen and graduates of synagogue schools who served as ambassadors of the kingdom and personal representatives of Jesus throughout his ministry.

name: apostles of John the Baptist
originalDescription: they were a group of 12 leaders appointed as apostles by John (144:6.1) these apostles first received their instruction from John (135:6.8) politics and selfish preferences provoked many arguments among John's disciples (135:9.5) they anointed the sick with oil, a rite that Jesus' apostles refused to adopt (150:0.2) Jesus saw to it that his apostles maintained good relations with John's apostles (141:3.3) (141:6.5) (143:3.1,5,7) (144:0.2) (144:6-7) (146:0.1) (146:3.9) (147:2.4) Jesus refused to participate in the joint conferences in which they discussed with his apostles (141:3.3) they were finally won to the cause of Jesus (144:7.2) (162:9.1-5) they never knew why Jesus did not prevent John's death (141:1.4)
improvedDescription: A group of twelve leaders appointed by John the Baptist as his apostles, following the precedent set by Jesus. They practiced anointing the sick with oil, maintained cooperative relations with Jesus' apostles despite occasional disagreements, and were eventually won over to Jesus' cause after John's death.

name: apostleship
originalDescription: The apostolate, unlike the less rigorous path of the simple disciples, is the commission that Jesus gave to those who wish to give everything in the service of the kingdom The apostleship, originally a requirement only for the twelve, Jesus did not restrict it only to them. A group of some seventy later disciples can also be considered apostles, and anyone willing to agree to pay the price was accepted by Jesus as an apostle. (163:2.11) the hard requirements made by Jesus to apostles: (140:3.1) (140:6.9-13) (140:8.16) (142:7.2) (163:2.6,11) (163:3.3) (165:4.5) (165:5.2) (171:2) He never taught his followers to avoid earthly possessions, only his twelve apostles (140:8.16) Jesus never taught that it was wrong to have wealth. He required only the twelve and the seventy to dedicate all of their worldly possessions to the common cause. (163:2.11) You who would follow after me must be willing to pay the price of wholehearted dedication to the doing of my Father’s will. If you would be my disciples, you must be willing to forsake father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, even your life. (171:2.2)
improvedDescription: The commission Jesus gave to those willing to dedicate everything in service to the kingdom, requiring complete devotion and the forsaking of all worldly possessions. Unlike simple discipleship, apostleship demanded wholehearted dedication to doing the Father's will and living as ambassadors of the heavenly kingdom.

name: Appalachian Mountains
originalDescription: Appalachian almost completely worn down in Cretaceous (60:3.5) many plants and animals have been found high upon the Appalachians and other mountains because they were left stranded by the last ice invasion (61:7.16)
improvedDescription: A mountain range in eastern North America that was almost completely worn down by the Cretaceous period and later elevated during continental drift. Many arctic plants and animals were left stranded on these peaks after the retreat of the final ice age.

name: Appian Way
originalDescription: In the time of Jesus, the Appian Way was a vital route for travel to Rome. Travelers like Gonod and Ganid used it to walk leisurely across Italy, encountering diverse people, including Roman citizens, Greek colonists, and descendants of slaves, while pack animals carried their belongings. (130:0.2) (130:8.6) (133:0.2)
improvedDescription: A major Roman road used for travel to Rome in Jesus' time. Travelers like Gonod and Ganid walked leisurely across Italy on this route, encountering diverse peoples including Roman citizens, Greek colonists, and descendants of slaves while pack animals carried their belongings.

name: apple
originalDescription: Every primitive tribe had its tree of forbidden fruit, literally the apple (89:2.1) The apple was among the first to be taboo; it was never eaten by the Levantine peoples. (88:1.3)
improvedDescription: A fruit that became taboo among early peoples, particularly the Levantine peoples who never ate it. Every primitive tribe had its forbidden fruit tree, literally the apple, which came to represent all manner of taboos and restrictions.

name: Aquila
originalDescription: Cynic; tentmaker; Jesus contacted Aquila in Rome; Paul lived with Aquila in Corinth (133:3.12)
improvedDescription: A Jewish tentmaker and Cynic philosopher whom Jesus contacted in Rome and who later lived in Corinth with his wife Priscilla, preparing the way for Paul's ministry there and providing him lodging and work.

name: Arabia
originalDescription: The more mixed and less progressive Indigo peoples east of the Sahara plateau migrated to Arabia, and then from there to Mesopotamia, India, and Ceylon. (80:2.2) The Melchizedek teachings of the one God became established in the Arabian Desert at a comparatively recent date. As in Greece, so in Arabia the Salem missionaries failed because of their misunderstanding of Machiventa’s instructions regarding overorganization. (95:7) the serpent always has been revered in Arabia (88:1.5) the brilliant culture of the Nile was cut short by internal warfare and invasions by inferior tribes from Arabia. As a result, social progress of Egipt steadily declined for more than 500 years. (80:6.5)
improvedDescription: A desert peninsula where less progressive indigo peoples migrated from Africa, serving as a pathway to Mesopotamia and India, and where Salem missionaries established Melchizedek teachings of monotheism but failed due to overorganization.

name: Arabian Sea
originalDescription: by 7000 B.C. Dravidian shipping was pushing coastwise across the Arabian Sea to the Sumerian cities of the Persian Gulf and was venturing on the waters of the Bay of Bengal as far as the East Indies. (79:3.7)
improvedDescription: A body of water crossed by Dravidian shipping by 7000 B.C., connecting their coastal trade routes between India and the Sumerian cities of the Persian Gulf.

name: Arabs
originalDescription: Arabs made use for adoption ceremony of the oath taken while the hand of the candidate rested upon the generative organ of the tribal native. (70:3.8) swarthy nomadic tribes of modern Arabs are descendants of a blend of blue race settled in old Persian peninsula, a part of yellow race, and some part of Adamite races (64:7.11) The Arabs and the natives of Africa are among the more recent pastoral peoples. (68:5.6)
improvedDescription: Swarthy nomadic tribes descended from a blend of blue race remnants in the Persian peninsula with yellow and Adamite races, representing one of the more recent pastoral peoples who used distinctive adoption ceremonies involving sacred oaths.

name: Aram
originalDescription: Aram was the home of Noah the wine maker who caused the legend of the universal deluge (78:7.5)
improvedDescription: A river settlement near Erech, home to Noah the wine maker who kept written records of annual floods and warned neighboring settlements, inspiring the legend of a universal deluge.

name: Aramaic
originalDescription: Jesus spoke, read, and wrote fluently in Aramaic, Greek, and Hebrew. (123:2.14) (123:5.1) In the days of Jesus three languages prevailed in Palestine: The common people spoke some dialect of Aramaic; the priests and rabbis spoke Hebrew; the educated classes and the better strata of Jews in general spoke Greek. (121:6.2)
improvedDescription: One of three languages prevalent in Palestine during Jesus' time, spoken by the common people in various dialects and fluently mastered by Jesus along with Greek and Hebrew.

name: Ararat
improvedDescription: A mountainous region mentioned in connection with ancient flood narratives and geographical references in Urantia Book teachings.

name: Arbela
originalDescription: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve and the seventy, during the Perea mission between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: One of the cities in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final three-month mission before Jesus' journey to Jerusalem.

name: archangels
originalDescription: they are the angels of the resurrection (47:3.2) (74:2.8) (189:2.1) Archangels are one of the few groups of local universe personalities who are not normally under the jurisdiction of Gabriel. (37:3.2) The archangel corps of Nebadon is directed by the first-born of this order. (37:3.3) they are counted in the group of Universe Aids (37:1.4) they are also counted in personality registers (30:1.39) (30:2.143) at the time of the last registry there were 800,000 in Nebadon (37:3.1) Origin Abodes Activities
improvedDescription: The highest type of spirit beings produced in large numbers in local universes, numbering almost 800,000 in Nebadon, who are offspring of the Creator Son and Universe Mother Spirit and serve as angels of resurrection and creature survival.

name: archangels’ circuit
originalDescription: Michael messages sometimes go direct via archangels’ circuit (46:3.4) on Urantia
improvedDescription: A special communication circuit established on Urantia due to the archangel divisional headquarters located there, used for direct Michael messages and circumventing planetary isolation caused by rebellion.

name: Archelais
originalDescription: Jesus and the apostles preached in this Greek city during their journey through Samaria and the Decapolis between June and August 27 (143:0.2) (143:1) (143:3.8)
improvedDescription: A Greek city near the Jordan River where Jesus and the apostles preached during their journey through Samaria and the Decapolis between June and August of 27 CE. Along with Phasaelis, Archelais was inhabited primarily by gentiles rather than Jews, giving the apostles their first extensive experience preaching to non-Jewish populations.

name: Archelaus
improvedDescription: A being mentioned in The Urantia Book but not further described in the available text.

name: archeology
originalDescription: The Urantia Book offers information on some sites where we could make important archaeological finds not yet discovered: * the first Eden lies submeerged under eastern end of Mediterranean (73:7.1-2) (78:7.7) * Later vases and implements of poorer quality than similar products from Anditepeoples (81:2.20) * we can find remnants of the second garden civilization in the regions ofMesopotamia and to the northeast and northwest (78:7.7) * To the east of the Badonan peoples, in the Siwalik Hills of northern India, may befound fossils that approach nearer to transition types between man and the variousprehuman groups than any others on earth. (64:3.4) * Archaeologists have already found these ancient Sumerian clay tablets that speakof Dilmun, the city that was confused with Dalamatia. These tablets are found inmany museums (77:4.8) *  A short way up in the foothills of Turkestan are the vestiges of what was onetimethe Adamsonite headquarters of the violet race. (77:5.10) * very old vestiges of the days of Dalamatia exist under the waters of the Persian Gulf (78:7.7)
improvedDescription: The Urantia Book provides information about potential archaeological discoveries, including the location of the submerged first Eden under the eastern Mediterranean Sea, remnants of Dalamatia civilization under the Persian Gulf, fossils of human transition types in the Siwalik Hills of northern India, and vestiges of the Adamsonite headquarters in the foothills of Turkestan. The book notes that later archaeological artifacts are sometimes inferior to earlier products of the purer Andite peoples.

name: Archeozoic
originalDescription: Archeozoic is the prelife era, from about the time the planet attained its present size to the time of life establishment, from 1 billion to 550 million years ago (59:0.2) The only time the surface of the earth was completely covered by water was during those Archeozoic ages before the land had begun to appear. (78:7.4)
improvedDescription: The prelife era extending from about the time Urantia attained its present size to the establishment of life, spanning from approximately 1 billion to 550 million years ago. During the Archeozoic ages, the earth's surface was completely covered by water before land began to appear.

name: Architects of Being
originalDescription: the Architects of Being are an order of unrevealed beings of which we are only told that they are the ones who formulate the plans for life on the planets (36:0.1)
improvedDescription: An order of unrevealed beings who formulate the plans for life on inhabited planets. Life appears on worlds either through direct importation or through the operations of Life Carriers working according to these beings' designs.

name: Architects of the Master Universe
originalDescription: The Architects of the Master Universe are the governing corps of the Paradise Transcendentalers. This governing corps numbers 28,011 personalities possessing master minds, superb spirits, and supernal absonites and they are organized in seven levels or groups (31:9.1-10) there is a tradition on Paradise that tells that the 28,012th Master Architect failed to absonitize, perhaps because the limit of absonity was reached (31:9.10) There is a very close association between the Master Architects and the Paradise Creator Sons (31:9.14) The function of Creator will and creature will, in the grand universe, operates within the limits, and in accordance with the possibilities, established by the Master Architects. (118:7.1) It is in connection with the segregation of potentials that the Architects of the Master Universe exist as the veritable embodiments of the Deity concept of the universes. (118:4.6) they appear in Paradise classification inside the Eventuated Transcendental beings (30:1.94) they are members of the first experiential Trinity, the Ultimate Trinity, that consist of the Supreme Being, the Supreme Creator Personalities, and the absonite Architects of the Master Universe (0:12.6) (31:9.14) (106:3.2) Personalized Adjusters are the all-wise and powerful executives of the Architects of the Master Universe. (109:6.7) (109:7.3) only Solitary Messengers and Inspired Trinity Spirits maintain any organic association with the Transcendentalers and the Architects of the Master Universe. (31:9.13) they are counted as transcendental beings, beings that are not finite neither absolute (30:1.93) (105:7.7) function senior Architect
improvedDescription: The governing corps of Paradise Transcendentalers numbering 28,011 personalities with master minds, superb spirits, and supernal absonite natures. They are organized in seven levels corresponding to different space levels, from Paradise and Havona to the four outer space levels. These beings serve as universe planners and coordinators, functioning as the veritable embodiments of the Deity concept of the universes and working closely with Paradise Creator Sons in the organization of local universes.

name: architectural spheres
originalDescription: The architectural spheres are worlds like our planets but they have not arisen by planetary evolution, but have been made to order (41:1.3) These are the worlds which are built according to plans and specifications for some special purpose (15:5.13) (15:7.1) (48:1.3) Figures characteristics Superuniverse headquarters Local universes headquarters
improvedDescription: Worlds specifically constructed according to plans and specifications for special purposes, rather than arising through natural planetary evolution. These include headquarters worlds like Salvington, Edentia, and Jerusem, which are independently lighted and heated through specialized energy systems. Architectural spheres contain one hundred physical elements and one hundred forms of morontia material, double the number found on evolutionary planets.

name: Arctic Ocean
originalDescription: Artic Ocean was once connected to Mediterranean (61:1.12)
improvedDescription: During the geological past, the Arctic Ocean was connected to the Mediterranean Sea through the Ural depression, and northern seas extended southward to invade North America during major continental submergences.

name: arctic regions
originalDescription: there were Andonites in arctic regions around 15,000 B.C. (78:3.7)
improvedDescription: Northern regions that enjoyed warm, uniform climates during earlier geological periods, much like the present climate of central and southern North America. Andonite peoples were scattered throughout these arctic and central Asian regions around 15,000 BCE.

name: Ardnon
originalDescription: Ardnon is a Chaldean priest who led the priests who visited Jesus in the manger. The arrival of Jesus was announced to Ardnon and these priests and this announcement was the only supernatural event related to the birth of Jesus (119:7.6)
improvedDescription: A Chaldean priest who led a group of wise men to visit the newborn Jesus in the manger. Ardnon and his associates were informed of Michael's birth by seraphim through midway creatures, making this announcement the only supernatural event associated with Jesus's birth.

name: area and building custodians
originalDescription: are the Morontia Companions on Jerusem in charge of the material and morontia structures that serve as abodes (48:3.15)
improvedDescription: Morontia Companions on Jerusem who serve as custodians of the material and morontia structures that function as abodes and meeting places on the mansion worlds.

name: Areopagus
originalDescription: It was at the Areopagus council in Athens that Paul memorably spoke about “the Unknown God”. (195:1.1)
improvedDescription: The council in Athens where the Apostle Paul delivered his famous speech about "the Unknown God," marking a significant moment in the Hellenization of Christianity.

name: Areopolis
originalDescription: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve and the seventy, during the Perea mission between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: One of the towns in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final three-month mission before Jesus went to Jerusalem.

name: arguments
originalDescription: The argumentative defense of any proposition is inversely proportional to the truth contained. (48:7.30) Overpowering arguments and mental superiority are not to be employed to coerce men and women into the kingdom. (159:3.2) Andon taught his children to settle disputes by each beating a tree with a stick, meanwhile cursing the tree; the one whose stick broke first was the victor. (70:1.3) Disputes in heavens Jesus and arguments
improvedDescription: Disputes or debates that Jesus taught should be avoided in spiritual matters, emphasizing that the argumentative defense of any proposition is inversely proportional to the truth it contains, and that spiritual victories can only be won by spiritual power, not by force or overpowering logic.

name: Arimathea
originalDescription: Jesus and apostles stay two weeks during his journey through Samaria and the Decapolis between June and August 27 (143:0.1)
improvedDescription: A village whose citizens invited Jesus to visit during his journey through Samaria, where Jesus and the apostles spent more than two weeks teaching Jews and Samaritans.

name: aristocracy
originalDescription: At the beggining assistants to early kings became the accepted nobility (70:6.5) The shamans dressed well and usually had a number of wives; they were the original aristocracy, being exempt from all tribal restrictions. (90:2.12) There early sprang up a commercialized nobility, admission to which depended on the performance of some special service to royalty or was granted frankly for the payment of money. (69:5.7) aristocracy exerted a restraining influence on early kings (70:6.6)
improvedDescription: The nobility class that developed from early royal assistants and shamans, which served as a restraining influence on kings and included both hereditary nobles and those who purchased their positions through service or payment.

name: Aristotle
originalDescription: Greek philosopher who taught that virtue is knowledge (98:2.6) his philosophy was revered by Hellenized Jews (121:6.3)
improvedDescription: Greek philosopher who taught that virtue is knowledge and goodness is health of the soul, whose philosophy was revered by Hellenized Jews and significantly influenced early Christian thought.

name: Arius
originalDescription: Arius was a priest who at the Council of Nicaea defended the idea of the subordination of the Son, of Jesus, to the Father, and that the words of Jesus did not correspond with the words of God himself. The Urantia Book indicates that his claims might put in danger the truth of Jesus' bestowal (195:0.18)
improvedDescription: A priest at the Council of Nicaea who defended the subordination of the Son to the Father, whose teachings were challenged by Athanasius to preserve the truth of Jesus' divine nature and bestowal mission.

name: ark of Noah
improvedDescription: The vessel built by Noah during the great flood, as referenced in Hebrew tradition and scripture.

name: ark of Yahweh
originalDescription: The origin of the veneration of the ark is due to the fact that Moses consented to put relics alongside the law and thereby form a combined war altar and religious shrine to avoid trying to displace too quickly the fetishes of his followers. (88:2.5) King David gained ark after defeat of Philistines, brought it to Jerusalem, and made the worship of Yahweh official for his kingdom (97:9.14) in each synagoge there is a chest containing sacred writings called the ark or Heikhal (150:8.6,8)
improvedDescription: The sacred chest containing the law and relics that Moses created as a combined war altar and religious shrine, later brought to Jerusalem by King David and used in synagogue worship to house sacred writings.

name: armadillos
originalDescription: Ten million years ago, armadillos migrated from South America to North America during the last great worldwide animal migration, as continents were temporarily connected. Along with sloths, anteaters, and other animals, they spread across the region before the continents became isolated. (61:4.3-4) (61:7.15)
improvedDescription: Ten million years ago, armadillos migrated from South America to North America during the last great worldwide animal migration, as continents were temporarily connected. Along with sloths, anteaters, and other animals, they spread across the region before the continents became isolated.

name: Armageddon
originalDescription: Armageddon was the lost seat of the orange race 400,000 years ago, when they were educated by the wise Porshunta (64:6.12)
improvedDescription: Armageddon was the headquarters of the orange race about 300,000 years ago, where they experienced a great revival of higher living under the wise leadership of Porshunta, the master mind who ministered to this unfortunate race.

name: Armenia
originalDescription: Apostle Andrew proclaimed gospel in Armenia (139:1.12)
improvedDescription: Armenia was one of the regions where the apostle Andrew journeyed and proclaimed the gospel after the later persecutions scattered the apostles from Jerusalem.

name: Aroer
originalDescription: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve and the seventy, during the Perea mission between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: Aroer was one of the towns visited during the Perean mission, the last ministry of Jesus that continued for almost three months before he went directly to Jerusalem for his final experiences in the flesh.

name: art
originalDescription: Art proves that man is not mechanistic, but it does not prove that he is spiritually immortal. Art is mortal morontia, the intervening field between man, the material, and man, the spiritual. Art results from man’s attempt to escape from the lack of beauty in his material environment; it is a gesture toward the morontia level. (195:7.15) (196:3.30) angels enjoy our efforts in art (38:2.1) the charm of human art consists in the harmony of its unity (2:7.8) Civilization is in danger when youth neglect to interest themselves in ethics, sociology, eugenics, philosophy, the fine arts, religion, and cosmology. (111:4.4) fine arts thrive best in cities supported by an agricultural and industrial population slightly under the land-man ratio (68:6.5) vanity, pride, ambition and honor gave birth to the first manifestations of all the arts, since these emotions need a social arena where they can be exhibited (68:2.10) Beauty, art, is largely a matter of the unification of contrasts. (56:10.3-4) history of art art in heavens value
improvedDescription: Art proves that man is not mechanistic and represents mortal morontia, the intervening field between man the material and man the spiritual. It results from man's attempt to escape from the lack of beauty in his material environment and is a gesture toward the morontia level.

name: art of living
originalDescription: Successful living is nothing more or less than the art of the mastery of dependable techniques for solving common problems. (160:1.7) Rodan of Alexandria showed in his philosophy a great interest in reaching higher levels of the art of living (160:2)
improvedDescription: The art of living is the mastery of dependable techniques for solving common problems, involving the honest recognition of difficulties and the fearless acknowledgment of their nature and gravity.

name: Artemis
originalDescription: Artemis was the perpetuation of the goddess of fertility in Anatolia. She had a temple in Ephesus (133:6.1)
improvedDescription: Artemis was the famous goddess of all Asia Minor and a perpetuation of the earlier mother goddess of ancient Anatolian times, worshiped at the enormous temple in Ephesus where a crude idol reputed to have fallen from heaven was displayed.

name: arthropods
improvedDescription: A group of invertebrate animals characterized by jointed legs and segmented bodies, including insects, spiders, and crustaceans.

name: artificiality
improvedDescription: The quality of being artificial, contrived, or lacking in naturalness and genuineness.

name: artists of odor
originalDescription: Artists of odor are part of the designers and embellishers group, the sixth corp of celestial artisans (44:6.6)
improvedDescription: Artists of odor are celestial artisans who create varied symphonies of spiritual fragrance for the edification and delight of advancing children of light, representing a type of spiritual grandeur that has no earthly comparison.

name: Aryans
originalDescription: Aryans are the Andite Vedic conquerors that penetrated in India ca. 2500 B.C. from Turkestan (78:5.6) (79:4) (94:1.1) Aryans also were the Hellenic invaders of Greece, and brought along with them anthropomorphic God concepts similar to those which their Aryan fellows had carried to India. The early influence of the Salem teachers was nearly destroyed by this so-called Aryan invasion from southern Europe and the East. (98:1.2) early Andites were not Aryan; they were pre-Aryan. (78:4.4) they came to have thirty-three deities (94:1.2-3) language of Aryans is what gave similarity to Occidental tongues (78:5.3) Sumerian language was not Semitic; had much in common with Aryan (77:4.7) Andite migration to the Levant and India from Turkestan occurred due to aridity that forced the Andonites to invade south into Andite territory (78:6.3) (79:1.6) In India
improvedDescription: Aryans were Andite conquerors who invaded India from Turkestan around 2500 BCE and became the Vedic rulers. These early invaders brought along multiple tribal deities and established the caste system to maintain racial identity. Their influence was primarily in northern India, and their Aryan language contributed to the similarity among Western tongues.

name: ascender-trinitized sons
improvedDescription: Ascender-trinitized sons are beings created through trinitization by ascendant creatures, representing one category of creature-trinitized sons who have achieved this unique creative act after their long journey through the universe hierarchy.

name: ascending mortals
originalDescription: the path of ascension the process of ascension names Destiny Jesus as ascending mortal
improvedDescription: Ascending mortals are evolutionary creatures who begin their existence on material worlds and progress through the morontia spheres, spirit training worlds, Havona circuits, and finally to Paradise to join the Corps of the Finality. This ascension career represents the divine plan for creature perfection, transforming animal-origin beings into perfected spirits through seven distinct stages of universe progression.

name: ascending pilgrims
improvedDescription: Ascending pilgrims are evolutionary mortals who have been accredited for the progressive ascent to Paradise. These pilgrims of time pass through the Havona circuits on their way to Paradise, representing the successful candidates in the divine plan of creature ascension from the material worlds to eternal spiritual status.

name: ascending sons of God
improvedDescription: Ascending sons of God are evolutionary mortals who have achieved sonship status through faith and fusion with their Thought Adjusters. They represent creatures of ascension potential who have embarked on the eternal journey from material existence to spiritual perfection and Paradise attainment.

name: Ascendington
originalDescription: Ascendington is the seventh world of the seven that make up the Father's circuit immediately surrounding Paradise (13:1.1) Ascendington is the “bosom of the Father, Son, and Spirit”, the rendezvous of the ascendant creatures of space, the receiving sphere of the pilgrims of time who are passing through the Havona universe on their way to Paradise (13:1.21) (13:2.4) As finaliters we will be domiciled on Paradise, but Ascendington will be our home address at all times, even when we enter service in outer space. (13:0.6) (13:1.21) (13:2.1-2) When mortals attain Havona, they are granted clearance for Ascendington, where they are most welcome, but are not permitted to visit the other six sacred worlds. (13:2.6) Ascendington is projected as the Paradise home world of time creatures of asecension potentital from outer space universes that are in the making (13:2.2) We mortals will spend most of our Havona “vacations” on Ascendington. (13:1.21) Fusion is the secret of the sacred sphere of Ascendington, and no creature, save those who have experienced fusion with the spirit of Deity, can comprehend the true meaning of the actual values which are conjoined when the identity of a creature of time becomes eternally one with the spirit of Paradise Deity. (13:1.22) (112:7.2)
improvedDescription: Ascendington is the seventh sacred sphere in the Father's circuit around Paradise, serving as the "bosom of the Father, Son, and Spirit." It functions as the Paradise home and rendezvous point for ascending creatures of time and space during their Havona journey, where mortals spend their "vacations" and experience thousands of unimaginable activities while developing new relationships between their human and divine selves.

name: ascension plan
originalDescription: After the life in the flesh, time is no longer available as a technique of dodging situations or of circumventing disagreeable obligations. (48:5.8) all spirits are devoted to ascension plan (77:9.12) ascesion plan has been described as: * bringing God down to man and then, by a sublime sort of partnership, carrying man up to God and on to eternity of service and divinity of attainment (77:9.12) * elevation and perfection of mortals (7:4.1) (113:0.1) * graduated opportunity to master universe administration (48:8.2) * work the way through the long school of Paradise attainment by serving as teachers to those pupils just behind you in the scale of progression (35:4.1) (37:10.5) (55:2.11) (30:3.9) * the privilege of participating in creating own destiny (54:2.4) The divine plan of perfection attainment embraces three unique, though marvelously correlated, enterprises of universal adventure: (7:4) * The Plan of Progressive Attainment (7:4.4) * The Bestowal Plan (7:4.5) * The Plan of Mercy Ministry (7:4.6) The Eternal Son is the personal trustee, the divine custodian, of the Father’s universal plan of creature ascension. (7:4.7) The spiritual-gravity pull of the Eternal Son constitutes the inherent secret of the Paradise ascension of surviving human souls. (7:3.2) Father bestowed Adjusters, personality; Son bestowed Creator Sons; Spirit gave mercy ministry of angels (7:4.4-6) harmony of Uversa testifies to wisdom of ascension plan (15:11.3) Infinite Spirit has devoted all to the stupendous plan of exalting surviving will creatures to the divine heights of Paradise perfection (8:3.5) no ascender is deprived of aught essential to his ascension experience (55:2.12) There are no royal roads, short cuts, or easy paths to Paradise. Irrespective of the individual variations of the route, we master the lessons of one sphere before we proceed to another; at least this is true after we once leave the world of your nativity. (48:5.7) mortal progression seems to be one of the chief businesses of the present organized universe (4:0.3) (48:8.3) that some mortals cannot fuse is not a flaw in ascension plan (40:10.3-4) wisdom of ascension plan is revealed in: * the perfection of the worlds in light and life (55:6.9) * by performance of Amadon (67:8.5) * in that no fused mortals fell in Lucifer rebellion (53:7.10,12)
improvedDescription: The ascension plan is the divine scheme for creature perfection that brings God down to mortals and then carries them up to God through a sublime partnership. This comprehensive plan includes progressive attainment through Thought Adjuster bestowal, the bestowal plan of Creator Sons, and mercy ministry by the Infinite Spirit, designed to elevate evolutionary creatures to Paradise perfection through graduated universe training and experiential education.

name: asceticism
originalDescription: We should not be passive mystics or colorless ascetics (178:1.14) Ancient asceticism Asceticism in Buddhism Asceticism in Jesus and the church
improvedDescription: Asceticism refers to extreme self-denial and physical deprivation practiced as religious devotion. The Urantia Book teaches against passive mysticism and colorless asceticism, noting that Jesus was not an ascetic and came to destroy such notions about approaching God through physical affliction and personal pain.

name: Ashdod
originalDescription: Jesus passed through Ashdod during his private journey along the Mediterranean coast in AD 25. (134:7.4)
improvedDescription: Ashdod was an ancient Mediterranean coastal city that Jesus passed through during his private journey along the coast in AD 25, traveling from Joppa inland toward Jamnia and Gaza.

name: Ashkhabad
originalDescription: ancient lost city of Adonia was located near Ashkhabad (79:1.4)
improvedDescription: Ashkhabad is a modern city near the location of ancient Adonia, which served as central Asia's commercial metropolis where the Andites engaged in trade of stone, metal, wood, and pottery both by land and water.

name: Ashtaroth
originalDescription: Aden, the preacher whom John the apostle forbade to preach, preached in Ashtaroth (159:2.1)
improvedDescription: A city where a man was preaching in Jesus' name, as mentioned by John the apostle who had forbidden this unauthorized preacher.

name: Ashtoreth
originalDescription: Astaroth is the name of the goddess Ishtar in Palestine (95:1.7)
improvedDescription: The Palestinian name for the goddess Ishtar, part of a widespread fertility cult that also appeared as Isis in Egypt, Aphrodite in Greece, and Astarte among northern tribes.

name: Ashur
originalDescription: Assur, or Ashur or Assur, was an Assyrian god, the most prominent of his pantheon (97:6.2)
improvedDescription: The most prominent god in the Assyrian pantheon, representing one of the national deities that the prophet Jeremiah declared was subordinate to Yahweh as the universal God of all nations.

name: Ashurbanipal
originalDescription: Ashurbanipal was a king of Assyria, who sent colonists to Samaria to live there (143:4.1)
improvedDescription: An Assyrian king who sent colonists to dwell in Samaria, contributing to the animosity between Jews and Samaritans that persisted for centuries.

name: Asia
originalDescription: geology races in Asia
improvedDescription: The continental landmass that served as the homeland of the human race and witnessed successive civilizations including the Dalamatians, Nodites, Adamites, and Andites, from which the potentials of modern civilization spread to the world.

name: Asia Minor
improvedDescription: A peninsula in western Asia, part of the broader Asian continent.

name: Asmonean dynasty
improvedDescription: The ruling dynasty also known as the Maccabees, whose palace in Jerusalem served as a residence for Herod the Great.

name: Asmonean palace
originalDescription: residence of Herod the Great in Jerusalem (125:1.5) (128:3.5) (185:4.1)
improvedDescription: The stately palace in Jerusalem that served as the residence of Herod the Great and later Herod Antipas, originally built by the Maccabean (Asmonean) dynasty.

name: Asoka
originalDescription: low-caste monarch who embraced Buddhism; sent 17,000 Buddhist missionaries to the world (94:9.1)
