originalDescription: Not long since a conclave extraordinary was held in Paradise of 150,000 Creator Sons belonging to Sovereign Michaels group, assembled in the parental presence and engaged in deliberations having to do with the progress of the unification and stabilization of the universe of universes (21:0.5) From time to time, great conclaves take place on these Paradise satellites of the Spirit. (17:1.9) The Eternals of Days are visible to all will creatures dwelling in Havona. They preside over the regular planetary conclaves. (18:2.2) Every millennium of sector standard time the various orders of the divine Sons forgather for their periodic conclaves. (7:6.1) As chief executive of Nebadon, Gabriel is ex officio chairman of, or observer at, most of the Salvington conclaves, and as many as one thousand of these are often in session simultaneously. (37:2.2)
improvedDescription: Formal assemblies or gatherings of divine beings, Creator Sons, and other celestial personalities held on Paradise and other administrative spheres for deliberation on universe affairs and spiritual progress.

name: concubinage
originalDescription: Concubinage was the steppingstone to monogamy, the first move away from frank polygyny. The concubines of the Jews, Romans, and Chinese were very frequently the handmaidens of the wife. (83:5.12)
improvedDescription: A historical marriage practice serving as a transitional step from polygamy to monogamy, where a man maintained one legal wife but could have sexual relations with multiple concubines, often handmaidens of the wife.

name: condemnation
improvedDescription: The act of expressing disapproval or judgment against someone or something, often related to moral or spiritual wrongdoing.

name: conduct
improvedDescription: The manner in which one behaves or acts, particularly in relation to moral and ethical standards.

name: conductors of worship
originalDescription: It is the task of the conductors of worship so to teach the ascendant creatures how to worship (27:7.4) Conductors of Worship are the first order inside primary Supernaphim (27:0.4) Never since the times of Grandfanda have the supernaphim been able fully to accommodate the spirit of worship on Paradise. Always is there an excess of worshipfulness (27:7.6-7)
improvedDescription: The first order of primary supernaphim on Paradise who teach ascendant creatures the proper techniques of worship, enabling them to express their spiritual emotions and appreciation more effectively and completely.

name: confession
originalDescription: The confession of sin is a manful repudiation of disloyalty, but it in no wise mitigates the time-space consequences of such disloyalty. But confession—sincere recognition of the nature of sin—is essential to religious growth and spiritual progress. (89:10.5) When we find ourselves in the wrong, we should not hesitate to confess our error and be quick to make amends. (131:9.3) Confession, repentance, and prayer have led individuals, cities, nations, and whole races to mighty efforts of reform and courageous deeds of valorous achievement. (91:5.2) we should leave no fault unconfessed (131:3.3) Said Jesus: “If you confess your sins, they are forgiven; therefore must you maintain a conscience void of offense”. (131:5.5) (156:2.7) ancient ritual
improvedDescription: The sincere acknowledgment and recognition of sin or wrongdoing, which is essential for religious growth and spiritual progress, though it does not eliminate the consequences of one's actions.

name: confidence
improvedDescription: A state of trust, assurance, or belief in oneself, others, or in divine guidance and support.

name: confiding
improvedDescription: The act of sharing personal thoughts, feelings, or secrets with another in a trusting manner.

name: conflict
originalDescription: All forms of soul conflict consist in the lack of harmony between the moral, or spiritual, self-consciousness and the purely intellectual self-consciousness. (133:6.6) The flight from duty is the sacrifice of truth. The escape from the service of light and life can only result in those distressing conflicts (130:1.2) All believers will have to pass through a fringe of conflict as they pass from the life lived in the flesh to the higher life lived in the spirit. (159:3.7) Misadaptation, disharmony, and conflict, all these things are inherent in evolutionary growth, from physical universes to personal creatures. (105:6.4) All conflict is evil in that it inhibits the creative function of the inner life—it is a species of civil war in the personality. (111:4.11) New religious insights arise out of conflicts which initiate the choosing of new and better reaction habits in the place of older and inferior reaction patterns. (100:4.1) conflicts occur when men dare to forsake a life of natural craving for one of adventurous art and uncertain logic (160:1.6) New meanings only emerge amid conflict; and conflict persists only in the face of refusal to espouse the higher values connoted in superior meanings. (100:4.1) For Jesus prayer a prevention of conflict (100:6.4) (196:0.10) Unresolved conflicts destroy unity and may terminate in mind disruption. (133:7.12)
improvedDescription: A state of discord or struggle, often arising from the lack of harmony between moral consciousness and intellectual consciousness, which can inhibit spiritual growth but may also lead to new religious insights when resolved constructively.

name: Confucianism
originalDescription: The Confucian preachment of morality, as in Plato, was predicated on the theory that the earthly way is the distorted shadow of the heavenly way; that the true pattern of temporal civilization is the mirror reflection of the eternal order of heaven. (94:6.10) Ganid elaborated with Jesus a summary on Confucianism when they were in Alexandria (131:9) Confucian precepts, while perpetuating the best of the past, were somewhat inimical to the very Chinese spirit of investigation that had produced those achievements which were so venerated. (94:6.11) The Zoroastrians had a religion of morals; the Hindus a religion of metaphysics; the Confucianists a religion of ethics. (5:4.7)
improvedDescription: A Chinese ethical and moral philosophy based on the teachings of Confucius, emphasizing that earthly civilization should mirror the eternal order of heaven. Confucianism became the moral foundation for almost a third of humanity, though it subordinated the God concept to the Way of Heaven and the pattern of the cosmos.

name: Confucius
originalDescription: Confucius (Kung Fu-tze) was a younger contemporary of Lao in sixth-century China. His chief work consisted in the compilation of the wise sayings of ancient philosophers. He was a rejected teacher during his lifetime, but his writings and teachings have ever since exerted a great influence in China and Japan. (94:6.9) he was deified by followers in those spiritually dark ages of China which intervened between the decline and perversion of the Taoist faith (94:6.12) he had a God concept that was almost completely subordinated to the emphasis placed upon the Way of Heaven, the pattern of the cosmos. (94:6.10) he made a new fetish out of order and established a respect for ancestral conduct that is still venerated by the Chinese at the time of this writing. (94:6.9) strictly speaking he was not a religious teacher (92:5.12) Confucius set a new pace for the shamans in that he put morality in the place of magic. (94:6.9) the writings of Confucius have ever since constituted the basis of the moral fabric of the culture of almost a third of Urantians. (94:6.11)
improvedDescription: A sixth-century Chinese philosopher and teacher who compiled the wise sayings of ancient philosophers and established moral teachings that became the foundation of Chinese culture. Though rejected during his lifetime, Confucius replaced magic with morality among the shamans and created a new reverence for ancestral conduct and order that profoundly influenced China and Japan.

name: confusion
originalDescription: Confusion, being puzzled, even sometimes discouraged and distracted, does not necessarily signify resistance to the leadings of the indwelling Adjuster. Ignorance alone can never prevent survival; neither can confusional doubts nor fearful uncertainty. (110:3.5) Confusion goes before growth as well as before destruction. (99:6.1) Many a new religion and strange “ism” has arisen from the aborted, imperfect, misunderstood, and garbled communications of the Thought Adjusters. (110:4.5) Jesus permitted confusion to pass most intense point to show that holding opinions too earnestly interfere our ability to fully grasp the great truth (151:2.5) John the Baptist still had confused ideas about the coming kingdom and its king. The longer he preached the more confused he became. Sometimes he questioned everything, but not for long. (135:7.1) present-day mortals would experience less of this apparent warfare between the flesh and the spirit if they would enter the spirit kingdom (34:7.6) Much, very much, of the difficulty which Urantia mortals have in understanding God is due to the far-reaching consequences of the Lucifer rebellion and the Caligastia betrayal. On worlds not segregated by sin they suffer less from confusion (4:3.3) Much that seems disjointed and haphazard to the mortal mind appears orderly and constructive to my understanding. (4:1.5,7) In Paradise the directors of conduct always teach to avoid confusion. Confusion never appears in Paradise (27:4.3) Religious perplexities are inevitable; there can be no growth without psychic conflict and spiritual agitation. (100:4.2) we must not be troubled with doubts of failure or perplexing confusion for in every dark hours, at all crossroad in the forward struggle, the Spirit of Truth will always show the way. (34:7.8)
improvedDescription: A state of mental uncertainty that naturally precedes both growth and destruction in spiritual development. While confusion does not prevent survival or indicate resistance to divine guidance, it often arises from imperfect communications with Thought Adjusters and can lead to fanaticism or intellectual upheaval when spiritual revelations are misunderstood.

name: conglomerates
originalDescription: Conglomerates are matter deposited near the shore lines. The coal layers alternate with shale, stone, and conglomerate. (59:5.15) Much of the older sandstone and conglomerates represents the sedimentary accumulations of ancient shores. (58:7.9) (59:1.10) (60:1.1)
improvedDescription: Sedimentary rock formations consisting of matter deposited near ancient shore lines. These geological formations alternate with coal layers, shale, and stone, representing accumulations from primitive marine environments when continental shores were submerged beneath the seas.

name: conifers
originalDescription: 120 million years ago, conifers increasingly resembled modern varieties. (60:2.7)
improvedDescription: Cone-bearing trees that, 120 million years ago during the Cretaceous period, increasingly resembled modern varieties while coexisting with persistent ferns and pines.

name: conjectures
improvedDescription: Theoretical suppositions or hypotheses formed without firm evidence, representing speculative thinking about uncertain matters.

name: Conjoint Actor
improvedDescription: An alternate designation for the Infinite Spirit, the Third Person of the Paradise Trinity who acts in conjunction with the Universal Father and Eternal Son.

name: Conjoint Creator
improvedDescription: Another title for the Infinite Spirit, emphasizing the Third Person's role as co-creator with the Universal Father and Eternal Son in the Paradise Trinity.

name: conjurers
originalDescription: practitioners of black arts were called conjurers (90:2.2)
improvedDescription: Practitioners of black arts and supernatural contact who were classified alongside sorcerers, magicians, wizards, witches, enchanters, necromancers, and soothsayers in ancient times.

name: Connecticut
originalDescription: 150 million years ago, the great eastern-Connecticut fault appeared, with one side eventually sinking two miles. (60:1.7)
improvedDescription: A northeastern American state where a great geological fault appeared 150 million years ago during the Cretaceous period, with one side eventually sinking two miles and creating significant geographical changes.

name: conquest
improvedDescription: The act of defeating or overcoming through military force or other means of dominance.

name: conscience
originalDescription: We should not confuse and confound the mission and influence of the Adjuster with what is commonly called conscience. Conscience, rightly, admonishes you to do right; but the Adjuster, in addition, endeavors to tell you what truly is right; that is, when and as you are able to perceive the Monitor’s leading. (110:5.1) it is hazardous to attempt the differentiation of the Adjusters’ concept registry from the more or less continuous and conscious reception of the dictations of mortal conscience. (110:5.5) Growth is predicated on the discovery of selfhood accompanied by self-criticism—conscience, for conscience is really the criticism of oneself by one’s own value-habits, personal ideals. (92:2.6) (100:1.5) Conscience is a human and purely psychic reaction. It is not to be despised, but it is hardly the voice of God to the soul, which indeed the Adjuster’s would be if such a voice could be heard. (92:2.6) (110:5.1,5) If we confess your sins, they are forgiven; therefore we must maintain a conscience void of offense. (156:2.7) A misguided conscience can become responsible for much conflict, worry, sorrow, and no end of human unhappiness. (103:2.10) Jesus knew how human beings are swayed by the preacher’s eloquence, and how the conscience responds to emotional appeal (154:6.8) Salem teachers proclaimed conscience as the mandate of God, the voice of Deity. (95:3.5)
improvedDescription: A human psychic reaction that represents the sum total of moral and ethical content of one's cultural development, distinct from the voice of the indwelling Thought Adjuster. Conscience admonishes one to do right but is merely the criticism of oneself by one's own value-habits and personal ideals, while the Adjuster endeavors to reveal what truly is right.

name: consciousness
originalDescription: Consciousness, much less self-consciousness, cannot be explained by any theory of mechanistic electronic association or materialistic energy phenomena. (112:2.10) Self-consciousness consists in intellectual awareness of personality actuality; it includes the ability to recognize the reality of other personalities. Connotes recognition of the actuality of mind ministration and the realization of relative independence of creative and determinative free will. (16:8.6) (16:9.4) One is free to choose and act only within the realm of one’s consciousness. (34:3.8) Self-consciousness is in essence a communal consciousness: God and man, Father and son, Creator and creature. In human self-consciousness four universe-reality realizations are latent and inherent: (16:6.9) (16:9.1,9-13) # The quest for knowledge # The quest for moral values # The quest for spiritual values # The quest for personality values, the ability to recognize the reality of God as a personality the endowment of mind even in impersonal beings qualifies them to think and endows them with consciousness despite the total absence of personality. (9:6.3) The evolutionary type of knowledge is but the accumulation of protoplasmic memory material; this is the most primitive form of creature consciousness. (101:6.4) Mind is not inherent in energy; energy is receptive and responsive to mind; mind can be superimposed upon energy, but consciousness is not inherent in the purely material level. (9:4.2) Mortal consciousness proceeds from the fact, to the meaning, and then to the value. (118:5.3) In the mortal experience the human intellect resides in the rhythmic pulsations of the adjutant mind-spirits and effects its decisions within the arena produced by encircuitment within this ministry. (102:2.5) (117:5.7) Human consciousness rests gently upon the electrochemical mechanism below and delicately touches the spirit-morontia energy system above. (111:1.5) And even as he is this living way of ascension from the self to God, so is the Supreme the living way from finite consciousness to transcendence of consciousness, even to the insight of absonity. (117:3.3) we should trust all matters of mind beyond the dead level of consciousness to the custody of the Adjusters. (110:4.4) self-consciousness
improvedDescription: The state of intellectual awareness that ranges from basic creature awareness to self-consciousness, which includes recognition of personality actuality and the ability to recognize other personalities. Human consciousness rests upon electrochemical mechanisms while touching spirit-morontia energy systems above, and proceeds from fact to meaning to value in mortal experience.

name: conscription
originalDescription: the question of conscription or voluntary military service does not enter into the problems of maintaining world-wide peace. (134:6.6) in the most continental nation of a neighboring planet, military service in peacetime is purely voluntary (72:11.3)
improvedDescription: Mandatory military service, as contrasted with voluntary enlistment. The question of conscription versus voluntary service does not factor into maintaining world peace, as nations will continue to fight regardless of how their armies are assembled.

name: consecration of choice
originalDescription: When man chooses to find God and to be like him, such a choice is superfinite (118:6.7) To God, as absolute, an ascending mortal who has made the eternal decision is already a Paradise finaliter. (118:1.10) creativity of inner life can be haphazard or constructive (111:4.9) Father desires only loyalty which is voluntary, wholehearted, and sophistry-proof (53:4.6) The affectionate dedication of the human will to the doing of the Father’s will is man’s choicest gift to God; in fact, such a consecration of creature will constitutes man’s only possible gift of true value to the Paradise Father. (1:1.2) (196:0.10) Jesus’ faith was so real and all-encompassing that it absolutely swept away any spiritual doubts and effectively destroyed every conflicting desire. (196:0.5) Material mind is the arena in which human personalities live, are self-conscious, make decisions, choose God or forsake him, eternalize or destroy themselves. (111:1) The first jubilee of mortal human marked the agreement with the Thought Adjuster when the purpose to survive was sealed (27:7.8) You are so devoid of courageous decisions and consecrated co-operation that your indwelling Adjusters find it next to impossible to communicate directly with the human mind. (110:4.5) such a will-union is effected by and through Jesus. (180:2.4) a Hebrew proverb says: “Whatsoever your hand finds to do, do that with all your might”. (133:8.3) definition examples of consecration parables regarding consecration result of consecration
improvedDescription: The affectionate dedication of the human will to doing the Father's will, representing man's choicest and only possible gift of true value to God. This consecration constitutes an expansion and glorification of will that raises creature will from temporal significance to communion with the spirit Father.

name: consequences
originalDescription: consequences of our actions belong to God (48:7.13) (95:4.3) (117:5.5-6)
improvedDescription: The results or outcomes of actions that belong to God's domain. While humans are responsible for their acts and decisions, the consequences of these choices are in God's hands and form part of the divine experiencing of the Supreme.

name: consideration
originalDescription: to be considerate of the limitations and inexperience of their juniors and subordinates is a divine injunction (107:3.5)
improvedDescription: The quality of being thoughtful toward others, particularly regarding the limitations and inexperience of juniors and subordinates, which represents a divine injunction for those in positions of authority.

name: consolation
improvedDescription: Comfort or solace provided to those experiencing distress, grief, or disappointment.

name: conspiracy
originalDescription: There truly exists within us a conspiracy of spiritual forces, a confederation of divine powers, whose exclusive purpose is to effect our final deliverance from material bondage and finite handicaps. (34:6.9)
improvedDescription: In spiritual context, the beneficial confederation of divine powers and spiritual forces within each mortal whose exclusive purpose is to effect final deliverance from material bondage and finite handicaps.

name: Constantine
originalDescription: Constantine was the first Roman emperor to be conquered by Christianity (195:0.10)
improvedDescription: The Roman emperor who became the first ruler to be won over to Christianity, representing a significant compromise between Christian leaders and paganism.

name: Constellation Centers
originalDescription: there are ten Constellation Centers assigned to each constellation (29:2.17) (41:1.4) (43:8.1) From these beings there go forth the power lines for communication and transport and for the energizing of those living creatures who are dependent upon certain forms of physical energy for the maintenance of life. (29:2.17) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.97)
improvedDescription: Ten Supreme Power Centers of the fifth order stationed in each constellation, functioning as living energy projectors that provide power lines for communication, transport, and the energizing of creatures dependent upon physical energy for life maintenance.

name: Constellation Father of Norlatiadek
improvedDescription: The reigning Most High ruler of Norlatiadek constellation at any given time, one of three Vorondadek Sons who govern the constellation on a rotating basis.

name: Constellation Fathers
originalDescription: Of the three Most Highs who govern the constellations, the Most High reigning at each moment is called the Constellation Father. (35:6.1)
improvedDescription: The reigning Most High ruler of each constellation, one of three Vorondadek Sons who govern constellations and serve as the head of government during their term of service.

name: constellations
originalDescription: The constellations are the primary and autonomous units of a local universe, each constellation being administered according to its own legislative enactments. (43:0.1) (43:2.1) consist of 100 local systems presided over by 3 Vorondadek Sons, the Most Highs (15:2.4) (32:0.1) they contain 100,000 inhabitable worlds (15:2.5,20) the name of our constellation is Norlatiadek, the number 70 in Nebadon, our local universe (15:14.6) (43:0.1) the spheres of each headquarters are comparable to Edentia, the capital of Norlatiadek. Edentia is a sphere one hundred times the size of Urantia, it is surrounded by 70 satellites ten times the size of Urantia, and each of the satellites is surrounded by ten sub-satellites the size of Urantia (43:0.2) The morontia spheres are the transition phases of mortal ascension through the progression worlds of the local universe. All 56 of the system transition abodes, in common with the higher spheres around the constellations and the universe headquarters, are called morontia worlds. (48:1.2) Nebadon time, broadcast from Salvington, is the standard for all constellations and systems in this local universe. (33:6.8) (43:0.3) government prominent inhabitants functions
improvedDescription: Primary divisions of a local universe, each containing 100 local systems with approximately 100,000 inhabitable worlds, administered by three Vorondadek Sons called the Most Highs and functioning as autonomous legislative units with their own governmental systems.

name: consuls (universe)
originalDescription: Ambassadors are appointed by judicial decree and represent universes to other universes. Consuls are representatives of constellations to one another and to the universe headquarters (33:6.4) Vorondadeks serve as ambassadors to other universes and as consuls representing constellations within their native universe. (35:5.5)
improvedDescription: Representatives of constellations to one another and to the universe headquarters, appointed by legislative decree and functioning only within the confines of their local universe, often served by Vorondadek Sons.

name: Consummator of Universe Destiny
originalDescription: destiny is probably consummated by the act of the Consummator of Universe Destiny, and this act is probably involved with the Supreme and the Ultimate in the Trinity Absolute. (106:7.3) the second experiential Trinity is the Trinity Absolute, the union of God the Supreme, God the Ultimate, and the unrevealed Consummator of Universe Destiny. (106:5.1) (0:12.7)
improvedDescription: An unrevealed deity figure who, along with God the Supreme and God the Ultimate, constitutes the Trinity Absolute and is involved with the consummation of universe destiny through acts that encompass infinite existential-experiential attainment.

name: contact personalities
originalDescription: In the contacts made with the mortal beings of the material worlds, such as with the subject through whom the Urantia revelations were transmitted, the midway creatures are always employed as contact personalities. (77:7.5) (77:8.8) there are less than twenty contact personalities in the Urantia destiny reservists who are aware of being prepared for a possible function in certain planetary crises (114:7.8)
improvedDescription: Fewer than twenty specially prepared members of the Urantia reserve corps of destiny who are consciously aware of their role as intermediaries between superhuman intelligences and mortal beings, with midway creatures serving as essential contact guardians during such communications.

name: contempt
originalDescription: Adam and Eve were absolved from charges of standing in “contempt of the universe government”. (75:7.2) Outmarriage finally dominated because it was favored by the man; to get a wife from the outside insured greater freedom from in-laws. Familiarity breeds contempt (82:5.6) In the universe of Nebadon there are ten thousand systems of inhabited worlds. In all the history of Lanonandek Sons, in all their work throughout these thousands of systems and at the universe headquarters, only three System Sovereigns have ever been found in contempt of the government of the Creator Son. (53:0.2) Van formally drew his indictment of Daligastia, Caligastia, and Lucifer as standing in contempt of the sovereignty of the universe of Nebadon; and he appealed to the Most Highs of Edentia for support and confirmation. (67:2.2)
improvedDescription: A charge of defiance against universe government, as when Adam and Eve were absolved of standing in contempt of universe government, or when Van formally indicted Caligastia, Daligastia, and Lucifer for contempt of Nebadon's sovereignty.

name: contention
originalDescription: By discretion we must show ourselves to be expert in ironing out minor disagreements and in adjusting trifling misunderstandings. (178:1.7) conciliating commissions settle contention (25:3) division results when not all in a family believe in the gospel (165:6.3) Let controversy come only when they who despise the truth force it upon you. (178:1.16) The moment the Creators bring into existence evolving individuals with the power of choice misunderstandings are certain to arise. (25:3.7) Jesus and the apostles
improvedDescription: Disagreements and misunderstandings that naturally arise when evolving individuals with the power of choice interact, requiring wisdom and discretion to resolve and the services of conciliating commissions for fair adjustment of honest differences of viewpoint.

name: contentment
improvedDescription: A state of peaceful satisfaction and acceptance, related to happiness and spiritual well-being in the context of universe relationships and personal growth.

name: continence cult
originalDescription: The normal urges of animal beings and the natural appetites and impulses of the physical nature are not in conflict with even the highest spiritual attainment (34:7.7) The continence cult originated as a ritual among soldiers prior to engaging in battle; in later days it became the practice of “saints”. ” This cult tolerated marriage only as an evil lesser than fornication. (89:3.6)
improvedDescription: A religious cult that originated as a ritual among soldiers before battle and later became practiced by religious ascetics or "saints." This cult viewed marriage as only a lesser evil than fornication and promoted sexual abstinence, significantly influencing Christianity through the Apostle Paul's teachings despite not being part of Jesus' original gospel.

name: continental drift
improvedDescription: The gradual movement and separation of Earth's continental land masses over geological time periods, beginning approximately 750 million years ago when the first breaks occurred in the original single continental mass.

name: continental nation
originalDescription: Extensive information is given in The Urantia Book about a neighboring planet of Urantia, not named, and located in the Satania system. The planet is also in quarantine and has many similarities to Urantia. Of this planet we are told above all of the most developed nation, a continental nation. (72:0.1-2) This nation numbers about 140 million. Its people are a mixed race, predominantly blue and yellow, having a slightly greater proportion of violet than the so-called white race of Urantia. (72:1.1) enjoys a unique topography and is aproximatedly the size of Australia (72:1.1-2) This continental nation now has a representative government with a centrally located national capital. The central government consists of a strong federation of 100 comparatively free states. (72:2) The transition from monarchy to a representative form of government was gradual (72:1.4-5) Just now this superior government is planning to establish ambassadorial relations with the inferior peoples (72:12.2) This nation is adjudicated by two major court systems—the law courts and the socioeconomic courts. (72:2.9-17) (72:5.4) (72:8.3) The methods of this people in dealing with crime, insanity, and degeneracy, while in some ways pleasing, will, no doubt, in others prove shocking to most Urantians. (72:10) The educational system of this nation is compulsory and coeducational in the precollege schools that the student attends from the ages of 5 to 18. Estas escuelas son muy diferentes a las de Urantia. No hay aulas, se estudia una sola materia a la vez, y después de los tres primeros años, todos los alumnos se convierten en profesores auxiliares (72:4.1) Spiritual teachers are under the direction of the Foundation of Spiritual Progress and monitor whether parents educate their children properly. (72:3.6) The home life of this people has greatly improved during the last century. Attendance of parents, both fathers and mothers, at the parental schools of child culture is compulsory.(72:3) The industrial situation among this people is far from their ideals; capital and labor still have their troubles. The abolition of slavery happened over one hundred years ago (72:5) They enjoy a brisk domestic commerce but have little foreign trade owing to the universal hostility of their less progressive neighbors. (72:1.3) Funds for old-age pensions come from taxes, bequests, compulsory labor wages, and natural resource revenues (72:6) statesmanship schools are of three classes: national, regional, and state, and prepare for the public offices (72:8.2) Military training is never given without a associated industrial, scientific, or professional schooling. In this way the creation of a professional military class is avoided. (72:7.9,14) (72:11) There are five different types of metropolitan government, depending on the size of the city, but no city is permitted to have more than one million inhabitants. (72:2.2) Cities have no taxing power, neither can they go in debt. There is little or no uniformity among the taxation schemes of the one hundred comparatively free and sovereign states as economic and other conditions vary greatly in different sections of the continent. (72:7) they has benefited by neither magisterial nor bestowal missions of the Paradise Sons (72:12.2-5) Schools of philosophy are affiliated with the temples of philosophy and are more or less associated with religion as a public function. (72:8.4)
improvedDescription: An advanced civilization on a neighboring planet in the Satania system, numbering about 140 million people of mixed blue, yellow, and violet races. This nation occupies a continent about the size of Australia and has developed a superior representative government, advanced educational system, and progressive social institutions, serving as an example of what human civilization might achieve.

name: continents
originalDescription: 950 millions years ago Urantia presents the picture of one great continent of land and one large body of water, the Pacific Ocean.  (57:8) The sea bottoms are more dense than the land masses, and this is what keeps the continents above water. (58:5.6-8) continental drift
improvedDescription: The large land masses of Urantia that began as a single continental mass 950 million years ago and gradually separated through continental drift. The continents are lighter than the ocean floors, which keeps them above water, though they tend to creep toward the oceans due to differential pressure.

name: control and overcontrol
improvedDescription: A concept relating to the proper balance between exercising necessary control and avoiding excessive or counterproductive overcontrol in various aspects of life and governance.

name: controversies
improvedDescription: Disputes, debates, or disagreements that arise over differing viewpoints, beliefs, or interpretations of important matters.

name: conversion
improvedDescription: The spiritual transformation process by which an individual changes from one set of beliefs or spiritual state to another, often involving a fundamental reorientation of faith and values.

name: convictions
improvedDescription: Firmly held beliefs or principles that guide an individual's thoughts, decisions, and actions, often based on deep personal or spiritual certainties.

name: cooking
originalDescription: Jesus cooked fish when he appeared before the apostles (192:1.8) Onagar’s missionaries were the world’s first missionaries; they were also the first human beings to cook meat, the first regularly to use fire in the preparation of food. (63:6.7) Prince’s staff taught mankind that cooking, boiling and roasting, was a means of avoiding sickness (66:5.18) cooking lessened the expenditure of vital energy necessary for the digestion of food and so left early man some strength for social culture (69:6.7)
improvedDescription: The preparation of food using fire and heat, first practiced regularly by Onagar's missionaries and taught by Prince's staff as a means of avoiding sickness and reducing the energy needed for digestion. Cooking enabled early humans to preserve strength for social and cultural development.

name: copper
originalDescription: copper was brought up in lava (58:7.6) cuttlefish oxygenate blood with copper (65:6.4) Much of the copper in these rock layers results from water deposition. (58:7.10) the Andites early learned to work in iron, gold, and copper, as well. Copper was next employed but not extensively until it was admixed with tin to make the harder bronze. (81:3.4-5)
improvedDescription: A metal brought to Earth's surface through volcanic lava flows and water deposition. Copper serves biological functions in some sea creatures for oxygen transport and was among the first metals worked by the Andites, later being mixed with tin to create the harder bronze alloy.

name: coral
originalDescription: lime-secreting algae were ancestry of coral (59:2.11)
improvedDescription: Marine organisms that evolved from lime-secreting algae and formed extensive reef formations throughout Earth's ancient seas, particularly during the Devonian period when coral reefs characterized the clear, shallow inland seas.

name: cords, knotted
originalDescription: an early writing knotted cords (69:4.7)
improvedDescription: An early form of written communication using knotted cords, representing one of the primitive stages in the evolution of writing before the development of symbolic alphabets.

name: Corinth
originalDescription: Corinth at the time of Jesus was the second city in Greece, and the third in the Roman Empire after Alexandria and Rome (133:3.4) (133:4.13) it was the capital of the Roman province of Achaia (133:2.5) You could travel in a small boat that could be transported overland on a rail from one of the Corinthian ports to the other, a distance of ten miles. (133:4.14) Jesus' personal work in Corinth led him to meet: (133:3-4) Peter visited Corinth (139:2.11)
improvedDescription: The second city in Greece and third in the Roman Empire after Alexandria and Rome, serving as the capital of the Roman province of Achaia. A major cosmopolitan commercial center where Jesus spent two months during his Mediterranean tour, meeting people from three continents.

name: Cornelius
originalDescription: Cornelius is the Roman centurion from Caesarea who was converted by Peter, and whose subordinate, Anaxand, met Jesus some time before and was moved by his words (130:2.5)
improvedDescription: A Roman centurion from Caesarea who was converted by Peter. His subordinate Anaxand had previously met Jesus and been moved by his words, later becoming the steward to Cornelius after both men joined the early Christian church.

name: cornerstone
originalDescription: There is a Jewish saying that said: the stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone when the people discovered it. (173:4.4)
improvedDescription: A metaphorical reference from Jewish scripture about 'the stone which the builders rejected' becoming the cornerstone, used by Jesus to warn religious leaders about rejecting the gospel message.

name: corporeal staffs of Planetary Princes
originalDescription: On going to a young world, a Planetary Prince usually takes with him a group of volunteer ascending beings from the local system headquarters.  This corps of material helpers constitutes the connecting link between the prince and the world races, called the corps of one hundred or staff of the Prince (50:3.1) The Life Carriers provided such volunteers with new physical bodies, which they occupied for the periods of their planetary sojourn. (50:3.3) The one hundred, while exempt from the ordinary diseases, are, like the early morontia bodies, subject to certain accidents. (50:3.3) their immortality was sustained by tree of life (73:6.4) origin descendants work in planets
improvedDescription: A corps of one hundred volunteer ascending beings from system headquarters who accompany Planetary Princes to new worlds, serving as advisers and helpers in early race improvement. These materialized beings receive new physical bodies from Life Carriers and serve as the connecting link between the prince and world races.

name: corporeality error
originalDescription: The corporeality error is shown in both extremes of human philosophy. In materialism, since man loses his body at death, he ceases to exist as a personality; in pantheism, since God has no body, he is not, therefore, a person. (1:5.12)
improvedDescription: A philosophical error shown in both materialism and pantheism regarding the relationship between personality and physical form. Materialism wrongly assumes personality ceases at bodily death, while pantheism incorrectly concludes God cannot be a person without a body.

name: Corps of Conjoint Trinitized Finaliters
originalDescription: The Corps of Conjoint Trinitized Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps. It has is headquarters on Vicegerington (31:1.5) (31:10.5) The Havona natives are received, in the same ratio, into the Corps of Conjoint Trinitized Finaliters on Vicegerington and into the Corps of Transcendental Finaliters on Paradise. The Havona citizens regard these three destinies as constituting the supreme goals of their supernal careers, together with their possible admission to the Corps of Havona Finaliters. (31:1.5) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.135)
improvedDescription: One of the seven finaliter corps with headquarters on Vicegerington. Havona natives are received into this corps along with the Corps of Transcendental Finaliters and Corps of Havona Finaliters as supreme career destinations.

name: Corps of Havona Finaliters
originalDescription: The Corps of Havona Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps (31:1.5) (31:10.6) it appears in Uversa personality register (30:2.136)
improvedDescription: One of the seven finaliter corps that represents one of the supreme career goals for Havona natives, alongside potential admission to other finaliter corps.

name: Corps of Light and Life
originalDescription: on Uversa is not known the “finality destiny” of the ascendant mortals of time. At present they reside on Paradise and temporarily serve in the Corps of Light and Life, although is expected for them greater tests of trust and more sublime services of responsibility. (31:3.2)
improvedDescription: A Paradise corps where ascendant mortals of time temporarily serve while their ultimate finality destiny remains unknown, designed to prepare them for greater tests of trust and more sublime services of responsibility.

name: Corps of Mortal Finaliters
originalDescription: The Corps of Mortal Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps (31:10.2) agondonters, ascenders that have lived in an isolated world due to rebellion, are functionally grouped inside the finaliters (50:7.2) they are a wholly self-governing body and their oath of allegiance is only to the Paradise Trinity (31:0.12) As yet there seems to be no specific or settled employment for the Mortal Corps of Finaliters, though they serve in many capacities on worlds settled in light and life. (30:4.32-33) they were attacked by Lucifer. He intimated that the finaliters were in collusion with the Paradise Sons to foist fraud upon all creation about the existence of the Father since they never brought back a very clear-cut idea of the Father’s actual personality as it is discernible on Paradise. (53:3.2,6) The primary finaliter corps is composed of Havona natives, Gravity Messengers, Glorified Mortals, Adopted Seraphim, and Glorified Material Sons and Midwayers (31:0.1) (31:2.1) Trinity Teacher Sons are intimately connected with finaliters (20:9.4-5) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.132) companies destiny mortal admission to finaliter corps
improvedDescription: The Corps of Mortal Finaliters is one of seven finaliter corps comprising primarily glorified mortals who have ascended from evolutionary worlds and achieved Paradise. This self-governing body takes an oath of allegiance only to the Paradise Trinity and serves throughout the universes in various capacities, particularly on worlds settled in light and life, while their ultimate destiny remains unrevealed.

name: Corps of Paradise Finaliters
originalDescription: the Corps of Paradise Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps (31:10.3) it appears in Uversa personality register (30:2.133) For ages upon ages there has been a mobilization on Paradise of beings in association with finaliter corps (31:10.17)
improvedDescription: The Corps of Paradise Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps composed of beings who have achieved Paradise status. This corps is part of the mysterious ongoing mobilization of perfected and ascendant beings of time and space on Paradise.

name: Corps of Perfection
originalDescription: the Corps of Perfection is a local universe corps. It is the destiny of Spirit-fused ascenders (37:5.3-4)
improvedDescription: The Corps of Perfection is a local universe organization that serves as the eternal destiny of Spirit-fused ascenders who have achieved spiritual perfection but are not destined for the Paradise Corps of the Finality.

name: corps of permanent citizens
originalDescription: all divisions of the administrative organization of the local universes and superuniverses have their more or less permanent populations, inhabitants of citizenship status. (37:9.12) members
improvedDescription: The corps of permanent citizens consists of various orders of beings who serve as stable, long-term inhabitants of administrative worlds throughout the local universes and superuniverses, providing continuity amid the constant changes of transient celestial ministers and mortal inhabitants.

name: Corps of Seraphic Completion
originalDescription: To climb to the supernal heights of finaliter sonship with God is a masterly achievement for an angel, an accomplishment far transcending your attainment of eternal survival through the plan of the Eternal Son and the ever-present help of the indwelling Adjuster; but the guardian seraphim, and occasionally others, do actually effect such ascensions. (40:1.2) bestowal attendants are completion seraphim; they have all traversed the circles of Seraphington and have attained the Seraphic Corps of Completion.  Such seraphim have all achieved Paradise and the personal embrace of the Second Source and Center, the Eternal Son. (39:1.5) Many of the Seraphington graduates, members of the Seraphic Corps of Completion who are associated with these seraphim, engage in teaching in the Salvington schools concerned with the preparation of the creatures of Nebadon for the next universe age. (39:1.12) (113:7.5)
improvedDescription: The Seraphic Corps of Completion is composed of angels who have traversed the circles of Seraphington, achieved Paradise, and attained the personal embrace of the Eternal Son. These completion seraphim serve in exalted capacities throughout the universes and as associates to high Paradise orders.

name: Corps of the Finality
improvedDescription: The Corps of the Finality refers to the collective organization of the seven finaliter corps, representing the highest achievement of evolutionary and perfected beings in their eternal service to the Paradise Trinity.

name: Corps of Transcendental Finaliters
originalDescription: Corps of Transcendental Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps (31:10.7) a Havona native can join each company of the Corps of Transcendental Finaliters of Paradise, one of the three supreme goals for the citizens of Havona (31:1.5) they are based on Paradise (31:1.5) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.137)
improvedDescription: The Corps of Transcendental Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps based on Paradise, representing one of the three supreme career goals available to Havona natives who join in the same ratio as other finaliter organizations.

name: Corps of Trinitized Finaliters
originalDescription: Corps of Trinitized Finaliters is one of the seven finaliter corps (31:10.4) Excepting the Trinitized Sons of Perfection and those who are forgathering on Vicegerington, the supreme destiny of all creature-trinitized sons and certain Paradise Citizens appears to be entrance into the Corps of Trinitized Finaliters. (22:8.6) (26:11.2) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.134)
improvedDescription: The Corps of Trinitized Finaliters is one of the seven Paradise Corps of the Finality, serving as the supreme destiny for most creature-trinitized sons and certain Paradise Citizens who have achieved Trinity embrace.

name: Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny
originalDescription: The Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny is one of the finaliter corps (31:10.8) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.138)
improvedDescription: The Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny is one of the seven finaliter corps, though their nature, composition, and purpose remain undisclosed in current revelations.

name: correctional rest
originalDescription: The Melchizedeks are well-nigh perfect in wisdom, but they are not infallible in judgment. When detached and alone on planetary missions they have sometimes erred in minor matters. These Melchizedeks receive correctional rest, purging disharmony (35:2.8)
improvedDescription: Correctional rest is a purifying process that Melchizedeks undergo when they have made errors in judgment during planetary missions, requiring instruction from the Creator Son to restore harmony before reinstatement to service on the third day.

name: cosmic citizenship
originalDescription: In the fraternal realities of the kingdom of heaven the faith sons of God find final deliverance from the isolation of the self, both personal and planetary. The God-knowing believer increasingly experiences the ecstasy and grandeur of spiritual socialization on a universe scale—citizenship on high in association with the eternal realization of the divine destiny of perfection attainment. (184:4.6) cosmic citizenship is the honest recognition of your progressive obligations to the Supreme Being (110:3.10) angels teach about the responsibility and morality of cosmic citizenship to mortals (39:4.9)
improvedDescription: The spiritual recognition and acceptance of one's progressive obligations to the Supreme Being and membership in the universal community. Cosmic citizenship encompasses the realization of interdependence between evolutionary mortals and evolving Deity, representing the birth of cosmic morality and universal duty. It offers faith sons of God deliverance from personal and planetary isolation through spiritual socialization on a universe scale.

name: cosmic cloud
originalDescription: The cosmic cloud, the great space blanket, consists for the most part of the modified atoms of calcium. Our whole superuniverse is sprinkled with minutely pulverized stone. (41:6.2)
improvedDescription: The great space blanket consisting primarily of modified atoms of calcium that permeates space throughout the superuniverse of Orvonton. This cosmic cloud represents minutely pulverized stone material that serves as the basic building matter for planets and spheres of space.

name: cosmic consciousness
improvedDescription: The awareness and recognition of cosmic realities and universal principles that extends beyond purely material or local perspectives to encompass the broader spiritual and intellectual dimensions of universe existence.

name: cosmic force
improvedDescription: The pre-energy manifestation of space potency that represents the first step in the individuation of primordial force-charge into physical energy forms. Cosmic force is one of the common names for absoluta and proceeds from Paradise as the source of all original force-energy that circulates throughout the master universe.

name: cosmic mind
originalDescription: definition location appearance function
improvedDescription: The sevenfold diversified mind of time and space ministered by the Seven Master Spirits to the seven superuniverses. As a subabsolute manifestation of the mind of the Third Source and Center, cosmic mind encompasses all finite-mind levels and provides the intellectual foundation for recognizing physical causation, moral duty, and spiritual worship across the grand universe.

name: cosmic reserve corps
improvedDescription: A specialized group of cosmic beings or personalities designated for particular universe service or emergency functions, likely related to the broader reserve corps organizations that serve various administrative and spiritual purposes throughout the universes.

name: cosmology
originalDescription: cosmology must be approached by proceeding from the complex and the infinite to the simple and finite. If the opposite is done, genetic knowledge about the origin of man is acquired, but not the spiritual wisdom that reveals the destiny of man. (19:1.5-12) Civilization is in danger when youth neglect to interest themselves in ethics, sociology, eugenics, philosophy, the fine arts, religion, and cosmology. (111:4.4) cosmology leads to the pursuit of divine reality values—to God-consciousness. (56:10.8) the cosmology contained in the Urantia Papers revelations is not inspired. It is limited by permission to revelators to co-ordinate and sort existing knowledge, and is destined to be outgrown quickly. (101:4.1-10)
improvedDescription: The study of the origin, structure, and destiny of the cosmos and universe reality. True cosmology requires understanding the correlation of origin, history, and destiny rather than approaching from simple to complex, and leads ultimately to the pursuit of divine reality values and God-consciousness. The cosmology presented in The Urantia Book is acknowledged as not inspired and destined to be outgrown.

name: consumados
improvedDescription: A term referring to beings or entities that have achieved completion or consummation in their development or function, likely representing a class of perfected personalities in the universe hierarchy.

name: cosmos absolute
improvedDescription: A conceptual designation for the absolute level of cosmic reality, representing the ultimate expression of universe existence beyond finite and even ultimate levels of manifestation.

name: cosmos-infinite
originalDescription: the cosmos-infinite is a kind of illimitable cosmos on beyond the master universe, in which it is postulated that the Absolute Trinity will achieve final expression in the creations of infinity, completing the absolute actualization of all potentials (106:6.3) cosmos-infinite is a limitless, boundless, never-beginning, never-ending existence the comprehension of which is really beyond man's ability (115:3.1) the fourth triunity contains the energy potential of the cosmos-infinite, of which the grand universe and the master universe are only partial manifestations. (104:4.26)
improvedDescription: An illimitable cosmos conceived to exist beyond the master universe where the Absolute Trinity may achieve final expression in the creations of infinity. This limitless, boundless, never-beginning, never-ending existence represents the absolute actualization of all potentials, though its comprehension is beyond mortal ability.

name: council of elders
originalDescription: In Antiquity the first real governmental body was the council of the elders. This regulative group was composed of old men who had distinguished themselves in some efficient manner.  (70:5.2-3) The early and diffuse powers of the primitive council of elders were gradually concentrated in the person of the absolute monarch. (70:12.2)
improvedDescription: The first real governmental body in primitive human societies, composed of distinguished older men who possessed wisdom and experience. This regulative group initially held all governmental functions - executive, legislative, and judicial - before their powers were gradually concentrated in absolute monarchs.

name: Council of Equilibrium
originalDescription: this council is comprised of Associate Master Force Organizers dispatched by Seven Master Spirits (29:4.2) one Force Organizer was dispatched by this council to Andronover 900,000 million years ago (29:4.2) (57:1.4) the are able to interpret registrations of master frandalanks (29:4.2)
improvedDescription: A governing body comprised of Associate Master Force Organizers dispatched by the Seven Master Spirits, responsible for directing and distributing Master Physical Controllers throughout the superuniverse. They are empowered to interpret the readings of master frandalanks and authorize the dispatch of force organizers for new material creations.

name: council of perfection
originalDescription: Universal Conciliators are translated to council of perfection on Paradise, from which they subsequently emerge as the co-ordinating corps evolved by the Infinite Spirit for the universe of universes (25:3.16)
improvedDescription: A Paradise council where Universal Conciliators are translated and trained before emerging as a coordinating corps evolved by the Infinite Spirit for the universe of universes.

name: council of Planetary Princes
originalDescription: System Sovereign presides over the system council of world rulers, the Planetary Princes and the resident governors general of the isolated worlds. This planetary council assembles from time to time on the headquarters of the system (45:2.4)
improvedDescription: A system council of world rulers presided over by the System Sovereign, consisting of Planetary Princes and resident governors general of isolated worlds. This planetary council assembles periodically at the system headquarters.

name: council of seven
originalDescription: council of seven was an ancient tribal government of red race (70:5.4)
improvedDescription: An ancient tribal government structure of the red race that preserved the teaching of Onamonalonton, following the unanimous rule of seven members.

name: council of seventy
originalDescription: the council of seventy was a council of seventy members chosen from among mortals, who advise the Planetary Princes, and which is analogous to the council of 24 counselors (50:2.2)
improvedDescription: A council of seventy members selected from evolutionary beings at constellation headquarters, analogous to the council of twenty-four counselors, that provides advice to planetary administration.

name: council on food and material welfare
improvedDescription: One of the specialized councils within the Caligastia one hundred, responsible for overseeing food production and material welfare of early human civilization.

name: counsel
improvedDescription: The fifth adjutant mind-spirit that provides guidance, advice, and wise judgment to evolutionary beings, enabling them to make thoughtful decisions and seek appropriate guidance.

name: counseling the rich man
originalDescription: These are the pieces of advice that Jesus offered to a wealthy Roman citizen who practiced Stoicism: (132:5)
improvedDescription: Jesus' advice to a wealthy Roman citizen and Stoic about the proper use of material wealth, emphasizing its role as a trust for enhancing material, intellectual, social, and spiritual life for current and future generations.

name: counselors and advisers
originalDescription: counselors and advisers are secondary supernaphim of second Havona circle, and are the instructors of the children of time regarding the career of eternity. (26:10.1-7) A special liaison exists between the counselors and advisers of the second Havona circle and these reflective angels. (28:5.19)
improvedDescription: Secondary supernaphim of the second Havona circle who serve as instructors for ascending mortals regarding their eternal career. They provide counsel to successful Paradise pilgrims and comfort to those who initially fail in Deity attainment, preparing both groups for their next stages of eternal progression.

name: counterirritants
originalDescription: ancients treated disease with Fasting, dieting, and counterirritants. (90:4.8)
improvedDescription: Medicinal treatments used by ancient peoples to cure disease, involving substances that cause irritation or inflammation to counteract illness, often used alongside fasting and dieting.

name: counterpart
originalDescription: we should allow Adjusters to show us the spiritual counterpart to material efforts (111:7.3) Teacher Sons show spiritual counterpart to material wisdom (20:9.2)
improvedDescription: The spiritual equivalent or reflection of material experiences and wisdom, revealed by Thought Adjusters to help mortals understand cosmic truths and by Trinity Teacher Sons who demonstrate spiritual realities corresponding to material knowledge.

name: counting the cost
originalDescription: This is a speech that Jesus delivered to his followers at the ford of Bethany in the Jordan: Jesus emphasized the importance of being willing to pay the full price of dedication to God's will. He urged his disciples to be ready to abandon everything, including family and even their own lives, to follow him. He illustrated this with examples of calculating the cost before building a tower or going to war. Jesus warned that following him would involve severe persecutions and sacrifices, and that only those who were fully committed and willing to renounce all worldly attachments would be worthy of being his disciples. He concluded by urging his followers to examine their motives and be prepared for the trials ahead. (171:2)
improvedDescription: A discourse delivered by Jesus at the ford of Bethany emphasizing the importance of full dedication to God's will and being prepared to sacrifice everything, including family and life itself, to follow him as a disciple.

name: courage
originalDescription: definition the achievement of courage Jesus’ courage
improvedDescription: The confidence that comes from thoroughgoing honesty about one's beliefs, representing the heart of Jesus' teachings and essential for spiritual growth, problem-solving, and the pursuit of truth.

name: court advisers
originalDescription: Court Advisers are supreme seraphim that see that all charges against mortal creatures are stated in justice and adjudicated in mercy. (39:1.7-9)
improvedDescription: Supreme seraphim who serve as advisers to all orders of adjudication, ensuring that charges against mortal creatures are stated in justice and adjudicated in mercy, often serving as defenders of mortals in judicial proceedings.

name: court of gentiles
originalDescription: the Jerusalem temple in Jesus' time had an ornate court for Gentiles, the only place Gentiles were tolerated in the temple (121:2.5) there was where sacrificial animals were sold and money-changers plied their trade (125:1.1,4) (162:4.3)
improvedDescription: The ornate outer court of the Jerusalem temple where non-Jews were permitted, serving as a marketplace for sacrificial animals and money-changers, which Jesus found irreverent and inappropriate for his Father's house.

name: court of Israel
originalDescription: the courtyard of Israel was a courtyard of the Jerusalem temple in the time of Jesus where Jewish men could watch the sacrifices (162:4.3)
improvedDescription: A courtyard in the Jerusalem temple where Jewish men could gather to observe religious ceremonies and sacrifices, positioned between the court of women and the priests' court.

name: court of priests
originalDescription: the court of the priests was a court in front of the court of Israel, in the temple of Jerusalem at the time of Jesus, where the priests were arranged. It was situated under a rock ledge where the altar was (125:1.4) (162:4.4)
improvedDescription: The innermost court of the Jerusalem temple located beneath a rock ledge in front of the temple where the altar stood and priests performed animal sacrifices and ritual washings.

name: court of women
originalDescription: the court of the women was a court in the temple of Jerusalem in which the presence of women was allowed, unlike the court of the Israelites or the priests, where they were forbidden to enter (125:0.4) (135:2.1) (150:1.3)
improvedDescription: A designated area in the Jerusalem temple where women were permitted to gather for worship, representing the furthest point women could enter in the temple complex due to gender restrictions of the time.

name: courtesans
improvedDescription: High-class prostitutes or companions who provided entertainment and companionship, often associated with wealthy or influential men in ancient societies.

name: courtesy
originalDescription: Jesus instructed to avoid unnecessary waste of time in social ceremonies, but he enjoined courtesy toward all. (163:4.14) originally, courtesy was an attempt to deceive envious spirits (87:5.7)
improvedDescription: A social virtue involving respectful and considerate behavior toward others. Jesus instructed his followers to avoid unnecessary waste of time in social ceremonies while enjoining courtesy toward all people they encountered. Originally, courtesy developed as an attempt to deceive envious spirits by showing humility and restraint.

name: courtesy colonies
originalDescription: courtesy colonies are found from local systems to superuniverse headquarters (30:2.157) (37:10.4) (46:5.30-31) the groups working in courtesy colonies are: (30:2.150-156) # Star Students (28:7.1) (30:3.2-5) # Celestial Artisans (44) (30:3.6) # Reversion Directors (30:3.7) (48:4) # Extension-school Instructors (30:3.8-9) # Various Reserve Corps (30:3.10) # Student Visitors (30:3.11-12) # Ascending Pilgrims (30:3.13) courtesy colonies of Uversa served by seconaphim (28:7.1) Omniaphim are of grand universe assignment, and in Orvonton their corps maintains headquarters in the northerly parts of Uversa, where they reside as a special courtesy colony. (28:2.1)
improvedDescription: Special residential communities found on headquarters worlds from local systems to superuniverse capitals, housing seven distinct groups of beings engaged in various missions and assignments. These colonies include Star Students, Celestial Artisans, Reversion Directors, Extension-school Instructors, Various Reserve Corps, Student Visitors, and Ascending Pilgrims, all working together in the furtherance of universe administration and education.

name: courts
originalDescription: evolution future of courts
improvedDescription: Judicial institutions that evolved from primitive regulated combat between disputants to formal legal proceedings. The first courts were merely refereed fights where judges served as umpires, but they gradually developed into systems of verbal arguments and formal trials with established procedures for determining justice and settling disputes.

name: courtship
improvedDescription: The process of romantic pursuit and relationship development between potential marriage partners.

name: covenants
originalDescription: In antiquity the gods were conceived of as entering into real agreements with man; and this was a major step in the stabilization of religion. Law, a covenant, takes the place of luck, fear, and superstition. (89:8.4)
improvedDescription: Sacred agreements or contracts between divine beings and humans that represented a major advancement in religious development. In antiquity, gods were conceived as entering into real agreements with mortals, with law and covenant replacing luck, fear, and superstition as the foundation of religious relationship.

name: cowardice
originalDescription: Abraham, although a brave man, exhibited cowardice three times (93:9.3) Jesus showed how prudence and discretion, when carried too far, lead to cowardice and failure. (149:4.4) The last words of the closing statement of Jesus’ sermon in the Capernaum synagogue: “Hate is the shadow of fear; revenge the mask of cowardice”. (145:3.4) cowards
improvedDescription: The quality of lacking courage or being excessively fearful when facing challenges or danger.

name: coyotes
originalDescription: Certain North American Indian tribes believed they originated from beavers and coyotes. (74:8.5)
improvedDescription: Wild canines that certain North American Indian tribes believed were among their ancestral origins. These primitive peoples practiced selecting animals of their supposed ancestry as totems, with some tribes teaching they descended from beavers and coyotes.

name: Crab nebula
originalDescription: The Crab nebula collapsed 900 years ago (41:8.4)
improvedDescription: An irregular nebular formation that originated from a stellar collapse approximately 900 years ago. The nebula still exhibits its mother sphere as a lone star near the center of the irregular nebular mass, representing the residual cooling sun surrounded by extensive clouds of nebular gases.

name: crabs
originalDescription: About 110 million years ago, crabs, along with lobsters and other modern types of crustaceans, matured and became prominent in marine life. Despite the presence of ferocious sea serpents that threatened the fish family, crabs and other crustaceans continued to thrive and evolve. (59:1.18) (60:2.9)
improvedDescription: Marine crustaceans that matured and became prominent in ocean life about 110 million years ago alongside lobsters and other modern crustacean types. Despite threats from ferocious sea serpents that endangered fish families, crabs continued to thrive and evolve as part of the diverse marine ecosystem.

name: cradle
originalDescription: Woman has always been the moral standard-bearer and the spiritual leader of mankind. The hand that rocks the cradle still fraternizes with destiny. (84:6.4)
improvedDescription: A metaphorical reference to the hand that rocks the cradle, symbolizing women's enduring influence as moral standard-bearers and spiritual leaders who continue to shape human destiny.

name: craftworkers of color
originalDescription: craftworkers of color are part of the designers and embellishers group inside Celestial Artisans (44:6.3)
improvedDescription: Celestial beings who create ten thousand color tones of spirit reflection that convey harmonious messages of beauty, representing activities beyond human experience in color perception.

name: creation
originalDescription: first transaction recorded in Paradise is the personification of Infinite Spirit (25:6.6) the first entry appearing in the personality section in Paradise is the recital of the trinitization of twenty-one Ancients of Days (18:3.4) creation according to The Urantia Book legends of creation
improvedDescription: The cosmic process beginning with the personification of the Infinite Spirit and encompassing all reality differentiation from the theoretical I AM through the establishment of finite universes, local creations, and evolutionary worlds.

name: creationism
originalDescription: the ideas of creationism are at best discouraging since they are predicated upon a belief in retrogression rather than progression, as well as implying a vengeful Deity, who had vented wrath upon the human race in retribution for the errors of certain onetime planetary administrators. (74:8.13)
improvedDescription: The belief in fiat creation of the human race that leads to discouraging outlooks predicated on retrogression rather than progression, implying a vengeful Deity who punished humanity for planetary administrative errors.

name: Creative Daughters
improvedDescription: Divine beings associated with the Creative Spirits and local universe administration, serving as partners in the creative work of local universes.

name: Creative Mother Spirits
improvedDescription: The local universe manifestations of the Infinite Spirit who serve as creative partners with Creator Sons in organizing and administering local universes.

name: Creative Spirits
improvedDescription: Divine beings who participate in the creative and administrative functions of local universes, working in association with Creator Sons.

name: creativity
originalDescription: creativity can be spontaneous and wholly haphazard or controlled, directed, and constructive. (111:4.9) freewill creativity embraces the potential of freewill destructivity. And when creativity is turned to destructivity, we are face to face with the devastation of evil and sin—oppression, war, and destruction. (111:4.11) in stock hybridization when such matings take place between the lower or inferior strata, creativity is diminished (82:6.5) divine creativity unfailingly characterized by unity (105:2.7) Inner creativity contributes to ennoblement of character through personality integration and selfhood unification. (111:4.12) All creational phenomena are reflective of antecedent creator-spirit activities. (56:8.4) Reflective Spirits were created upon Master Spirits’ attainment of perfect synchrony with Trinity (17:2.2) “Will is that manifestation of the human mind which enables the subjective consciousness to express itself objectively and to experience the phenomenon of aspiring to be Godlike. ” And it is in this same sense that every reflective and spiritually minded human being can become creative. (130:2.10)
improvedDescription: The divine capacity for bringing forth new realities that can be spontaneous or controlled, constructive or destructive, manifesting in both divine creation and human creative expression that aspires to be Godlike.

name: Creator Sons
originalDescription: they are listed among Supreme Creators (56:5.3) (116:2.4-12) an almost infinite variety characterizes the plans of the Creator Sons for organizing, evolving, disciplining, and settling their respective local universes. (21:2.10) (32:3.3) Creator Sons come near the creatures of their inhabited worlds and “draw all men to themselves” (1:4.2) their current number is over 700,000, and is constantly growing. There is no limit to the number of them that can be (21:1.4) (33:4.2) Creator Sons of God aspire to duplicate in time and to reproduce in space the pattern universe, Havona, in his own local universe (14:0.2) (14:6.27-31) (54:2.1) he who has seen a Creator Son has seen Father (1:5.5-6) (6:2.2) (20:5.1) (20:6.5) (32:3.6) (107:0.4) (157:6.13) (180:3.9) (182:1.9) the experiential discovery-career of the realization of God begins with the recognition of the divinity of the Creator Son of the local universe (0:8.9) they appear in personality registers (30:1.31) (30:2.39) Who are they names origin capabilities assitants creature bestowals (7:4.5) (23:4) | Michael of Nebadon:bestowals; Paradise Sons of God:bestowals Spirit of Truth function of Creator Sons relationship with his creatures destiny limitations local universe Mother Spirits local universe sovereignty Universal Father
improvedDescription: Paradise Sons of God who are created by the Universal Father and Eternal Son to organize and rule local universes, each unique in nature and undergoing seven creature bestowals to earn supreme sovereignty over their domains.

name: creatorship
originalDescription: Creatorship is hardly an attribute of God; it is rather the aggregate of his acting nature. (3:0.3)
improvedDescription: The aggregate of God's acting nature rather than a single divine attribute, representing the universal function of creation that is conditioned by all divine attributes and culminates in the Fatherhood of God.

name: creature-trinitized sons
originalDescription: creature-trinitized sons are the progeny of finaliters and Paradise-Havoners (17:1.7) (22:7.4-14) (22:8.1) (23:4.3) (30:2.63) the resultant sons of trinitizing union between a finaliter and a Paradise-Havona personality become wards of Architects of Master Universe; then this creature goes to Vicegerington in company of Solitary Messengers and never leave (22:7.10) (22:8.6) (23:4.3) (31:9.13) Solitary Messengers accompany offspring of finaliters and Paradise Citizens (23:4.3) all creature-trinitized sons are endowed with personality by the Father (22:8.1) they are apprentices of Trinity Teacher Sons (20:8.2) (22:1.12) (22:8.2-4) (22:9.1) (55:4.17) they come to the worlds established in light and life (55:4.17) (55:12.2) the command they receive is: “Be you understanding of your ascendant brethren, even as the Paradise Creator Sons know and love them” (26:11.4) trinitized personalities represent the conceptual potential mobilized in that trinitization (13:1.12) the supreme destiny of all creature-trinitized sons appears to be entrance into the Corps of Trinitized Finaliters, one of the seven Paradise Corps of the Finality. (13:1.12) (22:8.6) (26:11.2) creature-trinitized sons are born and live in the present universe age; they have personalities, together with mind and spirit endowments. They have experiences and the memory thereof, but they do not grow as do ascenders (117:2.3) they embody ideals and experience pertaining to future age (22:7.10) (117:2.3) they appear in personality register (30:1.28) (30:2.62-63) When new trinitized sons are young and untrained, they are usually dispatched for long periods of service on the seven Paradise spheres of the Infinite Spirit, where they serve under the tutelage of the Seven Supreme Executives. (17:1.7-8) (22:8.2) there are 9642 registered in Nebadon (35:0.1) supernaphim facilitate confraternity between the trinitized sons of the mortal finaliters and the trinitized sons of the Paradise Citizens. (26:11.3) The primary and the secondary supernaphim are the general custodians of the conjoint corps of these sons, including the trinitized offspring of the mortal finaliters and similar progeny of the Paradise Citizens. (26:11.2) there are many speculations concerning destiny of Trinity-origin beings but answers are not known (23:4.4) they represent a great value to superuniverse governments (22:10.2-7) (26:2.2) headquarters re-embraced creature-trinitized sons
improvedDescription: Creature-trinitized sons are unique beings created by the trinitizing union of finaliters and Paradise-Havona personalities, endowed with personality by the Universal Father. These beings represent concepts and experiences pertaining to future universe ages and become wards of the Architects of the Master Universe, with their supreme destiny being entrance into the Corps of Trinitized Finaliters.

name: creatures
originalDescription: At Edentia there are 70 major divisions of celestial creatures (43:1.9) Before a Creator Son may engage in the creation of any new type of being, any new design of creature, he must secure the consent of the Eternal and Original Mother Son. (21:2.5) Father delegates everything possible on his creatures (10:1.2) | delegation The planetary creatures of God’s spirit indwelling, scattered hither and yon throughout the universes of space, are so nearly infinite in number and order (3:2.8) (30:0.1) no entity has personality unless he is endowed with it by God who is spirit. Any mind entity that is not associated with either spiritual or physical energy is not a personality. (30:1.113) there are creatures of many other-than-personal types on Paradise spheres of Son (13:0.6) (13:3.2-3) personality registers contains a list of all reveled creature types (30:1-2) Intelligent beings are vertically related in twelve great groups of seven major divisions each. (49:5.30) successive universe ages will redistribute personality groupings and in fact it happens now (13:2.5) creature similarities differences between material and spiritual creatures
improvedDescription: Creatures are the diverse forms of intelligent life throughout the universes, ranging from material beings to spiritual entities, all created and sustained by God. They include mortals, celestials, and numerous other orders of beings who inhabit the material and spiritual realms of creation.

name: creeds
improvedDescription: Formal statements of religious beliefs or doctrines that systematize and codify spiritual teachings into organized sets of principles.

name: cremation
originalDescription: Danubians practiced mother worship and the religious rite of cremating the dead, for it was the custom of the mother cultists to burn their dead in stone huts. (80:8.5) The Nodites encouraged cremation as a means of combating cannibalism since it was once a common practice to dig up buried bodies and eat them. (89:5.14) for several thousands of years cremation of the dead was almost universal throughout Scandinavia. (80:9.4) Cremation was a later-day invention to prevent ghost trouble. (87:6.8)
improvedDescription: Cremation is the practice of burning the dead, which became widespread among certain ancient peoples.
