originalDescription: Endor is an ancient town in Galilee near Mount Tabor Joseph father of Jesus worked at Endor (123:3.8) King Saul used the services of a witch from Endor to allegedly communicate with the spirit of the late prophet Samuel (146:7.1)
improvedDescription: An ancient town in Galilee near Mount Tabor where Joseph, father of Jesus, worked and where Jesus escaped from crowds during his public ministry. It was also the location where King Saul consulted with a witch to allegedly communicate with the spirit of the prophet Samuel.

name: endowments
originalDescription: Spirit of Truth endows us with the power of the certain and joyous performance of the gracious, acceptable, and perfect will of God (143:2.4) Time is the one universal endowment of all will creatures; it is the “one talent” intrusted to all intelligent beings. (28:6.9)
improvedDescription: Gifts or abilities bestowed upon beings. The Spirit of Truth endows believers with the power to perform God's will joyously, while time is described as the one universal endowment given to all will creatures as their "one talent."

name: ends versus means
originalDescription: Everything nonspiritual in human experience, excepting personality, is a means to an end. (112:2.8) (112:5.22) Intelligence alone can discriminate as to the best means of attaining indiscriminate ends, but a moral being possesses an insight which enables him to discriminate between ends as well as between means. (16:7.4-10) (160:4.11) Jesus never did he resort to ignoble tactics in meeting the continuous pressure of his enemies, who did not hesitate to employ every sort of false, unfair, and unrighteous mode of attack upon him. (149:4.5) (173:2.7) Jesus would not serve evil that good might presumably derive therefrom (136:9.3) Everything nonspiritual in human experience, excepting personality, is a means to an end. Every true relationship of mortal man with other persons—human or divine—is an end in itself. (112:2.8) the work of this world, paramount though it is, is not nearly so important as the way in which you do this work (39:4.13)
improvedDescription: A moral and philosophical concept distinguishing between goals and the methods used to achieve them. Intelligence can discriminate between means, but moral beings can also discriminate between ends themselves, with everything nonspiritual serving as means to an end except personality and true relationships.

name: enemies
originalDescription: everyone admits, at least in theory, even those who do not have high ideals, that loving even enemies is a moral teaching, that it is right (103:5.2-3) The second Isaiah preached: “And the Lord will guide you continually and satisfy your soul. You shall be like a watered garden and like a spring whose waters fail not. And if the enemy shall come in like a flood, the spirit of the Lord will lift up a defense against him. ” (97:7.12) Jesus repetead many times: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you, bless those who curse you, and pray for those who despitefully use you. (140:3.15) (140:8.6) (188:5.7) Prayer may be an angry cry for vengeance or a merciful intercession for one’s enemies. (91:8.7) Said Jesus: “When an enemy smites you on one cheek, do not stand there dumb and passive but in positive attitude turn the other; that is, do the best thing possible actively to lead your brother in error away from the evil paths into the better ways of righteous living.”  (159:5.9) The wise man is a noble soul who is friendly in the midst of his enemies, tranquil among the turbulent, and generous among the grasping. (131:3.6)
improvedDescription: Those who oppose or are hostile toward others. Jesus taught his followers to love their enemies, do good to those who hate them, and pray for those who persecute them, representing one of the highest moral teachings of human brotherhood.

name: enemy
originalDescription: This is an appellation once used by Jesus for Caligastia; he warned the apostles that he would try to separate them from each other (138:7.3)
improvedDescription: An appellation used by Jesus for Caligastia, the fallen Planetary Prince, when warning the apostles that this enemy of the kingdom would try to separate them from each other.

name: energy
originalDescription: definition types of energy circuits of energy energy charge | absoluta manipulation of energy by celestial agencies Paradise source of energy physical energy unknown forms of energy
improvedDescription: The fundamental basis of all existence in the material universe. Energy denotes all phases and forms of phenomenal motion, action, and potential, with all force-energy originating from Paradise and being under the ultimate control of the Universal Father.

name: energy controllers
improvedDescription: Beings responsible for controlling and directing energy flows throughout the universe systems.

name: energy manipulators
originalDescription: energy manipulators are the fifth group of celestial artisans. They are divided in following types: (44:5.1-10) # physical-energy manipulators # mind-energy manipulators # spiritual-energy manipulators # compound manipulators # transport advisers # experts of communication # teachers of rest
improvedDescription: The fifth group of celestial artisans who specialize in manipulating physical, mindal, and spiritual energies. They are divided into seven types: physical-energy manipulators, mind-energy manipulators, spiritual-energy manipulators, compound manipulators, transport advisers, experts of communication, and teachers of rest.

name: energy-matter
improvedDescription: A concept representing the fundamental relationship between energy and matter as aspects of physical reality.

name: energy shields
improvedDescription: Protective barriers or devices that provide shielding against various forms of energy.

name: energy transformers
originalDescription: The number of these beings in a superuniverse is unbelievable. There are almost 1,000,000 in Satania alone, and the usual quota is 100 for each inhabited world. (29:4.22) events they enabled functions nature
improvedDescription: Powerful living beings among the Master Physical Controllers who can change the form and potential of twenty-seven of the thirty physical energies of the superuniverse. There are almost one million in Satania alone, with the usual quota being one hundred for each inhabited world.

name: energy transmitters
originalDescription: functions nature
improvedDescription: Automatic and intelligent beings among the Master Physical Controllers who can direct energy flows into new circuits and provide emergency communication lines. They function as living superconductors and can receive and transmit intelligence, though they cannot initiate communication.

name: Engedi
originalDescription: Old city near to Dead Sea. Jesus and Abner visited Nazarite colony at Engedi (142:8.1) For two and a half years John lived at Engedi, and he persuaded most of the brotherhood that “the end of the age was at hand”; that “the kingdom of heaven was about to appear. (135:4.3) Engedi waas the southern headquarters of Nazarites and other ascetic herdsmen (135:1.1) (135:2.3) (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: An ancient city near the Dead Sea that served as the southern headquarters of the Nazarite brotherhood and other ascetic communities. John the Baptist lived here for two and a half years, and Jesus and Abner later visited the Nazarite colony at this location.

name: England
originalDescription: England, Scotland, and Wales were always fighting each other until they gave up their respective sovereignties, reposing them in the United Kingdom. (134:6.3)
improvedDescription: A nation that, along with Scotland and Wales, was always fighting until they gave up their respective sovereignties and united under the United Kingdom.

name: English Channel land bridge
originalDescription: During most of the glacial period, England was connected by land to France, forming a land bridge across the English Channel. (64:1.5-6) (64:2.6)
improvedDescription: A land connection that existed between England and France during most of the glacial period, allowing early human migrations across what is now the English Channel.

name: English language
originalDescription: revelators of Urantia revelations were instructed to use the English language (0:0.1-3) (31:10.22) (56:10.23)
improvedDescription: The language chosen by celestial revelators for translating the Urantia Papers, despite its limitations in conveying enlarged cosmic concepts and advanced spiritual truths.

name: enmity
improvedDescription: A concept representing deep-seated hostility or hatred between individuals or groups.

name: Enoch
originalDescription: Enoch was a son of Cain and Remona, and became the chief of the Elamite Nodites (76:2.9) he was the first mortal to merge with his Adjuster in flesh; he currently is one of the 24 Jerusem counselors, number 11 (45:4.13) book of enoch
improvedDescription: Enoch was a son of Cain and Remona who became chief of the Elamite Nodites. He was the first mortal to fuse with his Thought Adjuster during mortal life in the flesh and currently serves as one of the 24 counselors on Jerusem. The Book of Enoch influenced Jesus' adoption of the title "Son of Man" for his mission.

name: Enos
originalDescription: Enos, a son of Seth and grandson of Adam and Eve, founded a new order of worship (76:3.4)
improvedDescription: Enos was a son of Seth and grandson of Adam and Eve who founded a new order of worship in the second garden.

name: Ensa
originalDescription: Ensa is our minor sector, the third in major sector of Splandon, that is comprised of one hundred minor sectors (15:14.7) (18:5.5) (29:4.18) Uminor the third, the headquarters of Ensa, is surrounded by seven spheres satellites (15:7.8) The rotational center of Ensa is situated far away in the enormous and dense star cloud of Sagittarius, around which your local universe and its associated creations all move, and from opposite sides of the vast Sagittarius subgalactic system you may observe two great streams of star clouds emerging in stupendous stellar coils. (15:3.5-11)
improvedDescription: Ensa is the minor sector containing our local universe, consisting of one hundred local universes with headquarters at Uminor the third. It is the third minor sector in the major sector of Splandon, and its rotational center is located in the dense star cloud of Sagittarius.

name: enseconaphimation
originalDescription: enseconaphimation is the process by which an ascending pilgrim is encased in a seconaphim for the long flight to Havona (112:7.5)
improvedDescription: Enseconaphimation is the process by which an ascending pilgrim is encased in a seconaphim for the long flight from the superuniverse headquarters to Havona.

name: enseraphimation
improvedDescription: The process of being transported by seraphim, one of the methods of celestial transport used in the universe.

name: Enta
originalDescription: Mary the mother of Jesus was a descendant of Enta (122:1.2)
improvedDescription: Enta was one of the notable women ancestors in Mary's lineage, the mother of Jesus, representing part of her illustrious hereditary background.

name: enthusiasm
originalDescription: Jesus was consistently cheerful, notwithstanding he sometimes drank deeply of the cup of human sorrow. He fearlessly faced the realities of existence, yet was he filled with enthusiasm for the gospel of the kingdom. (100:7.4,12) Jesus pointed out that overmuch sympathy and pity may degenerate into serious emotional instability; that enthusiasm may drive on into fanaticism. (149:4.3) And in welcoming Simon as his second apostle, Jesus said: “Simon, your enthusiasm is commendable, but it is dangerous to the work of the kingdom. I admonish you to become more thoughtful in your speech. I would change your name to Peter”. (137:1.3) apostles must be possessed with zeal and intelligent enthusiasm (163:4.13) (163:6.1) Ganid, the Hindu student of Jesus, once quoted a Hebrew proverb, and that word of wisdom was: “Whatsoever your hand finds to do, do that with all your might” (133:8.3) Loveless zeal is always harmful to religion, while persecution diverts the activities of religion into the achievement of some sociologic or theologic drive. (99:3.8) the religious enthusiasm with which social reforms are sometimes propagated has the great weakness that it is unable to profit from open religious criticism and thereby attain to profitable levels of self-correction (99:3.6-7)
improvedDescription: A divine quality characterized by zealous commitment to spiritual purposes. Jesus exemplified controlled enthusiasm for the gospel of the kingdom, never allowing it to become fanaticism. True enthusiasm must be balanced with wisdom and thoughtfulness to be effective in spiritual service.

name: environment
originalDescription: Neither can environmental limitations, even on an isolated world, thwart the personal attainment of the individual mortal; Jesus of Nazareth, as a man among men, personally achieved the status of light and life over nineteen hundred years ago on Urantia. (55:11.7) Such divinely watered souls are all but independent of material environment as regards the joys of living and the satisfactions of earthly existence. (34:6.8) Life Carriers manipulated environment to eliminate inferior prehuman strains (65:2.14-15) There can be no handicap of human heredity or deprivation of mortal environment which the morontia career will not fully compensate and wholly remove. (44:8.3) Religious experience is markedly influenced by physical health, inherited temperament, and social environment. (100:1.6) seraphim, Master Physical Controllers, and midwayers manipulate terrestial environment (113:3.5) (113:4.4) (114:6.18) The mortals may differ greatly in innate abilities and intellectual endowment, they may enjoy environments exceptionally favorable to social advancement and moral progress, or they may suffer from the lack of almost every human aid to culture and advancement in the arts of civilization (5:1.4) But spiritual progress is opened to all regardless of environment (5:1.4-7)
improvedDescription: The material and social conditions surrounding mortal life. While environmental limitations cannot prevent individual spiritual attainment, as demonstrated by Jesus on Urantia, they significantly influence physical health, social development, and religious experience. Spiritual progress remains equally available to all regardless of environmental handicaps.

name: envy
originalDescription: when the acknowledgment of our difficulties entails the reduction of our long-cherished conceit, the admission of envy, or the abandonment of deep-seated prejudices, the average person prefers to cling to the old illusions of safety and to the long-cherished false feelings of security. (160:1.7) Envy is a deep-seated human trait; therefore did primitive man ascribe it to his early gods. (87:5.6) Only a moral conscience can condemn the evils of national envy and racial jealousy. (52:6.5) Jesus comforted a young Phoenician in Ephesus who was envious of a young man who had received promotion over his head (133:6.2) Jesus insisted in keep free of covetousness: “Take heed and keep yourselves free from covetousness; a man’s life consists not in the abundance of the things which he may possess”. (140:8.17) (165:4.1,4) many Havona pilgrims look back upon the long, long struggle with a joyous envy almost wish they could begin ascension all again (26:10.5) man is able to transcend the material irritations of the lower levels of thinking—worry, jealousy, envy, revenge, and the pride of immature personality. (160:3.5) All physical poisons greatly retard the efforts of the Adjuster to exalt the material mind, while the mental poisons of fear, anger, envy, jealousy, suspicion, and intolerance likewise tremendously interfere with the spiritual progress of the evolving soul. (110:1.5) Pharisees’ inner souls filled with self-righteousness, covetousness, extortion, and all manner of spiritual wickedness. (166:1.4) Fear, envy, and conceit can be prevented only by intimate contact with other minds. (160:2.7) ‘There is he who waxes rich by his wariness and much pinching, and this is the portion of his reward: Whereas he says, I have found rest and now shall be able to eat continually of my goods, yet he knows not what time shall bring upon him, and also that he must leave all these things to others when he dies’ (165:4.8)
improvedDescription: A deep-seated human trait involving resentment over another's advantages or success. Envy represents one of the mental poisons that interfere with spiritual progress and Adjuster communication. It can only be prevented through intimate contact with other minds and must be transcended for higher spiritual living.

name: Eocene
originalDescription: Eocene was the early mammalian era from 50 to 35 million years ago (61:1.14)
improvedDescription: The Eocene was the early mammalian era from 50 to 35 million years ago, during which mammalian and other related forms of life evolved with little interruption while North America was connected by land to every continent except Australia.

name: Ephesus
originalDescription: Ephesus was the capital of Roman province of Asia (133:6.1) At Ephesus Jesus pronounced a discourse on the soul for a local thinker (133:6) Paul resided two years in Ephesus making tents for a living and conducting lectures on religion and philosophy each night in the main audience chamber of the school of Tyrannus. (133:6.3) Apostle John settled in Ephesus and there he became an aged bishop (139:4.6,15)
improvedDescription: Ephesus was the capital of the Roman province of Asia, famous for its temple of Artemis. Jesus visited there with Gonod and Ganid, while Paul later resided there for over two years making tents and conducting religious lectures at the school of Tyrannus. The apostle John eventually settled in Ephesus as an aged bishop, where he directed the writing of his Gospel and died at age 101 in A.D. 103.

name: Ephraim
originalDescription: Ahab was king of Ephraim (97:9.18-19) Canaanite city of Gibeon had peace treaty with Ephraim (97:9.13) The Israelitish consciousness took origin in the hill country of Ephraim; the later Jewish consciousness originated in the southern clan of Judah. But the northern kingdom did not vanish from history until the king of Israel conspired with the king of Egypt and refused to pay further tribute to Assyria. Then began the three years’ siege followed by the total dispersion of the northern kingdom. Ephraim, that is Israel, thus vanished. (97:9.2,21) The Jews (Judahites) always sought to defame and blacken the record of the northern Israelites (Ephraimites). (97:9.2) oppressed elders of Ephraim anointed David king of Israel (97:9.11)
improvedDescription: Ephraim was the hill country region where Israelitish consciousness first originated, later becoming synonymous with the northern kingdom of Israel. The oppressed elders of Ephraim anointed David king of Israel, but the northern kingdom eventually vanished when its king conspired with Egypt and refused tribute to Assyria, leading to a three-year siege and total dispersion.

name: Epicurean teacher in Corinth
originalDescription: Jesus had a talk with an epicurean teacher in Corinth (133:4.5)
improvedDescription: An Epicurean teacher in Corinth with whom Jesus had a conversation about choosing the best and esteeming good, while encouraging him to also discern the greater spiritual things of mortal life through realizing God's presence in the human heart.

name: Epicureanism
originalDescription: This school of thought was dedicated to the pursuit of happiness. It did effectually combat ignorant superstition. (121:4.2) Occidental religion thus languished until the days of the Skeptics, Cynics, Epicureans, and Stoics, but most important of all, until the times of the great contest between Mithraism and Paul’s new religion of Christianity. (98:6.2)
improvedDescription: A school of thought dedicated to the pursuit of happiness that helped deliver Romans from deadly fatalism by teaching that people could improve their earthly status. It effectively combated ignorant superstition, with the better Epicureans not given to sensual excesses.

name: epileptics
originalDescription: epilepsy was confused in old times with demon possession (145:2.13) Jesus healed epileptics in Capernaum (145:2.12-14) Jesus healed James of Safed’s son, who was both epileptic and possessed by a real demon (158:4.2) (158:5) epileptics often were priests and medicine men (88:1.9) (90:1.2) Lunatics, epileptics, and the feeble-minded were often worshiped by their normal-minded fellows, who believed that such abnormal beings were indwelt by the gods. (85:6.2)
improvedDescription: Individuals suffering from epilepsy, which in ancient times was often confused with demon possession. Jesus healed several epileptics, and such afflicted persons were frequently revered as priests or medicine men, with many believing they were indwelt by gods or spirits.

name: Epistle of First John
originalDescription: The Epistle known as “First John” was written by John himself as a covering letter for the work which Nathan executed under his direction for Gospel of John (121:8.10)
improvedDescription: The epistle known as First John was written by the apostle John himself as a covering letter for the Gospel work that his associate Nathan executed under John's direction.

name: epochal angels
improvedDescription: A type of seraphic angels associated with epochal events or revelations.

name: epochal revelations
originalDescription: Only two influences can modify and uplift the dogmas of natural religion: the pressure of the slowly advancing mores and the periodic illumination of epochal revelation. (92:3.5) There have been many events of religious revelation but only five of epochal significance. These were as follows: (92:4.4) # The first was Dalamatia teachings, performed by the 100 corporeal members of Prince Caligastia’s staff (92:4.5) # The second was Edenic teachings, by Adam and Eve and their descendants (92:4.6) # The third was the one from Melchizedek of Salem (92:4.7) # The fourth was the one from Jesus of Nazareth (92:4.8) # The fifth is Urantia Papers (92:4.9) Will it be the sixth in 1000 years? Not known, although some hints in the Urantia revelations seem to suggest it (30:0.2)
improvedDescription: Five major revelations of epochal significance to humanity: the Dalamatian teachings by Prince Caligastia's staff, the Edenic teachings of Adam and Eve, the revelation of Melchizedek of Salem, the teachings of Jesus of Nazareth, and The Urantia Papers. These revelations are evolutionary but progressive, each building upon previous spiritual understanding.

name: epochs
improvedDescription: Significant periods or ages in planetary development and spiritual evolution.

name: equality
originalDescription: the belief that all men are born equal is an erroneous belief, the same as it was an error in European Middle Ages that every man belonged to someone else. Human beings may differ greatly in innate abilities and intellectual endowment (5:1.4) (69:3.8) (70:8.1) (70:9.15) but in spirit all men are equal (92:7.4) The weak and the inferior have always contended for equal rights; they have always insisted that the state compel the strong and superior to supply their wants and otherwise make good those deficiencies which all too often are the natural result of their own indifference and indolence. (70:9.16) The concept of equality never brings peace except in the mutual recognition of some overcontrolling influence of supersovereignty. (134:4.9-10) Self-assertion was the battle cry of the Lucifer rebellion. One of his chief arguments was that, if self-government was good and right for the Melchizedeks and other groups, it was equally good for all orders of intelligence. He was bold and persistent in the advocacy of the “equality of mind” and “the brotherhood of intelligence.” (53:4.2) Jesus and equality of men and women
improvedDescription: The concept that while human beings differ greatly in physical abilities and intellectual endowment, all mortals are equal in spiritual potential and cosmic worth. True equality exists only in the recognition of spiritual brotherhood under divine sovereignty, while claims of inherent material equality are erroneous and can lead to social discord.

name: equalizations
improvedDescription: Cosmic adjustments and compensatory mechanisms that balance inequities and restore harmony in the universe system.

name: equation
originalDescription: We cannot pray to a chemical formula, supplicate a mathematical equation, worship a hypothesis, confide in a postulate, commune with a process, serve an abstraction, or hold loving fellowship with a law. Religious experience implies god, and such a God of personal experience must be a person. (102:7.3)
improvedDescription: A mathematical expression representing relationships between quantities. Religious experience requires a personal God rather than abstract formulas, as one cannot establish meaningful spiritual relationships with mathematical equations or other impersonal concepts.

name: equivocation
originalDescription: One of the purposes of the morontia career is to effect the permanent eradication from the mortal survivors of such animal vestigial traits as procrastination, equivocation, insincerity, problem avoidance, unfairness, and ease seeking. (48:5.8)
improvedDescription: The practice of being deliberately ambiguous or evasive in speech or action. This animal vestigial trait, along with procrastination and insincerity, is permanently eradicated during the morontia career as mortal survivors progress toward spiritual perfection.

name: Erech
originalDescription: Erech was a Mesopotamia city state. About 2500 B.C. the Sumerians suffered severe reverses at the hands of the northern Suites and Guites. Lagash, the Sumerian capital built on flood mounds, fell. Erech held out for thirty years after the fall of Akkad. (78:8.9-10) Noah wasn't a myth. Really lived; he was a wine maker of Aram, a river settlement near Erech. (78:7.5)
improvedDescription: An ancient Mesopotamian city-state that held out for thirty years after the fall of Akkad around 2500 B.C. Erech was located near Aram, a river settlement where Noah, a wine maker, lived and kept records of annual floods.

name: error
originalDescription: The ability to entertain error or experience evil will not be fully lost until the ascending human soul achieves final spirit levels. (132:2.6) Adam and Eve’s transgression had been an error of judgment and not the sin of conscious and deliberate rebellion. (76:5.1) Disappointment and sorrow attend upon error because, not being a reality, it cannot be realized in experience. (2:7.6) Evolutionary man finds it difficult fully to comprehend the significance and to grasp the meanings of evil, error, sin, and iniquity. Man is slow to perceive that contrastive perfection and imperfection produce potential evil; that conflicting truth and falsehood create confusing error (54:0.1) The mistakes of mortal mind and the errors of human conduct may markedly delay the evolution of the soul, although they cannot inhibit such a morontia phenomenon when once it has been initiated by the indwelling Adjuster with the consent of the creature will. (111:3.1) memories of past life without spiritual meaning will perish with material brain (112:5.22) (156:5.8) Error suggests lack of intellectual keenness; evil, deficiency of wisdom; sin, abject spiritual poverty; but iniquity is indicative of vanishing personality control. (67:1.4-5) dealing with error defined as biggest error
improvedDescription: Intellectual mistakes or misconceptions that result from imperfection and incomplete understanding. Error is distinguished from sin in that it represents lack of knowledge rather than willful rebellion, though persistent error can evolve into sin when consciously embraced despite better knowledge.

name: Esdraelon
originalDescription: The Plain of Esdraelon is a large fertile valley in northern Israel. It is also known as the Jezreel Valley (123:5.13) David with six hundred men entered into a Philistine alliance and marched up the coast to Esdraelon. But he left the plain at the request of the Philistines, allowing them to defeat Saul. (97:9.5)
improvedDescription: A large, fertile valley in northern Israel, also known as the Jezreel Valley. This plain was the site of many historical battles and was visible from the hills near Nazareth where Jesus lived as a boy, serving as a place where he would contemplate the conflicts of empires.

name: Eskimos
originalDescription: origin mores Beliefs
improvedDescription: A people who are descendants of the ancient Andonite and Foxhall peoples, representing the closest surviving physical type to the original human inhabitants of Urantia. They preserve much of the primitive Andonic culture and live primarily in the frigid northern regions, maintaining docile and cooperative social customs.

name: Essenes
originalDescription: Essenes were a mystic Jewish sect during the Second Temple period, originating during the Maccabean revolt they held possessions in common, something also made by Jesus' apostles (165:4.5) their requirements were in some respects more exacting than those of the Pharisees. They had adopted many Persian beliefs and practices, lived as a brotherhood in monasteries, refrained from marriage, and had all things in common. They specialized in teachings about angels. (137:7.8) many of the Essenes tolerated the theory of reincarnation, that men may reap in one incarnation what they have sown in a previous existence (164:3.4)
improvedDescription: A mystical Jewish religious sect that originated during the Maccabean revolt, characterized by communal living, celibacy, and ascetic practices. They adopted Persian beliefs, specialized in teachings about angels, held all possessions in common, and some tolerated theories of reincarnation.

name: Esta
originalDescription: She married James, the brother of Jesus (128:5.7) (128:7.10-11) she bolstered Mary's spirits when she slackened when she contemplated that Jesus did not intend to fulfill her messianic expectations (137:7.1)
improvedDescription: A young woman of Nazareth who married James, the brother of Jesus. She helped support Mary's spirits during difficult times when Mary struggled with uncertainty about Jesus' messianic mission and his apparent reluctance to fulfill her expectations.

name: Esther
originalDescription: In honor of this woman and the liberation she brought, the Jewish festival of Purim was celebrated. (123:3.5)
improvedDescription: A Jewish woman from history whose courage and liberation of her people is commemorated in the Jewish festival of Purim. She is referenced alongside other notable women like Miriam and Deborah as examples of women's important work in the religious life of Israel.

name: eternal life
originalDescription: The presence of the Adjuster in the mind of man constitutes the revelation promise and the faith pledge of an eternal existence of divine progression (132:3.9) the attainment of immortality is achieved with the fusion with our Adjuster. After the fusion of the immortal morontia soul and the associated Adjuster, all of the experience and all of the values of the one eventually become the possession of the other, so that we two are actually one entity. (110:7) The Creator Sons in the Deity association of God the Sevenfold provide the mechanism whereby the mortal becomes immortal and the finite attains the embrace of the infinite. (0:8.11) (180:3.7) That the way which leads to eternal life is straight and narrow, that the door which leads thereto is likewise narrow so that, of those who seek salvation, few can find entrance through this door. But even if the door to the way of life is narrow, it is wide enough to admit all who sincerely seek to enter, for Jesus is that door. (166:3.3) The life after death is no different in the essentials than the mortal existence. (103:5.7) the morontia soul, being superadjutant, does not retain self-consciousness without the Adjuster when deprived of the material-mind mechanism (112:6.7) no misbehavior by others can ever jeopardize one’s eternal prospects (54:6.4) No personal creature can be coerced into the eternal adventure; the portal of eternity opens only in response to the freewill choice of the freewill sons of the God of free will. (5:6.8,12) what would a man give in exchange for e.l.? (158:7.5) defined as believers according to different religions
improvedDescription: The continuation of existence beyond mortal death, achieved through fusion with the indwelling Adjuster. This immortal state represents divine progression where the mortal soul, created jointly by the material mind and Adjuster, ascends to Paradise and becomes part of the eternal adventure. Eternal life is dependent on the free will choice of the individual and cannot be coerced by others.

name: Eternal Son
originalDescription: personality of Eternal Son is master pattern for all personality (0:6.13) (6:7) (6:8.5) (115:3.14) On the seven sacred worlds of the Eternal Son there appear to take origin the impersonal energies of spirit luminosity. (13:0.4) (13:3) active spirit gravity of Eternal Son calculated is the same in the grand universe than in existing totality (12:3.9) Adjusters use spirit-gravity circuits of Eternal Son to convey worship to Father (5:3.2) attained only through Infinite Spirit (8:3.7-8) (8:6.2) he appears in Uversa personality register as the second person in the Paradise Deities catergory (30:2.12) Father speaks to universe only through Eternal Son (10:3.3) (16:2.2) his presence immediately enshrouds Father on Paradise (6:0.1) (11:1.1) Reflective Image Aids represent the superpersonal agents of Eternal Son (15:10.21) some unpredictable phenomena seems to indicate a relation between Eternal Son to Deity Absolute (7:1.9-11) (7:2.1) Havona affords the Eternal Son an almost unlimited base for the ever-expanding realization of spirit power. (14:6.12-16) spirit manifestations of the Son in the superuniverses are superpersonalities (7:2.3) (15:10.21) the Eternal Son in member of the First, Second and Third Triunities and of Triodities (104:4.3,9,16) (104:5.2) also known as Eternal Son and Jesus bestowals creation of Eternal Son functions of the Eternal Son nature recognition of the Eternal Son
improvedDescription: The Second Source and Center, the perfect and final expression of the Universal Father's first personal and absolute concept. The Eternal Son is the pattern personality for all personalities throughout the universes and serves as the absolute upholder of all spirit realities and spiritual beings. He is the divine Word made manifest and the only avenue of approach to the Universal Father.

name: Eternals of Days
originalDescription: they are Supreme Trinity Personalities (18:0.3) (18:2.1) They are close of kin to, and are the divine equals of, the Ancients of Days, who preside over the destinies of the seven supergovernments. (18:2.2) There are one billion and were created by Trinity. Each of them directs one of Havona spheres (14:3.3) (14:5.6,9) (18:2.1) each embellishes his sphere according to own original plans (14:5.6) (18:2.3-4) Eternals of Days are not creators, but they are perfect administrators. (14:3.3) Forever have Eternal of Days and Secrets of Supremacy ruled their exquisite worlds of the Paradise-Havona system, and they function without rotation or reassignment. (18:2.1) they appear in personality registers as the Stationary Sons of the Trinity (30:1.9) (30:2.24) most superuniverse personnel apprenticed under the Eternals of Days on the various worlds of the perfect Havona universe. (18:4.6) When an Eternal of Days is absent from his sphere, his world is directed by a Trinity Teacher Son. (18:2.2) The Eternals of Days are visible to all will creatures dwelling in their domains. (18:2.2) prior to the times of Grandfanda, high Paradise Citizens were the first executive assistants to the Eternals of Days (26:11.2)
improvedDescription: Supreme Trinity Personalities who rule the billion worlds of Havona, with exactly one Eternal of Days directing each Havona sphere. These perfect administrators are offspring of the Paradise Trinity who have ruled their exquisite worlds forever without rotation or reassignment. They are divine equals of the Ancients of Days and serve as visible rulers to all will creatures in their domains.

name: eternity
originalDescription: The attainment of the Universal Father is the passport to eternity, notwithstanding the remaining circuits to be traversed. (26:9.2) in hypothetical beginning there was no differentiation throughout all infinity (105:1.5) in eternity all is, even if unrevealed in time and space (115:3.11) there are records kept since personification of Infinite Spirit (25:6.6) time is the moving image of eternity and space is the fleeting shadow of Paradise realities (189:1.3) conception of eternity
improvedDescription: The timeless state of existence where all reality exists simultaneously, in contrast to the time-bound experience of finite creatures. From the perspective of eternity, all is present at once, while from the temporal viewpoint, reality appears as sequential events. Eternity represents the everlasting now and serves as the backdrop against which the temporal cycles of time unfold.

name: ether
originalDescription: The so-called ether is merely a collective name to designate a group of force and energy activities occurring in space. (42:5.14-16) Primordial-force behavior does give rise to phenomena which are in many ways analogous to your postulated ether. Space is not empty; the spheres of all space whirl and plunge on through a vast ocean of outspread force-energy; neither is the space content of an atom empty. Nevertheless there is no ether, and the very absence of this hypothetical ether enables the inhabited planet to escape falling into the sun and the encircling electron to resist falling into the nucleus. (42:5.16) Light is a real substance, not simply waves of hypothetical ether. (15:6.13) not all the suppositions of natural philosophy are valid; for example, the hypothetical ether, which represents an ingenious attempt of man to unify his ignorance of space phenomena. (42:9.4) the Unqualified Absolute is not an undifferentiated and all-pervading influence comparable either to the pantheistic concepts of metaphysics or to the sometime ether hypothesis of science (0:11.9)
improvedDescription: A hypothetical substance once postulated by science to explain space phenomena, but which does not actually exist. The term merely represents a collective name for various force and energy activities occurring in space. The absence of this hypothetical ether enables planets to maintain their orbits and electrons to resist falling into atomic nuclei.

name: Ethical Sensitizers
originalDescription: Ethical Sensitizers are supervisor seraphim with the mission to foster and to promote the growth of creature appreciation of the morality of interpersonal relationships, for such is the seed and secret of the continued and purposeful growth of society and government, human or superhuman. (39:3.7)
improvedDescription: Supervisor seraphim whose mission is to foster creature appreciation of the morality of interpersonal relationships. They serve as the seed and secret of continued societal growth, functioning as volunteer counselors to planetary rulers and exchange teachers on system training worlds, with their full guidance available in the brotherhood schools on Edentia.

name: ethics
originalDescription: All relationships and the application of ethics grow out of the fundamental facts of origin. (28:6.2) Static ethics and traditional morality are just slightly superanimal. Ethics and morals become truly human when they are dynamic and progressive, alive with universe reality. (12:5.10) human ethics and the natural consciousness of relative right and wrong were born from the concepts of the appeasement of ever-displeased spirits (86:6.7) Religion has handicapped social development in many ways, but without religion there would have been no enduring morality nor ethics, no worth-while civilization. (92:3.6-8) (101:0.2) Civilization is in danger when youth neglect to interest themselves in ethics, sociology, eugenics, philosophy, the fine arts, religion, and cosmology. (111:4.4) The evolutionary mind is able to discover law, morals, and ethics (196:3.26) the ethical conscience and the moral consciousness is indispensable to human progress and survival (101:3.1) man’s understanding of ethics is only relatively true (115:1.2) Secrecy, insincerity, and hypocrisy may obscure sex problems, but they do not provide solutions, nor do they advance ethics. (82:1.10) Man’s acquirement of ethical judgment, moral will, is usually coincident with the appearance of early language. (52:1.6) defined as learning Jesus and ethics
improvedDescription: The dynamic and progressive principles governing interpersonal relationships that grow out of the fundamental facts of origin. True ethics become human when they are alive with universe reality, transcending static traditional morality. Ethics develop from natural consciousness of relative right and wrong and serve as the external mirror reflecting internal spiritual and religious development.

name: etiquette
originalDescription: Methods of eating soon became taboo, and so originated ancient and modern table etiquette. (89:1.6) Morontia Companions are instructors of social conduct in tarrying worlds (48:3.11) Technical Advisors teach best and right way to do things (25:4.17)
improvedDescription: Social conduct and proper behavioral methods that originated from ancient taboos, particularly around eating practices. Modern table etiquette traces back to these early prohibitions. Proper social conduct is taught by Morontia Companions on tarrying worlds and by Technical Advisors who instruct in the best and right way to conduct oneself.

name: Etruscan priesthood
originalDescription: In the great monotheistic renaissance of Melchizedek’s gospel during the sixth century before Christ, too few of the Salem missionaries penetrated Italy, and those who did were unable to overcome the influence of the rapidly spreading Etruscan priesthood with its new galaxy of gods and temples, all of which became organized into the Roman state religion. (98:3.2)
improvedDescription: A rapidly spreading religious system in ancient Italy with a new galaxy of gods and temples that became organized into the Roman state religion. During the sixth century before Christ, this priesthood successfully resisted the influence of Salem missionaries who sought to spread Melchizedek's monotheistic gospel throughout Italy.

name: eugenics
originalDescription: Civilization is in danger when youth neglect to interest themselves in ethics, sociology, eugenics, philosophy, the fine arts, religion, and cosmology. (111:4.4) It is the false sentiment of our partially perfected civilizations that fosters, protects, and perpetuates the hopelessly defective strains of evolutionary human stocks. (52:2.11-12) (99:3.5) in the most developed continental nation of a neighboring planet, the feeble-minded are committed in colonies and cannot reproduce, slavery has been abolished a hundred years ago and the least capable slaves has been deported, and breeding is prohibited for criminals and defectives (72:4.2) (72:5.2) (72:10.1,3) there are mortals who are virtually disqualified for survival by disinheritance through the agency of unfit and inferior ancestors (109:3.8) Adamic age usually witnesses the completion of the elimination of the unfit and the still further purification of the racial strains (52:3.4) Present-day prejudice against “half-castes,” “hybrids,” and “mongrels” arises because modern racial crossbreeding is, for the greater part, between the grossly inferior strains of the races concerned. (82:6.3) dangers of an excess of inferior stocks recommended societal actions deplorable means
improvedDescription: The concept of improving human genetic inheritance and racial quality through selective breeding practices. The Urantia Book presents eugenics as part of normal planetary development, noting that civilizations advance through the intelligent management of biological, intellectual, and spiritual resources, including the selective elimination of inferior human strains and the encouragement of superior genetic qualities.

name: eunuchs
improvedDescription: Men who have been castrated, typically for religious, social, or administrative purposes in various historical cultures.

name: Euphrates River
originalDescription: Euphrates and Tigris were cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations (78:1.3) drought drove Andites to valleys of Euphrates (79:1.3) (80:1.1) the second garden was constructed in the middle of Euphrates and Tigris rivers, adjoined to Euphrates (76:0.1) (76:1.1-3) (76:3.8) (76:4.8) A small Nodite group at the mouth of the Tigris and Euphrates rivers maintained more of their racial integrity. The were the ascendants of Sumerians. (77:4.6) (78:8)
improvedDescription: One of the two major rivers of Mesopotamia, along with the Tigris River. The second Garden of Eden was located between these rivers, and the Euphrates valley served as a cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations. Andite migrations followed the river's course, and it was home to various early peoples including Nodites and Sumerians.

name: Europe
originalDescription: geology invasions history
improvedDescription: A continent that experienced extensive geological changes, ice ages, and human migrations throughout its history. Europe became home to various evolutionary races including the blue man, received significant Andite and Adamite influence, and later became a center of civilization that would carry forward religious and cultural developments to the world.

name: evaluation
originalDescription: The observer cannot be the thing observed; evaluation demands some degree of transcendence of the thing which is evaluated. (112:2.12)
improvedDescription: The intellectual process of assessing or judging something, which requires a degree of transcendence from the thing being evaluated. The observer cannot be the thing observed, and true evaluation demands some separation between the evaluator and the subject of evaluation.

name: evangelism
improvedDescription: The practice of spreading religious teachings and gospel messages, particularly focused on proclaiming spiritual truths and converting others to faith.

name: evangelists
originalDescription: they are a group of preachers ordained by Jesus and formed mainly from the original group of evangelists the selection committee for the original evangelists was composed of Peter, James, and Andrew; the method of the school of evangelists was to learn and practice (148:1.1) the period of preparation of the original evangelists took place in Bethsaida (148) only 75 of the 117 evangelists passed the preaching test (149:7.3) (153:5.2) the selection committee of the seventy was composed of Andrew, Abner, and the chief evangelist (163:2.1) Abner was put in charge of this group of seventy, which was made up of Abner, ten former apostles of John, 51 of the 75 evangelists, and 8 other disciples (163:1.1) they did not have everything in common as apostles (148:1.3) Jesus told them to work regardless of what might happen (171:3.2) they had to go completely unprovided of resources (163:4.12) they were 70 just by coincidence, not because they wanted to use a number of great Jewish significance (163:4.17) on November 19, AD 29, Jesus proceeded to ordinate the seventy (163:1) on December 30, AD 29, the seventy returned to Pella (163:6) Jesus assured that there were invisible messengers by the sides of the evangelists (175:1.8)
improvedDescription: A corps of gospel preachers and teachers trained and ordained by Jesus to spread the kingdom message. Originally numbering 117, later reduced to 75 who passed the preaching test, they were organized under a selection committee of Peter, James, and Andrew. A group of 70 evangelists was later chosen and ordained under Abner's leadership, consisting of former apostles of John and other dedicated disciples.

name: evangels of destiny
originalDescription: evangels of destiny proclaim spiritual liberty and divine sonship even to the remote worlds of the universe (35:3.21)
improvedDescription: Celestial beings who serve as messengers and teachers, proclaiming spiritual liberty and divine sonship throughout the universe. They are trained in the Melchizedek College of Spiritual Endowment and carry the gospel message even to remote worlds.

name: Evangels of Light
originalDescription: Evangels of Light are celestial beings temporarily attached to mortal finaliter companies (31:7.1) Any celestial personality assigned to the service of any finaliter corps is denominated an Evangel of Light. (31:7.2)
improvedDescription: Celestial personalities temporarily assigned to serve with mortal finaliter companies. These beings do not take the finaliter oath and are transient members who may include Solitary Messengers, supernaphim, seconaphim, Paradise Citizens, or their trinitized offspring, serving specific missions before returning to their former status.

name: Eve
originalDescription: Eve is the Material Daughter consort of Adam who accepted the mission of bringing the violet race to Urantia the Caligastia plot the fault after the fault
improvedDescription: The Material Daughter and consort of Adam who came to Urantia as part of the Adamic mission to uplift the evolutionary races. Eve was deceived by Caligastia's agents into the premature commingling of life plasm, leading to the Adamic default. Despite this failure, she and Adam worked to benefit humanity and established the second garden on the Euphrates, where Eve headed a commission for race improvement before her death.

name: Evening Stars
improvedDescription: An order of celestial beings mentioned in The Urantia Book, related to the Brilliant Evening Stars.

name: Eventod
originalDescription: Events is the name Miguel adopted when he made his first donation as an ascending mortal spirit on Uversa. (119:5.2)
improvedDescription: The name adopted by Michael of Nebadon during his bestowal as an ascending mortal spirit on Uversa, where he lived and functioned for eleven years, proving worthy of trust and commanding respect from his fellow spirits.

name: eventuated transcendental beings
improvedDescription: Transcendental beings who neither create nor are created but simply eventuate, existing on four ultimate levels of personality activity and functioning on seven absonite levels.

name: eventuation
originalDescription: eventuation is the process by which transcendental beings are brought into existence. These “eventuators” are neither finite nor infinite—they are absonite; and absonity is neither infinity nor absoluteness. (30:1.92-99) These eventuated beings include the following orders: # The Architects of the Master Universe. # Transcendental Recorders. # Other Transcendentalers. # Primary Eventuated Master Force Organizers. # Associate Transcendental Master Force Organizers. These Transcendentalers create no beings, neither were they ever created. In speaking of their origin, in order to avoid using a new term—an arbitrary and meaningless designation—we deem it best to say that Transcendentalers simply eventuate.(31:8.3) On Paradise there is a tradition that far back in eternity there was attempted the eventuation of the 28,012th Master Architect, but that this being failed to absonitize, experiencing personality seizure by the Universal Absolute.(31:9.10)
improvedDescription: The process by which transcendental beings are brought into existence, being neither finite nor infinite but absonite. These eventuated beings include the Architects of the Master Universe, Transcendental Recorders, and other transcendental orders who simply eventuate rather than being created or evolved.

name: eventuators
improvedDescription: Another term for eventuated transcendental beings, referring to those divine children of divinity, ultimacy, and eternity who are neither creators nor creatures but simply eventuate.

name: everywhereness
improvedDescription: A concept related to omnipresence, referring to the quality of being present everywhere simultaneously.

name: Eveson
originalDescription: Evason is the second son of Adam; he became a masterful leader. He predeceased Adam (74:6.2) (76:3.3)
improvedDescription: The second son of Adam and Eve who became a masterful leader and administrator, serving as his father's great helper before predeceasing Adam.

name: evidence
originalDescription: Evidence, the basis of fairness, justice in harmony with mercy, is supplied by the personalities of the Third Source and Center, the conjoint representative of the Father and the Son to all realms and to the minds of the intelligent beings of all creation. (10:6.3) Judgment, the final application of justice in accordance with the evidence submitted by the personalities of the Infinite Spirit, is the work of the Stationary Sons of the Trinity, beings partaking of the Trinity nature of the united Father, Son, and Spirit. (10:6.3-4)
improvedDescription: The basis of fairness and justice in harmony with mercy, supplied by the personalities of the Third Source and Center as the conjoint representative of the Father and Son to all realms and intelligent beings of creation.

name: evil
originalDescription: Adjusters are the watchful workers who pilot the God-conscious human mind away from the shoals of evil while expertly guiding the evolving soul of man toward the divine harbors of perfection on far-distant and eternal shores. (110:1.2) Almost every human being has some one thing which is held on to as a pet evil, and which the entrance into the kingdom of heaven requires as a part of the price of admission. (163:2.7) While religion produces growth of meanings and enhancement of values, evil always results when purely personal evaluations are elevated to the levels of absolutes. (19:1.4) (100:1.1) Cano assured Eve persons with good motives could do no evil (75:4.5) The Gods neither create evil nor permit sin and rebellion. Potential evil is time-existent in a universe embracing differential levels of perfection meanings and values. (54:0.2) Human likes and dislikes do not determine good and evil; moral values do not grow out of wish fulfillment or emotional frustration. (100:3.2) Human nature may tend toward evil, but it is not inherently sinful. (53:8.9) (143:2.5) (148:4.6) (156:5.8) (188:4.5) An isolated and purely selfish pleasure may connote a virtual devaluation of meanings, a meaningless enjoyment bordering on relative evil. (100:3.4) most destructive of personality status is betrayal and disloyalty to confiding friends (67:1.3) partial knowledge is potentially evil (2:7.4) dealing with evil defined as Jesus result of evil
improvedDescription: The immature choosing and unthinking misstep of those resistant to goodness, rejectful of beauty, and disloyal to truth. Evil is the misadaptation of immaturity or the disruptive influence of ignorance, representing potential rather than actual reality until consciously chosen and willfully endorsed as sin.

name: evil eye
originalDescription: Although Christianized for over 1,500 years, these Bretons still retain charms of the New Stone Age for warding off the evil eye. (80:9.14) Human prosperity was supposed to be especially provocative of the envy of evil spirits, and their method of retaliation was to strike back through a human agency and by the technique of the evil eye. The Koran contains a whole chapter devoted to the evil eye (87:5.4-5) And intelligent human beings still believe in good luck, the evil eye, and astrology. (88:6.7) The whole phallic cult grew up as a defense against the evil eye. (87:5.5) Many primitive peoples believed the soul looked out upon the world through human eyes; therefore did they so cravenly fear the malevolence of the evil eye. (111:0.7) primitives thought much sickness caused by evil eye (90:3.7) The monotheists keep their subordinate gods as spirits, demons, fates, Nereids, fairies, brownies, dwarfs, banshees, and the evil eye. (96:1.14)
improvedDescription: A widespread primitive superstition involving the belief that certain individuals could cause harm, sickness, or misfortune through malevolent gazing. This fear led to various protective practices including veiling women and wearing charms, and was thought to be used by evil spirits to retaliate against human prosperity.

name: evil one
improvedDescription: A reference to Satan or a malevolent spiritual being, often associated with Caligastia in Urantia terminology.

name: evil spirits
improvedDescription: Malevolent spiritual entities historically associated with demon possession and supernatural afflictions.

name: evolution
originalDescription: biologic evolution | genetics; life:biological principle of evolution social evolution | ascension plan; civilization
improvedDescription: The purposeful, progressive development of life forms, civilizations, and spiritual beings throughout the universe, guided by divine intelligence rather than occurring by chance. Evolution encompasses biological development, cultural advancement, and spiritual progression across all levels of creation.

name: evolutionary races
improvedDescription: The various human racial groups that developed through natural evolutionary processes on inhabited worlds, as distinguished from specially created or imported beings.

name: exaltation, self
improvedDescription: The tendency toward excessive pride or self-importance, elevating oneself above others in vanity or arrogance.

name: examinations
originalDescription: Selective Assorters examine us as soon as we register adequate spiritual achievement to certify it (48:2.23) From time to time Jerusem citizens present themselves to the Melchizedek examiners, who certify to their attainment of morontia wisdom. Then they go before the examining corps of the Brilliant Evening Stars or their designates, who ascertain the degree of spirit insight. Next they appear in the presence of the four and twenty counselors and their associates, who pass upon their status of experiential attainment of socialization. (45:7.7) A good and noble man may be consummately in love with his wife but utterly unable to pass a satisfactory written examination on the psychology of marital love. Inability to pass written examinations does not prove unfitness to live the reality (103:8.3) The Teacher Sons compose the faculties who administer all examinations and conduct all tests for the qualification and certification of all subordinate phases of universe service, from the duties of outpost sentinels to those of star students. (20:8.3) when we are considered to be competent to pass inward to the next circle, we will be taken before the twelve adjutants of the seventh Circuit Spirit and will be required to pass the tests of the circle determined by the superuniverse of our origin and by the system of our nativity. (26:5.5) When the supremacy guides deem their pupils ripe for advancement, they bring them before the commission of seventy, a mixed group serving as examiners on the pilot world of circuit number six. (26:6.4) the Trinity guides take their pupils to its pilot world and present them before one of the many triune commissions functioning as examiners and certifiers of candidates for the Deity adventure. (26:7.3) After a long sojourn on circuit number two the subjects of disappointment that have not attain Deity are examined by the councils of perfection sitting on the pilot world of this circle and are certified as having passed the Havona test (26:10.3) After the attainment of the Infinite Spirit, no more examinations are conducted. (26:8.3)
improvedDescription: Progressive spiritual and intellectual tests administered throughout the ascending career, from morontia worlds to Paradise, to certify readiness for advancement to higher levels of service and achievement.

name: example
originalDescription: Jesus of Nazareth’ earth life did not constitute an example for us, nor for any subsequent generation on Urantia or on any other world. Rather his life in the flesh was the inspiration for all lives upon all Nebadon worlds (120:2.7) (129:4.7) (136:0.1) (140:10.3) (181:1.3) (194:2.8) (196:1.5) Children are permanently impressed only by the loyalties of their adult associates; precept or even example is not lastingly influential. (100:1.4)
improvedDescription: Jesus' life served as divine inspiration for all beings rather than a literal pattern to copy. True influence comes from loyal living and spiritual demonstration rather than mere precept or imitation.

name: excitement
improvedDescription: A heightened emotional state of enthusiasm or agitation, often associated with intense experiences or anticipation.

name: excommunication
originalDescription: not only Jesus was denied entrance into all synagogues, but that all who believed in his teaching were likewise cast out of the synagogue, excommunicated from the congregation of Israel; and this meant denial of all rights and privileges of every sort throughout all Jewry except the right to buy the necessaries of life. (164:4.6) Josiah the blind was casted out of the synagogue (164:4.11) (164:5.4-5) Caiaphas called a meeting of the Sanhedrin to convene that action was taken to throw out of the synagogues any person who made mention of Jesus’ resurrection. (190:3.3) Cleopas, the elder brother of Emmaus disciples, had been casted out of the synagogue (190:5.1)
improvedDescription: The formal expulsion from religious communities, particularly the casting out of believers from Jewish synagogues for following Jesus' teachings, resulting in denial of religious and social privileges.

name: excreta
originalDescription: Secrecy attendant upon body elimination grew up out of fear that an enemy might get possession of something derived from the body and employ it in detrimental magic; all excreta of the body were therefore carefully buried. (88:5.1) Both food and water are fully utilized in the morontia body; there is no residual waste. (47:4.6)
improvedDescription: Bodily waste products, historically feared in primitive cultures as potential sources of harmful magic, leading to careful disposal practices. In morontia existence, there is no waste as all food and water are fully utilized.

name: excursion and reversion supervisors
originalDescription: The Morontia Companions are simply gracious hosts to those who are just beginning the long inward ascent. Excursion and Reversion Supervisors will accompany us on the longer trips to the headquarters sphere and to the surrounding worlds of transition culture. They plan, conduct, and supervise all such individual and group tours about the system worlds of training and culture. (25:7.1) (47:3.12) (48:3.14)
improvedDescription: A class of Morontia Companions who accompany ascending mortals on longer trips to the headquarters sphere and surrounding worlds of transition culture. They plan, conduct, and supervise all individual and group tours about the system worlds of training and culture.

name: executioners of Ancients of Days
improvedDescription: Divine beings who carry out the execution orders issued by the Ancients of Days, serving as their judicial enforcement agents in the superuniverses.

name: exhaustion
improvedDescription: A state of extreme physical or mental fatigue that depletes energy reserves and requires rest for recovery.

name: existential reality
improvedDescription: Reality that exists eternally and inherently, without beginning or need for evolutionary development, as opposed to experiential reality which must be achieved through growth and experience.

name: Exodus
originalDescription: The Book of Exodus is cited as one of the books that Jesus used in the book. (145:2.2)
improvedDescription: The second book of the Hebrew scriptures from which Jesus read during his Sabbath teaching in the synagogue, containing the law and commandments of God.

name: exogamy
improvedDescription: The practice of marrying outside one's own tribe, clan, or social group, as opposed to endogamy which involves marriage within the group.

name: exorcism
originalDescription: Exorcism was the employment of one spirit to control or banish another, and these tactics were also utilized for frightening ghosts and spirits. (87:6.13) The evolution of religious observances progressed from placation, avoidance, exorcism, coercion, conciliation, and propitiation to sacrifice, atonement, and redemption. (90:0.1) Propitiation was insurance against immediate ill luck rather than investment in future bliss. And the rituals of avoidance, exorcism, coercion, and propitiation all merge into one another. (89:0.2)
improvedDescription: The employment of one spirit to control or banish another, used as a primitive religious technique for frightening ghosts and spirits. It was part of early religious evolution that progressed from placation and avoidance to more complex spiritual practices.

name: expectations
improvedDescription: Anticipations or beliefs about what will happen in the future, forming part of human consciousness and motivation.

name: expectoration
improvedDescription: The act of coughing up and spitting out saliva, mucus, or other substances from the respiratory tract.

name: experience
originalDescription: age oftentimes represents experience, and that nothing in human affairs can take the place of actual experience (181:2.5) all experience is part of the Supreme (117:5.14) The positive always has the advantage over the negative, truth over error, experience over theory, spiritual realities over the isolated facts of time and space. (102:6.7) the ascension plan is characterized by giving experience to others as soon as acquired (30:3.9) beings created in perfection are deprived of experience (32:3.10-11) Experience, wisdom, and judgment are the concomitants of the lengthening of the time unit in mortal experience. (118:1.3-8) Skepticism may challenge the theories of theology, but confidence in the dependability of personal experience affirms the truth of that belief which has grown into faith. (102:6.4) (102:7.7) The motion of experience equals the force of expectant imagination plus the keenness of the sensory discovery of the external qualities of contacted reality. (102:4.2) The higher a creature’s education, the more respect he has for the knowledge, experience, and opinions of others. (25:3.12) the finaliters’ experience will embrace everything presently possible (48:8.4) actual experience gives the conceptual capacity adequate for the comprehension of such many-sided problems in cosmic equity and spiritual philosophy (54:6.10) the goal of experience is to attain wisdom (27:6.2) God has full understanding of the need of every intelligent creature for function and experience, and therefore, in every situation, be it concerned with the destiny of a universe or the welfare of the humblest of his creatures, God retires from activity (32:4.2) An experience is good when it heightens the appreciation of beauty, augments the moral will, enhances the discernment of truth, enlarges the capacity to love and serve one’s fellows, exalts the spiritual ideals, and unifies the supreme human motives (132:2.5) Nothing in the entire universe can substitute for the fact of experience on nonexistential levels. The infinity of the eternal God encompasses the potential for finite experience (108:0.2) Jesus was the perfectly unified human personality. And today, as in Galilee, he continues to unify mortal experience and to co-ordinate human endeavors. He unifies life, ennobles character, and simplifies experience. (100:7.18) Caligastia, with a maximum of intelligence and a vast experience in universe affairs, went astray—embraced sin. (67:3.9) The purpose of human existence is evolve by the technique of experience and, therefore, must be inherently and constitutively dependent on experience. (106:9.8) Michael was born a creator, educated an administrator, trained an executive, but he was required to earn his sovereignty by experience. (119:8.2) nothing takes the place of experience (22:9.6-8) (108:0.2) (109:1.3) (181:2.5,24) future outer universes currently uninhabited are predicted to have no finite experience and will not have participated in the evolution of the Almighty Supreme. (31:10.11) all human experience is really subjective except that intuitive realization of validity attaches to the unification of these three universe reality responses of cosmic recognition. (16:9.1)
improvedDescription: The actual living participation in events and situations that provides knowledge, wisdom, and understanding that cannot be obtained through theory alone. Experience is essential for creature growth and spiritual development, and nothing in the universe can substitute for actual personal experience on non-existential levels.

name: experientials
originalDescription: This is earned power, demonstrated power, experiential power; it stands in contrast to the eternity power, the unfathomable power, the existential power of the Paradise Deities. (106:2.3)
improvedDescription: This is earned power, demonstrated power, experiential power that stands in contrast to the eternity power, the unfathomable power, the existential power of the Paradise Deities.

name: experts of communication
originalDescription: experts of communication are part of the Energy Manipulator group of Celestial Artisans. Thhey are the technicians of interplanetary communication. Twelve serve on Urantia (44:5.7)
improvedDescription: Experts of communication are part of the Energy Manipulator group of Celestial Artisans who serve as technicians of interplanetary communication. Twelve serve on Urantia and are expert in the laws of transmittal and interference as applied to communications of the realms.

name: exploitation
originalDescription: Jesus’ advice on wealth: No mortal who knows God and seeks to do the divine will can stoop to engage in the oppressions of wealth. No noble man will strive to accumulate riches and amass wealth-power by the enslavement or unfair exploitation of his brothers in the flesh. (132:5.6,18) Jesus detested taking advantage of the weak, unlearned, or less fortunate (140:8.21) (147:8.4) (163:2.11) (173:1.11) Self-control leads to altruistic service; self-admiration tends towards the exploitation of others for the selfish aggrandizement of such a mistaken individual as is willing to sacrifice righteous attainment for the sake of possessing unjust power over his fellow beings. (54:1.6)
improvedDescription: The unfair taking advantage of the weak, unlearned, or less fortunate for selfish gain. Jesus condemned such treatment as incompatible with the ideals of the brotherhood of the kingdom of heaven and taught that no noble person should accumulate riches through the enslavement or unfair exploitation of others.

name: exploration
originalDescription: Andites never stopped until they had circumnavigated the globe and discovered the last remote continent. (78:4.6) Commerce, linked with adventure, led to exploration and discovery. (69:4.8) On an average world the post-Adamic dispensation is an age of great invention, energy control, and mechanical development. This is the era of the appearance of multiform manufacture and the control of natural forces; it is the golden age of exploration and the final subduing of the planet.  (52:3.6)
improvedDescription: Commerce linked with adventure that led to exploration and discovery in ancient times. On an average world, the post-Adamic dispensation becomes the golden age of exploration and the final subduing of the planet, while the adventurous Andites never stopped until they had circumnavigated the globe and discovered the last remote continent.

name: extension-school instructors
originalDescription: they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.153) Extension-School Instructors are the teachers on the world just below, a sort of preparatory school for the progressing residents of that sphere; this is a phase of the ascendant scheme for advancing the pilgrims of time. These schools, their methods of instruction and examinations, are wholly unlike anything we do on Urantia. (30:3.8)
improvedDescription: Extension-School Instructors are teachers who maintain a strong corps on worlds just below the next higher residential world of the ascendant career. They serve as a sort of preparatory school for progressing residents as part of the ascendant scheme for advancing the pilgrims of time.

name: extinction
originalDescription: God not willing that any perish (2:5.2) Father takes from vine branches not bearing fruit (180:2.1,6) Mortal mind subservient to matter is destined to become increasingly material and consequently to suffer eventual personality extinction (1:3.7) The wages of sin is death—eternal obliteration. (46:8.4) what mercy cannot rehabilitate, justice annihilates it (21:5.7) process Ancients of Days described as
improvedDescription: The eternal obliteration or annihilation of personality existence as the wages of sin and deliberate rebellion against God. This cessation of existence is decreed by the Ancients of Days after exhausting all provisions of mercy, resulting in the complete destruction of sin-identified beings who have wholly embraced iniquity.

name: eyes
originalDescription: Adam and his offspring had blue eyes, and the violet peoples were characterized by fair complexions and light hair color—yellow, red, and brown. (76:4.1) when Cain he honestly sought divine assistance, an Adjuster indwelt him. And this Adjuster, dwelling within and looking out, gave Cain a distinct advantage of superiority which classed him with the greatly feared tribe of Adam. (76:2.8) Society early adopted the paying-back attitude of retaliation: an eye for an eye, a life for a life. The law of Moses said, ‘An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth’. (70:10.9) (140:6.9) (140:8.5) (159:5.11) Jesus quoted the saying that it is ‘easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for the heathen to inherit eternal life.’ And he declared that it is as easy for this camel to go through the needle’s eye as for the self-satisfied rich ones to enter the kingdom of heaven” (163:3.1) On the mansion worlds, after our vision range is extended and we are freed from the fetters of material comparisons, we can begin to comprehend the meaning of those realities which “eye cannot see nor ear hear, and which have never entered the concept of human minds,” even those things which “God has prepared for those who love such eternal verities. (11:4.5) (24:6.2) (43:6.8) (44:2.1) Said Jesus: If the things you do with your hands, or the things you see with your eyes give offense in the progress of the kingdom, sacrifice these cherished idols, for it is better to enter the kingdom minus many of the beloved things of life rather than to cling to these idols and find yourself shut out of the kingdom. (158:8.1) Of all the ten phases of wavelike energy activity, the human eye can react to just one octave, the whole light of ordinary sunlight. (42:5.13) The material eyes are truly the windows of the spirit-born soul. (42:12.12) (86:5.14) (111:0.7) (140:6.12)
improvedDescription: The material organs of sight that are truly the windows of the spirit-born soul. The human eye can react to only one octave of wavelike energy activity - the light of ordinary sunlight - yet they serve as the means through which the soul looks out upon the world.

name: Ezda
originalDescription: Ezda is an orphan boy from Bet-zur who was adopted by John the Baptist (135:3.1)
improvedDescription: Ezda is an orphan boy from Beth-zur who was adopted by John the Baptist and cared for his herds of sheep and goats when John made trips to Hebron or Engedi.

name: Ezekiel
originalDescription: Ezekiel, the Jewish prophet, was wiser than his contemporaries as he combined personal moral responsibility with a faithful observance of a higher ritual (97:10.7) Ezekiel proclaimed deliverance through consecrated service (97:8.3) he spoke of a new spirit that would dwell in the soul of man (121:7.5) he was one of the few spiritual leaders who were not silenced (97:10.3)
improvedDescription: Ezekiel was a Jewish prophet who was wiser than his contemporaries, combining personal moral responsibility with faithful observance of purified ritual. He proclaimed deliverance through consecrated service and spoke of a new spirit that would dwell in the soul of man, teaching that religion must become a reality in individual experiences.

name: Ezra (disciple of John)
originalDescription: Ezra is a disciple of John the Baptist who did not accept Jesus, separated from John, and formed a separate group in the south, becoming the founder of Mandaean religion, a religion that still exists today (137:2.2)
improvedDescription: Ezra is a disciple of John the Baptist who rejected Jesus as the Deliverer, separated from John with many followers, and founded a sect that believed in John but refused to accept Jesus. A remnant of this group, which became the foundation of the Mandaean religion, persists in Mesopotamia even today.

name: Ezra of Alexandria
originalDescription: Ezra is the father of Susanna, the head of the twelve female evangelists (189:4.4)
improvedDescription: Ezra of Alexandria was the father of Susanna, who was one of the twelve female evangelists and among the women who went to anoint Jesus' body at the tomb.

name: Ezra of Nazareth
originalDescription: Ezra was a rich merchant from Nazareth, the father of Rebecca, the woman who fell in love with Jesus and asked for her hand (127:5.1,2,6)
improvedDescription: Ezra was a wealthy merchant and trader from Nazareth, father of Rebecca who fell in love with Jesus and sought his hand in marriage when she was seventeen years old.

name: Ezra of Syracuse
originalDescription: Ezra was a backslidden Jew, who had a tavern and hospice in Syracuse, and whom Jesus rehabilitated (130:8.2)
improvedDescription: Ezra was a backslidden Jew who kept a tavern and hospice in Syracuse, whom Jesus helped rehabilitate and return to the faith of Israel during their week-long stay in the city.

name: Ezra (Prophet)
originalDescription: Ezra the prophet promised prosperity through keeping the law (97:8.3)
improvedDescription: Ezra the prophet proclaimed that prosperity would come through adherence to the law, offering hope to the Jews during their period of bondage under foreign rulers.

name: Ezraeon
originalDescription: Joseph and Mary left Alexandria on boat of Esraeon (123:0.4)
improvedDescription: Ezraeon was a friend of Joseph and Mary who owned the boat that carried them from Alexandria to Joppa in August of 4 B.C. as they returned from Egypt.

name: fables
originalDescription: it should not been employed fables in proclaiming gospel (151:2.5) (151:3.3)
improvedDescription: Jesus advised against using fables or allegories in teaching gospel truths, instead recommending the use of parables, especially nature parables, to illustrate spiritual concepts.

name: facts
originalDescription: Only facts and truth court the full light of comprehension and rejoice in the illumination and enlightenment of scientific research.
