name: four noble truths
originalDescription: The original gospel of Gautama was based on the four noble truths: (94:8.3-7) # The noble truths of suffering. # The origins of suffering. # The destruction of suffering. # The way to the destruction of suffering.
improvedDescription: The foundational teachings of Gautama Buddha's original gospel, consisting of the truths of suffering, the origins of suffering, the destruction of suffering, and the way to the destruction of suffering.

name: fourth creatures (cherubim)
originalDescription: Cherubim and sanobim are by nature very near the morontia level of existence, and they prove to be most efficient in the borderland work of the physical, morontial, and spiritual domains. These children of the local universe Mother Spirit are characterized by “fourth creatures” are quasi-material (38:7.6-7) These “fourth creatures” of the angelic orders always retain their quasi-material characteristics. They will continue on as cherubim and sanobim and do not ascend to seraphic status (38:8.4) perform indispensible borderline tasks (38:7.6) they are similar to midwayers (38:7.6)
improvedDescription: Quasi-material angelic beings that are every fourth cherubim and sanobim, characterized by their morontia-like nature and efficiency in borderland work between physical, morontial, and spiritual domains.

name: fourth creatures (Havona Servitals)
originalDescription: There are certain types of beings who are capable of discerning the reality of the creatures of both the spirit and the material worlds. Belonging to this class are the so-called fourth creatures of the Havona Servitals (44:0.17) Every fourth servital is more physical in type than the others; that is, out of each thousand, seven hundred and fifty are apparently true to spirit type, but two hundred and fifty are semiphysical in nature. (25:1.3) (38:7.6)
improvedDescription: Semiphysical beings comprising every fourth Havona Servital, more material in nature than their spirit counterparts and capable of discerning both spirit and material realities.

name: fourth creatures (Universal Conciliators)
originalDescription: There are certain types of beings who are capable of discerning the reality of the creatures of both the spirit and the material worlds. Belonging to this class are the so-called fourth creatures of the conciliators.  (25:2.8) (38:7.6) (44:0.17) Divine executioners, being fourth creatures—quasi-material beings—are almost, but not quite, visible to the short-range vision of the mortal races. (25:2.8)
improvedDescription: Quasi-material beings who serve as Divine Executioners among the Universal Conciliators, capable of making contact with material beings and almost visible to mortal vision.

name: foxes
originalDescription: Jesus said: “My son, the foxes have holes, and the birds of heaven have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head”. (157:6.12) (163:2.2)
improvedDescription: Small carnivorous mammals that Jesus referenced in his teaching about the Son of Man having nowhere to lay his head, contrasting their secure dens with his own lack of earthly dwelling.

name: Foxhall peoples
originalDescription: Though the remains of the Foxhall peoples were the last to be discovered in England, these Andonites were really the first human beings to live in those regions. (64:2.6) During a long period of cultural decadence the Foxhall peoples of England and the Badonan tribes northwest of India continued to hold on to some of the traditions of Andon and certain remnants of the culture of Onagar. (64:2) The Foxhall peoples were farthest west and succeeded in retaining much of the Andonic culture; they also preserved their knowledge of flintworking, which they transmitted to their descendants, the ancient ancestors of the Eskimos. (64:2.5-7)
improvedDescription: An early Andonite race in England who were the first human beings to live in those regions and succeeded in retaining much of the Andonic culture, including knowledge of flintworking which they transmitted to their descendants, the ancient ancestors of the Eskimos.

name: fragmentation
originalDescription: Adjusters best typify fragmented entities of Deity (30:1.100-102) There exists other fragmentations apart from Thought Adjusters (42:11.1) The Eternal Son, as an infinite and exclusively personal being, cannot fragmentize his nature, cannot distribute and bestow individualized portions of his selfhood upon other entities or persons as do the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit. (6:5.5-6) (7:5.3)
improvedDescription: The divine process by which certain Deity beings distribute individualized portions of their nature to other entities, best exemplified by Thought Adjusters from the Universal Father, though the Eternal Son cannot fragmentize due to his exclusively personal nature.

name: France
originalDescription: There were Andonites in France. They lived along the river Somme for tens of thousands of years. Though the remains of the Foxhall peoples were the last to be discovered in England, these Andonites were really the first human beings to live in those regions. At that time the land bridge still connected France with England (63:5.2-3) (64:1.5-6) (64:2.6) The Neanderthalers were excellent fighters, and they traveled extensively. They gradually spread from the highland centers in northwest India to France on the west, China on the east, and even down into northern Africa. (64:4.1) The strongholds of the blue man which persisted longest were in southern France, but the last great military resistance was overcome along the Somme. (80:5.5) By 5000 B.C. the evolving white races were dominant throughout all of northern Europe, including northern Germany, northern France, and the British Isles. (80:5.8) In the times of the New Stone Age, in 3000 B.C., overlapping the oncoming Bronze Age. In southern France and Spain it was the New Stone Age associated with sun worship. sun worshippers  (80:9.13)
improvedDescription: A European region where early Andonites lived along the river Somme for tens of thousands of years, connected to England by a land bridge during ancient times, and later inhabited by various evolutionary races including the blue man and white races.

name: franchise
improvedDescription: The right to vote or participate in governmental decisions.

name: frandalanks
originalDescription: There are ten of them on each constellation capital (41:1.4) The frandalanks are created in thirty divisions, one for each form of basic universe force, and they function exclusively as living and automatic presence, pressure, and velocity gauges. (29:4.37) chronoldeks are frandalanks that register time in addition to quantitative and qualitative energy presence (29:4.37) These beings are the joint creation of all three orders of energy-control beings: the primary and secondary force organizers and the power directors. (29:4.36) they appear in Uversa personality register (30:2.107) master frandalanks are those living instruments which indicate the power pressure and the energy charge of an entire superuniverse (29:4.2) They are stationed on all inhabited worlds and are always attached to the higher orders of physical controllers. (29:4.36) they are one type of Master Physical Controllers (29:4.11) seem to be wholly automatic and mechanical in response to the impulses of their superiors and in reaction to existing energy conditions, but they are not automatons, they are living and intelligent machines (29:4.12,38)
improvedDescription: Living and intelligent Master Physical Controllers created in thirty divisions who function as automatic presence, pressure, and velocity gauges for all forms of universe force-energy, stationed on inhabited worlds and attached to higher orders of physical controllers.

name: frankness
improvedDescription: The quality of being open, honest, and straightforward in communication.

name: fraud
originalDescription: Be not deceived by the tempting rewards of dishonest achievement; be willing to toil for the later returns inherent in honest endeavor. (160:4.11) The early traders were scrupulously honest within their own tribes but regarded it as all right to cheat distant strangers. (69:4.3) Jesus’ advice to rich man on wealth derived from fraud: If any portion of your fortune has been knowingly derived from fraud; if aught of your wealth has been accumulated by dishonest practices or unfair methods; if your riches are the product of unjust dealings with your fellows, make haste to restore all these ill-gotten gains to the rightful owners. Make full amends and thus cleanse your fortune of all dishonest riches (132:5.10,18,22) secrecy has always been essential to the successful practice of fraud and superstition (90:4.9)
improvedDescription: Dishonest practices involving deception or trickery to secure unfair gain, which Jesus taught should be corrected through restoration of ill-gotten wealth to rightful owners and making full amends for dishonest riches.

name: free love
improvedDescription: Sexual relationships without formal marriage commitment or legal bonds.

name: free will
originalDescription: Adjuster, future finaliter, Father, and Supreme Being all depend on our decisions for the realization of a new reality through us (112:5.5) Adjusters may indwell beings who have free will (52:1.6) Man cannot choose beyond the range of that which is choosable. Man does not have unfettered free will; there are limits to his range of choice, but within the radius of this choice his will is relatively sovereign. (118:6.5) (118:9.2) Will is that manifestation of the human mind which enables the subjective consciousness to express itself objectively and to experience the phenomenon of aspiring to be Godlike (130:2.10) man must be fallible if he is to be free; misunderstandings are inevitable (3:5.15) (25:3.7) sons created with a large degree of personal liberty in choosing and planning result in early tribulations that are far more than compensated by later higher loyalty (35:9.8) The endowment of imperfect beings with freedom entails inevitable tragedy, and it is the nature of the perfect ancestral Deity to universally and affectionately share these sufferings in loving companionship. (110:0.1) free will, joined to self-consciousness, is an exclusive endowment of personalities (16:8.5) Father’s gift of personality makes mortals self-conscious and, relatively, self-determinative and self-creative (5:6.6) it is a fixed policy of universe law not to infringe free will (39:3.3) (53:4.4-7) hunger and fear provide external restraint to choice range (118:8.5) The relative free will is involved in moral decision, truth discernment, unselfish love, purposeful cooperation, cosmic insight, personality dedication, worship (16:8.7-14) Mortal man is neither a helpless slave of the inflexible sovereignty of an all-powerful God nor the victim of the hopeless fatality of a mechanistic cosmic determinism. (103:5.10) Mortal man may draw near God and may repeatedly forsake the divine will so long as the power of choice remains. (5:1.11) Only in the realms of creature volition has there been deviation from the divine paths and the original plans. (12:8.3) free will choice permits mortals to cocreate themselves with God as they are to be (116:4.8,11) while religion is normal and natural to man, it is also optional. Man does not have to be religious against his will. (5:5.5) spirit liberates and mechanism limits the function of free will (118:8.3) spiritual volition self-identified with God is liberated from time (118:6.6-7) temptation cannot be overcome merely by human will (156:5.5) defined as ordained by Father and not imposed historical decissions
improvedDescription: The divine endowment enabling conscious beings to make genuine choices within established limits, constituting the universe insignia of freewill creaturehood and making possible moral decisions, spiritual growth, and eternal destiny determination through voluntary cooperation with God's will.

name: friction shields
originalDescription: Friction shields are an energy insulator used by transport Seraphim. To place a sleeping personality inside the seraph and be able to be transported, the shields must be fully open. (39:5.12-14)
improvedDescription: Friction shields are energy insulators used by transport seraphim that appear as double sets of wings extending from head to foot. These shields must be fully open to safely deposit a sleeping personality onto the transport angel for interplanetary transit, after which they are carefully closed and adjusted for the journey.

name: Fridays
originalDescription: For ages Friday has been regarded as an unlucky day and the number thirteen as an evil numeral. (88:1.6)
improvedDescription: Friday has been regarded as an unlucky day for ages, along with the number thirteen as an evil numeral, representing ancient superstitions and fetishes about certain days of the week.

name: friends of the court
originalDescription: High Commissioners act as friends of the court, advising the presiding magistrates respecting the antecedents, environment, and inherent nature of those concerned in the adjudication. (37:5.7)
improvedDescription: High Commissioners serve as friends of the court in celestial tribunals, advising presiding magistrates about the background, environment, and inherent nature of those involved in legal proceedings without directly participating in the justice process.

name: friendship
originalDescription: all live religions encourage human friendship (99:6.2) ascenders as us never lose power to recognize former associates (44:0.18) (112:5.22) of all forms of evil, none are more destructive of personality status than betrayal of trust and disloyalty to one’s confiding friends. (67:1.3) The concept of the personality of Deity facilitates fellowship; it favors intelligent worship; it promotes refreshing trustfulness. (1:7.1) The fact of the cosmic mind explains the kinship of various types of human and superhuman minds. (16:6.3) Creatures crave association with other personal creatures (10:1.3) Only personalities can commune with each other, albeit this personal communion may be greatly facilitated by the presence of just such an impersonal entity as the Thought Adjuster. (1:7.1) Forsake not an old friend, for the new is not comparable to him (147:7.3) friendliness characterizes spirit-born sons of God (180:5.12) friends and close associates sometimes work together in the afterlife and reach Paradise at the same time (22:2.6) (25:8.5-7,11) (39:3.5) God is most loving of all friends (3:1.4) (102:7.10) Havona arrivals affords opportunity for looking up your friends, fellows, and associates of the long ascension experience (30:4.28) One of the most important lessons to be learned during our mortal career is teamwork. Few are the duties in the universe for the lone servant. The higher we ascend, the more lonely we become when temporarily without the association of our fellows. (28:5.14) The impulse of friendship transcends all convictions of duty, and the service of a friend for a friend can never be called a sacrifice. (180:1.6) The Creators well know that it is “not good for man to be alone,” and provision is accordingly made for companionship, even on Paradise. (25:8.4) It is a fact that religion does not grow unless it is disciplined by constructive criticism, amplified by philosophy, purified by science, and nourished by loyal fellowship. (99:3.7,12) Loving and intimate human associations tend to rob suffering of its sorrow and hardship of much of its bitterness. (160:2.8) spirit gravity creates cohesiveness among persons and groups (7:1.6) Spirit of Truth directs loving contact of one human being with another. (180:5.11) In preaching the gospel of the kingdom, we are simply teaching friendship with God. (159:3.9) ancient times Jesus and apostles Rodan discourse how make friends
improvedDescription: Friendship is a fundamental aspect of personality relationships that transcends duty and creates deep bonds between beings. True friendship involves mutual self-expression, shared wisdom, enhanced defense against evil, and loving service that enriches both participants and survives even death, as personality relationships have cosmic value and persist throughout the universe career.

name: frogs
originalDescription: frogs were the ancestors of land vertebrates (60:0.2) ancestral frog almost died. Had the ancestral frog of all humanity jumped two inches less on a certain occasion, the whole course of evolution would have been markedly changed. (62:3.9) (65:3.3) From the briny waters of the seas there crawled out upon the land snails, scorpions, and frogs. They sprang suddenly from fish family (59:5.6,23) (65:2.6) Among the land animals the frogs reached their climax in the preceding age and rapidly declined, but they survived because they could long live even in the drying-up pools and ponds of these far-distant and extremely trying times. They evolved into prereptiles in Permian (59:6.8) frogs are the only remaining group representative of man’s earlier ancestors. (61:2.6) (65:2.6-7) There is a saying about the importance of instilling loyalty in children: The quickest way for a tadpole to become a frog is to live loyally each moment as a tadpole. (100:1.4)
improvedDescription: Frogs were crucial ancestors of land vertebrates and represent one of the most important evolutionary developments leading to humanity. They evolved from fish and later developed into prereptiles, with the ancestral frog of all humanity narrowly escaping extinction on several occasions. Frogs are the only remaining group representative of man's earlier ancestors and serve as a reminder of evolutionary progression.

name: fruit
originalDescription: Earth evolution Spiritual fruits
improvedDescription: Fruit represents both literal plant reproduction and spiritual development. In Jesus' teachings, bearing spiritual fruit demonstrates genuine faith and connection to God, manifesting as loving service, devotion, loyalty, and other divine qualities in the lives of spirit-born individuals.

name: fruit vendor of Messina
originalDescription: Jesus had a conversation with a fruit vendor of Messina that changed his life. This boy converted to the Christian faith when he was older (130:8.3)
improvedDescription: A small boy who sold fruit in Messina whose life was changed by a brief encounter with Jesus. Jesus bought fruit from him and shared spiritual wisdom, placing his hand on the boy's shoulder with words of encouragement. The lad later became a devotee of Mithraism before converting to Christianity.

name: fruits of the spirit
originalDescription: Religious experience unfailingly yields the “fruits of the spirit” in the daily life of the spirit-led mortal. (5:2.4,6) (99:5.6) (155:6.15) The convincing evidence of this spiritual certainty consists in the social fruits of the spirit which such believers, faithers, yield as a result of this genuine spiritual experience. (102:6.7) the Father requires of us only that we shall bear much fruit. (180:2.1) (193:2.2) God’s will become increasingly discernible in the spirit fruits which are borne in the lives of the spirit-led children of God (12:7.7) we must bring forth the fruits of the Father’s spirit if we would continue to ascend in the progressive life of the divine fellowship (140:1.4) defined as enumerated
improvedDescription: The fruits of the spirit are the observable manifestations of divine influence in the lives of spirit-led mortals, including loving service, unselfish devotion, courageous loyalty, sincere fairness, enlightened honesty, undying hope, confiding trust, merciful ministry, unfailing goodness, forgiving tolerance, and enduring peace. These spiritual fruits serve as convincing evidence of genuine religious experience and God-connection.

name: fulcrum
originalDescription: man supplies psychic fulcrum for Adjuster’s work (108:5.8)
improvedDescription: The psychic fulcrum is the mental foundation that humans provide through their decisions and choices, which enables the Thought Adjuster to apply spiritual leverage for uplifting and advancing spiritual illumination.

name: fundraising
originalDescription: Matthew received freely tendered offerings from believing disciples and the immediate auditors of the Master’s teachings, but he never openly solicited funds from the multitudes. (139:7.8)
improvedDescription: Matthew conducted quiet, personal fundraising for Jesus' ministry, receiving voluntary offerings from believing disciples and immediate followers rather than openly soliciting funds from crowds. He raised most money from substantial believers while keeping his own generous contributions anonymous.

name: funerals
originalDescription: The funeral service originated in man’s effort to induce the ghost soul to depart for its future home, and the funeral sermon was originally designed to instruct the new ghost how to get there. (87:2.3) when a wealthy man died, fearing that their property would be confiscated, the funeral was held up until the family donated a large sum to public welfare or to the king, an inheritance tax (69:9.6) Egyptians believed preservation of body facilitated passage through future life (95:2.4-5) Only kings and the rich were promised a resurrection; therefore did they so carefully embalm and preserve their bodies in tombs against the day of judgment. (95:5.13) Almost every group had a different idea regarding the destiny of the ghost soul. The Chinese and Egyptians once believed that soul and body remained together. Among the Egyptians this led to careful tomb construction and efforts at body preservation. Even modern peoples seek to arrest the decay of the dead. (86:4.8) Ancient funeral wastes were enormous. Modern man is not supposed to fear ghosts, but custom is strong, and much terrestrial wealth is still consumed on funeral rituals and death ceremonies. (87:2.10) religion has unduly impoverished life for the pretended enrichment of death (92:3.7) On worlds settled in light and life, “funerals” are occasions of supreme joy, profound satisfaction, and inexpressible hope. (55:2.5-6)
improvedDescription: Ceremonial practices surrounding death that originated in humanity's efforts to ensure departed souls would successfully reach their afterlife destinations. Ancient funeral customs involved elaborate rituals, material provisions for the deceased, and significant expenditures of wealth and resources. On advanced worlds settled in light and life, funerals become occasions of supreme joy and inexpressible hope as mortals witness their loved ones ascend in spiritual flames during fusion with their indwelling Adjusters.

name: fungi
originalDescription: Many of the fungi represent a retrograde movement in evolution, being plants which have lost their chlorophyll-making ability and have become more or less parasitic. (65:2.3)
improvedDescription: Many fungi represent a retrograde movement in evolution, being plants that have lost their chlorophyll-making ability and become parasitic in nature.

name: fusion
originalDescription: Perfected creature existence can be attained, sustained, and eternalized by the fusion of self-conscious mind with a fragment of the pre-Trinity spirit endowment of some one of the persons of the Paradise Trinity, either Father, Son, or Spirit (56:3.5) In the fusion experience there is no overlapping; the will creature is either Spirit fused, Son fused, or Father fused. Those who are Adjuster or Father fused are never Spirit or Son fused. (37:5.1) (40:9.1) Adjuster fusion Son fusion Spirit fusion
improvedDescription: The eternal union of a mortal's immortal soul with a divine spirit fragment, enabling perfected creature existence that can be sustained throughout eternity. This can occur through fusion with fragments from the Universal Father (Adjuster fusion), Eternal Son (Son fusion), or Infinite Spirit (Spirit fusion). Fusion represents the ultimate achievement of harmony between creature will and divine will, transforming the mortal into a new order of universe citizen with unlimited potential for eternal progression.

name: future
originalDescription: The future signifies struggle and advancement; it bespeaks work, effort, and achievement (48:4.12) ignorance is essential to success; it would be a colossal blunder for the creature to know the future (39:5.9)
improvedDescription: The future signifies struggle and advancement, representing work, effort, and achievement for ascending beings. It embodies uncertainty as an essential element for continued growth, as knowledge of future events would be detrimental to creature development and success.

name: future eternals
originalDescription: experiential Deities are future eternals from the time when, and as, they power-personalize in the growing universes by the technique of the experiential actualization of the associative-creative potentials of the eternal Paradise Deities (0:7.1-5) God the Supreme and God the Ultimate, now evolving in the experiential universes, are not existential—not past eternals, only future eternals, time-space-conditioned and transcendental-conditioned eternals. They will never have an end, but they do have personality beginnings. (0:9.5)
improvedDescription: Experiential Deities such as God the Supreme and God the Ultimate who are actualizing in the post-Havona universes through evolutionary experience. Unlike the existential Paradise Deities who have always existed, these future eternals have personality beginnings in time but will achieve unlimited eternal existence as they power-personalize through the experiential actualization of divine potentials.

name: Gabriel
originalDescription: Brilliant Evening Stars are the liaison officers for Gabriel (37:2.1-3) the presidents of the hundred councils of supreme sanctions compose the cabinet of work of Gabriel (33:8.4) since Gabriel cannot be in two places at once the Bright Evening Stars compensate for that limitation (37:2.2) Father Melchizedek is Gabriel's first assistant (33:6.2) the Melchizedeks are his chief aids (35:2.5) mortals rarely meet Gabriel (33:4.8) The Most High executive is the one who represents Gabriel in Norlatiadek (43:5.7) he is never away from Salvington when Father Melchizedek is also away (35:1.2) status functions and actions involvement in Michael’s earth bestowal (189:0.1) curiosities
improvedDescription: The Bright and Morning Star of Nebadon, Gabriel serves as the chief executive of the local universe and personal representative of Creator Son Michael. As the first-born of all creatures native to the local universe, he functions as the supreme commander of the heavenly hosts and administers universe affairs during Michael's absence. Gabriel played a crucial role in Michael's earth bestowal, appearing to Mary and Elizabeth to announce the births of Jesus and John the Baptist.

name: Gad
originalDescription: Gad was a reputed tribe of Israel (128:1.14)
improvedDescription: One of the reputed tribes of Israel that traditionally dwelt in the regions east of the Jordan River, along with the tribes of Reuben and Gilead.

name: Gadara
originalDescription: Thomas Didymus was a fisherman of Tarichea but onetime carpenter and stone mason of Gadara (138:2.5) Gadara was one of the towns visited during the first work of the twelve, from mid-August 26 to the end of 26 (138:9.3) It was one of the sites visited by Jesus, the twelve and the twelve of John during the first preaching tour of the Decapolis, between November and December 27 (144:7.1) It was also one of the sites visited by Jesus, the twelve and the 117 evangelists during the second preaching tour in Galilee, between October 3, 28 and December 30, 28 (149:0.1) It was one of the towns visited by the twelve, the twelve evangelists and other evangelists during the second tour of the Decapolis from August 18 to September 16, 29 (159:0.2) It was one of the towns visited in the mission of Perea, from January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: A city in the Decapolis region where Thomas Didymus once worked as a carpenter and stone mason before becoming an apostle. Gadara was visited multiple times during Jesus' ministry, including the first work of the twelve apostles, the preaching tours of the Decapolis, the second tour of Galilee, and the final mission in Perea.

name: Gadda
originalDescription: It was one of the towns visited in the mission of Perea by Jesus, the twelve, the seventy and women corp, from January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: A town in Perea that was visited during the final three-month mission conducted by the seventy messengers, the twelve apostles, and Jesus before his journey to Jerusalem for his final experiences in the flesh.

name: Gadiah
originalDescription: Gadiah is a Philistine interpreter with whom Jesus reasoned about Jonah during his stay in Joppa. He would later become a Christian after listening to the apostle Peter, with whom he had a memorable controversy (130:1)
improvedDescription: A Philistine interpreter who worked for Simon the tanner in Joppa and became a close friend of Jesus during their stay there. As a truth seeker who met Jesus, the great truth giver, Gadiah experienced profound enlightenment. He later became a Christian after listening to the apostle Peter, with whom he had a memorable theological controversy.

name: Gaius
originalDescription: Jesus met Gaius at the house of Crispus in Corinth; he later became a loyal supporter of Paul (133:3.11)
improvedDescription: Jesus met Gaius at the house of Crispus in Corinth; he later became a loyal supporter of Paul.

name: Galantia
originalDescription: Galantia is the ascendant associate of Gavalia, Nebadon's first Brilliant Evening Star, and Galantia is the first ascendant to enter service in Nebadon (37:2.6) (46:5.25) she currently has her headquarters on Jerusem (37:2.6) (46:5.25) she is the associate chief of these super angels (46:5.25)
improvedDescription: Galantia is the ascendant associate of Gavalia, Nebadon's first Brilliant Evening Star, and is the first ascendant to enter service in Nebadon. She currently maintains headquarters on Jerusem and serves as associate chief of these superangels.

name: Galatia
originalDescription: Matthew preached in Galatia (139:7.10)
improvedDescription: A region where Matthew preached the gospel and baptized believers during his missionary journeys after the persecution of believers in Jerusalem.

name: galaxies
originalDescription: In The Urantia Book the word galaxy is used interchangeably with universe, or is used in the expression “universe of galaxies” to refer to star formations in uninhabited outer space (11:7.7-9) (12:4.15) (40:10.8) (112:7.16-17) The Book claims that 375 million new galaxies will soon be observed by astronomers. Today one of the largest catalogs of galaxies, the Sloan Digital Sky Survey has been able to catalog 100 million objects. The most recent catalogue, PS1-STRM, has been able to catalog 2.9 billion objects (12:2.3) The Milky Way galaxy appears to be closely related to the Orvonton superuniverse, or perhaps the two are equivalent (15:3.4) (15:4.8-9)
improvedDescription: In The Urantia Book, galaxies are vast star formations that race around Paradise in orderly procession within space levels. The book indicates that 375 million new galaxies will soon be observed by astronomers, and describes how galaxies in outer space levels revolve in alternating directions to maintain cosmic stability.

name: Galilee
originalDescription: The Galileans were not regarded with full favor by the Jerusalem religious leaders and rabbinical teachers. Galilee was more gentile than Jewish when Jesus was born. (121:2.12) Many of the Galilean fishermen carried heavy strains of gentile blood as a result of the forcible conversion of the gentile population of Galilee one hundred years previously. (139:0.2) Herod Antipas, like his father, was a great builder. He rebuilt many of the cities of Galilee, including the important trade center of Sepphoris. (121:2.11) Jesus always wished to meet apostles in the scenes and nature of Galilee (192:1.2) Jesus’ preaching tours of Galilee: # The first public preaching tour of Galilee began on Sunday, January 18, A. D. 28, and continued for about two months, ending with the return to Capernaum on March 17. In this tour were Jesus, the twelve, and the twelve of John (146:0.1) # The second public preaching tour of Galilee began on Sunday, October 3, A. D. 28, and continued for almost three months, ending on December 30. There were Jesus, the twelve and the 117 evangelists (149:0.1) (149:7.3) # The third public preaching tour in Galilee began on January 18, 29, and ended on March 10 of that year. Jesus, the twelve, the 75 evangelists who had passed the test, and the women corp participated (149:2.8) (150:0.3-4) # A preaching tour of the seventy evangelists covered Galilee, Samaria, and Judea, from mid-November to December 30, AD 29. (163:4.16) lack of wealth of Galileans did not imply social inferiority (126:5.1) (127:5.1) Galilee was a more beautiful and prosperous district than Judea, and it cost only about one fourth as much to live there as in Jerusalem and Judea. (124:2.9) Jews from Judea used to say about Gelileans: “Search the Scriptures, and you will discover that out of Galilee arises no prophet, much less the Messiah” (162:2.9) (162:6.4) Pilate mingled Galileans’ blood with sacrifices blood in the temple (166:4.4) (185:1.5) residents of Galilee only went to Jerusalem for three feasts each year (125:1.5) Although Jesus might have enjoyed a better opportunity for schooling at Alexandria than in Galilee, he could not have had such a splendid environment for working out his own life problems with a minimum of educational guidance, at the same time enjoying the great advantage of constantly contacting with such a large number of all classes of men and women hailing from every part of the civilized world. (123:0.5) (124:0.1)
improvedDescription: A northern province of Palestine where Jesus conducted his ministry and recruited most of his apostles. Galilee was more gentile than Jewish, more prosperous than Judea, and its residents were not fully favored by Jerusalem's religious leaders. Jesus conducted three major preaching tours throughout Galilee during his public ministry.

name: gall
originalDescription: It was the common belief of the Jews that the drop of gall on the point of the sword of the angel of death began to work by the end of the third day, so that it was taking full effect on the fourth day. (168:1.14)
improvedDescription: According to Jewish belief, a drop of gall on the point of the sword of the angel of death began to work by the end of the third day and took full effect on the fourth day after death.

name: Gamala
originalDescription: Gamala was one of the cities visited during the second public preaching tour of Galilee began on Sunday, October 3, A. D. 28, and continued for almost three months, ending on December 30. There were Jesus, the twelve and the 117 evangelists (149:0.1) During that tour, at Gamala Jesus taught the truth about the fear the Lord (149:6.1) Gamala is one of the towns visited during the second tour of the Decapolis between August 18 and September 16, 29 (159:0.2) Here took place the episode of the meeting of the apostle John with a strange preacher whom he disavowed (159:2.1)
improvedDescription: A city visited during Jesus' second public preaching tour of Galilee and later during the second tour of the Decapolis. It was here that Jesus taught about the fear of the Lord and where John encountered a strange preacher teaching in Jesus' name.

name: Gamaliel
originalDescription: Gamaliel was a renowned teacher in Jerusalem (126:5.3) advised the Sanhedrin against the imprisonment of Jesus followers (194:4.10)
improvedDescription: A renowned teacher and leading rabbi in Jerusalem who advised the Sanhedrin against imprisoning Jesus' followers, counseling them to let the movement alone since if it was of God, they could not overthrow it.

name: gambling
originalDescription: partially civilized people still believe today in chance and evince lingering predispositions to gambling. (86:1.4) primitive man was a natural-born gambler; he always wanted to get something for nothing, and all too often during these early times the success which accrued from patient practice was attributed to charms. (69:2.7)
improvedDescription: Primitive man was a natural-born gambler who always wanted to get something for nothing. Early humans lived in uncertainty and constant fear of chance, making existence itself a gamble that fascinated the savage mind.

name: games
originalDescription: about athletic games see link to Sports | sports Modern survivals of this proclivity for casting lots are illustrated, not only in the many games of chance, but also in the well-known “counting-out” rhymes. Once, the person counted out must die; now, he is only it in some childish game. (90:2.4)
improvedDescription: Modern games of chance and children's counting-out rhymes are survivals of ancient shamanic practices of casting lots to determine the will of spirits and make decisions.

name: gamma rays
originalDescription: Gamma rays are those emanations which characterize the spontaneous dissociation of atomic matter. The best illustration of this form of electronic activity is in the phenomena associated with radium disintegration. (42:5.1,7)
improvedDescription: Gamma rays are electromagnetic radiations that characterize the spontaneous dissociation of atomic matter, with radium disintegration being the best illustration of this form of electronic activity.

name: Ganges
originalDescription: Andites migrated into valley of Ganges about 15,000 B.C. during 1,500 years (79:2.4) Ancient India acted as a catch basin for the migrating races. The base of the peninsula was formerly somewhat narrower than now, much of the deltas of the Ganges and Indus being the work of the last 50,000 years (79:2.1) The Dravidian centers of culture were located in the river valleys, principally of the Indus and Ganges, and in the Deccan along the three great rivers flowing through the Eastern Ghats to the sea. (79:3.6) On the Gangetic plain Aryan and Dravidian eventually mingled to produce a high culture, and this center was later reinforced by contributions from the northeast, coming from China. (79:4.4) marine animals of primitive ages took temporary refuge in three favorable retreats: the present Gulf of Mexico region, the Ganges Bay of India, and the Sicilian Bay of the Mediterranean basin. (59:6.9)
improvedDescription: A major river in India where Andites migrated around 15,000 B.C., spreading over the Indus and Ganges valleys. The Ganges delta was largely formed over the last 50,000 years, and its valley became a center where Aryan and Dravidian peoples eventually mingled to produce a high culture.

name: Ganid
originalDescription: Ganid was a brilliant Indian boy who was taught by Jesus during a journey with him and his father Gonod (130) (133) the first meeting with Jesus took place during Passover of the year 22, in Jerusalem (129:2.9) Ganid suffered a serious illness in Cyprus and was lovingly cared for by Jesus and his father (133:7.3) he put into practice the teachings of his teacher when in Antioch he spoke with an employee of his father who was dissatisfied with the tasks assigned to him (133:8.3) together with Jesus, he produced a translated synopsis of the best texts on the religions of the world that they found in the library of Alexandria (131) (130:3.5-6) he was a worthy successor to his eminent father (133:9.5)
improvedDescription: A brilliant seventeen-year-old Indian boy who traveled with Jesus and his father Gonod for nearly two years, during which Jesus served as their interpreter and Ganid's tutor. Under Jesus' direction, he compiled teachings from world religions recognizing a Universal Deity and later became an influential man in India, spreading the noble truths he learned from his beloved teacher.

name: Garden of Eden
originalDescription: The Garden of Eden is the second world center of civilization to project onto a normal planet, after the headquarters city of the Planetary Prince. A great planetary university of culture destined to endure forever (51:6.1-3) When an Adam and Eve begin their sojourn on an evolutionary world, their place of abode is often called the Garden of Eden because it is characterized by the floral beauty and the botanic grandeur of Edentia, the constellation capital. (39:5.3) (51:3.2) (73:4.1) preparation physical description (73:5) life in the Garden Edenites destiny teachings
improvedDescription: The headquarters and dwelling place of Adam and Eve on Urantia, located on a Mediterranean peninsula and designed as a center of world culture and biological uplift. Named for its floral beauty patterned after Edentia, it served as a great planetary university until its submergence beneath the Mediterranean Sea following Adam and Eve's default.

name: gardeners
originalDescription: The spornagia are the landscape gardeners of the headquarters worlds, and they are both original and artistic in their treatment of the open spaces of Jerusem. (46:7.2)
improvedDescription: The spornagia are the landscape gardeners of the headquarters worlds, serving as both original and artistic cultivators of the open spaces of Jerusem and other administrative spheres.

name: Gardens of Eden
originalDescription: These Gardens of Eden are so named in honor of Edentia, the constellation capital, and because they are patterned after the botanic grandeur of the headquarters world of the Most High Fathers. (43:6.2) (51:3.2) (73:4.1) On normal worlds the garden headquarters of the violet race becomes the second center of world culture and, jointly with the headquarters city of the Planetary Prince, sets the pace for the development of civilization. (51:6.2) Such garden homes are usually located in a secluded section and in a near-tropic zone. (51:3.2)
improvedDescription: The garden homes of Material Sons and Daughters on evolutionary worlds, named in honor of Edentia and patterned after its botanic grandeur. These beautiful centers of culture serve as the second center of world civilization, working in cooperation with the Planetary Prince's headquarters city.

name: gardens of God
originalDescription: One half of Edentia is divided into seventy triangular sections, whose boundaries converge at the headquarters buildings of their respective sectors. The remainder of this sphere is one vast natural park, the gardens of God. (43:1.7) (43:6.1-2)
improvedDescription: The vast natural park comprising half of Edentia, the constellation capital, featuring exquisite botanic artistry and living embellishments that represent the acme of morontia activities and serve as the most entrancing creations of the local universe.

name: gas chambers
originalDescription: in most developed continental nation criminals executed in gas chambers (72:10.1)
improvedDescription: Lethal execution chambers used by courts on a highly developed continental nation to sentence serious habitual criminals and the incurably insane to death.

name: gastropods
originalDescription: 310 million years ago, gastropods, including drilling univalves, periwinkles, and snails, were present in the ancient seas, while bivalve gastropods like mussels, clams, oysters, and scallops have remained almost unchanged. (59:2.12) 290 million years ago, gastropods, brachiopods, sponges, and reef-building corals continued to increase, with warm seas bathing the polar coasts. (59:3.7)
improvedDescription: Ancient shell animals including single-shelled drills, periwinkles, snails, and bivalve forms like mussels, clams, oysters, and scallops that were present in primitive seas 310 million years ago and have remained largely unchanged through the geological ages.

name: gate beautiful
originalDescription: One of the gates in Jerusalem temple, mentioned by Acts of Apostles. Modern archaeology does not know exactly which gate this corresponded to, and it is sometimes identified with the gate called “Nicanor,” which connected the so-called “courtyard of the women” with the “courtyard of the priests.” However, the description in The Urantia Book clearly identifies this gate with the “eastern gate” of the sanctuary enclosure. (162:4.3)
improvedDescription: A gate in the Jerusalem temple that opened from the court of the women onto the court of the gentiles, where worshipers would turn westward during the Feast of Tabernacles ceremony to repeat their chants and continue their march for the symbolic water.

name: Gath
originalDescription: Philistines ordered King David off field at Gath. They feared he might go over to Saul. (97:9.5)
improvedDescription: A Philistine city where David and his forces were ordered off the battlefield by Philistine commanders who feared he might switch sides and join Saul during their attack on Israel.

name: Gautama Siddhartha
originalDescription: Siddartha was a great teacher of the 6th century BC, the son of an unimportant chieftain who ruled in a valley of the Himalayas his conversion to his new ideals occurred suddenly (100:5.3) he elaborated the trinitarian doctrine of Master, Law and Fraternity (104:1.6) followers strengths weaknesses
improvedDescription: A great religious teacher of the 6th century BC, born in Nepal as heir to a petty chieftain in the Himalayas. Known as Buddha to his followers, he experienced a sudden spiritual conversion and developed the trinitarian doctrine of Master, Law, and Brotherhood. His teachings emphasized universal salvation without sacrifice, torture, ritual, or priests, though he failed to perceive the personality of God or provide adequate spiritual driving power for lasting religious transformation.

name: Gavalia
originalDescription: Gavalia is the firstborn and chief of the Brilliant Evening Stars in Nebadon (37:2.6) (119:0.1) his ascendant associate is named Galantia, the first to attain this status in Nebadon (37:2.6) he is the author of Urantia paper 119 (119:0.1)
improvedDescription: The firstborn and chief of the Brilliant Evening Stars in Nebadon, assigned to ascendant mortal ministry since Michael's return from his bestowal on Urantia. His ascendant associate is Galantia, the first to attain superangel status in Nebadon. Gavalia authored Urantia Paper 119 on the seven bestowals of Michael of Nebadon.

name: Gaza
originalDescription: Jesus passed here in his personal voyage through Syria and Palestine (134:7.4) (194:4.13)
improvedDescription: A coastal city in Palestine that Jesus visited during his personal tour through Syria and Palestine. It later became part of the route followed by early Christian missionaries as they spread the gospel throughout the Roman Empire.

name: Geba
originalDescription: Jesus passed here in his personal voyage through Syria and Palestine (134:7.5)
improvedDescription: A city in Palestine that Jesus passed through during his final tour as a private individual, traveling northward from Beersheba to Dan through the heart of the country.

name: Gehenna
originalDescription: bodies of crucified thieves were taken to Gehenna (188:1.5) they were open burial pits south of Jerusalem (188:0.2)
improvedDescription: Open burial pits located south of Jerusalem where the bodies of crucified criminals, including thieves, were typically disposed of and exposed to wild beasts. The Jewish rulers originally planned to have Jesus' body thrown there after his crucifixion.

name: genealogies
originalDescription: The Significances of Origins are the living ready-reference genealogies of the vast hosts of beings—men, angels, and others—who inhabit the seven superuniverses. (28:6.2-4)
improvedDescription: Living ready-reference records maintained by the Significances of Origins, a type of reflective seconaphim, containing complete ancestral information and current status data for all beings—mortals, angels, and others—throughout the seven superuniverses.

name: general council
originalDescription: There was a general council of Jesus followers in Philadelphia (167:0.3) (167:2.3)
improvedDescription: A gathering of Jesus' followers held in Philadelphia where they discussed the progress of the gospel throughout Palestine and surrounding regions, and where Abner preached about Jesus' parable teachings.

name: Genesis, Book of
originalDescription: The story of creating Eve out of Adam’s rib is a confused condensation of the Adamic arrival and the celestial surgery connected with the interchange of living substances associated with the coming of the corporeal staff of the Planetary Prince more than four hundred and fifty thousand years previously. (74:8.3)
improvedDescription: The biblical account containing the story of Eve's creation from Adam's rib, which represents a confused condensation of the Adamic arrival and the celestial surgery involving living substance interchange that occurred with the Planetary Prince's corporeal staff over 450,000 years earlier.

name: genetics
originalDescription: Hybridization makes for species improvement because of the role of the dominant genes. Racial intermixture increases the likelihood of a larger number of the desirable dominants being present in the hybrid. (82:6.7) importance of genetic inheritance genetics at heavens genetic evolution historically
improvedDescription: The biological science of heredity and inheritance. Hybridization improves species through dominant genes, and racial intermixture increases the likelihood of desirable dominant traits appearing in offspring. While hereditary factors influence physical and mental capabilities, they do not prevent spiritual achievement, as all mortals possess uniform spiritual endowment regardless of their genetic inheritance.

name: Genghis Khan
originalDescription: The Andite military genius of the Mongols; he was a monotheist (79:1.9) his tribesmen still await his return (92:5.6)
improvedDescription: The Mongol military leader who represented the last great manifestation of Andite military genius in central Asia around A.D. 1200. He was a monotheist who proclaimed the existence of "one God in heaven," and his tribesmen still await his return.

name: genius
originalDescription: Primitive man could not distinguish between genius and insanity; idiots were either beaten to death or revered as fetish personalities. (88:1.9) Many people looked upon geniuses as fetish personalities possessed by a wise spirit. (88:1.10) every civilization is limited by available natural resources, inherent genius, and sufficient leisure to insure inventive fruition (78:2.4) In a religious genius, strong spiritual faith so many times leads directly to disastrous fanaticism, to exaggeration of the religious ego, but it was not so with Jesus. (196:0.6) Jesus’ advice regarding disposition of wealth was: “If you chance to secure wealth by flights of genius, if your riches are derived from the rewards of inventive endowment, do not lay claim to an unfair portion of such rewards. The genius owes something to both his ancestors and his progeny; likewise is he under obligation to the race, nation, and circumstances of his inventive discoveries” (132:5.8,20) the flight of genius is neutralized by the gravity of mediocrity (111:7.5) The normal man should be fostered; he is the backbone of civilization and the source of the mutant geniuses of the race. (68:6.11) genius and creative originality are very much fostered in the main continental nation of a closer inhabited planet (72:6.7) (72:7.1)
improvedDescription: Exceptional intellectual or creative ability that primitive peoples often confused with insanity or divine possession. Genius owes something to both ancestors and progeny, as well as to the race and circumstances that enabled inventive discoveries. Normal humans are the backbone of civilization and the source of mutant geniuses.

name: Gennesaret
originalDescription: A region of the Sea of Galilee where Jesus was resting after the events of the feeding of the five thousand. (152:6) This is where Jesus and the twelve ended their journey back from Phoenicia on August 1, 29. (156:6.3)
improvedDescription: A region on the western shores of the Sea of Galilee where Jesus held informal conferences with the twelve apostles after the feeding of the five thousand. This is also where Jesus and the twelve ended their journey back from Phoenicia on August 1, A.D. 29.

name: gentiles
originalDescription: Each Jewish synagogue tolerated a fringe of gentile believers, “devout” or “God-fearing” men, and it was among this fringe of proselytes that Paul made the bulk of his early converts to Christianity. Even the temple at Jerusalem possessed its ornate court of the gentiles. (121:2.5) in Jesus times Gentile philosophies were: (121:4) in Jesus times Gentile religions were: (121:5) Jews
improvedDescription: Non-Jewish peoples who, though morally somewhat inferior to the Jews, possessed abundant natural goodness and potential for spiritual growth. Each Jewish synagogue tolerated a fringe of gentile believers called "devout" or "God-fearing" men, among whom Paul made many early Christian converts.

name: gentleness
originalDescription: gentleness is one of fruits of the Spirit, an increasing exhibition of the characteristics of the Spirit in the life reactions (34:6.13)
improvedDescription: One of the fruits of the Spirit that increasingly manifests in the life reactions of spirit-led mortals, along with love, joy, peace, long-suffering, goodness, faith, meekness, and temperance.

name: geography
originalDescription: China protected by its geography, by the mountains to the west and the Pacific to the east (79:6.12-13) The failure of India to achieve the hegemony of Eurasia was largely a matter of topography. The inferior races could not be expelled in the south because the sea surrounded everything. (79:2.5) The configuration of continents and other land-arrangement situations are very influential in determining peace or war. (81:6.5) protection of North America by oceans allowed unmolested development. Few Urantian people has had this benefit. (81:6.5) Geography in Jesus times
improvedDescription: The physical features and land arrangements of continents that greatly influence the development of civilizations. Geographic barriers like mountains and oceans can protect peoples from invasion, as China was protected by mountains to the west and the Pacific to the east, allowing for unmolested cultural development.

name: geologic ages
originalDescription: # Archeozoic, prelife era #* 1 billion to 550 million years ago (59:0.2) (78:7.4) # Proterozoic, life-dawn era #* 550 to 400 million years ago (58:7.1) (59:0.3) # Paleozoic, marine-life era #* 400 to 150 million years ago (59:0.4) (59:6.11) #* Cambrian, trilobite age #** 400 to 350 million years ago (59:1) #* Ordovician, invertebrate-animal age #** 350 to 300 million years ago (59:2) #* Silurian, coral and brachiopod age #** 300 to 275 million years ago (59:3) #* Devonian, age of plant life and fishes #** 270 to 220 million years ago (59:4) #* Carboniferous, age of fern forests and frogs #** 220 to 180 million years ago (59:5) #* Permian, seed-plant age and biologic tribulation #** 170 to 150 million years ago (59:6) # Mesozoic, early land-life era #* 150 to 50 million years ago (59:0.5) (60:4.6) #* Triassic, early-reptilian age #** 150 to 125 million years ago (60:1) #* Jurassic, later-reptilian age #** 125 to 100 million years ago (60:2) #* Cretaceous, age of flowering plants and birds #** 100 to 50 million years ago (60:3-4) # Cenozoic, mammalian era #* 50 million years ago to the present (61:0) #* Eocene, early-mammal age #** 50 to 35 million years ago (61:1) #* Oligocene, advanced mammal age #** 35 to 25 million years ago (61:2) #* Miocene, age of elephant and horse #** 25 to 10 million years ago (61:3.15) #* Pliocene, mammalian migration stage #** 10 to 1 million years ago (61:4) #* Pleistocene, ice age #** 2 million to 100,000 years ago (61:5-7) #* Holocene, post-glacial period #** 35,000 years ago to the present (61:7.18)
improvedDescription: The major divisions of Earth's history spanning one billion years, including the Archeozoic (prelife era), Proterozoic (life-dawn era), Paleozoic (marine-life era), Mesozoic (early land-life era), and Cenozoic (mammalian era), each marked by distinctive forms of life and geological developments.

name: geology
originalDescription: The alternate periods of land and sea dominance have occurred in million-year cycles. There has been an agelong rhythm associated with this rise and fall of ocean floor and continental land levels. And these same rhythmical crustal movements will continue from this time on throughout the earth’s history but with diminishing frequency and extent. (60:4.1) The early crust of the earth was in a state of continual flux. Surface cooling alternated with immense lava flows. Nowhere can there be found on the surface of the world anything of this original planetary crust. It has all been mixed up too many times with extruding lavas of deep origins and admixed with subsequent deposits of the early world-wide ocean. (57:8.11) (58:7.1,6) All of the geological story is graphically told within the fossil pages of the vast “stone book” of world record. (58:7) One of the sciences that coordinates revelation is geology (102:4.6)
improvedDescription: The science that studies Earth's physical structure and history through rock formations and fossils. The planet's geological story is recorded in the "stone book" of stratified rock layers, revealing alternating periods of land and sea dominance that have occurred in million-year cycles throughout Earth's history.

name: Gerar
originalDescription: after Melchizedek’s departure, Abraham moved to Gerar. There he withheld his identity due to fear (93:9.2-3)
improvedDescription: A place where Abraham moved after Melchizedek's departure from Salem. There Abraham became fearful and timid, withholding his identity so that Abimelech appropriated his wife Sarah.

name: Gerasa
originalDescription: One of the towns of the Decapolis Jesus passed by with his little brother Simon when he first took him on Passover AD 17. (128:3.2) During his waiting period before beginning his preaching, Jesus passed through here on a trip to Jerusalem, accompanied by John Zebedee (134:9.1) It is one of the towns visited during the first preaching tour on the Sea of Galilee, from June 23 to July 7, 26, which the twelve made alone (138:9.3) It is one of the towns visited during the first preaching tour of the Decapolis, from November to December 27, carried out by Jesus, his twelve apostles and the twelve apostles of John the Baptist (144:7.1) After his return from Phoenicia on August 29 Jesus traveled to Jerusalem and passed through Gerasa (152:7.1) It is one of the towns visited during the second preaching tour of the Decapolis, from August to September 29, carried out by the twelve and a group of evangelists without the continuous presence of Jesus (159:0.2) It is one of the towns visited in the mission of Perea, the last missionary effort of Jesus in the year 30 (165:0.1) There Jesus gave a memorable speech about “how many will be saved” (166:3)
improvedDescription: One of the principal cities of the Decapolis that Jesus visited multiple times during his ministry. It was included in his first Passover journey with his brother Simon, his preaching tours of the Decapolis, and his final Perean mission, where he gave a memorable discourse on "how many will be saved."

name: Gerizim
improvedDescription: A mountain mentioned in The Urantia Book, also known as Mount Gerizim.

name: germ plasm
improvedDescription: The hereditary material of an organism, related to life plasm in biological processes.

name: Germany
originalDescription: The Nordic-Danish and the Danubian-Andonite cultures met and mingled on the Rhine as is witnessed by the existence of two racial groups in Germany today. (80:9.3) Germans are descendants of Andite and blue races. These races were the ancestors of the so-called Nordic races, the forefathers of the Scandinavian, German, and Anglo-Saxon peoples. (80:4.5) By 5000 B.C. the evolving white races were dominant throughout all of northern Europe, including northern Germany, northern France, and the British Isles. (80:5.8)
improvedDescription: A European nation where Nordic-Danish and Danubian-Andonite cultures met and mingled on the Rhine, resulting in two racial groups that exist today. Germans are descendants of Andite and blue races, ancestors of the Nordic peoples including Scandinavians and Anglo-Saxons.

name: Geshur
originalDescription: Talmai was king of Geshur and David married a daughter of him (97:9.10)
improvedDescription: A kingdom whose ruler Talmai was father-in-law to King David, who married Talmai's daughter as one of his political marriages.

name: gesture
originalDescription: Gesture, being older than speech, was the more holy and magical, and mimicry was believed to have strong magical power. (88:6.4) (92:2.2)
improvedDescription: A form of communication older than speech that was considered more holy and magical, with mimicry believed to possess strong magical power in primitive cultures.

name: Gethsemane
originalDescription: Jesus and the apostles spent the entire month of June, 27, in or near Jerusalem, and they lived for the most part in tents, which they pitched in a shaded park, or garden, known in that day as Gethsemane. This park was situated on the western slope of the Mount of Olives not far from the brook Kidron. (142:8.4) Jesus and the twelve returned to camp in Gethsemane on Passover 28 (147:2.2) (147:5.6) In his last stay at Jerusalem Jesus and the apostles pitched their tents in the hillside ravine overlooking the public camping park of Gethsemane, on a plot of ground belonging to Simon of Bethany. (173:5.5) (174:0.3) (174:4.4) (174:5.1) (175:4.1) (176:0.1-2) (177:1.5) (178:3.1) (182:0.1) (182:2.10) (183:4.8) (186:3.1) (187:0.1) (190:4.1) Jesus was alone praying in Gethsemane before his arrest (182:3) there was an olive press near entrance to Gethsemane (183:0.3) Annas Palace was on Mount Olivet, not far from the Garden of Gethsemane (184:0.3) Near Gethsemane, a little above Mount Olivet, is where Jesus addressed his last words of farewell to the apostles in his last appearance, and departed (193:5.1)
improvedDescription: A shaded park or garden on the western slope of Mount Olivet near Jerusalem where Jesus and his apostles camped during their stays in the area. It was the site of Jesus' final prayer before his arrest and served as their headquarters during the last week of his earthly life.

name: ghosts
originalDescription: The ghost cult evolved as an offset to the hazards of bad luck; its primitive religious observances were the outgrowth of anxiety about bad luck and of the inordinate fear of the dead. (87:0.1) Many early peoples associated ghosts with the sea; hence virgins were greatly restricted in their bathing practices; young women were far more afraid of bathing in the sea at high tide than of having sex relations. (84:1.4) Man inherited a natural environment, acquired a social environment, and imagined a ghost environment. The state is man’s reaction to his natural environment, the home to his social environment, the church to his illusory ghost environment. (86:6) The most primitive idea of the human soul, the ghost, was derived from the breath-dream idea-system. (86:4.3) Moses persistently sought to uproot the remnants of the ghost cult among his people, even prescribing the death penalty for its practitioners. (92:5.11) fear of ghosts nature and activities of ghosts influence in religion
improvedDescription: Spiritual entities believed by primitive peoples to be the souls of the dead, forming the foundation of early religion through ghost cults. These beliefs evolved as protection against bad luck and fear of the dead, with ghosts thought to influence the living and require appeasement through rituals and offerings.

name: giantism
originalDescription: mortal stature tends to decrease from the red man down to the indigo race, although on Urantia unexpected strains of giantism appeared among the green and orange peoples. (51:4.2)
improvedDescription: Unexpected strains of unusually large physical stature that appeared among the green and orange evolutionary races on Urantia, contrary to the general trend of decreasing mortal stature from red to indigo races.

name: gibbons
originalDescription: a gibbon raid killed parents of Andon and Fonta, the parents of humanity (62:5.10-11) The early lemurs concerned in the ancestry of the human species were not directly related to the pre-existent tribes of gibbons and apes then living in Eurasia and northern Africa, whose progeny have survived to the present time. (62:1.1)
improvedDescription: Primates that killed the parents of Andon and Fonta, the first human beings, in raids on their forest home. The early lemurs ancestral to humans were not directly related to the pre-existing gibbon tribes of Eurasia and northern Africa.

name: Gibeon
originalDescription: Canaanite city attacked by Saul (97:9.13)
improvedDescription: A Canaanite city that had a peace treaty with the Ephraimites and was attacked by King Saul, whose defeat David attributed to Yahweh's displeasure over this violation of the treaty.

name: Gibeonites
originalDescription: King David turned over 7 descendants of Saul to Gibeonites to be hanged (97:9.13)
improvedDescription: A Canaanite people who had a peace treaty with the Ephraimites and whose city was attacked by King Saul, leading to Yahweh forsaking him. King David later turned over seven of Saul's descendants to the Gibeonites to be hanged.

name: Gibraltar isthmus
originalDescription: About the time of certain climatic changes in Africa, England separated from the continent, and Denmark arose from the sea, while the isthmus of Gibraltar, protecting the western basin of the Mediterranean, gave way as the result of an earthquake, quickly raising this inland lake to the level of the Atlantic Ocean. (80:2.4) During the earlier days of the violet race the Mediterranean trough was protected by the Gibraltar isthmus and the Sicilian land bridge. (80:1.1)
improvedDescription: A land bridge that once connected Spain with Africa and protected the western basin of the Mediterranean Sea. The isthmus collapsed due to an earthquake, causing the Mediterranean to quickly rise to the level of the Atlantic Ocean and resulting in catastrophic flooding that caused the greatest loss of life by flood in world history.

name: Gideon
originalDescription: The Midianites invaded the Jordan Valley in the days of Gideon (124:6.6)
improvedDescription: A biblical judge in whose days the Midianites invaded the Jordan Valley and poured into the region to overrun the land.

name: gifts
originalDescription: The affectionate dedication of the human will to the doing of the Father’s will is man’s choicest gift to God; in fact, such a consecration of creature will constitutes man’s only possible gift of true value to the Paradise Father. Jesus brought to God, as a man of the realm, the greatest of all offerings: the consecration and dedication of his own will to the majestic service of doing the divine will. (1:1.2) (56:8.3) (196:0.10) “Every good gift and every perfect gift comes down from the Father of lights.” (2:6.3) If our earth parents, being of evil tendency, know how to love their children and bestow good gifts on them, how much more must the good Father in heaven know how wisely to love his children on earth and to bestow suitable blessings upon them. (131:10.3) (144:2.4) (146:2.6-10) Accumulations of wealth early became the badge of social distinction. Even modern peoples revel in the lavish distribution of Christmas gifts, while rich men endow great institutions of philanthropy and learning. (69:5.13) Salvation is the gift of God, and righteousness is the natural fruit of the spirit-born life of sonship in the kingdom. Said Jesus: “You see, then, that the Father gives salvation to the children of men, and this salvation is a free gift to all who have the faith to receive sonship in the divine family. (150:5.3,5) (167:5.1) (170:2.21) (193:1.2) (193:2.2)
improvedDescription: The affectionate dedication of human will to doing the Father's will is humanity's choicest gift to God and the only gift of true value to the Paradise Father. Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father of lights, and salvation itself is the free gift of God to all who have faith to receive sonship in the divine family.

name: Gilboa
originalDescription: Philistines defeated Saul at Mount Gilboa. King Saul took his life there.  (97:9.6) (97:9.13) (124:6.3) (134:9.5) Mary and Joseph camped on the slopes of Mount Gilboa on their way to Bethlehem to register (122:7.5) This mount was visible from the hill near the house of Jesus in Nazareth (123:5.13) Jesus passed by with his parents when they first took him to Jerusalem (124:6.1) (124:6.4) Between the months of September and October 27 Jesus and the apostles established their camp on Mount Gilboa (144:1) on October 27 the apostles of Jesus held a conference with the apostles of John on Mount Gilboa (144:6) Matthew and Philip were expelled from a Samaritan village on the slopes of Mount Gilboa on their way to the Feast of Tabernacles in the year 29 (162:0.1)
improvedDescription: A mountain where the Philistines defeated King Saul, who took his own life on its slopes. Located near Nazareth and visible from Jesus' childhood home, Mount Gilboa was a significant landmark where Jesus and his apostles established camps and held conferences, and where Mary and Joseph camped on their journey to Bethlehem for the census registration.

name: Gilead
originalDescription: One of the towns visited in the mission of Perea, from January 30 until the end of the life of Jesus (165:0.1)
improvedDescription: One of the cities in Perea where the seventy messengers worked during Jesus' final ministry from January 30 until the end of his earthly life.

name: Gileadites
originalDescription: Gileadites were a reputed biblical tribe (128:1.14) Pretentious Hebrew history begins with Saul’s rallying the northern clans to withstand an attack by the Ammonites upon their fellow tribesmen—the Gileadites—east of the Jordan. With an army of a little more than three thousand he defeated the enemy, and it was this exploit that led the hill tribes to make him king. (97:9.3)
improvedDescription: A biblical tribe that traditionally dwelt east of the Jordan River. King Saul rallied the northern clans to defend the Gileadites against an attack by the Ammonites, and his victory with an army of just over three thousand led the hill tribes to make him king.

name: giraffes
originalDescription: 10 million years ago, giraffes appeared in Africa with necks as long as they have today. (61:4.4)
improvedDescription: Large African animals that appeared about 10 million years ago with necks as long as they have today, during a period when the cat family dominated animal life.

name: girdle
originalDescription: Jesus wore a girdle (187:2.8) Judas hung himself with his girdle (186:1.7)
improvedDescription: A belt-like garment worn around the waist. Jesus wore a girdle which was taken by Roman soldiers during his crucifixion, and Judas used his girdle to hang himself after betraying Jesus.

name: Gischala
originalDescription: Place near the Sea of Galilee The apostles preached there on their first preaching tour of the lake between June and July 26 (138:9.3) It is one of the towns visited during the first preaching tour of Galilee between January and March 28 (146:0.1) (146:4.6) Jesus and the apostles passed by on their return from Phoenicia (156:6.3)
improvedDescription: A town near the Sea of Galilee where Jesus and his apostles preached during their various ministry tours, including their first preaching tour around the lake and their first tour of Galilee, as well as on their return journey from Phoenicia.

name: glaciers
originalDescription: the first regional glaciers appeared in the Permian, especially in the southern hemisphere (59:6.6) There are strong discrepancies in reckoning of the number of glaciers between The Urantia Book and current science. The time of the third glacier according to the book corresponds to the first according to the reckoning of geologists (64:1.4) 750,000 years ago the Siberian glacier was making its southernmost march, compelling early man to move southward, back toward the lands of his origin. (64:4.5) land elevation produces glaciers (57:8.22) (61:2.3) (61:5.1-2) No other terrestrial activity apart from glaciers leaves such characteristic evidence on the topography. (61:7.1)
improvedDescription: Massive ice formations that have significantly shaped Earth's topography throughout geological history. Glaciers first appeared regionally during the Permian period, especially in the southern hemisphere, and have continued to influence global climate and human migration patterns. These ice formations leave distinctive geological evidence including boulders, surface cleavages, potholes, lakes, and various types of moraines. The Urantia Book describes discrepancies between its glacier chronology and conventional geological dating, and notes that glacial activity has been a major factor in early human migrations, such as when the southward advance of Siberian glaciers forced early humans to migrate back toward their origins.

name: glands, ductless
originalDescription: There exist three human planetary types in inhabited worlds with one, two and three glands comparable to the pituitary bodies (49:5.19-20) At any moment of time, in the blood stream of any human being there exists the possibility of upward of 15 millions chemical reactions between the hormone output of a dozen ductless glands. (65:6.6) gland chemistry of those glands comparable to the pituitary bodies influences imagination and spiritual receptivity (49:5.19)
improvedDescription: Endocrine glands that secrete hormones directly into the bloodstream without ducts. These glands, particularly those comparable to the pituitary body, play a crucial role in spiritual receptivity and imagination across different human planetary types. Inhabited worlds contain mortals with one, two, or three such glands, with Urantians having two. The hormone output from about a dozen ductless glands can produce upward of 15 million potential chemical reactions in the human bloodstream at any given time.

name: Glantonia
originalDescription: Glantonia is a Norlatiadek system immediately neighboring Satania (41:2.1)
improvedDescription: A local system in the constellation of Norlatiadek that serves as one of Satania's immediate neighbors. Glantonia is one of the one hundred local systems that comprise the administrative organization of Norlatiadek, alongside other neighboring systems including Sandmatia, Assuntia, Porogia, Sortoria, and Rantulia.

name: glory
originalDescription: The Second Isaiah proclaimed that man was very closely related to God, saying: “Every one who is called by my name I have created for my glory, and they shall show forth my praise. I, even I, am he who blots out their transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember their sins.” (97:7.10) Jesus sought not his own glory but the glory of God (162:7.4-5) Some are tempted to love the glory of men more than the glory of God. (174:5.3) Said Jesus: “If any man really desires to do my Father’s will, he shall certainly know about my teaching, whether it be God’s or whether I speak for myself. He who speaks for himself seeks his own glory, but when I declare the words of the Father, I thereby seek the glory of him who sent me. Seek in your daily lives, not self-glorification, but the glory of God.” (162:2.1) (175:1.10)
improvedDescription: The manifestation of divine majesty, honor, and spiritual radiance associated with God and divine beings. Glory represents the visible expression of divine nature and purpose, as seen in Jesus's teachings about seeking God's glory rather than personal glorification. True glory comes from serving the Father's will and revealing divine truth, while self-glorification is contrasted with the authentic glory that flows from divine partnership and spiritual service.

name: gnats
originalDescription: Jesus said once: “My people take themselves too seriously; they are just about devoid of an appreciation of humor. The burdensome religion of the Pharisees could never have had origin among a people with a sense of humor. They also lack consistency; they strain at gnats and swallow camels.” (156:2.8) (185:0.4) And also: “Woe upon you, scribes and Pharisees and all other hypocrites who make sure that they tithe mint, anise, and cumin and at the same time disregard the weightier matters of the law—faith, mercy, and judgment! Within reason, the one you ought to have done but not to have left the other undone. You are truly blind guides and dumb teachers; you strain out the gnat and swallow the camel.” (175:1.17)
improvedDescription: Small flying insects used metaphorically by Jesus to criticize the Pharisees' tendency to focus on trivial religious details while ignoring weightier spiritual matters. The phrase "strain out the gnat and swallow the camel" illustrates the inconsistency of being scrupulous about minor ceremonial requirements while neglecting fundamental principles of faith, mercy, and judgment.

name: goals
originalDescription: achieving goals value of goals supreme goals
improvedDescription: Objectives or purposes that provide direction and meaning to existence across all levels of reality. Goals serve as essential motivators for progress, from material achievements to spiritual attainment, with the ultimate goal being the finding of God and achieving divine perfection. True goals should be spiritual rather than purely material, and the achievement of worthy goals brings satisfaction while serving as stepping stones to higher aspirations. The goal of human existence is described as attuning to the divinity of the indwelling Adjuster and achieving God-consciousness.

name: goats
originalDescription: goats were domesticated by 5000 B.C. thanks to Mesopotamian, Turkestan, and Chinese farmers (81:2.12) The first medium of exchange was a fish or a goat. (69:4.5) The broad-headed Nodite-Andonite Syrians very early introduced pottery and agriculture in connection with their settlements on the slowly rising Nile delta. They also imported sheep, goats, cattle, and other domesticated animals (80:1.2)
improvedDescription: Domesticated animals that were among the first livestock raised by early farmers, particularly in Mesopotamia, Turkestan, and China by 5000 B.C. Goats served as an early medium of exchange in primitive commerce and were introduced to various regions including the Nile delta by Nodite-Andonite peoples along with sheep, cattle, and other domesticated animals.

name: Gobi
originalDescription: Gobi was a an open grassland in Andite days (79:1.2)
improvedDescription: A region that was an open grassland during the Andite period, supporting herders who were gradually transitioning to agriculture. Located east of the fertile Tarim valley, the Gobi was part of a thriving civilization that eventually perished when rain patterns shifted to the southeast.

name: God
