(44:3.3) Ancient man did not have the modern notion of working within a time limit, only hurrying in the face of danger. (69:2.4) Ganid shared a Hebrew proverb with his disgruntled fellow countryman in Antioch, encouraging him to approach his work “with all one's might.” (133:8.3) The work of your status sphere, in this world or the next, takes precedence over self-importance, as exhausting the self diminishes energy for important tasks. (48:6.37) The way you do the work is more important than the work itself in the chain of mortal life across universes. (39:4.13)
improvedDescription: Physical and mental work that distinguishes humans from animals and represents one of humanity's paramount blessings. Labor should be approached with dignity and purpose, as even the most commonplace toil can be elevated to sacred service when performed with the right attitude. The division and specialization of labor has greatly advanced civilization, though society must ensure meaningful employment for all able-bodied citizens.

name: laborers
originalDescription: Come to Jesus, all you who labor, and find rest for your souls. (139:7.7) (141:3.7) (144:8.8) (163:6.7) (165:3.8) The parable of laborers in the vineyard illustrates the generous and fair nature of the kingdom of heaven. (163:3.5-7) Pray always for more laborers for the kingdom, ready to say, “Here am I; send me,” through consecrated devotion and daily worship. (163:4.10) The laborer is worthy of his hire as they go forth proclaiming peace on earth and good will among men. (140:9.3) (163:1.3)
improvedDescription: Workers who are invited by Jesus to find spiritual rest and strength in their service. The parable of laborers in the vineyard illustrates the generous nature of God's kingdom, while believers are encouraged to pray for more laborers to spread the gospel message, with the assurance that faithful workers are worthy of their hire.

name: ladder
originalDescription: The spirit personalities of the Divine and Infinite Spirit serve as a living ladder for mortals, guiding them from chaos to glory through love and mercy. (9:8.25)
improvedDescription: A symbolic representation of how spirit personalities of the Divine and Infinite Spirit serve as a living pathway for mortal beings, providing guidance and ministry that helps humans ascend from chaos to glory through love and mercy.

name: Lagash
originalDescription: The capital of Sumeria, Lagash, fell to northern Suites and Guites around 2500 B.C., leading to the absorption of Sumerians into the ranks of northern Semites. (78:8.10)
improvedDescription: The capital city of ancient Sumeria, built on flood mounds, which fell to northern Suites and Guites around 2500 B.C. Its conquest marked the beginning of the end for Sumerian civilization, as the Sumerians became absorbed into the ranks of the northern Semites.

name: Lake Superior
originalDescription: The ancient rocks around Lake Superior, dating back to the Proterozoic era, contain primitive fossil-bearing layers that testify to the upheavals and surface fluctuations of those times, with well-preserved examples of early marine life such as algae, coral-like plants, and protozoans. (58:7.5)
improvedDescription: A large lake in North America where ancient Proterozoic rocks are well-exposed and easily interpreted, containing primitive fossil-bearing layers that testify to early geological upheavals and surface fluctuations, with well-preserved examples of early marine life such as algae, coral-like plants, and protozoans.

name: Lake Urmia
improvedDescription: A lake mentioned in The Urantia Book, referenced in connection with the region of Urmia.

name: Lake Van
originalDescription: The descendants of Adamson, gathered around Lake Van, eagerly absorbed the teachings of the Salem gospel from Hittite teachers, spreading their message to distant lands. (93:7.2) Followers of Van settled at Lake Van, where Ararat became their sacred mountain and their moral law of seven commandments was given by the Gods. (77:4.11) Nodites mingled with Amadonites at Lake Van, leaving behind able progeny who joined Van and his loyal followers in lands north of Mesopotamia. (73:1.6)
improvedDescription: A lake in the region north of Mesopotamia where followers of Van settled after the Lucifer rebellion. The lake became the center of Vanite civilization, with nearby Mount Ararat serving as their sacred mountain where they believed their moral law of seven commandments was given by the Gods. Descendants of Adamson clustered around its shores and later spread Salem teachings throughout Europe and Asia, while Nodites mingled with Amadonites in the vicinity.

name: lakes
originalDescription: lakes in heavens lakes in the Earth
improvedDescription: Bodies of water found on various worlds including Urantia, Edentia, and Jerusem. On the architectural worlds, there are tens of thousands of sparkling lakes interconnected by streams but no great oceans, while on Urantia, glacial activity created many lakes through distinctive geological processes.

name: lambs
originalDescription: Jesus celebrated Passover without the paschal lamb, emphasizing the importance of spiritual truth over traditional rituals. (127:6.6-7) Jesus told Peter to feed his lambs, minister to the weak, the poor, and the young, preach the gospel fearlessly, serve others, forgive freely, and value meditation and reflection. (192:2.2) The apostles were accustomed to Jesus celebrating Passover without the lamb, as he did not participate in the sacrificial services of the Jewish system. (179:0.3) The lamb became a typical sacrificial animal in early evolutionary religion, representing a forgotten god or vanished cult. (85:3.4)
improvedDescription: Young sheep that became typical sacrificial animals in early evolutionary religion and were used in Jewish Passover ceremonies. Jesus celebrated Passover without the paschal lamb, emphasizing spiritual truth over ritual sacrifice, and instructed Peter to "feed my lambs," meaning to minister to the weak, poor, and young.

name: lame
originalDescription: “Sometimes invite the lame to banquets, for in humbling yourself to help others, you will be exalted.” (167:1.5)
improvedDescription: Those who are physically disabled or unable to walk properly. Jesus taught that when giving banquets, one should sometimes invite the lame along with the poor and blind, as this demonstrates humble service to others who cannot repay such kindness.

name: Lamech
originalDescription: Lamech's exultation over his son's metal weapons led to the illustration of mercy and forbearance derived from the Scriptures. (159:1.7)
improvedDescription: A biblical figure whose exultation over his son Tubal-Cain's metal weapons became the scriptural basis for Jesus' teaching about forgiveness "seventy-seven" times, illustrating mercy and forbearance.

name: lamp
originalDescription: The eye is the lamp of the body, let it shine with generosity and light, not darkness. (140:6.12) Let your light shine on a stand where all can behold. (151:3.1)
improvedDescription: A source of light used metaphorically by Jesus to teach spiritual truths. He taught that the eye is the lamp of the body, and that one's spiritual light should not be hidden but placed on a stand where all can see it, emphasizing the importance of letting one's light shine with wisdom and discretion.

name: Lanaforge
originalDescription: Lanaforge, a frequent visitor on Urantia, discloses great interest in the welfare of the isolated worlds of Satania. (45:2.4) Lanaforge gave permission for Paper 72 to advance civilization and augment governmental evolution on Urantia. (72:0.1) (72:12.3) Installed as System Sovereign 10 years after Lucifer rebellion, Lanaforge is a tested administrator, ruling Satania wisely and graciously. (45:2.2-4) (45:3.2) (53:7.12,13) (93:3.2) (114:2.4) Melchizedek introduced the idea of one God, El Elyon, but refrained from discussing Lucifer, leaving Lanaforge uninvolved with Urantia until Michael's bestowal. (93:3.2) Lanaforge, the only Lanonandek Son in Nebadon who remained loyal during the default of a superior, is a tested and capable leader in Satania. (45:2.3) Lanaforge ordered a special roll call of distinguished survivors of the Adamic default for a resurrection mandate on Urantia. (76:6.2) Lanaforge is the primary Lanonandek #2709. (45:3.2) Lanaforge, with the approval of the Most Highs of Edentia, selected Adam and Eve for their mission of biologic uplift on Urantia. (74:1.3-4)
improvedDescription: The current System Sovereign of Satania, primary Lanonandek Son #2709, who succeeded the fallen Lucifer. He is the only primary Lanonandek Son in Nebadon who remained loyal when his superior fell into rebellion. Tested and proven as a capable administrator, he frequently visits Urantia and takes great interest in the isolated worlds of Satania, having given permission for various revelations and selected Adam and Eve for their mission on Urantia.

name: land
originalDescription: The basic struggle of man is for land, a fundamental truth reflected in the very essence of human existence. (68:6.1) Land became a source of income and capital, with the state assigning property to individuals and landlords securing titles to collect rents. (69:9.15) Family crests and private property originated from priests consecrating land under protection of taboos, showing great respect for family landmarks. (69:9.13) Land is the stage of society; men are the actors, requiring adjustment to the land situation through maintenance arts to evolve the mores and cultural civilization. (68:5) Man's cultural civilization is shaped by his adjustment to the land, reflecting the evolving mores influenced by the land-man ratio. (68:5.1) Land tax limits in the most advanced nation on an inhabited planet near Urantia vary widely, with a maximum of one percent of property value allowed per year, with homesites exempt. (72:7.6) War is always caused by scarcity of land, leading to hunger, food raids, and the near extermination of peaceful tribes. (70:1.8) land-man ratio
improvedDescription: The foundation and stage upon which human civilization develops. The basic struggle of humanity has always been for land, as the land-man ratio underlies all social progress. Land serves as both the source of sustenance through agriculture and the basis for property rights, taxation, and economic systems, with its scarcity or abundance directly affecting population dynamics, warfare, and cultural development.

name: land bridges
originalDescription: 85 million years ago the Bering Strait closed, unifying the marine life of the Atlantic-Gulf waters and the Pacific Ocean. (60:3.8) (61:0.2) (61:1.12) (61:3.4) (61:4.6) (62:1.2-3) (64:6.5) (79:5.6) During these land elevations, the disappearance of certain land bridges drastically altered the connectivity between continents. (59:6.5) During the ice age, a land connection between England and France formed through the English Channel. (64:1.5-6) (64:2.6) The Gibraltar isthmus once connected Spain and Africa, forming a land bridge before geological shifts led to the creation of the Mediterranean and the Atlantic Ocean as separate bodies of water. (61:3.8) (80:1.1) (80:2.4) The Iceland land bridge submerged, isolating the Western Hemisphere as distinct life forms began to develop. (61:3.9) (61:4.6) Descendants of Andon and Fonta migrated to Java 950,000 years ago. (64:1.6) The land bridge connected the Mediterranean to the Indian Ocean, leading colored races to settle in Africa. (64:7.13) During the Cenozoic age, the Panama isthmus and Bering Strait land bridge rose and fell, connecting diverse animal types in an animal paradise amidst evolving species struggles. (61:0.2) (61:1.12) During the ice age, a Sicilian land bridge connected Africa to Europe, bringing in new animals that virtually exterminated others. (64:1.5) (64:4.7) (73:7.1) (80:1.1) (80:2.4)
improvedDescription: Geological formations that temporarily connected continents and islands throughout Earth's history, allowing migration of animal species and early human populations. Major land bridges included the Bering Strait connection between Asia and North America, the Gibraltar isthmus linking Spain and Africa, and the Sicilian bridge connecting Europe to Africa. These formations rose and fell with sea level changes and geological activity, profoundly influencing the distribution of life forms and the spread of human races across the globe.

name: land-life era, early
originalDescription: The Mesozoic was the era of the early land-life era, extending over the next one hundred million years. (59:0.5)
improvedDescription: The early phase of land-based life on Earth, also known as the Mesozoic era, extending over 100 million years and characterized by the evolution and dominance of early terrestrial organisms.

name: language
originalDescription: development of language one common universal language nature of language limitations of language Jesus and the language revelators of Urantia Papers celestial languages languages mentioned in The Urantia Book
improvedDescription: The system of communication that evolved from simple gestures and sounds to complex verbal and written forms, serving as humanity's greatest thinking tool. Language developed through group associations, with each local community creating its own word exchange system. It enables humans to communicate concepts, ideas, and spiritual meanings, distinguishing them from animals and facilitating the building of civilizations through the transmission of knowledge across generations.

name: Lanonandek Sons
originalDescription: Lanonandek Sons assist Planetary Princes in their autocratic rule over evolutionary planets. (50:2.3) Lanonandek Sons are best known as System Sovereigns and Planetary Princes in the universe. (35:8.1) Lanonandek Sons are brought into being by a Creator Son and Mother Spirit. (20:1.5-10) (35:8.1) Lanonandek Sons do not progress between groups or engage in reproduction, serving continuously in their assigned ranks. (35:8.7) The Lanonandek Sons in charge of 3rd Salvington circuit are experienced administrators of the ascending pilgrims and seraphic hosts, overseeing the co-ordination of administrative pronouncements within the Lanonandek cluster. (35:10) Lanonandek Sons embody unique qualities in personality registers. (30:1.37) (30:2.44) Lanonandek Sons are the most versatile local universe administrators, excelling in executive ability only second to Gabriel and his associates. (35:8.15) Nebadon's Lanonandek Sons have caused administrative trouble due to their large degree of personal liberty, but in the end, their loyalty and volitional service will outweigh the challenges faced in earlier ages. (35:9.8) On constellation headquarters, reserves of Lanonandek Sons are selected by the supreme council to fill vacancies left by recalled System Sovereigns or assistants. (35:9.3-4) The Lanonandek Sons find residence in Jerusem circles, where System Sovereigns, Planetary Princes, and system reserves come together as one. (46:5.16) The stationary number of 12 million Lanonandek Sons in Nebadon remain fixed in their assigned ranks after being divided into three classes through final tests in the Melchizedek sphere. (35:8.3,7) Lanonandek Sons were trained by Melchizedeks at the Melchizedek University before their subsequent service. (35:8.2) rebellion amongst Lanonandek Sons types
improvedDescription: The third order of Local Universe Sons created by the Creator Son and Universe Mother Spirit, best known as System Sovereigns who rule local systems and Planetary Princes who administer inhabited worlds. These versatile administrators are created with significant personal liberty in choosing and planning, making them excellent executives but also susceptible to rebellion. Nebadon contains exactly 12 million Lanonandek Sons divided into three classes: primary, secondary, and tertiary orders.

name: Lao-tse
originalDescription: Lao-tse declared Tao to be the One First Cause and taught the eternal destiny of man's union with Supreme God and Universal King. (92:5.12) (94:6.3-8) Lao-tse, an outstanding religious teacher, was eventually deified by his followers during a spiritually dark age in China. (94:6.12) (121:6.4)
improvedDescription: A Chinese spiritual teacher who built upon Salem traditions by declaring Tao to be the One First Cause of all creation. He taught that humanity's eternal destiny was everlasting union with Tao, the Supreme God and Universal King, and made early presentations of returning good for evil. Though later deified by his followers, his original teachings emphasized faith in the Absolute God as the source of divine energy for spiritual transformation.

name: Laotta
originalDescription: Adam deliberately impregnated Laotta, the brilliant Nodite woman, out of love for Eve and a fear of being alone on Urantia. (75:5.2) Before reaching Mesopotamia, Laotta perished giving birth to Sansa, who grew up to become a woman of great ability and married Sargan, chief of the northern blue races. (76:0.2)
improvedDescription: A brilliant Nodite woman who served as head of the western schools in the Garden of Eden. After Eve's default, Adam deliberately chose to unite with Laotta rather than face a lonely existence on Urantia without Eve. Laotta died giving birth to her daughter Sansa, who was raised by Eve alongside Cain and later became the wife of Sargan, chief of the northern blue races.

name: Lapland
originalDescription: The Lapps and Eskimos in Lapland are a blend of Andonite and blue races, preserving the aboriginal Andonic type amid Sangik hereditary dominance. (81:4.9) Older Andonites retained semblance of identity in Lapland as superior blue strains were absorbed. (80:4.6)
improvedDescription: A region where the Lapps, along with Eskimos, represent a blend of Andonite and blue Sangik races. Their skeletal structures preserve the closest resemblance to the original Andonic type, and Lapland is one of the few places where older Andonites retained some semblance of their original identity after extensive racial mixing in northern Europe.

name: Larissa
originalDescription: A town through which Jesus passed during his journey with Ganid and Gonod, located in Mesopotamia (130:0.3)
improvedDescription: A town in Mesopotamia through which Jesus, Ganid, and Gonod passed during their extensive journey, traveling by caravan from Damascus through Thapsacus and Larissa before reaching Babylon.

name: Lasea
originalDescription: Jesus passed through Lasea, a harbor in Crete, on his journey from Jerusalem to Rome. (130:0.2) (130:4.12)
improvedDescription: A harbor town on the island of Crete where Jesus stopped during his journey from Jerusalem to Rome with Ganid and Gonod, sailing there from Alexandria before continuing to Carthage.

name: last
originalDescription: Sometimes the last shall often be first in the kingdom of God, where the least can become the greatest in eternal salvation. (41:10.5) (163:3.4) (166:3.5)
improvedDescription: A spiritual principle taught by Jesus indicating that in God's kingdom, those who appear least significant may become the greatest in eternal salvation, while those who seem first may find themselves last. This reflects the Father's merciful consideration for all creatures according to their spiritual needs rather than worldly status.

name: Last Supper
improvedDescription: The final meal Jesus shared with his apostles before his crucifixion, also known as the Remembrance Supper.

name: Latin
originalDescription: Christianity brought liberating comfort to a spiritually hungry Rome lacking a word for unselfishness amidst Stoic teachings and mystery cult promises. (195:3.3)
improvedDescription: The Roman language that lacked a word for 'unselfishness,' making Christianity's message of spiritual comfort and liberating power particularly appealing to the spiritually hungry Roman people.

name: Lausanne
originalDescription: Pilate's downfall was precipitated by his betrayal of Jesus and the disastrous events involving a false messiah in Samaria, leading to his exile to Lausanne and eventual suicide. (185:1.6)
improvedDescription: A province where Pontius Pilate retired in exile after being deposed as procurator of Judea, and where he subsequently committed suicide, never having recovered from his regretful condemnation of consenting to Jesus' crucifixion.

name: lava
improvedDescription: Molten rock that flows from volcanic eruptions and hardens into solid rock formations.

name: law
originalDescription: historically divine law law if the ideal state
improvedDescription: A codified record of long human experience representing public opinion crystallized and legalized, evolving from negative prohibitions in primitive society to positive and directive principles in advancing civilizations. All law takes origin in the First Source and Center, with spiritual law administered by the Second Source and Center, while the revelation and interpretation of divine statutes is the function of the Third Source and Center.

name: law and the prophets
originalDescription: Jesus came to fulfill the law and the prophets, not to set them aside. (140:6.2)
improvedDescription: The traditional Jewish scriptures and teachings that Jesus came to fulfill, enlarge, and illuminate rather than to destroy or set aside.

name: law forecasters
originalDescription: Law forecasters speak on behalf of those unable to advocate for themselves before celestial lawmakers in order to ensure the fair and just evolution of universe law. (39:3.3) Supervisor seraphim forecast the effects of proposed laws for the greater coordination of the constellation while respecting free will. (39:3.3)
improvedDescription: Second-order supervisor seraphim who forecast how proposed constellation laws would affect the lives of freewill creatures, serving before celestial lawmakers to speak for those who cannot be present to advocate for themselves and ensuring the fair evolution of universe law while respecting moral free will.

name: lawyers
originalDescription: Lawyers have taken the key of knowledge from common people, hindering others from entering the way of truth and closing the doors of the kingdom of heaven. (166:1.5) The lawyer desired to know the greatest commandment, and Jesus answered with love for God and neighbor as foundational. (174:4.2-4) Romans were lawyers by nature and training, discerning in the laws of nature the laws of God, making them ideal for embracing Christianity and governing themselves. (195:2.4-6)
improvedDescription: Legal experts among the Jews who, according to Jesus, had taken away the key of knowledge from common people and hindered others from entering the way of truth. One lawyer who asked Jesus about the greatest commandment received a response emphasizing love for God and neighbor as foundational principles.

name: Lazarus (of Bethany)
originalDescription: Lazarus was one of the best friends of Jesus and a firmly believer is his teachings. Father Simon, a leading resident of Bethany, had left behind a wealthy and respected family, with extensive vineyards and olive orchards, who were comforted by many visitors after his passing. (168:0.4) In June A.D. 27, Jesus and the apostles stored their personal effects at Lazarus' home in Bethany before departing Jerusalem to preach in Samaria. (143:0.1) At Jesus' 10th appearance after his resurrection, he spoke words of love and unity to his followers in Philadelphia. (191:4) David Zebedee was self-appointed defender of Lazarus, joining him in Philadelphia after the persecution following his resurrection and the establishment of the kingdom in Jerusalem. (171:1.5) David became the self-appointed defender of Lazarus, who was persecuted by Pharisees in Bethany before fleeing to Philadelphia. (171:1.5) Jesus instructed David to defend Lazarus, who fled to Philadelphia to avoid persecution. (171:1.5) (172:2.4) (174:0.1) (178:2.2) (178:3.3) Lazarus of Bethany died at 67 as treasurer of the church in Philadelphia, siding with Abner against Paul and the Jerusalem church. (168:5.3) During their first meeting with Jesus in Bethany, Lazarus and the boys were the same age, forming a lifelong friendship. (124:6.9) (127:6.5) The following day, Lazarus took Jesus to explore Jerusalem, where they discovered teaching conferences at the temple that helped relieve the previous day's unpleasant memories. (125:2.5) In March, Jesus and the apostles journeyed to Jerusalem, where Lazarus of Bethany had visited them twice at the Jordan and prepared a place for them to stay in Bethany. (141:9.1) Jesus loved Lazarus with fervent affection, his desire to visit with Martha and Mary next to his own family being a significant part of his decision to go up to Jerusalem for the Passover. (125:2.7) (127:6.3) (167:4.2) Jesus received message about the illness of Lazarus, leading to a profound and divine plan to glorify God and exalt the Son through a stupendous outward work, despite the disciples' fears and doubts. (167:4) The resurrection of Lazarus by Jesus demonstrated his power and brought about both belief and disbelief among the people. (168) Jesus wept at the tomb of Lazarus before commanding for the stone to be rolled away, preparing for the greatest manifestation of divine power during his earthly ministry. (168:1) Martha and Mary joined Lazarus in Philadelphia after David joined them in Bethany. (190:1.9) Lazarus and his sisters, with a private tomb on their own premises, were respected and wealthy followers of Jesus in the village of Bethany. (168:0.4) The Sanhedrin decreed death for both Jesus and Lazarus, in response to their defiance and miraculous deeds. (168:5.1) (172:1.1,9) (175:3.1) Jesus formed a lifelong friendship with Lazarus of Bethany and his sisters Martha and Mary while stopping in their home on the eastern slopes of Olivet. (124:6.9) Lazarus and his sisters were summoned before the Sanhedrin, where despite clear testimony, his resurrection was attributed to the power of the prince of devils. (168:3.4) Lazarus, treasurer of the church at Philadelphia, supported Abner in his dispute with Paul, ultimately dying at the age of 67 from the same sickness that once struck him in Bethany. (168:5.3) Lazarus, a wealthy man who inherited extensive vineyards and olive orchards, was highly respected in Bethany and his family was comforted by many friends from near-by hamlets and Jerusalem. (168:0.4) (172:1.7)
improvedDescription: A wealthy resident of Bethany and one of Jesus' closest friends, along with his sisters Martha and Mary. After being raised from the dead by Jesus, he became treasurer of the church at Philadelphia, supported Abner in disputes with Paul, and eventually died at age 67 from the same sickness that had once afflicted him.

name: Lazarus (of Nazarite parable)
originalDescription: The parable of Lazarus and Dives warned against the dangers of loving riches and coveting dishonest wealth, emphasizing the importance of heeding the teachings of the kingdom of heaven. (169:3)
improvedDescription: A character in a parable about a rich man and a beggar that John the Baptist used to warn against loving riches and coveting dishonest wealth, emphasizing the importance of heeding spiritual teachings.

name: laziness
improvedDescription: A concept of idleness or lack of industrious effort, related to the broader theme of indolence in human behavior and character development.

name: lead
originalDescription: Two atomic weights indicate the existence of pre-electronic forms of lead, with the original formation weighing slightly more than that produced through uranium disintegration. (42:4.12) Early lava flows brought up lead, copper, and iron to the surface, crumpling the earth's oldest fossil-bearing stratum. (58:7.6) Lead is found in Silurian deposits along with gas, oil, and zinc, representing the sedimentation of sluggish bodies of water. (59:3.4) (59:5.9)
improvedDescription: A heavy metal found in various geological deposits on Urantia, including pre-electronic forms from original planetary formation and forms produced through uranium disintegration. Lead appears in ancient fossil-bearing strata brought to the surface by early volcanic activity and in Silurian deposits alongside gas, oil, and zinc from sluggish water sedimentation.

name: leadership
originalDescription: Leadership and material success rely on natural ability, discretion, determination, intelligent judgment, willpower, and seasoned wisdom. (156:5.7) The destiny of religious groups depends on intelligent leadership, as religion involves both personal spiritual experiences and social service. (99:5.1) Success hinges on tact, charm, and the ability to win cooperation from others in both secular and religious endeavors. (160:1.9) Teamwork is dependent upon effective and wise leadership in civilization. (81:6.37) Unfailingly providing for all groups, leadership is essential for order and direction in cooperative efforts. (181:2.16) Leadership is indispensable to progress, as wisdom, insight, and foresight are vital for the endurance of nations. (81:6.42) One percent of the population holds the indispensable wisdom, insight, and foresight necessary for vital leadership, essential for progress and the endurance of nations. (81:6.42)
improvedDescription: The essential quality for guiding groups and civilizations, dependent on natural ability, discretion, determination, and seasoned wisdom. Effective leadership enables cooperation, maintains order, and provides direction for progress, with only about one percent of the population possessing the wisdom, insight, and foresight necessary for vital leadership roles.

name: leadings
originalDescription: Adjuster leadings | submission dangers involved with supposed spirit leadings
improvedDescription: The subtle spiritual guidance provided by Thought Adjusters to mortal minds, distinguished from emotional impulses or mystical experiences. True spiritual leadings require careful discernment, as many supposed divine communications actually originate from the human subconscious rather than superconscious contact with the indwelling spirit.

name: Leagues of Nations
originalDescription: Lasting peace on Urantia requires the full surrender of sovereign powers to a mankind government, as Leagues of Nations cannot prevent war by the most powerful governments. (134:5.10)
improvedDescription: International confederations that can prevent minor conflicts but cannot achieve lasting world peace. Such organizations will fail to control the most powerful governments, as true peace requires nations to surrender their sovereign powers to a unified mankind government.

name: Leah
originalDescription: Leah, daughter of Philip, prophetess of Hierapolis, carried on her parents' evangelical mission with fearless dedication. (139:5.11)
improvedDescription: The eldest daughter of the apostle Philip and his wife, who continued her parents' evangelical work after their martyrdom and later became the renowned prophetess of Hierapolis.

name: leaping
originalDescription: Personality allows one to learn from looking, as well as from leaping, while nonpersonal animals learn only by leaping. (16:7.3)
improvedDescription: A method of learning through direct experience without forethought. While nonpersonal animals learn only by leaping into action, personalities can learn from both looking ahead and leaping, possessing insight that allows them to benefit from observation as well as experience.

name: least
originalDescription: Sometimes the least becomes the greatest when elevated by honor and great interest. (41:10.5)
improvedDescription: A principle demonstrating how those in humble positions can be elevated to greatness. Urantia exemplifies this concept, being among the least significant worlds until Michael's bestowal elevated it to a position of honor and great universe interest.

name: leaven
originalDescription: Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy, and strive for true religion based on living truth and spiritual realities. (157:2.2) (165:3.2) The parable of the leaven illustrates how the kingdom of heaven grows and spreads, transforming everything it touches. (151:4.3) (178:1.15) Place the leaven of new truth in the midst of old beliefs, and do not forget to preach only the good news. (178:1.16)
improvedDescription: A metaphor used by Jesus to describe both the transformative power of the kingdom of heaven and the corrupting influence of hypocrisy. The positive leaven represents how spiritual truth grows and spreads, transforming everything it touches, while the negative leaven of the Pharisees represents hypocrisy that should be avoided.

name: Lebanon, house of
originalDescription: Solomon built the house of Lebanon and established a vast Hebrew navy, trading globally. (97:9.16)
improvedDescription: A magnificent building constructed by King Solomon as part of his elaborate building program, which along with his lavish court and extensive projects, ultimately bankrupted the Hebrew nation despite his creation of a vast trading navy.

name: Lebbeus
originalDescription: Judas Alpheus, also called Lebbeus, was known among disciples as being among the least of all the apostles. (139:10.5)
improvedDescription: Judas Alpheus, also called Lebbeus, was known among disciples as being among the least of all the apostles.

name: Lebonah
originalDescription: Population through which Jesus passed during his journey through Palestine in the year 25 (134:7.5)
improvedDescription: A town through which Jesus passed during his final journey through Palestine in the year 25, traveling northward from Beersheba to Dan.

name: leeches
originalDescription: Leeches, closely related to worms, appeared later in the evolutionary timeline along with starfish, crinoids, sea urchins, centipedes, insects, spiders, and crustaceans, representing primitive and stationary types of animals that did not progress further. (65:2.5)
improvedDescription: Primitive animals closely related to earthworms that appeared in early evolutionary development along with starfish, sea urchins, and other invertebrates, representing nonprogressive specimens that failed to evolve further.

name: legalism
originalDescription: The scribes, Pharisees, and priesthood held the Jews in terrible bondage of ritualism and legalism, a bondage more real than Roman political rule. (121:7.3)
improvedDescription: A system of strict adherence to religious laws and traditions that held the Jews in terrible bondage through ritualism and minute regulations of conduct, creating a more restrictive control than Roman political rule.

name: legislatures
originalDescription: The legislative division of the most advanced nation in a nearby inhabited planet includes an upper house elected by economic function, a lower house elected by societal organizations, and a third house of elder statesmen with advisory power. (72:2.4-7) The evolution from absolute monarchy to legislatures marked great social advance. (70:12.2-3) The legislative assemblies of Nebadon are located on the constellations, governing with concern for justice and coordination. (33:8.1) (39:3.3) (43:2) Man may supply favorable conditions for growth through the mobilization of his total powers of personality, but social and moral growth cannot be secured by legislation or improved administration. (100:3.7) The superuniverse deliberative assembly on Uversa showcases the harmonious collaboration between the autocracy of perfection and the democracy of evolution. (15:11) (28:7.1)
improvedDescription: Representative governing bodies that evolved from primitive councils of elders, including constellation assemblies in Nebadon that codify universe laws and the three-house system of advanced planetary governments. True legislative bodies mark significant social advancement from absolute monarchy toward representative government.

name: leisure
originalDescription: Upon arrival in Havona, beings are accorded a period of leisure for free observation and reconnection with friends and associates from their ascension experience. (30:4.28,32) Accorded mansion world arrivals are granted ten days of personal liberty to explore, familiarize themselves, consult the registry, and visit loved ones before beginning their training on the training spheres. (47:3.6) Angels promote profitable utilization of human leisure by fostering play, humor, and rest. (114:6.15) Civilization can never flourish until man has leisure to think, plan, and imagine new ways of doing things. (78:2.4) (81:2.2,14) (81:6.6) Our early ancestors had little or no leisure to think about society and civilization, unlike us living today in a time of budding culture and social progress. (81:2.2) Language did not function until social groups had acquired leisure, enabling the evolution of dialects and the development of slang into language. (81:6.17) The ideal state encourages profitable and uplifting utilization of leisure, requiring that it must produce as well as consume. (71:3.7) Religion's influence on society, through the leisure of priests promoting art and knowledge, has been crucial in driving humanity towards intellectual progress and wisdom. (92:3.9) Pastoral living allowed for freedom from constant food gathering, leading to increased leisure for cultural and intellectual pursuits. (68:5.7) Leisure is seldom taken advantage of in the tropics, where civilizations tend to prioritize survival over advancement. (81:6.7) Unearned leisure, often perceived as good luck, may actually be the greatest human affliction due to its potential to hinder personal growth and character development. (118:10.9)
improvedDescription: Free time essential for cultural and intellectual progress, allowing humans to think, plan, and imagine new ways of doing things. Civilization cannot flourish without leisure, though unearned leisure can become humanity's greatest affliction if not used constructively.

name: lemurs
originalDescription: The dawn mammals, ancestors of primitive man, suddenly appeared among the offspring of Asiatic migrants of the North American lemur types and evolved into higher mid-mammals and eventually the Primates. (61:6.1) (62:2.1) The appearance of early lemurs in western North America marked the establishment of the line from which the true lemurs later evolved. (61:2.10) (62:1) The Malay tribe teaches descent from lemurs, reflecting ancient beliefs in human origins from animals. (74:8.5) The lemurs migrated over the Bering land bridge, eventually uniting with other strains to establish the ancestry of the human race in southwestern Asia. (62:1.2-3) (65:2.15) Early lemurs, ancestors of humans, were not directly related to gibbons and apes, nor the modern type of lemur, originating from a now-extinct common ancestor. (62:1.1)
improvedDescription: Early mammalian ancestors of humans that evolved in North America and migrated to southwestern Asia via the Bering land bridge. These dawn mammals, not directly related to modern lemurs or apes, established the ancestral line leading to the human species.

name: lepers
originalDescription: Lepers were forbidden to attend synagogue, but Jesus healed a man of leprosy out of compassion and faith. (146:4.3) Jesus performed healing at Iron on a leper, demonstrating compassion and power. (146:4.3-5) Jesus healed the ten lepers, but only the Samaritan returned to give thanks. (166:2)
improvedDescription: Individuals afflicted with leprosy who were forbidden to attend synagogue or engage in public worship. Jesus healed several lepers during his ministry, including one at Iron and ten near Amathus, demonstrating compassion for the socially outcast.

name: Lesser Road
originalDescription: The Hinayana division of Buddhism in the south clings to the early or asocial doctrine. (94:9.4-5)
improvedDescription: The Hinayana division of Buddhism that persisted in southern regions like Ceylon, Burma, and Indo-China, clinging to the early asocial doctrine of Siddhartha in contrast to the Mahayana or "Great Road" of northern Buddhism.

name: Levant
originalDescription: In the Levant, Caucasoid and Negroid have intermingled to create a diverse human population. (81:4.14) Just as India gave birth to many eastern religions, the Levant was the cradle of Occidental faiths. (95:0.1)
improvedDescription: A region where Caucasoid and Negroid peoples have intermingled to create diverse human populations, and the homeland of Occidental faiths just as India was the birthplace of eastern religions.

name: Levites
originalDescription: Levites officiated in the Jerusalem temple during the feast of feasts, combining vacation pleasures with solemn religious worship, including sacrifices, chants, and trumpet blasts. (162:4.2-4) The priests and Levites sent a delegation to John the Baptist to inquire if he claimed to be the Messiah and by whose authority he preached, to which John responded with a quote from the prophet Isaiah. (135:6.6)
improvedDescription: A hereditary priestly class in ancient Judaism who officiated in religious ceremonies at the Jerusalem temple, performing sacrifices, chanting, and playing trumpets during festivals. The priests and Levites sent a delegation to John the Baptist to inquire about his authority and whether he claimed to be the Messiah.

name: liaison stabilizers
originalDescription: Liaison stabilizers are identified in the Uversa personality register. (30:2.113) Liaison stabilizers slow morontia energy revolutions to enable physicalization of morontia material, crucial for the organization of existence beyond human imagination. (48:2.20)
improvedDescription: Morontia energy regulators that slow down energy revolutions to enable the physicalization of morontia material. They make possible the conversion of morontia energy into morontia material, and the entire morontia organization of existence depends on their stabilization work.

name: liberty
originalDescription: defined as attainment restraining liberty false liberty evolution
improvedDescription: True liberty is defined as the progressive attainment of freedom that is related to reality and regardful of social equity, cosmic fairness, and divine obligations. It differs from false liberty, which is license without restraint, and requires the conquest of self and intelligent self-control to be genuine.

name: libraries
originalDescription: Alexandria’s library, the greatest in the world, housed nearly a million manuscripts from all the civilized world. (130:3.4) The custodians of knowledge, living records of truth, are divine libraries in the minds of supernaphim in Havona. (25:5.1) (27:5.1) While in Rome, Ganid immersed himself in study and explored the city's treasures, including the Greek and Latin libraries, as his father prepared him for a future in managing their vast commercial interests. (132:0.2) The “house of Fad in Dalamatia” was a vast library of knowledge organized and directed by Fad, the presiding figure in dissemination and conservation of knowledge during those early ages. (66:5.9)
improvedDescription: Collections of knowledge and manuscripts, including Alexandria's great library with nearly a million manuscripts from the civilized world, and the Dalamatia library known as the "house of Fad" which contained over two million records. The custodians of knowledge in Havona serve as divine living libraries, storing perfect records of truth in their minds.

name: Libya
originalDescription: During the war against Egypt, Moses bravely seized the opportunity for liberation, leading his people in a successful night escape from their oppressors. (96:3.5)
improvedDescription: A North African region that launched a military thrust against Egypt, creating an opportunity that Moses seized to lead the Hebrew exodus during a time when Egyptian forces were occupied fighting on multiple fronts.

name: license
originalDescription: To enjoy liberty without license is a mark of high civilization. (48:7.8) (89:3.7) Unmitigated selfishness arises when unbridled self-will and unregulated self-expression are mistaken for liberty, but in truth, they lead to the forerunner of abject bondage known as license. (54:1.5)
improvedDescription: False liberty that masquerades as freedom but represents unbridled self-will and unregulated self-expression, leading to unmitigated selfishness. License is the forerunner of abject bondage and stands in contrast to true liberty, which requires self-control and restraint.

name: Liege
originalDescription: Liege in Belgium was the most northerly Danubian settlement, where their pottery skills thrived before their tribes deteriorated away from their cultural center. (80:8.4)
improvedDescription: The most northerly settlement of the Danubian peoples in Belgium, where these Andonite farmers and herders practiced pottery-making and agriculture before their tribes deteriorated as they moved away from their cultural center.

name: life
originalDescription: biological existence mortal life
improvedDescription: A phenomenon that represents the animation of pattern-configured energy systems, bestowed through Life Carriers but originating from the Universe Mother Spirit. Life is both mechanistic and vitalistic, involving material and spiritual elements, and serves as the foundation for mind development and spiritual growth in evolutionary creatures.

name: Life Carrier worlds
originalDescription: The Melchizedeks oversee the Life Carrier worlds in the 4th Salvington circuit, which consist of seven primary spheres, each with specific purposes and characteristics, surrounded by satellites focused on different aspects of life and evolution. (36:2) The central emplacement of adjutant mind-spirits on World Number One is devoted to the study of universal life in all of its known phases of manifestation. (36:2.10)
improvedDescription: Seven primary spheres in the fourth Salvington circuit overseen by the Melchizedeks, dedicated to the study and development of life. World Number One serves as headquarters and houses the central emplacement of the adjutant mind-spirits, while the other worlds focus on various aspects of life conservation, evolution, and planning.

name: Life Carriers
originalDescription: Life Carriers are segregated into three divisions: senior, assistants, and custodians, each overseen by the Melchizedeks during the establishment of life on new planets. (36:1.3) The archangel commission modifies Life Carriers for service in life implantation on planets. (65:1.6-8) In the long future ahead, ascending mortals sometimes attached to Life Carriers will have ample opportunities to offer suggestions and improvements in life management and transplantation. (65:3.7) With the help of adjutant mind-spirits and Master Physical Controllers, the Life Carriers concluded their supervision of the planet, as evolution brought about the power of choosing to worship and ascend among its inhabitants. (62:7.6) Life on planets is initiated by Life Carriers, who install a Planetary Prince before departing. (49:5.22) Before Lucifer's rule in Satania, Caligastia served as an adviser to the Life Carriers on Jerusem before he was promoted to Lucifer's personal staff where he excelled in five important roles. (66:1.2) Life Carriers are created by Creator Son, Mother Spirit, and 1 Ancient of Days. (20:1.10) (36:1.1) Life Carriers do not interfere with the will of moral creatures once they have been endowed with the power of moral decision and spiritual choice. (36:3.8) (62:5.9) (62:7.4) Although spornagia do not possess survival souls or personality, they evolve individuality that can experience reincarnation, with Life Carriers fabricating new bodies for them as their physical forms deteriorate. (46:7.4,6) The Life Carriers play a crucial role in the resurrection of the dead within the central resurrection hall of the first mansion world. (47:3.2) Life Carriers function on electrochemical, morontial, and semispirit levels. (65:1.2-5) The arrival of a Life Carrier signifies the second stage of light and life, as they advise planetary rulers on further efforts to purify and stabilize the mortal race, actively participating in the evolution of humanity on all levels and guiding the elimination of inferior potential. (55:4.11) Midway creatures are released by the System Sovereign to humanize in the morontia with the help of Life Carriers, physical controllers, and Thought Adjusters, beginning their journey towards Paradise ascension. (55:4.9) The red race receives the full bestowal of living energies from the Life Carriers, as subsequent races experience a decrease in stature and vitality. (51:4.2) Life Carriers in charge of the fourth Salvington circuit oversee the evolution and coordination of life on seven primary spheres and their satellites. (36:2) Life Carrier Sons register in personality as carriers of life. (30:1.40) (30:2.45) they institute new patterns on decimal planets. See link about decimal or experimental planets | decimal planets The Life Carriers are local universe Sons of God. (20:1.9) Life Carriers manipulate the life environment before emergence of human creatures with moral status, guiding biologic evolution until the appearance of free will. (36:3.7-8) (65:0.6) (65:1.8) (65:2.13-15) The Master Spirits guide the life-initiation procedures of the local universe Life Carriers. (16:4.9) Melchizedeks conducted tests to grade Life Carriers into senior, assistant, and custodian divisions, always accompanying them in their mission to establish life on new planets. (36:1.3) Midsonite creatures, offspring of Melchizedeks serving as Life Carriers, dwell on specific worlds in local systems throughout Nebadon, with a mysterious purpose yet to be revealed. (36:4) Life Carriers and Melchizedek Sons both possess a metamorphic range of personality function, enabling them to excel in diverse and challenging situations. (93:0.1) The Life Carriers, as mid-phase Sons, possess unique abilities for personal transformation and operate in three distinct phases of existence. (65:1.1) Life Carriers are not allowed to manipulate evolution mechanically, despite utilizing natural resources to enhance developmental progress, due to a purposeful plan guiding early evolutionary occurrences beyond human understanding. (65:3.1-2) Life Carriers participate in human evolution in light and life, serving as advisors in the higher deliberative bodies of settled planets, entrusted with new duties in the evolving universe. (36:1.4) The Life Carriers reach the possible 4th stage of existence in light and life after taking renunciation vows to refrain from influencing organic evolution. (65:1.8-9) Life Carriers request dispatch of Planetary Princes for inhabited planets to ensure proper governance and evolution of intelligent beings. (50:0.2) Life Carriers restore dematerialized Adams to normal existence upon arrival on their planetary destination. (51:2.2,4) Despite the influence of the Lucifer rebellion, the Life Carriers stationed on the rebel planets were the only ones swayed, as the higher orders of local universe sonship remained loyal to Michael. (53:7.4) Life Carriers receive valuable guidance and advice from Technical Advisers, who are intimately linked to the work of the Universal Censors and provide essential counsel on matters of life propagation and spiritual ethics. (25:4.18) headquarters they plan and initiate life work on Urantia wrote Papers 57–65
improvedDescription: Local universe Sons of God created by a Creator Son, Universe Mother Spirit, and one of the Ancients of Days, responsible for designing, carrying, and establishing life on evolutionary worlds. They are organized into three divisions: senior carriers, assistants, and custodians, and can function on physical, morontial, and semispiritual levels throughout their service to foster life development.

name: life-dawn era
originalDescription: The Proterozoic era, characterized by ancient fossil remains, marks the transition between prelife cataclysmic age and highly developed marine life. (58:7.1) (59:0.3)
improvedDescription: The Proterozoic era, characterized by ancient fossil remains, marks the transition between the prelife cataclysmic age and highly developed marine life.

name: life-experiment planets
improvedDescription: Planets designated for conducting special life experiments by the Life Carriers.

name: life flash
originalDescription: The translation flash marks the moment of ascension from the material domain to the morontia level, with celestial beings protecting from the energies released at translation. (55:2.3-5)
improvedDescription: The translation flash marks the moment of ascension from the material domain to the morontia level, with celestial beings protecting observers from the energies released at translation.

name: life-modification planets
improvedDescription: Planets where the Life Carriers modify existing life forms to create unique evolutionary patterns.

name: life plasm
originalDescription: All three-brained, one-brained, and two-brained races share the same type of life plasm, guiding their planetary evolution in similar ways. (49:5.17) The Andonic life plasm transplanted into Caligastia 100 brought corporeal, relatively human forms of special planetary service to Urantia. (66:2.5-6) (66:4.4) The vital spark of life, the mystery of life, is bestowed through the Life Carriers, who catalyze and organize inert material to become living matter. (36:3.3-5) Life plasm consists of essential units of life energy manipulated by universe physicists and electrochemists. (36:2.14) The metamorphosis of the germ plasm by the action of system life currents resulted in the creation of a new type of mortal designed for social sovereignty, though the project ultimately failed. (77:2.2,5) The mutant traits in the first Nodite generation were caused by modifications induced by X-rays on the Andonic germ plasm, influenced by the Satania life-maintenance circuits. (77:2.5) The original life plasm of evolutionary worlds must contain full potential for all future development, including the appearance of seemingly useless forms essential to the final function of higher intelligent life. (36:2.17) Life springs only from life, as the Spirit of God contributes the vital spark to all living beings. (36:6.2-4)
improvedDescription: The essential material substance containing the potential for all life manifestations, organized and catalyzed by Life Carriers into living matter through the vital spark bestowed by the Universe Mother Spirit.

name: life-story tellers
originalDescription: Heavenly reproducers and celestial artisans, life-story tellers perpetuate the meaning and significance of life experience by projecting present personal experiences into future attainment values. (44:2.8)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who perpetuate the meaning and significance of life experience by projecting present personal experiences into future attainment values.

name: light
originalDescription: material light spiritual light
improvedDescription: A fundamental energy manifestation existing in three forms: material light from physical sources, intellectual insight of the mind, and spirit luminosity characteristic of divine personality manifestation.

name: light and life
originalDescription: Progressing to the 5th mansonia is a significant step towards experiencing the early light and life of evolving planets. (47:7.1) Advancing through the 6th mansonia brings a status akin to the enlightened era of light and life on planets, with a highly organized society and decreasing mortal shadows. (47:8.7) Seven stages achievement changes in celestial duties upon entry into light and life mortal life in light and life mortal survivors from planets in light and life physical changes in light and life
improvedDescription: The settled status representing the evolutionary goal of inhabited worlds, characterized by spiritual harmony, advanced civilization, and the culmination of planetary development through seven progressive stages.

name: light picturizers
originalDescription: The light picturizers are heavenly reproducers, celestial artisans creating real semispirit-phenomena preservations far beyond the crude illustration of motion pictures. (44:2.5)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who create real semispirit-phenomena preservations, far superior to crude motion pictures.

name: light-years
originalDescription: Distance to Andromeda, a spiral nebula far distant from Orvonton, is almost one million light-years. (15:4.7) From Jerusem, Satania is over 200,000 light-years away from the center of Orvonton in the Milky Way. (32:2.11) The Satania system is located 250,000 light-years away from the center of the Orvonton superuniverse. (32:2.11) Between the seven superuniverses and the 1st outer space level lies a space zone of around 500,000 light-years. (12:1.14) The zone of increasing energy in the 1st outer space level spans over 25 million light-years. (12:1.14) Uversa physicists have detected force manifestations more than fifty million light-years beyond the first outer space level, indicating upcoming organization of the material creations in the second outer space level. (12:1.15) Astronomers can measure stellar distances accurately up to more than one million light-years using variable stars as universe lighthouses. (41:3.10) The estimation of astronomic velocities using spectral lines is reliable within the superuniverse, but unreliable in outer space due to red shift's influence on light-years. (12:4.14) Solitary Messengers act as emergency communication lines in remote space, able to transmit messages up to 100 light-years away. (23:3.5)
improvedDescription: A unit of astronomical distance measurement used to describe vast cosmic distances, such as the 250,000 light-years from Satania to the center of Orvonton and the millions of light-years spanning outer space levels.

name: lighthouse
originalDescription: Pharos in Alexandria was a magnificent and lifesaving device, inspiring Ganid to become a beacon of light for others in darkness. (130:3.2)
improvedDescription: The great lighthouse of Pharos in Alexandria, one of the seven wonders of the ancient world, served as a magnificent and lifesaving device that inspired Ganid to become a beacon of light for others in darkness.

name: lighting
originalDescription: The lighting system on Jerusem projects gentle, even light similar to natural sunlight but with much less heat, emanating equally from all directions in the sky. (46:1.4-7)
improvedDescription: The lighting system on Jerusem projects gentle, even light similar to natural sunlight but with much less heat, emanating equally from all directions in the sky through power transformers and atmospheric energy projection.

name: lightning
originalDescription: Jesus beheld Satan fall as lightning, marking the end of the Lucifer rebellion in Satania. (53:8.3) Fire coming down from the gods, based on myth and lightning, led to fire worship and the custom of passing through fire, a tradition that endures even after death. (69:6.6) Early man was overawed by thunder and lightning as the voice of an angry god, linking worship of fire and fear of lightning. (85:4.3) Primates replenished fire kindled by lightning long before the twins discovered how to start fires intentionally. (63:2.7) Thor was master of the lightning, a ghost hero intertwined with nature worship. (85:6.4) Thunderstones believed to be protection against lightning still persist in the folkways of Brittany. (80:9.14)
improvedDescription: Lightning served as both a natural phenomenon that early humans feared and worshiped as the voice of angry gods, and as a symbolic reference when Jesus declared he beheld Satan fall as lightning from heaven, marking the end of the Lucifer rebellion.

name: lignite
originalDescription: 85 million years ago, the deposits of chalk and green sandstone marl, which give this period its name, included small amounts of limestone and lignite, with thicknesses varying from 60 meters to 3,000 meters, and can be observed in the eastern foothills of the Rocky Mountains. (60:3.9)
improvedDescription: A type of inferior coal found in sedimentary deposits from 85 million years ago, particularly observed in the chalk and greensand marl formations along the eastern foothills of the Rocky Mountains.

name: lilies
originalDescription: Consider the lilies, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin - trust in God for all your needs. (165:5.3) Mortal man can raise his spiritual nature from the muck of human origins, like a white lily with its snowy head. (156:5.1)
improvedDescription: Jesus used lilies as a teaching illustration about trusting in God's provision, noting how they grow without toil or spinning, and as a metaphor for how mortals can raise their spiritual nature from earthly origins like a white lily emerging from dark soil.

name: limestone
originalDescription: The pressure and folding have transformed limestone into quartz, shale, and marble, altering the original character of the deposits. (59:1.16) (59:3.1) Limestone deposits, including trilobite shells from 340,000,000 years ago, built up thick layers over Europe and North America, now seen in the Carrara marble of the southern Alps. (59:1.13) (59:2.3) (59:3.1,9) (59:5.8) (59:5.10) (60:1.4) (60:3.9) (61:1.13) Limestone can form through chemical precipitation and marine deposition, creating layers of fossil-free stratified stone on ancient ocean bottoms. (57:8.13)
improvedDescription: A sedimentary rock formed through chemical precipitation and marine deposition of lime-shelled life, creating extensive fossil-bearing layers throughout geological history that have been transformed by pressure into marble and other formations.

name: lions
originalDescription: Egyptian tribes worshiped the lion as their special group god, along with other nature gods, before transitioning from totem tribes. (95:2.3) Lions, arriving in Europe from Africa, virtually exterminated tigers and hippopotamuses. (64:4.7)
improvedDescription: Large predatory mammals that arrived in Europe from Africa via the Sicilian land bridge, virtually exterminating tigers and hippopotamuses, and were worshiped as special group gods by various Egyptian tribes.

name: lips
originalDescription: Before you will learn to set a guard upon your lips, you must pass through the experience ahead. (181:2.27)
improvedDescription: Jesus warned Peter about the need to learn to set a guard upon his lips before speaking thoughtlessly, indicating that controlling one's speech requires experience and spiritual maturity.

name: listening
originalDescription: Jesus' charm as a listener provided comfort and eased the souls of his friends. (171:7.5) Jesus' essential message was always about the love and mercy of the heavenly Father, and the truth that man is a beloved faith-son of this loving God. (132:4.2)
improvedDescription: Jesus demonstrated exceptional skill as a charming listener who made people feel comfortable and provided them opportunity to unburden their souls to a sympathetic and understanding ear.

name: literality
originalDescription: Error to interpret Jesus’ teachings in literal terms; must discern the spirit behind his message. (140:6.8) (140:8.10) (140:10.2-4) (142:7.17) (147:4.2-3)
improvedDescription: The error of interpreting Jesus' spiritual teachings in literal, concrete terms rather than discerning the spirit and deeper meaning behind his message, which the apostles frequently struggled with despite his guidance.

name: literature
originalDescription: The international exchange of literature fosters intellectual cross-fertilization, crucial for breaking down ignorance and suspicion to build brotherhood and understanding among races and nations. (52:6.4)
improvedDescription: The international exchange of literature fosters intellectual cross-fertilization, crucial for breaking down ignorance and suspicion to build brotherhood and understanding among races and nations.

name: lithographic stone
originalDescription: The lithographic stone of southern Germany preserves ancient fossils underwater from a bygone era when much of Europe was inundated. (60:2.6)
improvedDescription: The lithographic stone of southern Germany was laid down during an ancient submergence when much of Europe was inundated, preserving delicate fossils such as insect wings with remarkable clarity.

name: littleness
originalDescription: Jesus does not look down upon littleness, only upon evil and sin; he welcomes simple souls like James and Judas with open arms. (139:10.8)
improvedDescription: Jesus does not look down upon littleness, only upon evil and sin; he welcomes simple souls with open arms, valuing faithfulness over talent or sophistication.

name: liver
originalDescription: Ancients believed the soul resided in various parts of the body, such as the head, heart, liver, blood, and fat. (86:5.15)
improvedDescription: Ancient peoples believed the soul resided in various parts of the body, including the head, heart, liver, blood, and fat.

name: Livias
originalDescription: One of the towns visited by Jesus, the twelve and the seventy, during the Perea mission between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1) (171:4)
improvedDescription: A town in Perea visited by Jesus, the twelve apostles, and the seventy messengers during their final mission, and where the apostles secretly received and distributed swords before Jesus's betrayal.

name: living
originalDescription: We should teach that man's whole duty is loving God and neighbors rather than following Pharisees' 613 rules of living. (163:4.8) About the art of living see link below. | art of living About the water of life see link below. | water of life
improvedDescription: Man's whole duty is summed up in loving God with all mind and soul and loving one's neighbor as oneself, rather than following the Pharisees' 613 rules of living.

name: living libraries of Paradise
improvedDescription: Repositories of knowledge and wisdom maintained on Paradise for the benefit of celestial beings and ascending mortals.

name: living newspapers of Havona
originalDescription: The tertiary supernaphim, Intelligence Co-ordinators of Havona, serve as wise and sympathetic living newspapers, keeping all inhabitants informed of universe affairs. (26:3.8)
improvedDescription: The tertiary supernaphim Intelligence Co-ordinators of Havona serve as wise and sympathetic living newspapers, keeping all inhabitants informed of universe affairs through advanced communication methods.

name: lizards
originalDescription: Lizards, descended from nonprogressive reptiles, are one of the four surviving divisions in the kingdom of reptiles, along with snakes, alligators, and turtles. (65:2.10)
improvedDescription: Lizards are one of the four surviving divisions in the kingdom of reptiles, descended from nonprogressive reptiles along with snakes, alligators, and turtles.

name: llamas
originalDescription: Llamas originated in North America and spread to the western plains, where they coexisted with horses and giraffe-like camels. Later, they migrated to South America, becoming extinct in North America during the last glaciation. (61:2.9) (61:4.4) (61:7.15)
improvedDescription: Llamas originated in North America and spread to the western plains, where they coexisted with horses and giraffe-like camels before migrating to South America and becoming extinct in North America during the last glaciation.

name: loaves
improvedDescription: Bread loaves, often referenced in the context of feeding multitudes and as symbols of sustenance in religious teachings.

name: lobsters
originalDescription: Lobsters are among the ancient, relatively unchanged marine life forms that have endured for millions of years. (59:1.18) (60:2.9)
improvedDescription: Lobsters are among the ancient, relatively unchanged marine life forms that have endured for millions of years, representing early crustacean life that has survived from ancient seas to the present.

name: local system centers
originalDescription: There is one Supreme Power Center permanently assigned to each local system, responsible for dispatching power circuits and ensuring the satisfactory distribution of power. (29:2.18) (41:1.5) 6th order Supreme Power Centers stationed at exact gravity focus of local systems dispatch power to Physical Controllers on inhabited worlds. (29:2.18) (41:1.5) System Centers are in Uversa personality register. (30:2.98) Local system centers are efficiently lighted, heated, and energized to support the architectural worlds through coordination with power centers and physical controllers. (45:0.3)
improvedDescription: Sixth-order Supreme Power Centers permanently assigned to each local system who dispatch power circuits to inhabited worlds and coordinate with Master Physical Controllers to ensure satisfactory energy distribution throughout the system.

name: local system conclave
originalDescription: When a system achieves light and life, Planetary Sovereigns join a system conclave, a supreme governing body subject to the Constellation Fathers' veto, establishing a virtually self-governing administrative system with a legislative assembly and courts on each planet. (55:8.1)
improvedDescription: When a system achieves light and life, Planetary Sovereigns join a system conclave, a supreme governing body subject to the Constellation Fathers' veto, establishing a virtually self-governing administrative system with a legislative assembly and courts on each planet.

name: local systems
originalDescription: In each local system there are 10,000 planets, making a total of 7,000,000,000 in the universe. (15:2.16-19) (53:1.1) A local system consists of approximately one thousand evolutionary planets, excluding worlds in light and life or in the prehuman stage. (15:2.3,19) (32:0.1) (49:0.2) The planets of Satania are numbered serially according to their registration as inhabited worlds, with Urantia being the 606th world in this local system. (49:0.3) Throughout Nebadon, each local system has a midsonite world where Melchizedeks serve as life carriers, with Mater Eves selected from volunteers to mate with them, as chosen by the System Sovereign. (36:4.1) The local systems neighboring Satania in Norlatiadek are evolutionary and progressive, making the functions of the Master Physical Controllers within Satania easier to comprehend. (41:2.1) government headquarters spheres life circuits of local systems In light and life
improvedDescription: Administrative units consisting of approximately 1,000 inhabited evolutionary worlds, each presided over by a System Sovereign from an architectural headquarters world. Local systems are the basic units of supergovernment, with Satania being Urantia's local system containing 619 inhabited worlds.

name: local universe centers
originalDescription: The fourth order Supreme Power Centers downstep superuniverse power for constellations and systems. (29:2.16) (41:1.2) One hundred power centers of the fourth order are stationed on each local universe capital to modify power circuits and assist in energy distribution within the constellations and systems. (29:2.16) (32:2.2) (41:1) Local universe centers assist Creator Sons in universe organization by providing intensified lanes of energy for interplanetary communication and playing a crucial role in energy mobilization and universe material organization. (29:2.16) (29:3.3) (41:1.1) Presented by an Archangel in collaboration with the Chief of Nebadon Power Centers. (41:10.6) Uversa personality register records local universe centers. (30:2.96) Local universe centers provide energy lanes for interplanetary communication, facilitating efficient transmission between key inhabited locations. (29:2.16)
improvedDescription: Fourth-order Supreme Power Centers stationed on local universe headquarters who downstep superuniverse power circuits for distribution to constellations and systems, and provide intensified energy lanes for interplanetary communication between important inhabited points.

name: local universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon
originalDescription: The Master Creator Son and the Universe Spirit have equality of authority with subordination to Michael, manifesting a harmonious co-director relationship in managing the universe. (33:3.3-5) The Holy Spirit is the personal spirit of the local universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon, always present at the universe headquarters world. (15:9.13) (34:4.7) The Divine Minister of Salvington is everywhere in Nebadon, including the World of the Spirit. (45:1.8) The Mother Spirit of Nebadon knows us fully, reflecting divine knowledge directly for our benefit. (28:5.22) The local universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon issued a mandate not to suppress Lucifer rebellion, instead focusing on full disclosure for the perfect cure of evil and sin. (54:5.11) The Universe Mother Spirit of Salvington is the 611,121st of the sixth group of Supreme Spirits in control of Nebadon alongside Michael. (33:3.2) also known as
improvedDescription: The Universe Mother Spirit of Nebadon, numbered 611,121 of the sixth group of Supreme Spirits, who serves as co-director with Michael in universe administration and is known as the Divine Minister of Salvington, providing the Holy Spirit presence throughout Nebadon.

name: local universe Mother Spirits
originalDescription: The Local Universe Mother Spirits are the 6th group of Supreme Spirits, serving Creator Sons as associate in controlling and overseeing Local Universes. (9:8.18) (33:3.2) They are counted between the Supreme Creators, along with Creator Sons. The present functioning of divinity in the superuniverses is actively manifest among Supreme Creators, leading inward to the Universal Father. (56:5.3) (116:2.4-12) Local Universe Mother Spirits are in personality registers with Local Universe Creative Spirits, guiding and nurturing the evolution of life in their respective universes. (30:1.61) (30:2.20) attributes creation and personalization functions mind and spirit circuits offspring (37:0.1) also known as
improvedDescription: Creative Spirits of the sixth group of Supreme Spirits who serve as co-creators and administrators of local universes alongside Creator Sons, providing mind circuits and spiritual ministry while evolving through distinct stages from initial association to full personalization and sovereignty.

name: local universe power centers
improvedDescription: Fourth-order Supreme Power Centers assigned to local universe headquarters who modify and distribute superuniverse power circuits to constellations and systems within the local universe.

name: Local Universe Sons of God
originalDescription: Defection of Local Universe Sons of God is never adjudicated locally, but rather in the superuniverse courts. (33:7.5)
improvedDescription: Orders of divine Sons serving in local universe administration whose defection or default is never adjudicated locally but is immediately carried to superuniverse courts due to the implications for their status and authority.

name: local universes
originalDescription: In each local universe, the Creator Son personifies the inseparability of the Universal Father and the Eternal Son to the inhabitants of ten million worlds. (6:8.3) (15:2.5,21) (35:4.2) The physical conditions on individual planets are influenced by local factors within the same system, leading to similarities in worlds within a local universe. (15:6.16) Each local universe, composed of 100 constellations, is an autonomous unit governed by a Creator Son of God and blessed by the presence of a Union of Days representing the Paradise Trinity. (15:2.5) (32:0.1) (43:2.1) Salvington, the capital of Nebadon, is located in the minor sector of Ensa, which is number eighty-four, and has one hundred constellations, with Norlatiadek being number seventy, consisting of one hundred local systems, including Jerusem, the headquarters world of Satania in system number twenty-four. (15:14.6) Nebadon is surrounded by neighboring universes Avalon, Henselon, Sanselon, Portalon, Wolvering, Fanoving, and Alvoring in the superuniverse circuit of Orvonton. (32:2.12) creation, physical emergence (32:1-2) future, in light and life (55:10) headquarters worlds permanent inhabitants courts circuits of local universes | local systems:life circuits
improvedDescription: Administrative divisions of the grand universe, each containing 100 constellations and approximately 10 million inhabited worlds, ruled by a Creator Son of the Michael order and blessed by a Union of Days representative. Each local universe serves as an autonomous unit within a superuniverse, with its own architectural headquarters world and distinct administrative structure governed by the combined authority of the Creator Son and local universe Mother Spirit.

name: location
originalDescription: Simultaneous determination of a moving object's exact location and velocity is impossible due to the inherent trade-off between the two measurements. (65:6.1)
improvedDescription: A fundamental principle of physics stating that it is impossible to accurately determine both the exact location and velocity of a moving object simultaneously, as any attempt to measure one inevitably involves change in the other.

name: locusts
originalDescription: The locusts appeared along with the rest of the insects, overspreading continents with giant dragonflies and four-inch cockroaches. (59:5.7) John the Baptist ate locusts and honey, living simply along the valley with his adopted orphan, Ezda, while tending to his herds of sheep and goats in stone shelters. (135:3.1)
improvedDescription: Insects that appeared early in evolutionary history alongside other arthropods, overspreading the continents during ancient geological periods. John the Baptist subsisted on edible locusts and wild honey during his life as a shepherd in the Jordan valley.

name: logic
originalDescription: Early mortals' confusion about the nature of souls, ghosts, and spirits was due to the absence of reasoned thought in the presence of perplexity, leading to gross inconsistencies in their beliefs. (86:5.3) Children best relate to reality by mastering child-parent relationships and then expanding to embrace larger concepts of family, community, race, world, universe, superuniverse, and beyond. (8:1.11) Logic is the synthetic truth-seeking progression of the unity of faith and reason, based on innate recognition of things, meanings, and values. (103:7.13) The savage mind, uneducated and unsophisticated, attributed cause and effect to events without understanding the true nature of consequences. (86:2.3) Revelation transforms scientific reasoning about nature's First Cause into the God of salvation, requiring spiritual insight for validation. (101:2.3) Logic is the method of philosophy, reason is the method of science, and faith is the method of religion, all upheld by the experiential harmony of revelation. (101:2.2,8) (103:7.6) Intelligent men must use consistent logic, tolerating truth alongside fact, and recognizing the activities of a purposive Creator in the evolution of spiritual experiences. (102:6.9) limitations of logic
improvedDescription: The synthetic truth-seeking progression that unites faith and reason, founded on the innate recognition of things, meanings, and values. Logic serves as the technique of philosophy and provides a method for achieving consistency in thought, though it has limitations when applied to spiritual realities and life problems that transcend purely material considerations.

name: loneliness
originalDescription: In loneliness or with the fellows of my kind, I find comfort in the 23rd Psalm reference.
