improvedDescription: Inevitable conflicts arising from ignorance and differing viewpoints among evolving beings with free will, which require wisdom, tolerance, and fair adjustment mechanisms to resolve harmoniously.

name: mites
originalDescription: The widow contributed all, giving more than the others in accordance with her possessions. (172:4.2)
improvedDescription: Small copper coins given by a poor widow who, according to Jesus, contributed more than all others because she gave everything she had rather than giving from her surplus.

name: Mithraic priest from Persia
originalDescription: Jesus talked to the Mithraic priest from Persia about immortality, time, and eternity. (130:7.3)
improvedDescription: A Persian priest of Mithraism, educated at Alexandria, who had a memorable conversation with Jesus at Carthage about immortality, time, and eternity.

name: Mithraic priest in Corinth
originalDescription: Jesus talked to the Mithraic priest in Corinth, urging him to seek eternal salvation within his own soul rather than in man-made mysteries or human philosophies. (133:4.4)
improvedDescription: Jesus talked to the Mithraic priest in Corinth, urging him to seek eternal salvation within his own soul rather than in man-made mysteries or human philosophies.

name: Mithraism
originalDescription: The cult of Mithras, savior of sinful mankind, arose in Iran despite opposition from followers of Zoroaster, and later influenced Christianity through its teachings. (98:4.5) (98:5.2) Sun worship in Persia gave rise to the Mithraic cult, a lasting legacy of solar reverence across ancient civilizations. (85:5.2) Mithraism, with its militaristic encouragement and tolerance of other religions, ultimately fell due to its admission of women into Christian fellowship. (98:6.4) Mithraism was greatly improved by Zoroaster's teachings, influencing Christianity through the Mithraic cult's absorption of Zoroastrian beliefs. (98:5.2) (98:7.6) Iranian priests resurrected Mithraism to combat Zoroastrianism, influencing the religions of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. (95:6.7) Abner's choice of teachers for Cymboyton's son led to orgiastic celebrations that aimed to dominate other beliefs. (134:6.16) Roman legions spread the worship of Mithras across the Empire, supplanting the Phrygian and Egyptian mysteries. (98:5.1) The school at Urmia reopened as a temple of unyielding and uncompromising teachers seeking to make their religion dominant. (134:6.16) Sol Invictus, the sun-god of Mithraism, was a degeneration of the deity concept from Zoroastrianism, with Mithras depicted as a militant god and champion of the sun-god in his struggle with darkness. (98:5.3) Mithraism supplanted Phrygian and Egyptian mysteries, becoming the dominant mystery cult in the Roman Empire. (98:5.1) Mithraism and Christianity
improvedDescription: An ancient mystery religion that arose in Iran, worshipping Mithras as the savior and redeemer of sinful mankind. The cult spread throughout the Roman Empire via Roman legions, absorbing Zoroastrian teachings and becoming a major competitor to early Christianity before ultimately declining due to its exclusion of women from full fellowship.

name: Mithras
originalDescription: Mithras was the champion of the sun-god in his struggle against the god of darkness, immortalized for slaying the sacred bull and becoming an intercessor for humanity among the gods. (98:5.3)
improvedDescription: The central deity of Mithraism, portrayed as a militant god who emerged from a great rock and became the champion of the sun-god in his struggle against darkness. Mithras was made immortal after slaying the mythical sacred bull and was elevated to serve as an intercessor for humanity among the gods.

name: mixed commission of twelve
originalDescription: The Jerusem group approved the planetary life initiation plans for Urantia, as proposed by the advisory counselors of the Life Carriers, and subsequently endorsed by the planetary commission of seventy on Edentia and accepted on Salvington. (57:8.9)
improvedDescription: A Jerusem administrative body that completed arrangements for Urantia's planetary occupation and approved the Life Carriers' plans for their sixtieth Satania experiment to improve the Nebadon life patterns.

name: Mo Ti
originalDescription: Mo Ti combated Confucianism, proclaiming a brotherhood founded on love of God in an attempt to revive the ancient quest for new truth amidst the overwhelming influence of Confucian precepts in Chinese culture. (94:6.11)
improvedDescription: A Chinese philosopher who combated Confucianism by proclaiming a brotherhood founded on the love of God rather than ethical duty. He attempted to rekindle the ancient quest for new truth but failed against the vigorous opposition of Confucian disciples.

name: Moab, hills of
originalDescription: From the high hill near Nazareth, Joseph and Jesus could see the hills of Moab in the east, Mount Carmel to the northwest, and Mount Hermon to the north, among other sights. (123:5.12)
improvedDescription: Rocky hills visible to the far east from the high hill near Nazareth, where Joseph and Jesus would take their Sabbath afternoon walks and enjoy panoramic views of Galilee.

name: Moabites
originalDescription: King David laid heavy tribute on the Moabites after securing the ark of Yahweh and establishing official worship for his kingdom. (97:9.14)
improvedDescription: A neighboring tribe upon whom King David laid heavy tribute after securing the ark of Yahweh and establishing official worship for his kingdom.

name: mob
improvedDescription: A disorganized crowd or mass of people, often acting emotionally or irrationally in group situations.

name: moderation
originalDescription: Strength is achieved through action, while charm is bestowed through moderation. (48:7.11) Restraint in all things is good; wisdom and virtue lead to true nobility and peace. (131:3.6)
improvedDescription: The virtue of restraint and balance in all things, which leads to charm, happiness, and wisdom. Action achieves strength while moderation brings charm, and restraint in all things is considered good.

name: modern civilization
originalDescription: Amidst the psychological turmoil of the twentieth century, marked by economic upheavals and sociologic transitions, there is spiritual stagnation and philosophic chaos in modern civilization. (99:4.4-6)
improvedDescription: The contemporary state of human society marked by unprecedented scientific achievement and mechanical development, yet characterized by spiritual stagnation, philosophic chaos, and widespread psychological unsettlement during the twentieth century's economic upheavals and social transitions.

name: modesty
originalDescription: The ancient practice of heaping vituperation on lucky individuals evolved into civilized modesty and restraint to prevent envy from spirits. (87:5.7) The fear of being observed during menstruation led to the enforcement of modesty in women's clothing and behavior throughout history. (84:4.8)
improvedDescription: A cultural concept that evolved from ancient practices of self-deprecation to prevent spiritual envy, eventually developing into civilized restraint and courtesy. In women, modesty in clothing and behavior historically originated from fears surrounding menstruation taboos.

name: Mogul dynasty
originalDescription: The establishment of the Mogul dynasty marked a key moment in the political evolution towards greater centralized authority and control. (134:5.9)
improvedDescription: An Indian dynasty whose establishment, along with the Ming dynasty in China, marked a significant phase in the progressive transfer of political sovereignty from smaller to larger governmental organizations in the East.

name: Mohammed
originalDescription: Mohammed was a great religious teacher. He founded Islam to protest foreign religious beliefs and the incoherence of his people's faith. (92:5.14) The return looked for in Islam, like many other races anticipate the return of their revered leaders. (92:5.6)
improvedDescription: The founder of Islam, a great religious teacher who created his faith as a protest against foreign religious beliefs and the incoherence of his people's religious life. Like other revered leaders, his followers anticipate his eventual return.

name: mollusks
originalDescription: The nonprogressive species of mollusks—oysters, octopuses, and snails—represent the stationary types of early and lower animals that failed to progress in the evolutionary tree of life. (65:2.5)
improvedDescription: A group of marine animals including oysters, octopuses, and snails that represent nonprogressive evolutionary branches. Early cephalopods, growing up to fifteen feet long, once dominated the seas before declining, while simpler mollusks remain as stationary types that failed to advance evolutionarily.

name: monastic orders
originalDescription: During the dark ages, the church's alliance with politics led to a decline in spirituality and the rise of monastic orders. (195:4.1) Peculiar and celibate orders of men and women arose, maintained by individuals lacking normal sex urges, as puberty advanced as the common age of marriage with the progress of civilization. (82:3.9)
improvedDescription: Religious communities of celibate men and women that arose during Christianity's decline in the dark ages, often founded by individuals lacking normal sexual urges. These orders emerged as the church became increasingly monasticized and legalized.

name: money
originalDescription: Money cannot love, and the first duty of anybody is to his family's physical necessities and watchcare, despite the offers of material support. (127:2.8) The earliest medium of exchange was a fish, goat, or cow, while the first weights were grains of wheat and other cereals. (69:4.5) The first commercial credit was practiced in Dalamatia, where tokens replaced barter objects in exchange. (66:5.12) Money has become the universal language of trade, uniting modern society and fueling the industrial market for the greater good. (70:3.5) Jesus Judas
improvedDescription: A medium of exchange that evolved from early barter systems using fish, goats, and wheat grains to become the universal language of modern trade. While money serves as a civilizing force in commerce, it cannot provide love or replace the deeper obligations of family care.

name: money-changers
originalDescription: In the court of gentiles, Jesus was shocked by the irreverence of the money-changers and vendors in his Father's house. (125:1.1) Jesus cleansed the temple from money-changers profiting from commercializing religious practices to protect the poor from unfair profiteering. (173:1) Licensed money-changers exchanged various currencies for shekels in the temple precincts, profiting from temple dues and commercial transactions. (173:1.3) (173:2.3)
improvedDescription: Licensed currency exchangers who operated in the temple precincts, converting various Roman Empire currencies into Jewish shekels required for temple dues and purchases. Their commercial activities, along with animal vendors, profaned the temple courts and prompted Jesus's cleansing of the temple.

name: moneylenders
originalDescription: Moneylenders made themselves kings by creating a standing army of debtors, early creditors controlled body after death. (69:5.8) Legitimate wealth deserves fair interest, but usury should never be used to exploit others. (132:5.19) The parable of the two debtors illustrates the power of forgiveness and love, as seen in the woman's repentance and gratitude towards Jesus. (147:5.4)
improvedDescription: Ancient creditors who charged extremely high interest rates, often 100 percent annually, and created standing armies of debtors through debt slavery. While legitimate interest on fair loans is acceptable, usury that exploits others is condemned.

name: Mongolia
originalDescription: An influential river cult thrives in Mongolia, where worship of flowing waters remains strong. (85:4.1)
improvedDescription: A region in Asia where an influential river cult flourishes, representing the continued worship of flowing waters and natural spirits that has persisted from ancient times.

name: Mongolian merchant
originalDescription: The Mongolian merchant who talked with Jesus in Caesarea became a devoted teacher of advanced truths, inspiring generations of followers to embrace the concept of the Supreme Ruler of Heaven. (130:2.2)
improvedDescription: A Taoist merchant from Mongolia who met Jesus in Caesarea and was deeply impressed by his philosophy of living the heavenly life through daily submission to the Father's will. He became a devoted teacher of these truths, inspiring his son to become a Taoist priest and establishing a family lineage dedicated to the doctrine of the One God.

name: Mongoloids
originalDescription: The Mongoloid race is a blend of red, yellow, and blue Sangiks, encompassing the Chinese, Amerinds, Europeans modified by secondary Sangik and Andonic mixture, Andite infusion, as well as the Malayan and other Indonesian peoples with a high percentage of secondary Sangik blood. (81:4.12)
improvedDescription: The Mongoloid race is a blend of red, yellow, and blue Sangiks, encompassing the Chinese, Amerinds, Europeans modified by secondary Sangik and Andonic mixture, Andite infusion, as well as the Malayan and other Indonesian peoples with a high percentage of secondary Sangik blood.

name: monkeys
originalDescription: The appearance of primitive monkeys played a significant role in the evolution of mammalian life 45,000,000 years ago. (61:1.9) (61:3.12) (61:4.4) Monkeys, like apes, became fetish animals leading to various taboos on consuming their flesh, as seen in the reverence of other animals and belief in their charm powers. (88:1.5) The retarded mid-mammals were ancestors of monkeys, baboons, chimpanzees, and gorillas. (62:3.12)
improvedDescription: Primitive monkeys appeared 45 million years ago as part of mammalian evolution. The retarded mid-mammals were ancestors of monkeys, baboons, chimpanzees, and gorillas. Monkeys became fetish animals in many cultures, leading to taboos on consuming their flesh due to their resemblance to humans.

name: Monmatia
originalDescription: The retrograde motion of three Angona tributaries injected new directional forces into the emerging solar system. (57:5.13-14) Three planets in this superuniverse, including Earth, are currently suitable for hosting life like ours. (15:6.15) The 5th planet fragmentized into asteroids, creating swarms of shooting stars. (57:6.5) The twelve planets of the solar system evolved from a column of solar gases separated from the sun, with meteors and space dust condensed from the repercussional ejection of gas. (57:5.7) Monmatia's potential fate could end as double star formation. (15:5.8) 4.5 billion years ago, Monmatia originated in an Angona approach, leading to a gravity explosion of the sun and the capture of meteors, creating the nucleus of the physical system to which our sun and its planets belong. (15:3.6) (15:5.5) (41:10.1) (57:5.4-14) 4 billion years ago, Monmatia witnessed the organization of the Jupiter and Saturn systems much as observed today, with planets still growing from meteoric captures. (57:6.6-10) Planets orbit in Angona solar extrusion plane, not sun's equator plane. (57:5.12) Monmatia was registered 3 billion years ago in the physical registry of Nebadon. (57:6.9) Monmatia's existence revolves around the former Andronover nebula, distorted by a near collision and now situated in a fairly central position within a distorted spiral system. (15:3.6-9) Monmatia, the name of Urantia's solar system, originated uniquely from the detached solar gases of the giant Angona system 4,500,000,000 years ago, impacting the orbital movement of the solar system material. (57:2.1) (57:5)
improvedDescription: Monmatia is the name of Urantia's solar system, which originated 4.5 billion years ago from solar gases separated from the sun during the near approach of the massive Angona system. The solar system was registered in Nebadon's physical registry 3 billion years ago and occupies a fairly central position in one of the arms of the former Andronover nebula.

name: monogamy
improvedDescription: The practice of marriage between one man and one woman, representing an evolved form of marital relationship.

name: monota
originalDescription: On Paradise, monota and spirit are one—indistinguishable except by name; in the superuniverses, however, there is great divergence between cosmic energy and divine spirit, leading to a greater potential for harmonizing physical pattern with spiritual purposes. (116:6.7) Monota is the living nonspirit energy of Paradise, closely related to divinity and serving as the nonspiritual energy system of the Universal Father. (42:2.19) The expansive journey from Paradise monota to space potency reveals absolute, nonspiritual realities of First Source. (42:10.1)
improvedDescription: Monota is the living nonspirit energy of Paradise, closely related to divinity and serving as the nonspiritual energy system of the Universal Father. On Paradise, monota and spirit are indistinguishable except by name, representing the eternity counterpart of the Original Son's living spirit energy.

name: monotheism
originalDescription: The breakup of the Mongol empire greatly handicapped the growth of monotheism in Asia. (79:1.9) Egyptian political factors favored the spread of monotheistic beliefs in ancient Egypt. (95:2.2) The priests in Egypt struggled to fully overcome the monotheistic wave, leading them to combine and hyphenate their gods in a contracting family of deities, only for the concept of monotheism to persist until the arrival of the Creator Son. (95:5.11) Monotheism enabled man to see the Father as the creator and controller of all creation. (1:0.1) The evolution of monotheism progresses from inclusion of all gods to the subordination of lesser deities, culminating in the exclusive worship of the one supreme God. (96:0.1) (96:1.14) (104:2.1) Trinitarianism arises from the human struggle to grasp the concept of a personal deity within the framework of pure monotheism, which tends to strip away personal qualities and reduce God to a pantheistic Absolute. (104:2.2)
improvedDescription: The belief in one supreme God, which evolved through the inclusion, subordination, and eventual exclusion of lesser deities. Monotheism enabled humanity to understand the Father as creator and controller of all creation, though it sometimes faces the challenge of maintaining the personal nature of Deity against pantheistic tendencies.

name: monotony
originalDescription: Monotony indicates immaturity of the creative imagination and inactivity of intellectual co-ordination with the spiritual endowment, a contrast to the emotional, intellectual, and social maturity attained before exploring the heavenly worlds. (14:5.8) The holiday brought improved relations with John's followers, highlighting the dangers of monotony multiplying perplexities and difficulties. (143:3.7) In the universal economy, progress stems from action and adventure, not monotony or stagnation; responsibility lies in recognizing and serving the diverse beings that populate the vast creation. (28:6.18) Variety is restful; monotony wears and exhausts, do not let self-importance diminish your energy for important work. (48:6.37)
improvedDescription: Monotony indicates immaturity of creative imagination and inactivity of intellectual coordination with spiritual endowment. It is restful in contrast to variety, but when excessive, it wears and exhausts, multiplying perplexities and magnifying difficulties in both personal development and social relations.

name: Montana
originalDescription: Montana is known for the abundant fresh-water fossils found in the Morrison beds of Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming. (60:2.5)
improvedDescription: A state known for the abundant fresh-water fossils found in the Morrison beds of Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming from the Jurassic period.

name: Montreal
originalDescription: Montreal is home to Mount Royal, an eroded neck of a volcano from the great volcanic activity in the region 230 million years ago. (59:4.16)
improvedDescription: A city in the St. Lawrence region of North America, home to Mount Royal, which is the eroded neck of a volcano from the great volcanic activity that occurred 230 million years ago.

name: moon (Urantia’s)
originalDescription: 1,500 million years ago, the earth, two thirds its present size, slowly robbed the moon of its lost atmosphere to earth. (57:7.4) Due to tidal friction, Urantia's moon has stopped rotating, always showing the same face to the Earth. (57:6.2) In the distant future, the moon will shatter from tidal gravity when it approaches the earth within about eleven thousand miles. (57:6.3-4) Mortals living on the Moon would be considered nonbreathers. (49:2.13) moon worship moon on last week of Jesus
improvedDescription: Urantia's natural satellite, which has stopped rotating due to tidal friction and always shows the same face to Earth. In the distant future, when the moon approaches within about eleven thousand miles of Earth, tidal gravity will cause it to shatter into fragments that may form rings or be drawn to Earth as meteors.

name: Moqui tribe
originalDescription: The Moqui tribe revered the snake in their dance. (88:1.5)
improvedDescription: A Native American tribe that revered serpents and performed snake dances, representing one example of the widespread reverence for snakes found across many cultures from Arabia through India.

name: moraines
originalDescription: The evidence of moraines left behind by glaciers overshadows all other terrestrial activities in the northern latitudes. (61:7.1)
improvedDescription: Geological formations left behind by glaciers, including ground, lateral, and terminal moraines that provide distinctive evidence of glacial activity and serve as telltale signs of ice movement across the landscape.

name: morality
originalDescription: definition in antiquity Jesus dissemination of morality increasing morality
improvedDescription: The recognition of duty and awareness of right and wrong that emerges from human personality and serves as the essential foundation for spiritual consciousness. Morality represents the superanimal but subspiritual level of mind that enables moral choosing and prepares the way for religious experience.

name: mores
originalDescription: Before the Dalamatia teachers arrived, ancient man was merely a helpless victim of the suffocating mores, trapped in a never-ending cycle of ritual and tradition, devoid of any true freedom or progress. (68:4.4) Ghost fear and belief in supernatural punishment helped establish and maintain societal rules and customs from generation to generation. (68:4.3) The evolution of mores is always dependent on the land-man ratio, as man must adjust his performances to conform to the land situation, shaping the sum total of cultural civilization. (68:5.1) (68:6.1) Mores are man's first social institution, originating from the desire to avoid pain and humiliation while seeking pleasure and power in group living. (68:4.2) Respected mores restrain and control sex urge, indicating power and integrity of society. (82:3.3) Saving and preserving food helped man prioritize future needs over present desires, ensuring security and comfort. (69:5.4) Human institutions are merely accumulated mores conserved by taboos and dignified by religion, ultimately metamorphosing into conventions. (69:0.3) Mores and laws are intertwined in their evolution, with mores serving as the foundation for the creation of precise laws and social conventions. (70:11.1) Instinct remains unchanging, despite shifting societal norms, ensuring that the behavior divide between genders will always persist. (84:5.13) Mistake for evangelists to reform mores led to defeat of Melchizedek missionaries in Mesopotamia. (95:1.6,8) (95:7.3) The unconscious origins of mores were shaped by tribal reactions striving for pleasure and power while avoiding pain and humiliation in adjusting group living to mass existence. (68:4.2) Prince’s staff respected the mores of each tribe they encountered, never imposing their own customs. (66:6.5) Primitive man's customs act as a biologic safety brake against too rapid advancement, ensuring the evolution of civilization continues without descending into ruin. (68:4.5-6)
improvedDescription: The traditional customs and folkways that govern group behavior, originating from humanity's unconscious efforts to avoid pain and seek pleasure in social living. These accumulated social practices serve as the foundation for laws and institutions while acting as a biological safety brake against overly rapid social change.

name: morontia
originalDescription: definition Jesus in morontia | Jesus:events in life nature of morontia morontia energy
improvedDescription: A vast level of reality intervening between the material and spiritual realms, encompassing both personal and impersonal realities, living and nonliving energies. Morontia substance has a spiritual warp and physical woof, bridging the gap between mortal material existence and advanced spiritual status.

name: morontia cherubim
improvedDescription: Morontia-level beings that serve alongside other cherubim and sanobim in various capacities within the transition realms of the local universe.

name: Morontia Companions
originalDescription: Morontia Companions, almost wholly self-governing, maintain ten thousand headquarters in a local universe, proving to be intelligent and loyal beings, though susceptible to occasional astray incidents like during the Lucifer rebellion in Satania. (48:3.3) On the first mansion world, one Morontia Companion is assigned to every thousand ascending mortals, but larger numbers await as you progress. (47:3.12) Morontia Companions, beautiful, versatile, and companionable guides, accompany ascending mortals on their journey through the mansion worlds and transition-culture spheres. (47:3.12) Morontia Companions appear in personality registers (30:1.80) (30:2.80) Morontia Companions come in two distinct types: one aggressive, the other retiring, but equal in status, dedicated to facilitating morontia intercourse and preventing confusion. (48:3.4,11) (84:6.5) Companions are not permanently assigned to individuals, varying based on requirements and availability. (48:3.9) Morontia Companions are gracious hosts and skillful play sponsors, contributing to the enjoyment of ascending mortals, though not technically essential to survival experience. (25:7.1) (48:3.18) Seventy billion Morontia Companions in Nebadon minister to pilgrims in transition planets. (37:8.9) (48:3.1) The Morontia Companions, offspring of the Mother Spirit, assist ascending mortals on their journey through the mansion worlds with grace, skill, and cooperation. (25:7.1) (47:3.11) (48:3.1) Morontia Companions serve exclusively in local universes, from mansion worlds to Salvington, providing friendly and companionable assistance throughout the ascending career. (25:7.3) (48:3.2) During the Lucifer rebellion, thousands of Morontia Companions were lost, but now your local system has its full quota after recent replacements. (48:3.3) (53:7.5) Morontia Companions, trained by and serving under Melchizedeks, are tasked with a wide range of service duties throughout the universe. (48:3.2) There are two equal types of Morontia Companions - one aggressive, the other retiring - each showing beautiful affection for one another, closely related to human races in the order of creature existence. (48:3.4) Morontia Companions share a touching affection and are closely related to human races, with two types - one aggressive and the other retiring, but equal in status. (48:3.4) Morontia Companions manage morontia affairs supremely and will always be Morontia Companions. (48:3.16) service types
improvedDescription: Friendly and companionable beings created by the local universe Mother Spirit who serve as gracious hosts and guides for ascending mortals throughout the mansion worlds and morontia spheres. These beautiful and versatile creatures, numbering over seventy billion in Nebadon, facilitate the morontia journey from the first mansion world to Salvington.

name: morontia counselors
originalDescription: morontia counselors are the fourth order of transition minister seraphim and educate surviving mortals on mansion worlds. (39:6.1) Morontia counselors teach morontia progressors the philosophy of mota for integration of meanings and unification of values. (48:6.27-28)
improvedDescription: The fourth order of transition minister seraphim who teach and counsel surviving mortals on the mansion worlds, specializing in the philosophy of mota to help students integrate meanings, unify values, and achieve cosmic perspective.

name: morontia life
improvedDescription: The transitional phase of existence between material and spiritual life, experienced by ascending mortals as they progress through the mansion worlds and morontia spheres of the local universe on their journey toward Paradise.

name: morontia mind
improvedDescription: The transitional form of mind that functions between the material and spiritual levels of existence, enabling morontia beings to perceive and interact with both physical and spiritual realities during their ascension career.

name: morontia mota
improvedDescription: A form of wisdom or knowledge that transcends material understanding, representing the higher philosophical insights gained during morontia existence.

name: morontia planners
originalDescription: Divine builders and celestial artisans collaborate with Morontia planners to co-ordinate personalities from all realms on each sphere. (44:3.7)
improvedDescription: Celestial beings who design and coordinate the associations of all personalities from different realms present on any sphere, working with Morontia Power Supervisors to enhance the coordination of progressive morontia life.

name: Morontia Power Supervisors
originalDescription: The physical controllers and Morontia Power Supervisors are absent from occasions of rejoicing on Jerusem when new mansion world graduates arrive. (47:10.1) Morontia Power Supervisors are wholly self-governing beings, directed by the joint spirit activity of the Universe Son and the Universe Spirit, serving in their native universe from headquarters on the first mansion worlds of local systems in close association with other celestial beings. (48:2.11) Morontial Power Supervisors construct planetary temples according to approved plans from the system capital. (55:1.3) Morontia Power Supervisors sustain and energize the morontia phases of transition worlds, exclusively devoted to facilitating morontia progression. (48:2.1) Morontia Power Supervisors create survivors' 570 successive morontia life vehicles, guiding evolving minds through various levels of morontia existence in the local universe. (42:10.5) (48:1.5) The creation of the first Morontia Power Supervisors is simultaneous with the arrival of the first mortal survivor on the shores of some one of the first mansion worlds in a local universe. (48:2.3) The director of the system Morontia Power Supervisors serves on Jerusem executive council. (45:3.19) The Morontia Power Supervisors facilitate the harmonious blending of material and spiritual energies to form morontia bodies for progressive transformation. (48:1.5) The Morontia Power Supervisors enable survivors to glimpse high spirit personalities in the Finaliter World. (45:1.2) Morontia Power Supervisors possess unquestioned volitional capacity and are involved in mind control. (29:2.9) Morontia Power Supervisors can modify primary units of matter to create new morontia substance on architectural spheres with unique energy organization. (48:1.3) Morontia planners collaborate with Morontia Power Supervisors to enhance the coordination of progressive morontia life. (44:3.7) Morontia Power Supervisors, offspring of a local universe Mother Spirit, require no training for their specific function. (48:2.2) The energy systems of Edentia and its surrounding spheres, directly fueled by universal space currents, are expertly supervised by Morontia Power Supervisors on Jerusem and Edentia. (43:8.1) (46:5.26) Physical-energy manipulators of celestial artisans assist Morontia Power Supervisors in manipulating and controlling various forms of physical energy. (44:5.2) The Morontia Power Supervisors produce morontia substance and morontia mind, revealing unrevealed activities of the Master Spirits. (16:4.5) Morontia Power Supervisors provide unique morontia material for personalized morontia life to begin and evolve, mirroring the individuality of mortal forms. (42:12.11) Morontia Power Supervisors rarely serve on inhabited planets but sometimes work in connection with supermaterial phenomena on evolutionary worlds. (48:2.12) Self-governing Morontia Power Supervisors serve exclusively in their native universe, directed by the joint spirit activity of the Universe Son and the Universe Spirit. (48:2.11) Technician seraphim are liaisons with Morontia Power Supervisors, guiding ascending pilgrims through the energies of the transition worlds. (48:6.29) types
improvedDescription: Self-governing beings exclusively devoted to supervising activities that combine spiritual and physical energies, creating and maintaining the morontia environment for progressing ascenders. They are offspring of the local universe Mother Spirit, directed by the joint spirit activity of the Universe Son and Universe Spirit, and provide ascending mortals with 570 successive morontia bodies during their journey through the local universe.

name: morontia progressors
originalDescription: Throughout morontia progression, assurance of truth replaces assurance of faith, leading to pure spirit insight in the spirit world. (101:5.14) Morontia progressors rely on the Adjuster for self-consciousness as they transition from material to spiritual existence. (112:6.7) The pursuit of the ideal and striving to be Godlike is a continuous effort both before and after death, as life after death is no different in essentials from mortal existence. (103:5.7) As morontia progressors, you may enhance and customize your personal abodes on the mansion worlds while experiencing the increasing perfection and grandeur of the material and morontia structures. (48:3.15) Morontia progressors may visit a superuniverse upon attaining spirit status. (30:3.12) The morontia progressors of Endantum were witness to Michael's extraordinary and amazing career as a morontia mortal of ascending status on the fifth constellation's headquarters planet. (119:6) Morontia progressors traverse the transition spheres of mortal ascension, advancing in intellect, spirit, and personality form until they attain spirit status at the end of their local universe sojourn. (30:4.18-21,26) (47:7.1) (48:0.2) (48:8.1) Not one Jerusem citizen fell in Lucifer's rebellion, showcasing the unwavering loyalty of the ascending mortals who stood strong against the forces of evil. (53:7.10-12) No marriage between morontia progressors is needed in the worlds to come, for they are eternally the children of light resurrected into the progress of eternal life. (174:3.2) Morontia progressors are hosts to celestial visitors, providing ample opportunity to visit any realm experientially attained. (48:3.10) Morontia progressors, remaining in full contact with the material world, will discern and fraternize with spirit beings on their journey through transition realms. (48:2.26) Morontia progressors never lose the ability to recognize beings of previous levels as they ascend inward in the scale of life. (44:0.18) Translated souls from advanced planets in the fourth stage of light and life bypass the traditional morontia progression, heading straight to universe headquarters for their ascension training. (55:2.8-10) (55:6.8) Morontia progressors utilize material and semimaterial means for atmospheric passage on architectural spheres like Edentia. (43:1.2) Morontia progressors, as three-natured beings, advance intellect, spirit, and personality form within the local universe, under its care, before moving on to the superuniverse. (30:4.19) morontia bodies training
improvedDescription: Ascending mortals who traverse the transition spheres from mansion world graduation until attaining spirit status in the superuniverse career. These three-natured beings advance in intellect, spirit, and personality form throughout their morontia experience in the local universe, serving as the evolutionary portal to spirit life and eventual creature perfection.

name: morontia recorders
originalDescription: Morontia recorders are celestial artisans who save vital scenes and associations of progressive epochs in the archives of the transition worlds. (44:4.12)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who function as group photographers of the transition worlds, preserving vital scenes and associations of progressive morontia epochs in the archives of morontia halls of records.

name: morontia synthesizers
originalDescription: The morontia synthesizers are the master craftsmen who add the culminating and finishing touches to achieve an inspiring portrayal of the divinely beautiful in the morontia ensemble, an enduring inspiration to spirit beings and their morontia associates. (44:6.9)
improvedDescription: Master craftsmen among the celestial artisans who add the culminating and finishing touches to morontia ensembles, achieving inspiring portrayals of divine beauty that serve as enduring inspiration to spirit beings and their morontia associates.

name: morontia temple
originalDescription: The morontia temple appears when a planet enters light and life, marking the beginning of a new era of evolutionary attainment and spiritual growth. (40:3.1) (55:1) The Morontia temple, built by Morontia Power Supervisors and Master Physical Controllers, seats about 300,000 spectators for special planet ceremonies, cosmic contemplation, and graduation exercises. (55:1.3-4) Morontia temples are dedicated to special ceremonies, not for worship, play, or receiving broadcasts, accommodating up to three hundred thousand spectators for a variety of spiritual and cosmic events. (55:1.4) The morontia temple is where the Planetary Sovereign, finaliters, and Paradise bestowal Son are visible to spiritual mortals. (55:1.2) (55:6.6) (55:7.2) After the appearance of the morontia temple, finaliters actively participate in world affairs alongside the Trinity Teacher Sons and superangels. (55:1.2) (55:4.1-2,14) The order of ‘supreme service’ is the only degree granted in the average morontia temple, where ceremonies recognizing outstanding achievements are held. (55:1.4) (55:3.8) Translation of living mortals takes place in the morontia temple, where they witness the ascension to the morontia existence. (55:1.5) (55:2.3-5) The translation temple, composed of morontia materials, provides a place of assembly for witnessing the transformation of living mortals to the morontia existence without being destroyed by consuming fire. (55:1.5) The Trinity Teacher Sons inaugurate the era of light and life on a world when they establish a morontia temple, signaling its admission to the settled ages of evolution. (55:1.1) (55:7.1)
improvedDescription: A magnificent structure that appears when a planet enters the era of light and life, serving as the headquarters for planetary ceremonies, translation of living mortals, and cosmic contemplation. Built by Morontia Power Supervisors according to approved plans, these temples mark the admission of a sphere to the settled ages of evolutionary attainment.

name: morontia world
originalDescription: The second transitional culture world serves as the central hub for the supervision and training of morontia beings and ascending mortals. (45:1.3-4)
improvedDescription: The second transitional culture world of Jerusem that serves as headquarters for the supervisors of morontia life, surrounded by seven training spheres where morontia chiefs educate their associates and ascending mortals.

name: morontia world transition ministers
improvedDescription: Specialized ministers who assist with the transition processes on morontia worlds, helping ascending mortals adapt to their new environments and progressive spiritual development.

name: morontia worlds
originalDescription: Edentia and its associated worlds have a harmonious atmosphere, a three-gas mixture including one tailored for morontia beings' respiration, fostering a serene environment devoid of extreme weather and seasonal changes, perfect for creating beautiful outdoor spaces. (43:1.3) (46:2.2-3) On the architectural worlds like Edentia, there are ten divisions of material life, including unique forms of animal and plant life that surpass the imagination of mortal minds. (43:6.4-8) The morontia worlds serve as the transition phases for mortal ascension through the progression worlds of the local universe, encompassing the 56 system transition abodes circling local system headquarters planet, as well as the spheres of constellation and local universe headquarters worlds. (48:1.2) Morontia worlds are made-to-order architectural spheres with double the elements of evolved planets, abundant in heavy metals and crystals, and featuring a unique energy organization of one hundred forms of morontia material. (48:1.3) The constellation capitals are the acme of morontia living embellishments, known as “the gardens of God” for their botanic artistry. (43:6.1) Morontia Power Supervisors creatively combine material and spiritual energies to produce morontia substance and mind, bridging the gap between the physical and spiritual realms. (16:4.5,6) (48:2.1) Morontia worlds are architectural spheres with 100 physical elements and 100 unique forms of energy organization known as morontia material. (48:1.3) (48:2.22) Living things on morontia worlds thrive on moisture provided by the subsoil circulation system, with no rainfall but daily condensation during cooler temperatures, nourishing both plant life and the inhabitants of these architectural spheres. (46:2.2) The morontia transition on Nebadon’s typical worlds is an intermediary step for surviving human beings before reaching a spirit status. (48:0.3) On morontia worlds, vocation builders ensure necessary economy and division of labor for the specialized ministry and construction of spirit abodes. (44:3.3) On world number four, ascending mortals discover the true social life of morontia creatures, founded on appreciation and mutual service, leading them towards a common destiny of divine perfection. (47:6.3) The univitatia, spiritually alike yet intellectually varying like mortals, resemble the morontia state and function in seventy orders, each exhibiting ten variations of intellect, overseeing training on satellites around Edentia worlds; settled in light, they deal directly with superuniverse government in Havona and Paradise relationships, while overseeing the administration of constellation morontia worlds. (43:7.2) (55:9.2)
improvedDescription: Architectural spheres serving as transition phases for mortal ascension through the local universe, including the 56 system transition abodes, constellation spheres, and universe headquarters worlds. These made-to-order worlds contain 100 physical elements and 100 forms of unique morontia material, bridging the gulf between material and spiritual realms.

name: morontians
improvedDescription: Morontia beings or entities progressing through the transitional stages between material and spiritual existence.

name: Morrison beds
originalDescription: The Morrison beds of Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming contain abundant fresh-water fossils, indicating a fresh-water age with many inland lakes and combined salt- and fresh-water deposits of 2,000 to 5,000 feet in thickness, with little limestone. (60:2.5)
improvedDescription: Geological formations in Colorado, Montana, and Wyoming containing abundant fresh-water fossils from ancient inland lakes, with combined salt- and fresh-water deposits ranging from 2,000 to 5,000 feet in thickness and very little limestone present.

name: Mortal Corps of the Finality
improvedDescription: A corps of perfected mortals who have achieved the ultimate of creature evolution and serve in the eternal purposes of the Universal Father.

name: mortal mind
originalDescription: The universe is intelligible because man's mind can only perceive mind phenomena of other minds, suggesting a divine mind and personality concealed within it. (1:6.7) Ideas and concepts of Jesus' life and teachings originating in mortal mind are more acceptable and helpful to all human minds. (121:8.12-14) The mortal mind can achieve spiritual insight and divine values through the evidence of spirit indwelling manifested in humanitarian fellowship, interpretation of the universe, and spiritual evaluation of life. (196:3.6-9) The evolution of mortal mind on Urantia brought great joy and supreme satisfaction to the Life Carriers. (62:6) (65:7) The immeasurable gulf between material mind and spiritual love cannot be explained by any mechanistic theory. (112:2.10) In Jesus, mortal mind was transformed into the mind of God, leading to a period of reflection on the entire history of humanity on Earth. (136:4.5) The quality of mind determines the reception of culture; higher intellects pursue noble culture while inferior minds reject even the highest offerings. (50:6.4) The Father bestows human personality, while the Infinite Spirit is the absolute source of mortal mind, leading to a deeper understanding of the Eternal Son's spiritual personality. (6:8.5) Your mind is the ship, the Adjuster is the pilot, and your will is the captain guiding your soul towards eternal survival. (111:1.9) Jesus delivered discourse on mortal mind at Cyprus, revealing the potential of human self-consciousness and unity with the divine spirit. (133:7) Jesus enters mortal mind to unify, elevate, transform, and transfigure, bringing about a new creation within. (100:7.18) The moral nature of humanity is only comprehensible when acknowledging the existence of the Universal Father, as the source of higher orders of thought and purposeful will in a unified universe ruled by one God. (3:6.3) Children first learn to think out loud and converse with imaginary companions, a technique that carries into adulthood where much thinking is done in conversational form. (91:3.1) The pure mind discerns God through spiritual insight, not emotions or feelings, leading man Godward in the realm of highest and most spiritualized thinking. (101:1.3) The reflective powers deepened by worship illuminate destiny more than prayer in religious experience. (102:4.5) Through the transformations of indwelling spirit, you are empowered to embody the perfect will of God, becoming liberated sons of the spirit with the divine nature dwelling within you. (143:2.4) (144:5.76) Through the mediation of mind, the self and environment establish meaningful contact, reflecting the attitude of the whole personality. (112:1.15) After physical death, the mortal mind's meanings and values persist in the morontia estate alongside the ascended soul, held by the Adjuster and recorded in the local universe. (112:6.4) Adjusters nature of mortal mind primitive mind subconscious mind superconscious mind
improvedDescription: The intellectual capacity and consciousness of evolutionary human beings, characterized by its ability to recognize divine realities, evolve spiritually through Adjuster cooperation, and serve as the foundation for morontia soul development. The mortal mind is the cosmic loom that carries the morontia fabrics on which the indwelling Thought Adjuster weaves patterns of eternal character and divine meanings.

name: mortals
originalDescription: creation after death origin relation with God human nature | flesh planetary-mortal epochs | planetary epochs planetary types primitive mortals | planetary epochs:1st–pre-Planetary Prince era; religion:primitive religion role of adjutant mind-spirits | adjutant mind-spirits Son-fused mortals | Son-fused mortals Spirit-fused mortals | Spirit-fused mortals spiritual influences impinging upon mortals | spiritual influences
improvedDescription: Animal-origin evolutionary beings of ascendant potential who represent the lowest order of intelligent and personal creation in the universe. Mortals are divinely beloved creatures who may choose to accept the destiny of eternal survival and ascension to Paradise through fusion with their indwelling Thought Adjusters.

name: Moses
originalDescription: Moses is one of 24 counselors in Jerusem. He led a remnant of the submerged violet race and revived worship of the Universal Father as “The God of Israel.” (45:4.14) in Egypt character about the seat of Moses see Sanhedrin | Sanhedrin teachings
improvedDescription: The great Hebrew leader, teacher, and organizer who emancipated a remnant of the submerged violet race from Egypt and established monotheistic worship of the Universal Father as 'The God of Israel.' Moses was an extraordinary combination of military leader, social organizer, and religious teacher who laid the foundation for the Hebrew nation and perpetuation of their race.

name: Most High
originalDescription: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty, where there is no limitation to the forces and personalities used to uphold his purpose. (4:1.4) The Semite appellation of Deity as The Most High is one of many names used to refer to God in ancient times. (96:1.9)
improvedDescription: A divine title and designation for Deity, particularly used by ancient Semites as one of many appellations for God, referring to the supreme spiritual authority under whose shadow believers find protection and refuge.

name: Most High Assistants
originalDescription: Most High Assistants assist Nebadon Paradise personalities with advanced and complex plans and rulings, serving as invaluable resources to universe rulers and administrators. (37:4.5) The Most High Assistants, high celestial volunteers from outside Nebadon, serve as central and superuniverse representatives or observers, aiding in bringing Nebadon into harmony with Orvonton and Paradise ideals. (37:4) Most High Assistants are count as part of the Universe Aids group (37:1.5) Most High Assistants are recorded in Uversa's personality register. (30:2.144) Many tertiaphim function as Most High Assistants, providing temporary help from higher spirits outside the local creation. (37:8.10) Most High Assistants operate within local universe and constellation roles without ties to system or planetary governments, fulfilling a wide range of functions in Nebadon activities. (37:4.4) Millions of Most High Assistants from beyond the local universe serve as representatives in local creations. (37:4.1) The Most High Assistants, supervised by the Union of Days, operate in Nebadon under the oversupervision of the ambassador of the Paradise Trinity, following their assigned mission and regulations of the local universe. (37:4.3)
improvedDescription: A corps of high celestial volunteers from outside Nebadon who serve as central and superuniverse representatives and observers in the local universe. They assist Paradise personalities with advanced plans and rulings, helping to bring Nebadon into harmony with Orvonton and Paradise ideals.

name: Most High observer of Urantia
originalDescription: The Most High observer of Urantia, who recently assumed emergency authority on the planet, immediately began his administration with a chosen staff, but did not assign duties to the three Personalized Adjusters who appeared in his presence. (109:7.7) The Most High observer of Urantia, trustee of Urantia sovereignty, rules in the kingdoms of men with authority and wisdom. (43:5.8,16-17) (114:4)
improvedDescription: The current Vorondadek Son who serves as planetary observer and emergency regent of Urantia, being the twenty-third to serve in this capacity. As trustee of planetary sovereignty, he rules in the kingdoms of men and can exercise absolute authority over all celestial beings on the planet during times of crisis.

name: Most High observers
originalDescription: Most High observers are empowered to exercise absolute and arbitrary authority over every celestial being on a planet and seize planetary government in times of crisis. (35:5.5-6) (114:4.4) The instrumentality of Most Highs’ rule in kingdoms of men is exercised by Vorondadek observers on isolated worlds, such as Urantia. (43:5.16-17) Vorondadek Sons serve as ambassadors, consuls, and Most High observers in local universes, exercising sovereign powers in critical situations, including the rare ability to exercise absolute authority over celestial beings on dark, isolated planets. (35:5.5-6)
improvedDescription: Vorondadek Sons who serve as planetary observers on quarantined or isolated worlds, empowered to exercise absolute authority over all celestial beings and seize planetary government during times of grave crisis. They act as the instrumentality through which the Most Highs rule in the kingdoms of men.

name: Most Highs
originalDescription: The Most Highs, Vorondadek Sons, are the Constellation Fathers of each architectural headquarters sphere presiding over a hundred systems. (15:2.4) (20:1.10) (35:5.1) (35:6.1) (43:3.1-2) Three Vorondadek Sons rule each constellation for 150,000 years, with the Constellation Father reigning for 50,000 years. (15:2.4) (35:6.1) (43:3.1-2) The Faithful of Days advises the Most Highs of a constellation in counsel and guidance. (43:4.2) Gabriel commissions three Vorondadeks to serve as Most Highs in each constellation for one dekamillennium. (35:6.1) The Most Highs, exalted in constellation light and life, will attain true dignity as rulers of autonomous unified constellations, dealing directly with superuniverse rulers. (55:9.3) The Most Highs, with veto power over local system affairs, ensure a self-governing system of inhabited worlds under the authority of the new system conclave, subject to the Constellation Fathers. (55:8.1) The Most Highs focus on group welfare over individual progress in the rule of the kingdoms of men. (43:3.5) (43:9.4) The rule of the Most Highs is not biased towards any group, but aims to promote the greatest good for the most people over the longest period. (134:5.4) Constellation Fathers government
improvedDescription: The Most Highs are Vorondadek Sons who serve as Constellation Fathers, ruling over constellations of one hundred systems each. Three Most Highs govern each constellation for 150,000 years, with the senior Most High serving as Constellation Father for 50,000 years before rotating positions. They embody the highest administrative wisdom and loyalty among all Local Universe Sons of God, focusing on group welfare and the greatest good for the greatest number over the longest period of time.

name: Most Highs of Norlatiadek
originalDescription: The Ancients of Days confirmed and continue to uphold the assumption of control by the Edentia Most Highs over the rebellious worlds of Satania, including jurisdiction over the wicked rebel Lucifer. (43:3.7) (53:7.14) The Most Highs of Norlatiadek broadcast to local systems daily, offering continual foretastes of advancing beauty and grandeur to mortal survivors on their eternal journey inward. (46:3.3) Children of Adam under 20 became wards of the Most Highs of Norlatiadek, as they chose to go to Edentia with their parents or remain on Urantia. (75:6.3) (77:5.3) Vorondadek Son #617,318 is the Constellation Father in Norlatiadek, having served in various constellations in Nebadon before assuming his current role. (43:5.3) The Faithful of Days advised the Most Highs of Norlatiadek to allow rebels free course so that evil may be swiftly uprooted. (54:5.9) The headquarters of the Most Highs of Norlatiadek is the massive architectural sphere of Edentia, centerpiece of a cluster of 771 spheres that house the government of the constellation. (43:0.2) Lucifer's disaffection, known by the Most Highs of Norlatiadek 500 years before rebellion, originated in his own mind without suggestion from any other being, despite his intimate and cordial relations with the Creator Son. (53:2.3) Lanaforge's message to the Constellation Father of Norlatiadek assured that every ascendant mortal from Jerusem emerged triumphant and victorious from the fiery trial, a security against rebellion and safeguard against sin. (53:7.12) Melchizedek introduced the concept of El Elyon, the Most High, as the universal Deity to Abraham and his followers, who believed in this expanded idea of God. (93:3.2) (142:3.4) During his time in the tomb on Urantia, the Constellation Father of Norlatiadek received instructions from Michael. (188:3.15) The Most Highs of Norlatiadek find residence in Jerusem circles during visits of inspection to the local system. (46:5.15) The rotation of the Most Highs on Edentia remains suspended from the time of the Lucifer rebellion, indicating their continuous reign until Lucifer and his associates are dealt with. (43:5.1) The Most Highs of Norlatiadek maintain supervision over Satania, where no changes have been made in system administration since the upheaval, with Lanaforge showing great interest in world welfare. (45:2.2,4) actions
improvedDescription: The Most Highs of Norlatiadek are the three Vorondadek Sons who rule the constellation of Norlatiadek from their headquarters on Edentia. They assumed special jurisdiction over the rebellious worlds of Satania during the Lucifer rebellion and continue to exercise this authority with the support of the Ancients of Days. Their rotation has been suspended since the rebellion, with the current Constellation Father being Vorondadek Son number 617,318.

name: Most Holy Sphere
originalDescription: The Most Holy Sphere on upper Paradise, reserved for worship and spiritual attainment, is a realm of divine beauty and purely spiritual reality. (11:3.1-2) There are no physical materializations on the Most Holy Sphere, but souvenirs of earthly days can be found in the Holy Land sectors and historic areas of peripheral Paradise. (11:3.2) The Most Holy Sphere on upper Paradise is reserved for worship, trinitization, and high spiritual attainment. (11:3.1) Trinitized Sons of God have their sole homes on the Isle of Paradise near the Most Holy Sphere. (13:2.4)
improvedDescription: The Most Holy Sphere is one of three grand spheres of activity on upper Paradise, reserved for worship, trinitization, and high spiritual attainment. This realm of pure spiritual reality surrounds the Deity presence and contains no material structures or intellectual creations, only divine beauty and spiritual grandeur. Trinity-origin beings have their homes on Paradise in close proximity to this sacred sphere.

name: mota
originalDescription: definition effect mota levels
improvedDescription: Mota is morontia wisdom, a superphilosophical sensitivity for truth discernment and unity perception that bridges the gap between material science and spiritual religion. It is the understanding technique of the morontia level, combining knowledge-reason with faith-insight to provide cosmic perspective and reconcile divergent reality perceptions that material personalities cannot achieve.

name: mote
originalDescription: Save men, show mercy, and worship in spirit and truth, not judge or pluck mote from brother's eye. (140:3.17) (143:5.6)
improvedDescription: A mote refers to a small speck or particle, used metaphorically by Jesus in his teaching about not judging others while having greater faults oneself. He taught his followers to first remove the beam from their own eye before attempting to remove the mote from their brother's eye, emphasizing mercy and self-examination over criticism of others.

name: mother cult
originalDescription: The Danubians led Nordics into mother worship, fostering a tradition of cremation and cultural advancement. (80:9.4) During the age in Crete, the mother cult of the descendants of Cain reached its peak in popularity through the worship of Eve as the “great mother.” (80:7.7) The Danubians, influenced by Crete missionaries and Andonite sailors, practiced mother worship and cremated their dead in stone huts. (80:8.5) The Hellenic Greeks found the Mediterranean world largely dominated by the mother cult, but they imposed their man-god Dyaus-Zeus as head of the Greek pantheon, on the brink of achieving true monotheism. (98:1.3) The priests of the mother cult set the example by submitting to castration, teaching the virtue of physical suffering embraced by Hebrews, Hindus, and Buddhists. (89:3.4)
improvedDescription: The mother cult was an ancient religious movement that glorified feminine deities and practiced mother worship, reaching its peak during the age in Crete through worship of Eve as the "great mother." This cult spread throughout the Mediterranean world, influencing various cultures including the Danubians and Greeks, and incorporated practices such as cremation of the dead and physical self-discipline including castration among its priests.

name: Mother Eves
improvedDescription: Mother Eves refer to the female Material Sons, also known as Material Daughters, who serve alongside Material Sons as biologic uplifters on evolutionary worlds. They are created beings who function as Adams and Eves to improve the biological status of mortal races on inhabited planets.

name: mother force of space
originalDescription: it is one of the names for absoluta
improvedDescription: The mother force of space is an alternative name for absoluta, representing the primordial space-force that serves as the foundation for all physical energy manifestations in the universe. This cosmic force is the source from which all material energy and matter ultimately derive.

name: Mother of God cult
originalDescription: The Salem gospel struggled to take hold among the tribes due to their deep-rooted adherence to ancient cults like the Great Mother. (93:7.3) The mysterious cult of the Mother of God was the greatest and most destructive of Roman mystery cults, destined for collapse despite its formal and unemotional nature. (98:3.5) The influence of the Mother of God cult on Christianity is seen in the parallels between the miraculous birth of Jesus and the Iranian savior-hero, Mithras. (98:7.7) The popularity of Ishtar, the mother of gods and spirit of sex fertility, persisted despite efforts to refine her worship and the universal practice of women submitting to strangers' embrace in devotion to her. (95:1.5) The Mithraic Great Mother ritual for women evolved from the male-only religion with seven orders, incorporating elements of the Phrygian cult of Cybele. (98:5.5) The sacrament of cakes and wine replaced human sacrifices in the Mother of God cult, originating from Hebrew Passover ceremonies and later adapted by Christians. (89:9.2) Tibetans pray to an overgrown brotherhood of priests with rigid dogmas, mystic rites, and elaborate rituals, including a Holy Mother. (94:10.2)
improvedDescription: The Mother of God cult was a mystery religion centered on the worship of a divine feminine figure, with its headquarters located on the site of present-day St. Peter's in Rome. This formal but emotionally powerful cult incorporated elements from various traditions including Mithraism and the Phrygian cult of Cybele, practiced sacraments of cakes and wine, and significantly influenced early Christianity through parallels with the veneration of Mary.

name: Mother-Son
improvedDescription: Mother-Son appears to be an alternative designation for the Eternal Son in relation to the Universal Father, emphasizing the maternal-paternal relationship within the Paradise Trinity. The Eternal Son represents the spiritual and personal aspects of deity in conjunction with the Father's universal presence.

name: Mother Spirits
improvedDescription: Divine feminine beings who serve as creative partners and administrators in local universes, working in conjunction with Creator Sons to foster spiritual development and coordinate universe activities.

name: mothers
originalDescription: Believers must love gospel more than family to be worthy of the kingdom. (150:4.3) (163:3.4) (171:2.2) Children derive first impressions of the universe from their mother's care and are dependent on their earthly father for their first ideas of the heavenly Father, shaping their subsequent life and afterlife. (177:2.5) Devoted mother love, not sex gratification, drove the early establishment of home maintenance by primitive men and women, as females of all higher animals share this sentiment. (68:2.6) Mothers forget anguish of childbirth, just as Jesus promised sorrow turning into joy and a new revelation of salvation. (180:6.7) Mother love is a powerful, instinctive emotion that has always placed women at a disadvantage in their struggles with men. (84:1.6-7) Whoever does the will of my Father is Jesus' true family. (154:6.5)
improvedDescription: Human women who bear and nurture children, providing crucial early impressions of the universe through their care and forming the foundation for children's understanding of both earthly and divine relationships. Mother love is described as a powerful, instinctive emotion that drives the establishment and maintenance of homes and families.

name: motion
originalDescription: Absolute motion is defined as motion in relation to Paradise, while primary and secondary motions differ as they are not evaluated with Paradise as a base point. (12:4.10) All cosmic energy is in constant motion except for Paradise, the center of gravity in the master universe. (12:4.1) Motion and mind can attenuate the action of gravity on mass. (42:11.5) Motion is determined through Paradise relationship, the eternal Isle of Paradise serving as the foundation for gravity control throughout the universes. (105:3.4) Motion is a complement of gravity in the coordination of space forces and tensions within the universes. (12:4.16) Infinite Spirit is the apparent ancestor of motion and change. (9:1.1) Opinions concerning origin of motion vary, with some attributing it to the Conjoint Actor and others to the force organizers in outer space. (12:4.2-6) The universal economy is based on intake and output, with progress made possible by inherent motion and achievement being the child of imaginative adventure, all under the responsibility of ethics in a world filled with diverse beings. (28:6.18)
improvedDescription: The fundamental activity of cosmic energy throughout the master universe, with all units of energy in constant revolution around universal orbits. Motion is classified as absolute when measured in relation to Paradise, while space itself exists relative to the stationary Paradise Isle, and the chronicity of motion is determined through this Paradise relationship.

name: motivation
improvedDescription: The underlying driving forces and purposes that compel action and behavior in conscious beings, representing the spiritual content that validates conduct and reveals the true character of individuals.

name: motives
originalDescription: Despite receiving patient instruction and loving warnings about their critical tendencies and pride, Caligastia and Lucifer misconstrued helpful advice as unwarranted criticism, projecting their own evolving selfishness onto their well-meaning advisors. (66:8.2) Eve believed Cano's reassurance that those with good motives would not commit evil. (75:4.5) Do not prejudge others’ motives; leave judgment to those with superior spiritual status. (38:2.4) (133:1.2) The motive for gaining entrance into the kingdom of heaven is what truly matters, as it is by their fruits that false prophets are known. (140:3.19) God judges based on motives, not outward actions, emphasizing the intimate parent-child relationship with divine love and mercy. (103:4.5) (131:9.3) (140:6.4-5) (140:10.5) (166:1.4) The motivating thought behind prayer validates supplication; words hold no value. (7:3.7) (146:2.8) Jesus' merciful intercession for his executioners on the cross revealed a lifetime of unselfish service and affectionate devotion. (187:2.4) Primitives rendered judgment based on injury done, disregarding the motives of transgressors. (70:10.3) Secoraphic Discerners of Spirits and Sanctities of Service reveal the true motives of individuals through reflective scrutiny, laying bare the deep motives of the human heart and angelic mind. (28:5.19) (28:6.19) To understand others, fathom their motives, and discover the true values of life through love and empathy. (100:4.4-5) In the realm of wisdom, the Universal Censors rule as to motives with divine guidance and discernment. (28:5.9) Beliefs, not knowledge, dictate behavior and performance, particularly when emotionally activated through religious engagement. (99:4.5)
improvedDescription: The inner purposes and intentions behind actions that determine the moral value of conduct. God judges based on motives rather than outward acts, emphasizing that the spiritual content and sincere intentions of the heart matter more than external appearances or deeds.

name: mottos
improvedDescription: Brief phrases or statements that encapsulate guiding principles or beliefs, serving as memorable expressions of fundamental truths or values.

name: Mount Ararat
originalDescription: Mount Ararat, akin to Mount Sinai for the Hebrews, was the sacred mountain of the Vanites, with traditions dating back ten thousand years. (77:4.11-12) Mount Ararat is the sacred Vanite mountain in northern Mesopotamia, where ancient traditions and moral laws were believed to have originated. (77:4.11-12)
improvedDescription: The sacred mountain of northern Mesopotamia revered by the Vanites, where ancient traditions claim their moral law of seven commandments was given to Van by the Gods. This mountain held significance similar to Mount Sinai for the Hebrews and became woven into later flood narratives.

name: Mount Assembly
originalDescription: The most holy mount of assembly is the dwelling place of the Faithful of Days, representative of the Paradise Trinity on Edentia, in constant communication with all universes. (43:4) Morontia mortals assemble on Mount Assembly to hear the Faithful of Days share the journey of progressing mortals through Havona and on to Paradise. (43:4.5)
improvedDescription: The most holy mount on Edentia where the Faithful of Days resides, serving as the dwelling place of the Paradise Trinity representative. Morontia mortals gather here to hear about the journey through Havona and on to Paradise.

name: Mount Carmel
originalDescription: John the Baptist, at sixteen, was greatly impressed by the prophet of Mount Carmel Elijah's dress style and decided to adopt it, always wearing a hairy garment with a leather girdle. (135:1.4)
improvedDescription: A mountain ridge in Palestine running down to the sea, associated with the prophet Elijah who confronted the priests of Baal. John the Baptist was inspired by Elijah's connection to Mount Carmel and adopted his style of dress, wearing a hairy garment with a leather girdle.

name: Mount Gerizim
originalDescription: A vacillating Roman ruler sacrificed Jesus and was deposed after a false Messiah led troops to Mount Gerizim. (185:1.6) Jesus and the apostles camped on Mount Gerizim, preaching the good news of the kingdom and preparing souls for the fatherhood of God. (143:6.2-6) The Samaritans worshiped Yahweh on Mount Gerizim, but their temple was destroyed by John Hyrcanus. (143:4.2) (143:5.5) Worship not on Mount Gerizim, but where you are, in spirit and in truth, for it is your faith that saves your souls. (193:1.2)
improvedDescription: A sacred mountain in Samaria where the Samaritans built a temple to worship Yahweh until it was destroyed by John Hyrcanus. Jesus and his apostles camped here while preaching to the Samaritans, and it was the site where Jesus taught that true worship occurs in spirit and truth rather than at any specific location.

name: Mount Gilboa
improvedDescription: A mountain referenced in The Urantia Book, likely the biblical Mount Gilboa located in northern Israel.

name: Mount Hermon
originalDescription: Mount Hermon, glistening with perpetual snow, stood majestically in the north as Jesus and Joseph took their Sabbath afternoon walks on the high hill near their home in Nazareth. (123:5.12) (124:6.5) Mount Hermon, visible from Caesarea-Philippi, held a special significance for Jesus and his apostles during their final journey together. (157:3.1-2) Jesus
improvedDescription: A majestic snow-capped mountain north of Galilee where Jesus underwent his great temptation and met Caligastia before his baptism. Mount Hermon was visible from Nazareth and Caesarea-Philippi, serving as a significant location for Jesus's ministry and the site where he established headquarters during his forty-day retirement to formulate plans for proclaiming the kingdom of God.

name: Mount Hood
originalDescription: ten million years ago, Mount Hood began its mountain career alongside Shasta and Rainier amid widespread local land deposits on the lowlands of continents. (61:4.2)
improvedDescription: A North American mountain that began forming ten million years ago during an age of widespread land deposits, alongside Mount Shasta and Mount Rainier.

name: Mount Horeb
improvedDescription: A mountain referenced in The Urantia Book, also known as Mount Sinai in biblical tradition.

name: Mount Lebanon
originalDescription: A group of twenty-five teachers of truth left Caesarea-Philippi and journeyed to the Phoenician coast, passing Mount Lebanon along the way. (155:4.1)
improvedDescription: A mountain range in the Phoenician region that twenty-five teachers of truth passed while traveling from Caesarea-Philippi to the Phoenician coast.

name: Mount of Olives
improvedDescription: A mountain east of Jerusalem, also known as Mount Olivet, significant in Jesus's life and ministry.

name: mount of transfiguration
improvedDescription: The location where Jesus's transfiguration occurred, associated with Mount Hermon where he took his apostles to witness his divine authority as the Son of God.

name: Mount Olivet
originalDescription: Jesus' first view of the Mount of Olives filled his heart with joyous anticipation of soon beholding the city and house of his heavenly Father. (124:6.8-10) As a youth, Jesus vowed to return and cleanse the temple atop Mount Olivet. (125:4.2) (125:6.9) Gethsemane on Mount Olivet was a sacred haven where Jesus and his apostles resided in peaceful seclusion during their time in Jerusalem. (142:8.4) The commercial traffic at Mount Olivet centered around selling sacrificial animals purchased from the temple. (173:1.1) On the brow of Mount Olivet, Jesus wept over Jerusalem's missed opportunity for peace and salvation. (125:4.2) (172:3.10) On Mount Olivet Tuesday evening, Jesus foretold the destruction of the temple and warned his apostles of the trials and tribulations to come, instructing them to remain faithful and prepared for his eventual return. (176) Joseph of Arimathea’s tomb on Mount Olivet became the resting place for Jesus, defying Jewish burial laws and revealing their secret discipleship. (188:1.2) The last group prayer on Mount Olivet was a sacred and uplifting moment as Jesus lifted his eyes toward heaven in the mellow moonlight. (182:1.2) Jesus spent three hours at Mount Olivet's palace of Annas, near Gethsemane, where he was arrested, while John Zebedee resided safely at the palace due to his familiarity with the older servants and family ties to the former high priest. (184:0.3) Jesus bid his farewell to his apostles on Mount Olivet, the site of his ascension from. (193:5.1)
improvedDescription: A mountain east of Jerusalem where Jesus experienced many significant moments, including his first view of the Holy City as a youth, his weeping over Jerusalem, his teachings to the apostles about the temple's destruction, his final prayers in Gethsemane, and his ascension. The Mount of Olives served as Jesus's primary residence area during his final ministry in Jerusalem.

name: Mount Olympus
originalDescription: The happy-go-lucky Greek gods of Mount Olympus were never revered or feared by the intelligent Greeks, who viewed them as more human than divine. (98:1.4)
improvedDescription: The legendary home of the Greek gods, whom the intelligent Greeks viewed as happy-go-lucky deities more human than divine, never greatly revered or feared.

name: Mount Rainier
originalDescription: Rainier began its mountain career during an age of widespread land deposits on the continents ten million years ago. (61:4.2)
improvedDescription: A North American mountain that began forming ten million years ago during an age of widespread land deposits, alongside Mount Shasta and Mount Hood.

name: Mount Royal
originalDescription: Mount Royal in Montreal is the eroded neck of a volcanic activity that occurred 230 million years ago. (59:4.16)
improvedDescription: Mount Royal in Montreal is the eroded neck of a volcano that was active 230 million years ago during a period of great volcanic activity in the St. Lawrence region.

name: Mount Sartaba
originalDescription: A 3 day vacation on Mount Sartaba brought much-needed peace and rest to the apostles amidst growing tensions and emotional turmoil. (143:3.1) Near the Jabbok River, travelers reached Mount Sartaba, the site of Herod's fortress where he imprisoned his wife and buried his sons. (124:6.6) On their second day's journey, they camped near the base of Mount Sartaba, the highest mountain overlooking the Jordan valley. (124:6.6)
improvedDescription: Mount Sartaba is the highest mountain overlooking the Jordan valley, site of Herod's Alexandrian fortress where he imprisoned one of his wives and buried his strangled sons. Jesus took his apostles there for a three-day period of rest and relaxation during a time of great tension among the disciples.

name: Mount Seraph
originalDescription: Mount Seraph, at almost fifteen thousand feet, is the highest elevation on Jerusem and the point of departure for all transport seraphim. (46:2.8)
improvedDescription: Mount Seraph is the highest elevation on Jerusem at almost fifteen thousand feet and serves as the point of departure for all transport seraphim, with mechanical developments providing initial energy for escaping planetary gravity.

name: Mount Shasta
originalDescription: Ten million years ago, Mount Shasta and Shasta began their mountain careers, with extensive land deposits and great fresh-water lakes forming in North America. (61:4.2)
improvedDescription: Mount Shasta began its mountain formation ten million years ago during a period when extensive land deposits and great fresh-water lakes were forming in western North America.

name: Mount Sinai
originalDescription: Mount Sinai, like Mount Ararat, held sacred significance to ancient cultures such as the Assyrians and Hebrews. (77:4.11-12) Egyptians worked iron ores from Mount Sinai, greatly enhancing their metalworking skill with the expertise of Andite artisans from the Euphrates valley. (80:6.3) Moses' advanced ceremonial worship and followers were kept intact by the violent eruption of Horeb during their sojourn at Mount Sinai. (96:4.5) Mount Sinai's intermittent volcanic activity up to 2000 B.C. awed the Bedouins and inspired the Hebrew Semites to worship Yahweh.
