(61:5.1-2) In A.D. 1, Jesus experienced a record-breaking snowstorm in Galilee, where two feet of snow fell, marking an eventful year in his life. (123:4.1)
improvedDescription: Frozen precipitation that played a significant role in geological changes and climate patterns. Increased snowfall in northern highlands led to glacier formation during the ice age, and Jesus experienced a record-breaking two-foot snowfall in Galilee during his lifetime.

name: social architects
originalDescription: Social Architects work to remove artificiality from relationships and promote genuine connections among beings in the universe. (39:3.4) Supervisor seraphim bring together efficient and agreeable working groups to enhance sincere social contacts and further the social evolution of universe creatures. (39:3.4-6)
improvedDescription: A class of seraphim who work to enhance sincere social contacts and promote genuine relationships among intelligent beings by removing artificiality from associations. They bring together suitable individuals to form efficient working groups and continue their ministry on mansion and morontia worlds.

name: social classes
originalDescription: The eventual obliteration of social classes is a fundamental aspect of an advancing civilization, achieved through intelligent manipulation of biologic, intellectual, and spiritual resources. (70:8) Flexible and shifting social classes are indispensable to an evolving civilization, as social stability from class becoming caste diminishes personal initiative and curtails individual development, preventing social cooperation. (70:8.13) The differentiation of social levels is insured by mankind's mental and physical inequality. (70:8.1) True religion is the only power that can increase the responsiveness of one social group to the needs of others, as lower social classes are no longer ignorant or politically helpless. (99:1.5) The upper classes viewed the free proletariat as worthless except for breeding purposes. (121:3.5) States resulting from conquest were inevitably stratified, leading to class struggles throughout history. (71:1.2) In Jesus' time, a small middle class emerged, providing moral backbone to the early Christian church and encouraging tradesmen to continue their crafts. (121:1.8) (121:3.4)
improvedDescription: Divisions in society based on mental and physical inequalities that naturally emerge in evolving civilizations. Flexible and shifting social classes are essential for civilization's progress, but when classes become rigid castes, they diminish personal initiative and prevent social cooperation.

name: socialism
originalDescription: Local governments in main continental nation of a nearby inhabited planet practice socialism through their socialistic enterprises in areas such as health, sanitation, and recreation. (72:7.1,3) Untrue teachings on socialism contradict the role of religion as a conservator of morals and stabilizer of society. (99:5.1)
improvedDescription: An economic and political system where local governments operate socialistic enterprises in areas like health, sanitation, and recreation. Some modern socialist teachings are criticized as contradicting religion's role as a conservator of morals and stabilizer of society.

name: society
improvedDescription: The collective organization of human beings living together in communities, characterized by shared institutions, relationships, and patterns of behavior that facilitate cooperation and mutual support.

name: sociology
originalDescription: Civilization faces peril when youth ignore essential fields like ethics, sociology, and philosophy. (111:4.4) Sociologists survey and leave people as found, just like the unlearned Galileans did with Jesus. (195:6.9)
improvedDescription: The study of society and social behavior, identified as one of the essential fields that youth must not neglect for civilization's welfare. Materialistic sociologists are contrasted with those who can inspire transformative social change through spiritual insight.

name: Socrates
originalDescription: Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle believed that virtue is knowledge and it is better to suffer injustice than to be guilty of it. (98:2.6) Socrates' ideals dominated the Western world through the fusion of Melchizedek teachings brought from Egypt, resulting in the Christian church's religious philosophy based on Jesus. (98:0.1)
improvedDescription: Ancient Greek philosopher who, along with Plato and Aristotle, taught that virtue is knowledge and that it is better to suffer injustice than to commit it. His ideals became foundational to Western philosophy and influenced the development of Christian religious thought.

name: sodium
originalDescription: Sodium and calcium, capable of light and energy locomotion, permeate space as wandering derelicts from solar elemental battles, with calcium escaping the solar interior by riding outgoing sunbeams. (41:6.1,3) sodium chloride | salt
improvedDescription: A chemical element capable of light and energy locomotion that permeates space as wandering atomic remnants from solar elemental battles. Sodium atoms can literally ride light beams across vast distances throughout space.

name: Sodom
originalDescription: Following the real surrender of personal ambitions, the fabricated narratives of destruction of Sodom were associated with the appearance of three celestial beings to Abraham. (93:6.7) (93:8.1) Abraham's diplomatic skills and military prowess led to the king of Sodom joining his confederation, paving the way for a powerful state in Palestine. (93:5.14) Lot went to Sodom for trade and husbandry, while Abraham plotted to conquer Canaan with the help of Melchizedek's influence. (93:5.9,12)
improvedDescription: An ancient city where Lot engaged in trade and animal husbandry, and whose king joined Abraham's military confederation. The city's destruction became associated with fabricated narratives linked to the appearance of three celestial beings to Abraham.

name: Sol Invictus
originalDescription: Mithras was exalted as the intercessor for the human race among the gods after slaying the sacred bull in his struggle with the god of darkness, the Mithraic sun-god, or Sol Invictus. (98:5.3)
improvedDescription: The sun-god of Mithraic religion, representing a degeneration of the Zoroastrian Ahura-Mazda deity concept. Sol Invictus was associated with Mithras, who was believed to be the surviving champion of the sun-god in his struggle against the god of darkness.

name: solar energy
originalDescription: Solar radiation penetrates space for millions upon millions of miles to energize, light, and heat distant planets, proving the suns of space are not very dense. (41:5)
improvedDescription: Light and energy streams that flow from suns across millions of miles through space to energize, light, and heat distant planets. The steady escape of these light-energies proves that suns are not very dense.

name: solar radiation
improvedDescription: Energy emitted by suns in the form of light and other electromagnetic radiation that travels through space to provide energy, light, and heat to planets across vast distances.

name: solar supergases
improvedDescription: A concept related to stellar formation and composition, mentioned in connection with solar phenomena.

name: solar systems
improvedDescription: Astronomical arrangements consisting of a central sun with orbiting planets and other celestial bodies, such as the Monmatia system containing Urantia.

name: Solemnities of Trust
originalDescription: Trustworthiness is the true measure of self-mastery, reflecting individuals' exact trustworthiness. (28:6.13-15) Trust is the crucial test and true measure of self-mastery and character for tertiary seconaphim assigned to Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number. (28:6.1,13-15)
improvedDescription: A specialized order of tertiary seconaphim who evaluate and measure trustworthiness of individuals, serving as living scales of character assessment for superuniverse administrators. These beings weigh candidates in the secoraphic balances to determine their capacity for increased responsibilities and trust.

name: solicitations
improvedDescription: Requests or appeals for support, assistance, or participation in various activities or causes.

name: Solitarington
originalDescription: Solitarington is the divine rendezvous of unrevealed beings, born from the conjoint acts of the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit. (13:1.15) Solitarington holds the secrets of trinitization and the intimate association of spiritual beings with the Trinity. (13:1.18) (22:7.1) Solitarington, home of Solitary Messengers and Universe Power Directors, is the rendezvous of unrevealed beings originating from the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit, embodying their traits and Spirit inheritance. (13:1.15-16)
improvedDescription: One of the seven sacred spheres of Paradise, serving as the divine rendezvous of unrevealed beings born from the conjoint acts of the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit. This world holds the secrets of trinitization and houses Solitary Messengers, Universe Power Directors, and numerous unrevealed orders of spirit personalities.

name: Solitary Messengers
originalDescription: The Melchizedeks teach that Inspired Trinity Spirits, similar to Solitary Messengers but distinct personalities, will eventually replace the depleting ranks of the latter, with Solitary Messengers uniquely capable of sensing their divine presence and even determining their classification or number. (19:5.4-8,10) (23:1.9) (108:3.9) Solitary Messengers are spiritually alike and equal, constantly transferred between services and known by number or personal name. (23:2.10) In personality registers, Solitary Messengers are a formidable force. (30:1.71) (30:2.67) Inspired Trinity Spirits will replace Solitary Messengers as taught by the Melchizedeks in Nebadon. (19:5.4) Mortals only recognize Solitary Messengers in Havona. (23:2.13) (48:2.26) nature headquarters service
improvedDescription: The first and senior order of Higher Personalities of the Infinite Spirit, serving as the personal and universal messenger corps of the Conjoint Creator. These formless spirit beings possess tremendous transit velocities and function alone throughout the universes, serving in roles ranging from Trinity messengers to explorers of undirected assignment and revelators of truth.

name: Solomon
originalDescription: The ancestry of Joseph, the father of Jesus, did not include David or Solomon and did not trace directly back to Adam. (122:1.1) Solomon, son of King David and Bathsheba, continued his father’s tyranny and taxation while building a temple, navy, and harem of 1000, following a legacy of corruption and power struggles in ancient Israel. (97:9.15-16)
improvedDescription: Son of King David and Bathsheba who continued his father's tyrannical rule over Israel. Solomon bankrupted the nation through lavish spending on building projects including a temple, palace complexes, and maintaining a navy and harem of nearly a thousand members.

name: Solomon’s Porch
originalDescription: Jesus taught in Solomon's Porch to announce his divinity to the Jewish people and the world, despite the Sanhedrin's fear of summoning him. (162:1.9) (162:7.1) (164:5)
improvedDescription: A section of the Jerusalem temple where Jesus frequently taught during his visits to the city. This location served as the setting for his official announcement of his divinity to the Jewish people and the world.

name: Solonia
originalDescription: Solonia, chief of Urantia planetary helper seraphim, facilitates the physical and intellectual upliftment of the material races on evolutionary worlds through her ministering work. (39:5) (51:3.5) Solonia reproved Adam and Eve for disobedience in the moonlit Garden, proclaiming the miscarriage of the divine plan. (51:3.5) (75:4.2,8) Solonia, the seraphic voice in the Garden, rebuked Adam and Eve for their departure from the ordained plan, illustrating how Urantian traditions often attribute supernatural events to the Lord God. (51:3.5) (73:7.5) (74:8.15) (75:8.8) (76:6.5) Solonia, the seraphic “voice in the Garden,” narrated and presented Papers 73-76. (73:7.5) (74:8.15) (75:8.8) (76:6.5)
improvedDescription: The seraphic chief of Urantia's planetary helper seraphim who served during the Adamic mission. Known as the "voice in the Garden," Solonia reproved Adam and Eve for their disobedience and proclaimed the miscarriage of the divine plan following their default.

name: Soma
originalDescription: Soma is a secondary Hindu deity among the ancient gods of the Aryans. (94:4.6)
improvedDescription: A secondary Hindu deity from among the ancient gods of the Aryans, persisting in the Hindu pantheon alongside other Vedic deities such as Agni and Indra.

name: Somme River
originalDescription: The Cro-Magnoids resisted white invaders along the Somme River, where decisive struggles between the blue man and the white man determined the fate of their territories. (80:5.4-5) The evidence of Andonites along the Somme River reflects the ancient river dwellers of France. (63:5.3) The Somme River flows today in the same preglacial channel as it did tens of thousands of years ago, where evidence of the Andonic tribes can still be found. (63:5.3) (80:3.1)
improvedDescription: A river in northern France that flows in the same preglacial channel as it did tens of thousands of years ago, where evidence of the ancient Andonic tribes can still be found. The Somme was the site of decisive struggles between the blue man and white invaders, where the Cro-Magnoids made their final stand before succumbing to superior military strategy.

name: Son finders
originalDescription: Secondary supernaphim of Havona’s 4th circuit are the Son finders who guide ascending mortals in understanding the Eternal Son. (26:8)
improvedDescription: Secondary supernaphim of Havona's fourth circuit who guide ascending mortals in understanding the Eternal Son, helping pilgrims achieve understanding contact with the Eternal Son on their journey to Paradise.

name: Son-fused mortals
originalDescription: Adjusters serve mortal beings during life in flesh, building up souls and guiding them through ascension to the mansion worlds, even if they are not candidates for fusion with their Adjusters. (40:8.2) (40:9.2) The Ancients of Days convene a court of inquiry to certify the lack of guilt in inhibiting Father fusion for Son-fused mortals. (40:8.3) As ascending sons, Son-fused mortals journey through the educational worlds of the universe, striving for identity fusion with their Adjusters. (40:8) Ascendant Son-fused mortals assist Celestial Guardians across the major sectors of Orvonton. (22:9.3) Spirit-fused mortals rise to the source of spirit endowment through fusion implemented in Reflective Spirits of higher universes. (40:10.2) Union with the individualized spirit of a Creator Son transforms a creature into a Son-fused mortal, a concept difficult to describe with words. (30:1.102) (107:1.7) Son-fused mortals in personality registers unite with the individualized spirit of a Creator Son. (30:1.51,102) (30:2.48,123) The Trinitized Sons of Selection and Ambassadors are recruited from Spirit-fused and Son-fused mortals who have ascended to Paradise and are commissioned as representatives of the Ancients of Days in the superuniverses. (22:1.11) (22:6.1) (40:10.12) The purpose of Son-fused mortals is to provide each superuniverse with a permanent ascendant citizenship. (40:10.4,7) Son-fused mortals, numbering less than one million in the superuniverse of Orvonton, sometimes journey to Paradise but are primarily confined to their nativity superuniverse, equal to Adjuster-fused associates in every way. (40:8.5) (40:10.1-2) Some Son-fused mortals ascend to Paradise after undergoing necessary transformations. (40:10.10) The Son-fused mortals on Uversa supervise the abandonters in administering routine affairs. (37:9.12) When synchronization issues impede fusion with the Father, the Creator Son's referees convene to investigate and certify, leading to the immediate fusion of the mortal with the spirit of the Creator Son. (40:8.3) Urantia mortals will not become Son- and Spirit-fused. (30:4.9) Son-fused mortals, residing on Uversa, contribute to the eventual settlement of the seventh superuniverse, alongside their Spirit-fused counterparts, while Father-fused beings attain the Universal Father as their ultimate destiny. (40:10)
improvedDescription: Evolutionary mortals who fuse with an individualized gift of the spirit of their Creator Son rather than with their Thought Adjusters. These beings are primarily confined to their superuniverse of origin, numbering less than one million in Orvonton, and serve as permanent ascendant citizens in superuniverse administration.

name: son of commandment
originalDescription: Jesus graduated from the synagogue school at age thirteen, becoming a “son of the commandment” and a responsible citizen of Israel, attending Passover in Jerusalem. (123:5.2) (125:2.6) Upon graduation, Jesus became a “son of the commandment,” participating in temple discussions with candor and hunger for knowledge. (124:5.4) (125:4.3)
improvedDescription: A Jewish term for a young man who has graduated from synagogue school at age thirteen, becoming a responsible citizen of Israel with the privilege of attending Passover in Jerusalem. Jesus received this designation upon completing his education at the Nazareth synagogue.

name: Son of God
originalDescription: The Jewish concept of a heavenly Prince ruling the earth made new evolved from the servant of the Lord to the son of David, Son of Man, and Son of God. (135:5.7) (136:1.1)
improvedDescription: A Jewish Messianic concept that evolved from earlier expectations of "the servant of the Lord" and "son of David" to include the idea of a divine heavenly Prince who would rule the earth made new as part of God's kingdom.

name: Son of Man
originalDescription: Amenemope, known as the son of man, taught salvation through conscience and calling upon the solar deity. (95:4.1) Immanuel counseled Michael to end rebellion as the Son of Man, achieving justice through humility and mercy. (120:2.2) (120:3.11) The Son of Man, a heavenly Prince and Son of God, was seen as the divine ruler of the new kingdom in Jewish belief. (135:5.7) (136:1.1) Jesus
improvedDescription: The title Jesus chose for himself based on passages in the Book of Enoch, referring to a divine being who walked through heavenly glory with the Father but came to earth to bring salvation to mankind. Jesus adopted this as his inaugural title, emphasizing both his human nature and divine mission.

name: Son seizure
originalDescription: Translation from among the living results in Son-seized mortals appearing immediately in the presence of the Sovereign Son. (49:6.18-21) (55:2.9)
improvedDescription: A form of translation from among the living whereby mortals who have fused with their Adjusters during mortal life are taken directly to the presence of the Sovereign Son at universe headquarters, bypassing death and the usual mansion world experiences.

name: Son-Spirit ministers
originalDescription: Son-Spirit ministers are closely affiliated with the intelligence corps of Bright and Morning Star in serving the high Sons and Spirit-origin beings in the local universe. (39:1.2) Supreme seraphim help orchestrate the transition between planetary dispensations for the high Sons and Spirits. (39:1.3)
improvedDescription: Supreme seraphim assigned to serve the high Sons and Spirit-origin beings in the local universe, closely affiliated with the Bright and Morning Star's intelligence corps and responsible for organizing dispensational transitions on evolutionary worlds.

name: Sonarington
originalDescription: Sonarington is the paradise home of the Sons of God, within the bosom of the Son. (13:1.7) Mortal finaliters granted clearance to Sonarington, save for one seventh of its secrets. (13:2.6) The secrets of incarnation and bestowal are fully known only on Sonarington. (7:5.4) (13:1.8) (13:2.6) (119:1.6) (119:3.6) (119:7.5)
improvedDescription: One of the seven sacred spheres of Paradise, serving as the personal receiving world and headquarters of the Eternal Son. This sphere is the Paradise home for all Sons of God and contains the secrets of divine incarnation, accessible only to those who have personally experienced the bestowal mystery.

name: sons of Annas
improvedDescription: The descendants or followers of Annas, the former Jewish high priest who played a role in Jesus's trial and crucifixion.

name: Sons of God
originalDescription: Although seraphim are not sex-emotion creatures, they are beings descending from Father and Son, much like the sons of God. (38:2.2) God's divine entities are part of him and his knowledge is universal and perfect. (3:3.1) The Sons of God in Uversa's personality register include Creator Sons, Magisterial Sons, Trinity Teacher Sons, Melchizedek Sons, Vorondadek Sons, Lanonandek Sons, Life Carrier Sons, Father-fused Mortals, Son-fused Mortals, Spirit-fused Mortals, Evolutionary Seraphim, Ascending Material Sons, Translated Midwayers, Personalized Adjusters, Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, Those without Name and Number, Trinitized Custodians, Trinitized Ambassadors, Celestial Guardians, High Son Assistants, Ascender-trinitized Sons, Paradise-Havona-trinitized Sons, and Trinitized Sons of Destiny. (30:2.37-64) The local universe Sons of God, including Creator Sons, Avonal Sons, and Trinity Teacher Sons, serve as powerful and effective leaders and teachers within Nebadon, each fulfilling unique roles in the grand design of the universe. (35) Sonarington is the Paradise headquarters of the descending and ascending Sons of God. (13:1.7) mortals as sons of God orders of Sons of God
improvedDescription: Sons of God encompass all beings of divine sonship including descending orders (Creator Sons, Magisterial Sons, Trinity Teacher Sons, Melchizedek Sons, and others), ascending orders (mortals fused with Thought Adjusters, evolutionary seraphim, and other ascending beings), and trinitized sons. This classification represents the broad category of divine beings who share spiritual kinship with the Paradise Father, with Sonarington serving as their Paradise headquarters.

name: sons of thunder
originalDescription: James, one of the apostle sons of Zebedee nicknamed “sons of thunder,” was married with four children and lived near his parents, fishing alongside his brother John in Capernaum. (139:3.1,5) (139:4.6) (181:2.4)
improvedDescription: James and John, the apostle sons of Zebedee, were nicknamed "sons of thunder" by Jesus due to their headstrong and sometimes impetuous nature. Despite their vehement temperaments, they developed great affection for one another and eventually became devoted followers who learned to channel their passionate natures into spiritual service.

name: sonship with God
originalDescription: The Adjuster, Holy Spirit, and Spirit of Truth enable mortals to truly recognize their sonship with God. (16:8.19) (34:5.7) Full function of personality is the starting point for realizing sonship with God, as a selfhood indwelt by a fragment of God the Father discloses the capacity for receiving divine presence and responding to the gravitational pull of the Paradise Father. (16:8.19) Only through spiritual insight can man break the fetters of mortal status and find true sonship with God. (101:10.4) Through acceptance of sonship, belief in our spiritual promise, and the indwelling of the Father's fragment, mortals become sons of God in eternal reality, united with Christ and drawn toward the Universal Father. (40:6.4-8) The key to survival is the willingness to accept sonship and cooperate with the indwelling Adjuster, leading to comprehension and eventual passage to Paradise. (26:4.15) Sonship with God does not negate citizenship in earthly governments; believers are called to be exemplary citizens in both realms. (178:1) In the final analysis, the destinies of the ascending orders of sonship cannot be labeled as “greater” or “lesser”, as every son of God shares the fatherhood of God and is loved by him equally, making sonship the supreme relationship of the creature to the Creator. (40:10.13) To have fellowship with God, assume the obligations of sonship and be willing to make the supreme sacrifice in love. (157:6.8) how sonship is obtained
improvedDescription: Sonship with God is the supreme relationship between creature and Creator, realized through spiritual insight, faith acceptance, and cooperation with the indwelling Thought Adjuster. This status transforms mortals from material beings into spiritual sons of God, enabling divine fellowship and eternal survival through the recognition of their divine kinship.

name: Sonta-an
originalDescription: Sonta-an and Sonta-en were names given to Andon and Fonta, signifying their mutual regard and affection as “loved by mother” and “loved by father” during their mortal sojourn on Urantia. (63:0.3)
improvedDescription: Sonta-an, meaning "loved by mother," was the name Fonta used for Andon during their mortal life on Urantia. These were the personal names they gave each other, signifying their mutual regard and affection before receiving their official Nebadon names upon fusion with their Thought Adjusters.

name: Sonta-en
originalDescription: Sonta-en means “loved by father,” signifying the mutual regard and affection between Andon and Fonta during their mortal sojourn on Urantia. (63:0.3)
improvedDescription: Sonta-en, meaning "loved by father," was the name Andon used for Fonta during their mortal life on Urantia. These were the personal names they gave each other, signifying their mutual regard and affection before receiving their official Nebadon names upon fusion with their Thought Adjusters.

name: Sontad
originalDescription: Sontad, the first child of Andon and Fonta, assumed leadership of the clan upon his parents' death, assisted by his wife and eldest sister, and together they buried their deceased family members. (63:3.1,5) Sontad was fused with his Adjuster on Jerusem, like Andon, Fonta, and some of their children, while most of their descendants only achieved Spirit fusion. (63:7.2)
improvedDescription: Sontad was the first child of Andon and Fonta, notable as the first creature born on Urantia to be wrapped in protective coverings at birth. He assumed leadership of the clan after his parents' death and was later fused with his Thought Adjuster on Jerusem, like his parents.

name: soothsaying
originalDescription: Spirit messages were interpreted through divination and soothsaying in a complex scheme to appease the will of the spirits. (87:5.9) Soothsayers practiced black arts, while priests, seers, shamans, and medicine men practiced white art, evolving over time into witchcraft or shamancraft. (90:2.2)
improvedDescription: Soothsaying was a form of divination practiced to interpret spirit messages and determine the will of spirits. Along with omens, oracles, and signs, it was part of ancient religious practices designed to communicate with supernatural forces, later classified as either witchcraft or shamancraft.

name: sop
originalDescription: Jesus gave Judas a sop, revealing his role as the betrayer. (179:4.3)
improvedDescription: A sop is a piece of bread dipped in a dish, which Jesus gave to Judas during the Last Supper. This act served as a subtle indication to John of Judas's identity as the betrayer, though the other apostles did not understand its significance at the time.

name: sophistries
originalDescription: Faith persists despite all intellectual sophistries. (101:3.11) The mechanistic sophistries of material philosophy are dispelled by a brave stretch of faith towards man's eternal destiny. (102:0.2) International trade organizations promote peace on Urantia more effectively than sentimental sophistry advocating visionary peace planning, due to the facilitation of trade relations through the development of language, improved communication methods, and better transportation. (70:3.4)
improvedDescription: Sophistries refer to clever but fallacious arguments, particularly those of materialistic philosophy that deny spiritual reality. True faith successfully withstands all intellectual sophistries, while practical endeavors like international trade organizations often achieve more real progress than abstract philosophical arguments.

name: sorrow
originalDescription: Sorrow often arises from disappointed ambitions and wounded pride, but true peace can be found in accepting one's lot and making the most of it. (149:5.3) Sorrow cannot exist in the face of duty faithfully performed by the Havona Servitals and Graduate Guides. (25:1.6) A powerful philosophy of living arises from a blend of cosmic wisdom and emotional reactions to one's surroundings, where deficient unification creates unhappiness. (140:4.8-9) Doubt not truth in sorrow, for I have triumphed and shown you the way to eternal joy. (181:1.6) Let us forsake sorrow and disown fear, embracing the faith that sustains us in righteousness and manliness. (131:3.2) Friendship lessens sting of sorrow by providing enhanced defense against all evil and mutual affection as a source of comfort and strength. (160:2.9) God heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds; his mercy endures forever. (131:2.4) Judas's persistent isolation multiplied his grief, sorrows, anxieties, and despair almost beyond endurance. (193:4.12) Do not depict your teacher as a man of sorrows; rather, let future generations know the radiance of our joy, the buoyance of our good will, and the inspiration of our good humor. (159:3.10) (181:1.8) Jesus suffered untold sorrow as he prayed in the ravine, heavy-laden and oppressed, before accepting his fate with a heavy heart. (182:3.1,7) Sorrow is predominantly a material reaction to your internal psychic climate and external environment. (108:5.6) Sorrow is none on Paradise, where eternal joy and peace reign forever. (27:1.5) Beware of false prophets and deceitful friends, for there is no sorrow more terrible than being wounded by a trusted friend. (149:6.12) The sorrows of the Infinite Spirit are real; do not grieve the Spirit of God, who speaks and intercedes for us. (8:6.4) Our sorrows and trials are essential components of the divine plan for our development. (23:2.12) Peter learned a needful lesson through much trouble and many sorrows. (181:2.29) The story of Fortune is a tale of transformation from sorrow to service and victory, guided by the wise words of Jesus to a fearful young man. (130:6) Suffer less through sorrow by making fewer personal plans for others and accepting your lot after faithfully performing your duty. (48:6.36) The measure of human strength lies in resisting grudges and withstanding brooding in the face of sorrow. (156:5.17)
improvedDescription: Sorrow often stems from disappointed ambitions and wounded pride, but can be transcended through faithful duty performance and acceptance of one's lot. While sorrow is a natural human emotional response to difficult circumstances, spiritual beings find that the joy of divine service eclipses such feelings, and ultimate reality promises freedom from all sorrow.

name: Sortoria
originalDescription: Sortoria is one of Satania's immediate neighboring systems within the Norlatiadek constellation. (41:2.1)
improvedDescription: Sortoria is one of Satania's neighboring local systems within the Norlatiadek constellation, along with Sandmatia, Assuntia, Porogia, Rantulia, and Glantonia.

name: soul
originalDescription: The evolving soul and the Adjuster work together to guide and nurture the human personality towards cosmic insight and spiritual discovery, beyond the limitations of science and philosophy. (111) We should commit the keeping of our souls to God, our faithful Creator, as we strive to love Him and all his children. (131:10.2) about fusion of soul with Adjuster see link on fusion | fusion Jesus warned the young man about gaining the whole world and losing his soul. (165:4.9) Judge not the soul by one unfortunate human episode, but by spiritual longings and purposes. (156:5.9) Most impoverished souls are rich, yet disbelief prevails among mortals. (48:7.6) The material eyes serve as the windows of the spirit-born soul, with the spirit as the architect, the mind as the builder, and the body as the material building. (42:12.12) (86:5.15) (111:0.7) Prayer is the breath of the soul, leading to persistence in seeking the Father's will and receiving the bread of life through unwavering spirit. (144:2.3) Salvation is achieved through the spiritualization of moral consciousness, reconciling it with intellectual self-awareness to attain eternal survival status. (133:6.6) The saving or losing of a soul depends on achieving harmony between moral and intellectual self-consciousness for eternal survival. (133:6.6) about winning souls see link to gospel | gospel:proclaiming The world is filled with lost souls, wandering in confusion among the isms and cults of a frustrated philosophic era. (100:5.1) after mortal death | extinction; nonsurvivors; resurrection; roll call creation and evolution of the soul historically The nature of the soul
improvedDescription: The soul is the evolving, immortal reality jointly created by the material mind and the indwelling Thought Adjuster through moral choices and spiritual decisions. This morontia entity represents the survival potential of human personality and serves as the vehicle for eternal ascension, growing through spiritual experiences and divine cooperation until it achieves fusion with the Adjuster or other destiny assignments.

name: Souls of Goodness
originalDescription: Soul of Goodness angels are created in seven types and collectively assigned to Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number for interchangeable use. (28:6.1) The Secret of Greatness and the Soul of Goodness are inseparable reflections of divinity, showcasing the importance of unselfishness and self-control in the path towards true greatness. (28:6.20-22)
improvedDescription: The Soul of Goodness are tertiary seconaphim created in seven types and collectively assigned to Trinitized Sons of Attainment for interchangeable use. They work inseparably with the Secret of Greatness to reflect the divine qualities of greatness and goodness, demonstrating that true greatness is measured by unselfishness and goodness rather than worldly achievements.

name: souls of peace
originalDescription: The Souls of Peace, led by Vevona, minister peace to evolutionary men, striving for peace on earth through the ministry of planetary helper seraphim. (39:5.5-6)
improvedDescription: The Souls of Peace are planetary helper seraphim who minister to evolutionary mortals, working to establish peace on earth and good will among men. Led by Vevona, who was left on Urantia after Adam's time and later heralded Michael's birth, these angels help replace primitive atonement concepts with divine attunement philosophy.

name: Souls of Philosophy
originalDescription: Secondary seconaphim reflect the wisdom of divinity and philosophy of Paradise, in synchrony with Perfectors of Wisdom and masters of philosophy on Paradise. (28:5.11)
improvedDescription: The Soul of Philosophy are secondary seconaphim who reflect the wisdom of divinity and philosophy of Paradise. Attached to the Perfectors of Wisdom, they remain in focal synchrony with the masters of philosophy on Paradise and can adapt divine philosophy for practical application on lower worlds.

name: sound designers
originalDescription: The celestial artisans are spirit waves that sound designers embellish with diverse identity and morontia appreciation, reflecting the glorious spirit-souls of the celestial hosts. (44:6.4)
improvedDescription: Sound designers are celestial artisans who work with spirit waves of diverse identity and morontia appreciation, creating what mortals would understand as sound. These impulses reflect the glorious spirit-souls of the celestial hosts.

name: South Africa
originalDescription: Dinosaurs buried in South Africa were some of the largest in the world, originating from western North America. (60:2.2)
improvedDescription: South Africa is a continent that has been connected to other landmasses at various geological periods and contains some of the largest dinosaur fossils in the world, which originated from western North America.

name: South America
originalDescription: 132 Polynesian Andites reached South America, founding the ancestry of the later rulers of the Incas by intermarrying with the natives of the Andes. (64:7.5) (78:5.7) (79:5.9) Africa connected South America to Europe 220 million years ago during the same epoch when North America began to rise from the sea before a short interruption that left half of its previous beds covered. (59:5.11) An amalgamated race of superior potential occupied highlands of South America. (64:7.5,16) In South America, basic racial types represented in Caucasoid, Mongoloid, and Negroid persist in skeletal characteristics that identify present-day human ancestry. (81:4.14) The east wind is a god in South America, bringing rain, but a devil in India, bringing dust and causing drought. (85:4.2) South America's geology played a crucial role in the evolutionary development of marine life 400 million years ago. (59:1) (59:2.4-7) (59:3.9) (59:4.5-7,15) (59:5.4,11,18) (59:6.5,8) (60:1.4) (60:2.6) (60:3.4,6,11) (61:1.13) (61:2.3) (61:4.3,6) There were no rhinoceroses in South America during the last great world-wide animal migration. (61:4.3) The predominantly red race in South America was influenced by the yellow, orange, and blue races in the development of enduring civilizations. (79:5.8) South America rose out of Pacific depths during the period when one third of the earth's surface became land mass. (57:8.21) 500,000,000 years ago, South America began its slow westward drift alongside all the other continents. (58:4.3)
improvedDescription: South America is a continent that has undergone extensive geological changes throughout Earth's history, rising from Pacific depths and connecting with other continents at various periods. It was populated by Polynesian Andites who established the ancestry of the Incan rulers, and developed advanced civilizations predominantly from red race peoples with admixtures of yellow, orange, and blue races.

name: Sovereign Michaels
improvedDescription: Sovereign Michaels are Creator Sons who have completed their seven bestowal experiences and achieved full sovereignty over their local universes.

name: Sovereign Sons
improvedDescription: Sovereign Sons are Creator Sons who have attained full sovereignty over their local universes through completion of their required bestowal experiences.

name: sovereignty
originalDescription: The Ancients of Days, despite being the most perfect rulers, will be replaced by the Supreme Being, who is earning sovereignty through experiential service. (18:3.8-9) The Eternal Son's control of spiritual gravity signifies his absolute spiritual sovereignty. (7:1.1) local universe s | Creator Sons:local universe sovereignty political sovereignty | nationalism
improvedDescription: The supreme ruling authority and power that characterizes different levels of cosmic governance. Creator Sons earn experiential sovereignty over their local universes through bestowal service, while the Ancients of Days possess perfect but non-experiential sovereignty over superuniverses. The Supreme Being is achieving sovereignty through experiential service and will eventually supersede the Ancients of Days. Political sovereignty evolves from family authority to larger territorial groupings, but ultimate spiritual sovereignty belongs to God.

name: sower
originalDescription: Jesus first began teaching the multitudes the parable of the sower as he sat in the old fishing boat of Andrew and Peter by the seaside, utilizing the story to convey the varying success and challenges faced in spreading the gospel. (95:0.1) (151:1-2) (151:3.13-16) (152:6.3) (155:2.3)
improvedDescription: A central figure in Jesus' parable teaching, first delivered from Andrew and Peter's fishing boat by the seaside. The parable of the sower illustrated both Jesus' ministry and the varying success that future messengers of the kingdom would experience in spreading the gospel.

name: sowing
originalDescription: Before marriage, young men are given a brief period of freedom, known as sowing wild oats, before committing to lifelong tribal taboos. (70:7.12) Reaping is in accordance with sowing, and with trust and patience, one can achieve spiritual insight and peace. (148:6.3) (192:2.8)
improvedDescription: The act of planting seeds, both literally and metaphorically. Used to describe a period of youthful freedom before marriage in ancient customs, and spiritually represents the principle that reaping is in accordance with what one sows, emphasizing that consequences follow actions.

name: space
originalDescription: Space potency, as the primal endowment of Paradise, encompasses absoluta potencies existing within space, beyond the reach of gravitational interactions. (11:8.8) Absolute presence circuits are independent of nether Paradise force center and not related to force, energy, or power circuits. (12:3.6) Pilgrims in Havona can freely explore achieved space but must rely on supernatural help to navigate unachieved space. (14:5.5) Adjusted-fused personalities traverse space freely before receiving morontia investiture in the higher morontia spheres. (49:6.19) Seven distinct space conditions and motions exist from the periphery of Paradise to the inner borders of the seven superuniverses, including quiescent midspace zones, clockwise processional circuits, and dark gravity bodies revolving counterclockwise and clockwise. (14:1.1-7) about force-charge of space see link on absoluta | absoluta Time, space, and experience are both the greatest aids and most formidable obstacles to reality perception for finite creatures attempting to conceive infinite unification. (106:9.2-3) The mind transcends space, a reality often overlooked due to the confusion surrounding the concept of space as related to time. (130:7.6) The motions of space are complex and mysterious, with the Unqualified Absolute's limited function within it raising questions about the origin and nature of motion itself. (12:4.2,7-11) Pilgrims may freely traverse achieved space without being ensupernaphimed during their sojourn in Havona. (14:5.5) The extra-Paradise reflectivity service operates independently of space, time, and known subabsolute universe circuits within the seven superuniverses. (17:3.10) The storage of energy into ultimatons causes measurable vibrations in the content of space, known as ultimatonic rays. (42:5.4) In certain sectors of space, small planets slowly accumulate from a vast amount of matter, growing through meteoric accretion and minor collisions to become inhabited worlds. (15:5.9) Deities and space nature of space pervaded space unpervaded space space respiration
improvedDescription: A fundamental cosmic reality that serves as the arena for all material existence and motion. Space is not empty but contains various energy manifestations and responds to the presence of matter. It exhibits complex motions including respiration cycles and serves as a pathway for cosmic forces. Space is pervaded by the Unqualified Absolute and originates from Paradise, functioning as both a container for and participant in universal activities.

name: space bodies
originalDescription: All cosmic energy units execute their missions by revolving around the universal orbit as spheres in the endless circuits of the master universe, with only Havona at the center remaining stationary. (12:4.1) Space bodies appear according to plans of Master Architects, reflecting the organization and principles governing the segmentation of the total energy charge of a superuniverse. (41:0.2) Rare collisions among giant space bodies are far less common than the disruptions of smaller bodies caused by gravitational forces. (57:6.4) Collisions among space bodies release energy but do not occur in established circuits. (15:5.12) (15:8.6) God knows all things and calls them all by name, revealing his presence in the vast expanse of the universe. (1:5.3) (3:3.1-2) Space bodies of various origins form through concentric contraction rings, whirled stars, gravity-explosion planets, centrifugal planetary daughters, gravity-deficiency spheres, contractural stars, cumulative spheres, burned-out suns, collisional spheres, and architectural worlds. (15:5.3-14) about different types of space bodies described in The Urantia Book see links | architectural spheres; comets; dark gravity bodies; dark islands of space; meteors; planets; suns
improvedDescription: Various cosmic objects and spheres that exist throughout space, including suns, planets, dark islands, comets, and architectural worlds. These bodies form through diverse processes such as gravitational condensation, stellar disruption, meteoric accumulation, and deliberate construction. They range from naturally evolved celestial objects to purpose-built administrative spheres, all participating in the endless circuits of the master universe.

name: space energy
originalDescription: This is one of the usual names for the absolute
improvedDescription: One of the usual names for absoluta, representing the primal force-energy that emanates from Paradise and pervades all space.

name: space-force
originalDescription: This is one of the usual names for the absolute
improvedDescription: One of the usual names for absoluta, referring to the fundamental cosmic force that originates from Paradise and operates throughout the master universe.

name: space levels
originalDescription: The alternate zoning and motion of space levels, with their clockwise and counterclockwise flow, play a crucial role in stabilizing physical gravity and preventing disruptive activities. (11:7.9) (12:4.9,15-16) Without the successive space levels of the master universe, cosmic energies would shoot off into infinite trackless space in straight-line paths. (12:1.2) Space levels are elliptical regions of motion surrounded by zones of motionlessness, constituting a curved path of lessened resistance to cosmic force and emergent energy in the master universe. (11:7.8) (12:1.2,14) Force, energy, and matter ever swing onward in the successive space levels of the master universe. (12:1.2) list of levels outer space levels
improvedDescription: Elliptical regions of motion separated by zones of relative quiescence that constitute the major divisions of the master universe. These six concentric levels include the central universe, seven superuniverses, and four outer space levels. They provide curved paths of lessened resistance for cosmic energies and exhibit alternating clockwise and counterclockwise motions that help stabilize physical gravity throughout the cosmos.

name: space potency
originalDescription: This is one of the usual names for the absolute
improvedDescription: One of the usual names for absoluta, representing the ancestral potencies and potentials that exist within space and emanate from Paradise.

name: space rays
originalDescription: Ultimatonic rays consist of ultimatons or electrons and are the initial activity of the ultimatons as they aggregate into electrons, leading to the storage of energy in matter. (42:5.4-5) Outer-space energy rays frequently commingled with highly energized particles. (42:5.1) Implanted life, fully resistant to short space rays, thrives in the cosmic chemical laboratories of vast hydrogen clouds and binary stars. (58:3.4) Many mesotrons in space rays impinge upon your planet abundantly. (42:8.7) Short space rays, the dominant form of radiant energy in the universe, are produced through various cosmic occurrences and atom disruptions in hydrogen clouds interspersed throughout space. (58:3.1-3) Short space rays accompany atomic birth or dissolution, originating from the superdense Milky Way plane. (42:5.5)
improvedDescription: High-energy radiations that pervade space, including ultimatonic rays produced by ultimaton assembly, short space rays from atomic processes, and other forms of radiant energy. These rays represent the dominant form of energy in the universe and are produced through stellar processes, atomic disruption, and various cosmic phenomena, with short space rays being four hundred times more abundant than all other forms of radiant energy.

name: space reports
improvedDescription: Communications or broadcasts that provide information about celestial activities and space-related matters, likely transmitted through the universe communication systems.

name: Spain
originalDescription: Large numbers of Andites entered Spain and mingled with their brethren in Switzerland. (80:7.11) Claudus preached in Spain after being inspired by Jesus in Malta. (130:8.1) Sun worshippers in 3000 B.C. built circular sun temples in southern France and Spain during the New Stone Age, reflecting a period of agriculture and energetic stone setting by the European white races. (80:9.13) Superior Saharans emigrated to Spain and adjacent parts of Europe, forming the nucleus of the Mediterranean long-headed brunet races. (80:2.2) (80:3.7)
improvedDescription: A European peninsula where Andite peoples entered and mixed with earlier populations, and where later Christian preaching occurred through disciples inspired by Jesus, including Claudus who proclaimed the gospel there after Peter's death.

name: sparrows
originalDescription: Fear not, for you are of much more value than five sparrows sold for two pennies. (165:3.4) In God’s sight, not one sparrow is forgotten; He knows all things. (3:3.1) (150:4.3)
improvedDescription: Small birds that Jesus used in his teachings to illustrate God's knowledge and care for all creation, noting that not one sparrow falls to the ground without the Father's awareness, demonstrating that humans are of much greater value than many sparrows.

name: speaker at forum
originalDescription: Jesus told the speaker at the forum: “Your eloquence and logic are impressive, but true teaching comes from knowing God as your spiritual Father.” (132:4.7)
improvedDescription: A man named Marcus who spoke at a Roman forum, whom Jesus told that eloquence and logic were admirable but true teaching comes from knowing God as spiritual Father; he later heard Peter preach, became his successor, and boldly continued preaching after Peter's crucifixion.

name: spear
originalDescription: The spear was invented by the blue man. (65:6.21) Jesus' side pierced with a soldier's spear as he hung on the cross at Golgotha. (187:5.8)
improvedDescription: A weapon invented by the blue man and used by Roman soldiers, one of whom pierced Jesus' left side with a spear while he hung on the cross to ensure his death.

name: specialists
originalDescription: The development of effective means for coordinating specialists is essential for the continued advancement and cohesion of civilization as it expands and diversifies. (81:6.29-30)
improvedDescription: Individuals with specialized skills and knowledge whose coordination is essential for civilization's continued advancement, as society's growing complexity requires effective methods to unite various technical, social, artistic, and industrial experts.

name: spectral lines
originalDescription: Spectral analyses reveal only the sun's surface compositions, showcasing iron lines despite not being the dominant element, a result of the sun's temperature of nearly 6,000 degrees. (41:6.7) Some unknown spectral lines are shattered elements floating throughout space, modified by energy and matter circulating in organized space. (41:6.1)
improvedDescription: Observable features in stellar spectra that reveal surface compositions of stars and indicate the presence of shattered elements floating in space, with some unknown lines resulting from atomic casualties of solar battles and energy modifications in organized space.

name: spectrum
originalDescription: The seven colors in the natural spectrum reflect a sevenfold constitution of ancestral energy, demonstrating a fundamental reality of creation in time and space. (42:9.3)
improvedDescription: The natural light spectrum containing seven colors that demonstrates the fundamental sevenfold constitution of ancestral energy and reflects the basic reality of creation's sevenfold diversity in time and space.

name: speculations
originalDescription: regarding Deities regarding future of being orders regarding the future of the universe regarding future of Urantia usefulness of speculations
improvedDescription: Theoretical considerations and conjectures about divine mysteries, future destinies, and cosmic purposes that celestial beings engage in regarding topics beyond current revelation, though such speculation can be detrimental to religious experience when it falsifies spiritual realities.

name: speech, freedom of
originalDescription: Representative government is unthinkable without freedom of all forms of expression. (71:2.13)
improvedDescription: The essential liberty allowing all forms of human expression and opinion that makes representative government possible, as democratic governance cannot function without the free exchange of ideas and aspirations.

name: spells
originalDescription: Jesus exposed and denounced belief in spells and all forms of ignorant superstition. (150:3.12)
improvedDescription: Jesus exposed and denounced belief in spells and all forms of ignorant superstition, teaching against these enslaving practices that included bewitching, cursing, signs, and other forms of primitive magical thinking.

name: spheres of space
improvedDescription: Celestial bodies distributed throughout the cosmos, representing the physical worlds and astronomical structures that exist within the vast expanse of space.

name: spheres of the Father
improvedDescription: Sacred worlds associated with the Universal Father, likely referring to the divine spheres that surround Paradise and serve special functions in the cosmic hierarchy.

name: spicules
originalDescription: The Andonites, ancestors of primates, developed skill in using sharp spicules for hunting and survival. (63:1.2-3) (63:5.6)
improvedDescription: Sharp pieces of stone, flint, and bone used by the Andonites and early human ancestors as tools and weapons for hunting and survival, representing humanity's first steps in tool-making technology.

name: spiders
originalDescription: Spiders, along with other creatures, soon overspread the continents of the world, evolving alongside various species before becoming stationary types of early and lower animals. (59:5.7) (65:2.5)
improvedDescription: Arthropods that evolved early in planetary history alongside insects and other creatures, becoming part of the stationary types of early and lower animals that failed to progress further in evolutionary development.

name: spies
originalDescription: The first spies, secret societies were first to impose oaths, enforce secrecy, and maintain security, intimidating and manipulating the masses, acting as vigilance societies and secret police, and keeping deceitful rulers in check. (70:7.16) Women employed as spies eventually evolved into jobbers for trade, expanding into a merchant class charging commissions for their services. (69:3.11)
improvedDescription: Early practitioners of espionage and surveillance who emerged from secret societies and evolved into various roles including vigilance committees, secret police, and eventually merchant intermediaries in early trade systems.

name: spikenard
originalDescription: Mary poured the costly spikenard upon Jesus, a gesture of love and faith that rebuked those who criticized her act of devotion. (172:1.5-8)
improvedDescription: A costly and rare ointment that Mary used to anoint Jesus at Bethany, an act of devotion that Jesus defended against criticism, declaring it a preparation for his burial and a demonstration of faith.

name: spirit
originalDescription: The Absolutes seem to supervene over matter, transcend mind, and mystify spirit in their complex transactions. (4:1.8) The Eternal Son has absolute control over all spirit energy and motivates cosmic reality with infinite drawing power. (6:4.1) Spirit ministry on evolving worlds is directly unified in Creator Sons and Mother Spirits. (56:3.4) inside Man nature of spirit source of spirit spirit mind spirit energy spiritual circuits spiritual gravity
improvedDescription: The fundamental divine reality that originates from God and represents the highest form of personal existence, transcending matter and mind while serving as the eternal goal of all personality development and cosmic evolution.

name: spirit, new-name
originalDescription: Ascending mortal in Corps of Finality, subject to the sevenfold appeal of universe spirit influences. (194:2.12)
improvedDescription: One of seven higher spirit influences that progressively contact and dwell within ascending mortals as they advance in intelligence and spiritual perception beyond the initial adjutant mind-spirits.

name: Spirit-Advocate
originalDescription: The Spirit-Advocate ensures fairness and protection for all parties before the conciliating commission. (25:2.7)
improvedDescription: A celestial being appointed by a judge-arbiter to present evidence and safeguard the rights of all personalities involved in matters before conciliating commissions, ensuring fair representation in universe tribunals.

name: Spirit co-ordinators
originalDescription: The superior seraphim serving ascenders on Salvington as Spirit Co-ordinators prepare them for their final transition in the local universe to newborn spirit beings. (39:2.6)
improvedDescription: The third group of superior seraphim based on Salvington who prepare ascending sojourners for their final transition in the local universe from the highest morontia level to the status of newborn spirit beings.

name: spirit conjuring
originalDescription: Shamans utilized powerful techniques to control the hazards of living through spirit conjuring. (90:2.1)
improvedDescription: A precise and highly complicated procedure used by shamans, comparable to present-day church rituals, to control the hazards of living through spiritual manipulation and revelatory practices.

name: Spirit-fused mortals
originalDescription: Spirit-fused mortals, when required on Uversa, are escorted by Celestial Guardians, who serve as messengers and agents of the Ancients of Days. (22:9.4) (40:10.9) Spirit-fused mortals, unlike their ascendant brethren, do not retain human memory after death, relying on seraphic associates and records for the immediate reconstruction of their past experiences. (40:9.4-9) (112:6.8) Spirit-fused mortals go through mansion worlds on their path to immortality and eternal fusion with the local universe Mother Spirit. (37:5.2) (47:4.2) Spirit-fused mortals failed to personalize on the mansion worlds, prompting guardian angels serving as Mind Planners to organize their teamwork. (48:6.24) The Spirit-fused mortals are in charge of the 8th Salvington circuit worlds. (37:5.10) Spirit-fused mortals in personality registers differ greatly from Adjusters, as they are not prepersonal entities nor dwell on Spiritington, traverse mind-gravity circuits, or indwell mortal creatures during life in the flesh. (30:1.52,101) (30:2.49,121) Spirit-fused mortals are included in Father’s personality circuit, having fused with individualizations of the premind spirit of the Third Source and Center in the morontia existence. (40:9.1) Spirit-fused survivors may revisit their nativity worlds in subsequent planetary dispensations to learn about their past lives in the flesh. (40:9.9) Spirit-fused mortals are few in number in Nebadon, attaining immortality through fusion with a Mother Spirit fragment, journeying with you to Salvington before serving the local universe eternally. (37:5.2) The resurrection procedure for spirit-fused mortals involves the reawakening of primitive beings through guardian seraphim and a portion of the immortal spirit of the Third Source and Center. (49:6.6) Spirit-fused mortals are bestowed with fragmentations of the premind spirit, which differ greatly from Adjusters and do not indwell mortals during life in flesh. (30:1.101) Spirit-fused mortals of the Nebadon Corps of Perfection are closely associated with the Susatia on Salvington. (37:9.7) Urantia mortals will not become Adjuster-fused, but Spirit-fused mortals share portions of their universe career. (30:4.9) Spirit-fused mortals are generally confined to a local universe. (40:10) The worlds of Spirit-fused mortals evolve identically with the seven stages of light and life on an evolutionary world. (55:0.4) destiny Spirit fusion
improvedDescription: Ascendant mortals who achieve immortality through eternal fusion with individualized fragments of the local universe Mother Spirit rather than with Thought Adjusters, serving permanently within their native local universe.

name: spirit mortals
improvedDescription: Mortals who have achieved spirit status through their ascension journey, representing an advanced stage of evolutionary development beyond their material origins.

name: Spirit Mother
improvedDescription: A reference to the Divine Minister or Universe Mother Spirit, the local universe representative of the Infinite Spirit who serves as the spiritual mother figure for the local creation.

name: spirit of God
originalDescription: The Infinite Spirit is the Conjoint Actor, manifesting as the Father, Son, and Father-Son, known as the spirit of God. (8:5.5) The union of God the Father, God the Son, God the Spirit, and God the Sevenfold forms the spirit of God, the Supreme Being. (8:5.6)
improvedDescription: The spirit presence of the Infinite Spirit functioning as the Conjoint Actor, representing both the Father and the Son as well as himself, and encompassing the liaison of all spiritual ministry from God the Father, Son, Spirit, and Sevenfold.

name: Spirit of Truth
originalDescription: Master Sons, in perfect communication with bestowal worlds through Spirit of Truth, maintain unbroken connection with Eternal Mother Son and extend sympathetic reach from Universal Father to planetary life. (21:5.10) Jesus had to leave before sending the Spirit of Truth, which he fulfilled on the day of Pentecost. (52:5.6) (176:4.3) (180:6.2) The cup of remembrance, emblem of Spirit of Truth, was blessed by Jesus during the new remembrance supper, symbolizing the liberation from bondage into spiritual fellowship and unity with the living God. (179:5.1-3,9) After the Creator Son's final bestowal, the Spirit of Truth undergoes a transformation in nature, becoming more closely aligned with the sovereign Michael, as it is sent to the mortal races inhabiting the bestowal world. (20:6.8-9) Adamic blood prepares the mortal nature for the Spirit of Truth to bear the fruits of the divine Spirit. (34:7.1) Let Spirit of Truth do his own work and stand in defense of truth when attacked. (178:1.16) The apostles laid hands on believers in token of receiving the Spirit of Truth. (139:5.9) Caligastia and Daligastia, once rulers of darkness on Urantia, now servile before the Spirit of Truth. (53:8.7) The pure mind discerns God through spiritual insight with the combined operations of the Adjuster and Spirit of Truth in the realm of highest thinking. (101:1.3) The Spirit of Truth does not indwell individual minds, unlike the Thought Adjusters which are definite individualizations of the prepersonal reality of the Universal Father and actually indwell the mortal mind as a very part of that mind. (34:5.6) The true child of universe insight seeks the living Spirit of Truth in every wise saying, elevating wisdom to divine truth levels. (180:5.4) The association of the Spirit of Truth with the souls of mankind remains a mystery that becomes more unfathomable as time passes. (124:6.17) The effectiveness of the planetary government is enhanced by the ever-ready circuit of the archangels and their timesaving assistance, which can circumvent the planet's spiritual isolation through the Spirit of Truth. (114:5.4) Sinful decisions separate individuals from the communion with the Spirit of Truth, causing them to hide in their own willful choices and become detached from the spiritual presence of the Father and his Son. (5:1.10) The endowment of the Spirit of Truth in spiritual philosophy enables the co-ordination of faith-insight and soul intelligence, making man a spirit personality with potential destiny. (101:3.2) The beauty of truth is discovered by intellectual self-consciousness through the unerring response of the ever-present Spirit of Truth. (2:7.6) bestowal definition effect of receiving the Spirit of Truth
improvedDescription: The combined spirit of the Creator Son and Universal Father poured out upon all flesh following a bestowal Son's mission, serving as the divine teacher and guide that leads believers into all truth and spiritual understanding.

name: Spiritington
originalDescription: Spiritington is the Paradise home of high beings from all universes who represent the Infinite Spirit. (13:1.9) The secret of Spiritington lies in the enigmatic power of reflectivity. (13:1.10)
improvedDescription: The Paradise home of high spirit beings representing the Infinite Spirit, including the Seven Master Spirits, where the impenetrable mysteries of reflectivity are centered.

name: spiritism, dual
improvedDescription: A form of spiritual belief or practice involving dual aspects of spirit influence or manifestation, though specific details are not provided in the available context.

name: spirits
originalDescription: The Mortal Corps of the Finality, despite their achievements and assignments, are only sixth-stage spirits, hinting at a mysterious next step in their eternal journey. (31:3.3-6) (40:10.12) (107:5.6) about evil spirits see link to demon possession | demon possession
improvedDescription: Advanced evolutionary beings who have progressed through various stages of spirit development, with mortal finaliters currently achieving sixth-stage spirit status while potentially advancing to a mysterious seventh stage.

name: spirits of brotherhood
originalDescription: The planetary helper seraphim work tirelessly for racial harmony among diverse races on evolutionary worlds. (39:5.4)
improvedDescription: Planetary helper seraphim who work to achieve racial harmony and social cooperation among diverse races on evolutionary worlds, fostering the wisdom of peaceful interassociation and human brotherhood.

name: Spirits of the Havona Circuits
improvedDescription: An order of celestial beings associated with the Seven Spirits of Havona Circuits.

name: spirits of trust
originalDescription: Planetary helper seraphim inculcate trust, fostering a civilization that embodies ideals of confidence and trust in uncertainty. (39:5.7-9)
improvedDescription: Planetary helper seraphim who inculcate trust into the minds of evolving humans, fostering a civilization that embodies confidence and the understanding that uncertainty is essential for spiritual growth and contentment.

name: spiritual assurance
improvedDescription: The confidence and certainty that comes from spiritual communion and relationship with God.

name: spiritual attainment
improvedDescription: The achievement of higher levels of spiritual development and God-consciousness through progressive spiritual growth.

name: spiritual communion
originalDescription: Rejoice in the ever-present possibility of immediate spiritual communion with the Father, as you progress towards his Paradise presence. (5:1.3,8) The personality of God can be grasped in spiritual communion with Jesus, who realized the full potential of spirit personality in human experience. (1:6.8) (5:0.1) Deliverance from the sense of guilt stemming from interrupted spiritual communion or lowered moral ideals comes from realizing that one's highest moral standards may not align with the will of God; striving to find God and emulate Him is the true purpose. (103:4.3) The spiritual communion of Adjuster involves moral status, mental motivation, and spiritual experience, leading to self-realization through the manifestation of the fruits of the spirit. (5:2.6) Lack of consciousness of spiritual communion does not disprove the presence and transforming power of divine influences surrounding and impinging upon you, as evidenced by the fruits of the spirit yielded in your life experience. (5:2.4-5) Maintaining unbroken communion with the Maker through sincere prayer and true worship enriches the capacity of receptivity in individual believers. (194:3.20) (196:0.10) Modern man thinks he is too busy for spiritual meditation and religious devotion, leading to a barren existence devoid of real values and eternal assurances. (195:6.7) Civilization must deliver mankind from fear and intermediaries in communion with Deity for spiritual progress. (92:7.11) True spiritual communion is not fostered by excessive ornateness or ostentatious art, but by simplicity, beauty, and holiness in worship spaces. (167:6.6) Perversity, self-indulgence, and intolerance separate us from spiritual communion with the Father. (5:1.10) The Remembrance Supper is a parable of spiritual communion with Jesus, symbolizing the emergence from bondage into spiritual fellowship and liberty, free from traditional interpretations and formal limitations. (179:5) Believers should renew their spirits by stepping aside from the rush of life and having implicit faith in the certain triumph of righteousness. (156:5.12) Living truth is a spirit-discerned reality, existing only on high spiritual levels of communion with God. (180:5.2) effect of spiritual communion Jesus
improvedDescription: The intimate, personal fellowship and communication between the human soul and God, fostered through prayer, worship, and meditation, which provides spiritual refreshment, guidance, and assurance of divine companionship.

name: spiritual consecration
improvedDescription: The dedicated commitment and wholehearted devotion of one's will and life to doing God's will and serving divine purposes.

name: spiritual-energy manipulators
originalDescription: Celestial artisans meticulously manipulate spiritual energy with reliable laws and improved techniques. (44:5.4)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who study and manipulate spiritual energy according to established laws, developing improved techniques for the intake and application of spirit force among morontia and celestial beings.

name: spiritual growth
originalDescription: definition achievement difficulties problems of spiritual growth result of spiritual growth
improvedDescription: The progressive development of the soul characterized by awakening to spiritual needs, discernment of divine meanings, discovery of eternal values, and increasing God-consciousness, demonstrated through love, unselfish service, and wholehearted worship.

name: spiritual influences
originalDescription: The indwelling God fragment allows us to discern and harmonize with various spiritual influences surrounding us. (5:2.4) In the practicalities of daily life, we are guided by spirit personalities originating from the Third Source and Center, cooperating with the agencies of the Conjoint Actor. (5:3.5) The conspiracy of spirit forces works to deliver us from the bondage of matter and reconcile our dual nature of animal tendencies and spiritual endowment. (34:6.9) For mortal man, becoming conscious of the spiritual influences of entities like the Paradise Adjusters is a challenging task. (5:2.5) Group spiritual association enhances personal endowment and augments all phases of personal potential in a profound way, enabling moral creatures to quadruple their achievement possibilities. (43:8.11) In Havona, the intense and inescapable Godward urge draws all spiritual values and personalities towards the abode of the Gods, guided by the universal spiritual gravity of the Eternal Son. (14:2.7) Life Carriers conspired to guide Andon and Fonta north, ensuring their escape from potential biologic degradation and preserving their intelligent choice. (62:5.9) Man's threefold endowment of the Adjuster, Spirit of Truth, and Holy Spirit ensures that Spirit becomes Trinity-unified in final realization. (56:3.6) (194:2.11) Direct lines of spiritual force connect Urantians with Deities, including Thought Adjusters with the Universal Father, the spiritual-gravity urge of the Eternal Son, and the presence of the Conjoint Creator. (9:2.3) All spiritual influences operate as one, becoming the ministry of the Supreme, guiding and uplifting mankind towards divine destiny. (8:5.4) (9:2.5) (34:5.4-7) (34:6.1-2) about relationship to free will see link below | free will The transactions of the spirit world are real and harmonize with the immutable laws of the universal domains. (28:5.21) Spiritual influences guide the gradual and reciprocal transitions of spirit co-operation, leading individuals to higher levels of development through the ministry of the spirit world. (65:7.7) list of spiritual influences
improvedDescription: The various divine spiritual forces and ministries that surround and guide mortals, including the Thought Adjuster, Spirit of Truth, Holy Spirit, and other celestial agencies that work in unified cooperation to foster spiritual development and lead souls toward divine perfection.

name: spiritual insight
originalDescription: True religion originates in man's moral consciousness and is born of a wholly rational insight, growing through spiritual insight from the presence of the God-revealing Adjuster in a hungry mortal mind. (101:1.5) Spiritual progress is achieved independently of sociomoral differentials in the diverse material environments of the evolutionary worlds. (5:1.4) The feeling of reality is attached to spiritual insight by the indwelling Thought Adjuster. (102:3.12) Spiritual insight connotes the individual's choice between truth and error in discerning moral and spiritual values. (196:3.10,17) Ascending personalities, with completed spiritual insight, detect harmony in chaos as they reach upward towards Providence. (118:10.19) The discerners of spirits have inherent spiritual insight, functioning intuitively and unerringly alongside the Universal Censors in their reflective and perfect judgment. (28:5.20) Spiritual insight discerns the real and absolute realm of spiritual gravity, drawing all spiritual realities to the Eternal Son. (12:8.4) (112:2.11) The divine personality is only grasped by genuine spiritual insight. (1:6.4) Spiritual insight, the endowment of cosmic mind and Adjuster, distinguishes man's moral and religious natures from the animal world. (16:7.1) (101:3.2) Human happiness is achieved through the harmonious alignment of ego desire and altruistic urge, guided by the integrating and supervising personality fueled by genuine religious reflection. (103:5.5) Human wisdom must evolve, for spiritual insight is a gift of God that is limited by the instructions of our superiors. (101:4.2) Spiritual insight is attained through inner and spiritual communion, as the mind, guided by the divine spirit, perceives God in the realm of the highest and most spiritualized thinking. (101:1.3) Religious insights lead to unselfish acts of social service. (102:3.3) Attainment of spirit status following morontia ascension will clarify spirit-concealed mysteries, as you are endowed with the “mind of the spirit” during your journey through the superuniverse to Havona. (6:6.4) Worship's quality is determined by the depth of perception towards the intimate relationship between Creators and creatures. (27:7.1) Seeing God by faith means acquiring true spiritual insight, enhancing Adjuster guidance, augmenting God-consciousness, confirming divine sonship, and loving others as a father would. (140:5.13) Social leadership is transformed by spiritual insight, preventing collective movements from losing sight of their true objectives. (99:4.2) The insight of spirit transcends and supervenes mind consciousness. (9:4.2) Religious insight turns defeat into higher desires and determinations, fueled by love's redefinition on ascending spiritual levels. (196:3.29)
improvedDescription: The faculty of human personality that develops as a consequence of the indwelling Thought Adjuster, enabling the recognition of moral values, discernment of spiritual meanings, and perception of cosmic realities. Spiritual insight represents the individual's ability to choose between good and evil, truth and error, and distinguishes humans from the animal world through divine endowment of cosmic mind.

name: spiritual peace
improvedDescription: The tranquil state of harmony that arises from spiritual attunement and alignment with divine will, representing inner serenity derived from spiritual communion and God-consciousness.

name: spiritual presence
improvedDescription: The conscious awareness and experiential reality of divine spiritual forces, particularly the indwelling presence of God through the Thought Adjuster and other spiritual influences that guide and uplift human consciousness.

name: spiritual progress
improvedDescription: The evolutionary advancement of the soul through increasing levels of spiritual insight, cosmic understanding, and divine attunement, achieved independently of material circumstances and leading toward eternal spiritual goals.

name: spiritual rebirth
originalDescription: Through living faith, you can become divinely God-conscious and experience spiritual rebirth as children of light and life. (193:0.3) True spiritual rebirth only comes after a complete disillusionment with selfish pursuits and a rejection of shallow religious practices. (195:9.7) Jesus within brings unity, transformation, and newness to all who receive him. (100:7.18) Impart advanced instruction only after men are born of the spirit in the kingdom. (141:6.4) To achieve stability and eternal goals in a changing world, one must experience spiritual rebirth and embrace the living God. (160:1.14) definition conversion experience results in spiritual rebirth
improvedDescription: The transformative experience of being born of the spirit, marking the transition from material-minded existence to spiritual consciousness as a child of God. This rebirth involves a fundamental reorientation of personality values and the beginning of eternal life through living faith.

name: spiritual unity
originalDescription: achieved current situation
improvedDescription: The harmonious fellowship among believers derived from the common indwelling of the Father's spirit, creating bonds of shared spiritual purpose and divine dedication. Unlike uniformity, spiritual unity allows for diversity of thought and experience while maintaining fundamental spiritual harmony.

name: spiritualism
originalDescription: Dead humans never communicate with the living on earth, as spirits of the departed do not return to establish communication after death. (112:3.7) (150:3.5) Dead souls never return to their native planet in the same dispensation, but may be carried by transport seraphim to other worlds in the system for spiritual growth and ultimately reunite in eternal manifestation on the mansion worlds.
