(29:2.10) The Supreme Power Directors and Supreme Center Supervisors oversee cosmic phenomena below gravity energy levels, working individually and collaboratively like the Seven Supreme Executives and Seven Master Spirits in their respective domains. (29:2.11)
improvedDescription: Seven beings who coordinate and regulate the master energy circuits of the grand universe, each headquartered on a special world of the Seven Supreme Executives and working in close association with them as regulators of universal energy distribution.

name: Supreme Chief
originalDescription: Orlandof led a revival among the blue race, teaching them to worship the true God as the “Supreme Chief.” (64:6.23)
improvedDescription: The name used by Orlandof to teach the blue race to worship the true God, leading a widespread religious revival about five hundred years after Caligastia's downfall that brought many tribes back to proper worship.

name: Supreme Circuit Supervisors
originalDescription: Supreme Circuit Supervisors, number 7, serve exclusively as agents of the Infinite Spirit on the Havona circuits. (24:1.2-8)
improvedDescription: Seven completed beings created exclusively by the Infinite Spirit who serve as agents regulating the circuits of the seven Havona circuits, stationed on the pilot worlds and working in coordination with Paradise spheres.

name: Supreme Council of Destiny
originalDescription: The Supreme Council of Destiny is comprised of heads of all 7 finaliter corps, overseen by the senior Master Architect on Paradise, Grandfanda. (31:10.1-10,20)
improvedDescription: A Paradise council comprised of the presiding heads of all seven finaliter corps, led by Grandfanda as chief, representing the mobilization of universe personalities and potentials for future outer space activities and inconceivable cosmic developments.

name: supreme council of Salvington
originalDescription: The supreme council of Salvington, an advisory local universe council, consists of three members from each system and seven representatives from each constellation, with authority to issue rulings of execution based on fundamental laws of the universe. (33:8.3-5) During the time of Jesus' tomb on Urantia, the supreme council of Salvington met under Gabriel's presidency while the Ancients of Days communicated with Michael. (188:3.12) A Mighty Messenger temporarily attached to the Supreme Council of Salvington was assigned to this mission by Gabriel of Salvington. (32:5.9) Rebellion has suspended Satania representation on the supreme council at universe headquarters. (45:3.22)
improvedDescription: An advisory local universe council consisting of three members from each system and seven representatives from each constellation, functioning as a superlegislature that makes recommendations based on fundamental universe laws but lacks enforcement authority.

name: Supreme Council of Universe Adjustments
originalDescription: The reserve corps of the Unions of Days, with seven hundred thousand beings in existence, functions as the Supreme Council of Universe Adjustments on Paradise. (18:6.2)
improvedDescription: The reserve corps of seven hundred thousand Unions of Days that functions on Paradise as the Supreme Council of Universe Adjustments, handling cosmic administrative matters requiring high-level coordination.

name: supreme council of unlimited authority
originalDescription: The finaliter corps, for the first time, acknowledges the jurisdiction of an extra-Paradise authority, the supreme council, having recognized no supervision this side of Paradise. (55:10.7) Settled local universes in light and life establish a supreme council of unlimited authority, led by the Union of Days and consisting of 100 Faithfuls of Days, to address the new challenges of advanced status. (55:10) Trinity Teacher Sons ascend to serve the supreme council of unlimited authority after advising Planetary Sovereigns and Constellation Fathers in groups of three during the epoch of light and life. (55:10.6)
improvedDescription: A governing body established when a universe becomes settled in light and life, consisting of one hundred Faithfuls of Days presided over by the Union of Days, representing the first extra-Paradise authority acknowledged by the finaliter corps.

name: supreme court of tribal co-ordination and racial co-operation
improvedDescription: A judicial body that coordinated tribal affairs and promoted racial cooperation among early human societies.

name: Supreme Creators
originalDescription: The Master Architects coordinate the assignment of Creator Sons for local universes, in close association with the Supreme Creators and the Trinity Ultimate. (31:9.14) (36:6.7) (56:5.3) (56:6.1-2) (106:3.2) (106:5.2) (115:4.7) (116:2) (116:4.2) (117:1.2) (117:3.7) (118:0.9) (118:4.7) The function of Supreme Creators originated with the organization of the 7 superuniverses and will likely evolve further in outer space creations. (0:8.12) Supreme Creators descended from Paradise to evolve creatures with Paradise attainment capacity, revealing the mutual understanding and eternal brotherhood between descenders and ascenders. (0:2.16) (117:1.2) The divine unity of power and personality of the Supreme Creators constitutes a new power potential of experiential Deity, inevitably united with the experiential Deity of Trinity origin—the Supreme Being. (116:2.13) (116:4) The singular ministry of the Supreme Creator unifies all spiritual influences in human experience. (34:6.2) The sovereignty of Supreme grows out of achievements of Supreme Creators in the grand universe. (0:7.7) (56:6.2) (106:5.2) (115:4.7) (116:2) (116:4.2) (117:3.7) (118:0.9) Supreme Creators transmute matured potentials into experiential actuals within the master universe's time-transmutations of matured potentials into experiential actuals. (118:4.7)
improvedDescription: The collective designation for the first three levels of God the Sevenfold, including the local universe Creator Sons and Spirits, superuniverse Ancients of Days, and Seven Master Spirits of Paradise, who actively manifest divinity in the superuniverses and contribute to the evolution of the Supreme Being.

name: Supreme Executive #7 (of Orvonton)
originalDescription: The Associate Inspector on Salvington reports to the Supreme Executive #7 of Orvonton, along with his Assigned Sentinels. (37:8.5) The omniaphim on Uversa report exclusively to Supreme Executive #7 of Orvonton for overseeing the superuniverses' administrative coordination. (28:2.2)
improvedDescription: One of the Seven Supreme Executives who functions from executive sphere number seven on Paradise and is wholly devoted to managing the affairs of Orvonton, the seventh superuniverse, receiving reports from omniaphim and other representatives throughout the superuniverse.

name: Supreme Executives
improvedDescription: One of the seven groups of Supreme Spirits, derived from the Paradise Trinity and serving as universal coordinating directors alongside the Master Spirits and Reflective Spirits in the administration of the grand universe.

name: Supreme Lanonandek councils
originalDescription: System Sovereigns are selected by Supreme Lanonandek councils on constellation headquarters, with reserves larger on Edentia than average. (35:9.3-4)
improvedDescription: Governing bodies stationed on constellation headquarters that select System Sovereigns and assistants from reserves of the Lanonandek order, presided over by senior and junior Most High associates of the Constellation Father.

name: Supreme Mind
originalDescription: Reflectivity in the cosmos is accounted for by the evolving experiential mind of the Supreme. (9:7.4) A Supreme Mind activates the living mechanism of the grand universe, guiding the emerging personality of the cosmos towards mastery of energy-matter. (116:7.3) (118:9.4) The Supreme Mind was bestowed on Supreme by Infinite Spirit for the unification of three phases of Deity reality. (22:7.11) (56:6.2) (115:4.7) The Supreme Mind's cosmic coordination with the Seven Master Spirits ensures unity and harmony across the superuniverses. (42:10.6) (56:2.3) Trinitized Sons of Destiny resulting from finaliter and Paradise-Havoner trinitization repercuss in phases of the Supreme-Ultimate Mind. (22:7.14) (23:4.2-3) Majeston focalizes presence of the Supreme Mind in the cosmic scheme of creation. (0:3.14) (116:4.3) The dominance of the Supreme Mind is indicated by progressive evolution just as appreciative creature mind is implied by the existence of beauty. (56:10.11) The creation of Solitary Messengers by the Infinite Spirit may be related to the bestowal of Supreme-Ultimate Mind, as they demonstrate the ability to co-ordinate all types of personalities and make contact with the master universe overcontrollers. (23:4.2-3) The Supreme Mind unifies the spirit person of the Supreme with the attained power of the Almighty, resulting in a unified and personal Deity. (56:6.2) (116:1.2) (116:3.2-3) (117:7.14) The unknown potential of Supreme Mind complements the spirit-personal qualities of the Supreme and power prerogatives of the Almighty, illustrating the unification of the cosmos. (106:5.2)
improvedDescription: The experiential mind of the Supreme Being, bestowed by the Infinite Spirit to unify the spirit person of God the Supreme with the power of the Almighty Supreme, serving as the individual contribution of the Third Source and Center to the reality of the Supreme Being.

name: Supreme Power Centers
originalDescription: Each power center is made up of 1 million units of mobile and kaleidoscopic functional control, coordinating with segregated energy concentrations. (29:3.7) Power centers utilize their antigravity endowments to establish physical equilibrium in the universe of universes. (9:3.6) (29:3.6) (42:4.4) Ascenders on minor sector worlds study the activities of Supreme Power Centers and Master Physical Controllers in relation to the great physical problems of the superuniverses. (18:5.3) The power centers, created by the children of the Infinite Spirit, are linked to the Supreme Being's cosmic oversight. (29:3.3) The Supreme Power Centers and Supreme Power Directors are beings of high will, freedom, and action, possessing Third-Source personality, unquestioned volitional capacity, exquisite intelligence, and intellect for controlling the vast universe power system. (29:2.9) Living power mechanisms exert near-supremacy in local energy situations, always conscious of the superenergy presence and unfathomable influence of the Unqualified Absolute. (29:3.12) The power centers control the mechanical responses of living organisms, while the adjutant spirits operate on higher energy domains. (65:7.5) Supreme Power Centers coordinate activities with Creator Son's universe plans, directing physical evolution inspired by Paradise force organizers. (41:1.1) Over 10 billion Supreme Power Centers, tasked with controlling the grand universe, operate in perfect synchrony under the guidance of the Seven Master Spirits and Seven Supreme Power Directors. (29:3.1) Living power centers on specially constructed spheres distribute light without heat to directionize, modify, and concentrate energy in the universe. (29:3.9) 700,000 million years ago, early work in Andronover nebulae provided support and cooperation to evolving power centers in the rapidly expanding Andronover system. (57:2.3) Supreme Power Centers, endowed with Third-Source personality, possess high will freedom, action, exquisite intelligence, and unparalleled volitional capacity. (29:2.9) The Supreme Power Directors and Centers have existed since near eternity, with no more beings of these orders created. (29:0.10) Supreme Power Centers operate exclusively on purpose-built spheres outside of Havona to manipulate and distribute energy efficiently. (29:3.9) Gigantic beings manipulate and regulate master circuits of universe energy, beyond human understanding. (29:3.8) Supreme Power Centers are listed in personality registers. (30:1.89) (30:2.92) Master Physical Controllers are mobile subordinates of the Supreme Power Centers, capable of individuality metamorphosis and autotransport at speeds comparable to Solitary Messengers, relying on assistance to overcome gravity and inertia in leaving a material sphere. (29:4.1) Master Spirit Number Five, adviser to power directors, power centers, and physical controllers, fosters all personalities originating in the Universal Father and the Infinite Spirit, and speaks on Father-Spirit matters in the councils of the Seven Master Spirits. (16:3.10) Supreme Power Centers modify, manipulate, and directionize physical energies of space by their presence, collaborating with other beings to produce an integrated administrative unit within the vast universe. (29:3.6) (41:1.1-5) (42:2.22-23) Power centers and physical controllers, never playing but always on duty, are thoroughly businesslike in their actions, overseeing the energy circuits of time and space without interruption. (29:3.5) (48:4.11) The Supreme Power Centers operate autonomously, free from external interference or influence. (29:3.2) The Supreme Power Centers are part of God the Sevenfold. (116:5.3) The Supreme Power Centers perfectly control 7 of 10 forms of energy in each basic universe, with the remaining realms potentially under the influence of the Unqualified Absolute. (29:3.11) Power directors and energy controllers originating from the Third Source and Center precede Creator Sons in universe organization, collaborating with them to establish and administer inhabited realms and create diverse spirit personalities. (32:2.1-2) (41:1.1) The Supreme Power Centers are beings endowed with Third-Source personality, exquisite intelligence, and unquestioned volitional capacity, revealing the secret of mind control by Master Physical Controllers and Morontia Power Supervisors. (29:2.9) Power centers and their associates transmute ultimatons into electrons, controlling energy's circulation until its range of action is curtailed in the whirls of atomic systems. (42:4.3) Supreme power centers are created in perfection and never evolve in ranks, maintaining their original assignments. (29:3.4) Supreme power centers utilize vast material mechanisms in coordination with living energy concentrations in a kaleidoscope of functional control. (29:3.7) The Master Physical Controllers built Salvington in one billion years for physical and spiritual beings. (32:2.3) orders
improvedDescription: Beings created by the Seven Supreme Power Directors in association with the Seven Master Spirits who function as living mechanisms to control, modify, and directionize the physical energies of space throughout the grand universe, operating in seven orders numbering over ten billion.

name: Supreme Power Directors
improvedDescription: Seven beings personalized by the Seven Master Spirits who serve as the Paradise progenitors and directors of the Supreme Power Centers, responsible for the power control of the grand universe.

name: supreme reaction of complement
originalDescription: The birth of a local universe Mother Spirit is a result of the supreme reaction of complement during the initial Paradise differentiation of a Creator Son. (17:6.3)
improvedDescription: An inherent modification that occurs within the person of the Infinite Spirit when a Creator Son is personalized, designating the birth of the potential for the future local universe Creative Mother Spirit.

name: Supreme Rulers
improvedDescription: A conceptual reference to the highest governing authorities in the universe, encompassing the supreme administrative powers that rule over creation.

name: supreme seraphim
originalDescription: Michael's fourth bestowal as a teaching counselor of the supreme seraphim marked a significant milestone in the history of Nebadon. (39:1.15) (119:4) None of the supreme order of seraphim were lost in Lucifer's rebellion, but a significant number of the next order were deceived, along with a few of the third order angels. The real devastation occurred with the administrator angels, where nearly one third joined the rebels despite Manotia saving almost two thirds. (53:7.6) Supreme seraphim, as advisers to morontia mortals, bring material and morontial viewpoints into true focus, enabling a stereoscopic effect on meanings and values. (48:6.28) types
improvedDescription: The highest of the seven revealed orders of local universe angels, functioning in seven groups closely associated with the Seraphic Corps of Completion and serving as teaching counselors and advisers to morontia mortals.

name: supreme service
originalDescription: The order of “supreme service” is the highest honor granted in the morontia temple for distinguished supermaterial or social service. (55:3.8,19)
improvedDescription: The highest honor conferred in the morontia temple, granted to citizens who have distinguished themselves in supermaterial discovery or planetary social service.

name: Supreme Spirits
originalDescription: The seven Supreme Spirit groups are the universal co-ordinating directors of the grand universe's administration. (17:0.1-12) (17:8.3) All ministering spirits spring from the Supreme Spirit groups, together with their Infinite Mother Spirit, as the immediate creators of the vast creature family of the Third Source and Center. (17:8.2) The Supreme Spirits appear in personality registers and listed as: (30:1.56) (30:2.14) The seven groups of Supreme Spirits form the core of the functional family of the Infinite Spirit, uniting administrative levels and coordinating functions across the central, super, and local universes. (17:8.1)
improvedDescription: Seven groups of beings that serve as the universal coordinating directors of the grand universe administration, including the Master Spirits, Supreme Executives, Reflective Spirits, and four other orders created by the Infinite Spirit or associates.

name: Supreme Trinity Personalities
originalDescription: Supreme Trinity Personalities, created in perfection and of definite and final numbers, represent the administrative policies, justice, and executive judgment of the Paradise Trinity throughout the grand universe. (18:0.9-11) Groups comprising Supreme Trinity Personalities are created by the divine Trinity for specific duties, serving with perfection and devotion in seven orders. (18:0.1) The Supreme Trinity Personalities, representing the justice and executive judgment of the Paradise Trinity, have never strayed from the divine path throughout the grand universe. (18:0.10-11)
improvedDescription: Supreme Trinity Personalities are beings of administrative perfection created in definite and final numbers by the Paradise Trinity for specific duties throughout the grand universe. They represent the administrative policies, justice, and executive judgment of the Paradise Trinity, serving with perfection and devotion in seven distinct orders. These beings have never strayed from the divine path and form an interrelated line of administrative perfection extending from Paradise to local universe headquarters.

name: Supreme-Ultimate
originalDescription: It is postulated the sometime appearance of the Supreme-Ultimate as a potential realization of the Absolute Trinity's full unification. (0:12.8)
improvedDescription: The Supreme-Ultimate is a postulated future realization that may appear through the potential full unification of the Absolute Trinity. This being represents the possible trinitization-factualization of God the Absolute, though the complete unification of the Absolute Trinity is considered doubtful by celestial observers.

name: surgery
originalDescription: Surgeons from Avalon transplanted life plasm from fifty males and fifty females of the Andon and Fonta posterity to the one hundred Jerusemite members of the Prince's staff, chosen by the Life Carriers. (66:2.7) Trephining skulls allowed headache spirits to escape, marking the early roots of surgical practices in ancient medicine. (90:4.4)
improvedDescription: Surgery had early origins in primitive medicine when shamans performed trephining operations to allow supposed headache spirits to escape from patients' skulls. Later surgical practices included treating fractures and dislocations, opening boils and abscesses, and midwifery. Advanced surgical techniques were also used by Avalon surgeons who transplanted life plasm from Andonite descendants to the Jerusemite members of the Prince's staff.

name: surprise
originalDescription: To God, no event comes as a surprise; he inhabits the circle of eternity and is never subjected to surprise. (2:1.5) (3:2.6-7) (3:3.4)
improvedDescription: God is never subjected to surprise because He inhabits the circle of eternity and foresees all cosmic events. To God there is no past, present, or future; all time is present at any given moment, and His power and wisdom are wholly adequate to cope with any universe exigencies in accordance with eternal wisdom and infinite judgment.

name: survival (of mortal death)
originalDescription: Your Adjuster memory survives while animalistic memories perish with physical brain, retaining worth while experiences throughout ascendant career. (47:4.5) Only by becoming God-identified can finite beings truly become real in the universes. (118:7.5) Through evolutionary ascension, the ascendant career has been mastered step by step, life by life, world by world, culminating in survival complete in perfection. (26:9.4) Eye has not seen what God has prepared for survivors in the heavenly realms beyond mortal death. (11:4.5) (24:6.2) (43:6.8) (44:2.1) Man's eternal destiny is assured when Adjuster fusion proclaims the final and irrevocable choice to do the Father's will, despite mortal man's fluctuating ability to draw near or forsake it. (5:1.11) Utilize time wisely to ensure soul survival, for with that, all other losses can be recovered. (28:6.9) Survival of mortal death is a gift of God which must be desired, never forced on anyone. (5:6.12) (54:6.9) (110:2.2) (146:2.5) Intellectual and spiritual growth continues through morontia existence, transitioning from physicality to true morontial states in local systems and ultimately reaching spiritual heights on Salvington spheres. (48:1.4) The pursuit of the ideal, striving to be Godlike, is a continuous effort before and after death, as life after death is no different in essentials than mortal existence, with every good deed in this life contributing to the enhancement of the future life. (103:5.7) The contemplation of nature does not afford ground for belief in human personality survival; it reveals only a God of motion and life, in whom the religious man has already found in his own soul. (101:2.9) Survival beyond mortal death is not tied to knowledge or wisdom, but rather to the choice to achieve perfection and do the will of the Father in heaven. (65:8.4) Personal survival beyond mortal death is not detectable in nature or mind, only spiritually. (101:10.2-4) The mortal-survival plan is not for endless bliss, but for transcendent service beyond the universe age. (48:8.3) The presence of evil is a sufficient test for the ascension of man, as sin is not essential to survival in the natural order of this world. (148:5.2) The ministry of Thought Adjusters and guardian angels is essential for the terrestrial escape of mortals from mortal death. (49:6) The Adjuster possession of spirit personality survives natural death, enabling the composite entity of spirit origin to endure beyond the dissolution of the material self. (101:3.3) The true concept of God is indispensable to survival beyond mortal death. (1:2.7) historically adjudication of survival | judgment:divine judgment assurance of survival how is attained rejecting survival
improvedDescription: Survival of mortal death is the continuation of personality existence beyond physical death through the evolution of an immortal soul in association with the indwelling Thought Adjuster. This survival depends on the mortal's choice to do the Father's will and seek God-likeness, not on knowledge or wisdom. The Adjuster preserves all experiences and memories of survival value throughout the ascendant career, while purely material and animalistic associations perish with the physical brain.

name: survival value
originalDescription: Every meaningful value in every will creature is assured of eternal survival by their Adjuster. (109:6.1,7) Your Adjuster counterparts memories of survival value as you ascend the morontia life. (47:4.5) 160 million years ago, biologic tribulation eliminated species without survival value, paving the way for the highly differentiated life of planetary evolution. (59:6.10) Man can exhibit apparently religious traits while denying God, but godless humanism lacks survival values, God-knowingness, and spiritual growth. (102:7.4) The government, evolving by trial and error, has survival value as it slowly emerges to establish law and order in response to the coercive demands of the struggle for existence. (70:0.3) If intentions of survival value are demonstrated, mortals will be granted additional time on mansion worlds for probation extension to attain cosmic circles of personality growth. (112:5.6) In association, enhanced survival value has propelled the progression of civilization on Urantia, despite numerous setbacks. (68:1.5) The love of offspring has undeniable survival value, evident in the sacrificial behaviors of ancient and primitive mothers, as well as the enduring joy and pride experienced by civilized parents in their children's advancement. (84:7.10) Every meaningful value in every will creature is certain of survival, for nothing of survival value is ever lost in all the wide universe. (109:3.2) (109:6.1) The evolution of selfhood with survival value involves transferring mortal identity to the immortal nature of the soul. (112:2.20) The immortal soul emerges through the collaboration of moral mind, divine spirit, and intellect, uniting in a physical tabernacle with cosmic survival qualities. (16:9.2) (108:1.6) (133:6.5-6)
improvedDescription: Survival value refers to the worth-while qualities, experiences, and meanings that persist beyond mortal death and contribute to eternal existence. These values include moral decisions, spiritual insights, and meaningful experiences that the Thought Adjuster counterparts and preserves throughout the ascendant career. Every meaningful value in every will creature is assured of eternal survival, as nothing of true worth is ever lost in the universe.

name: survivors
improvedDescription: Survivors are mortals who achieve continuation of personality existence beyond physical death through fusion with their Thought Adjusters or other spirit entities, thereby gaining access to the ascending career toward Paradise and eternal service.

name: Susa
originalDescription: Jesus and Ganid were captivated by the early history and ruins of Susa. (133:9.2) Susa greatly prospered during floodtimes, succeeding other cities as the headquarters for artcrafts. (78:8.2) (78:8.9-10)
improvedDescription: Susa was an ancient city that greatly prospered during flood times, serving as headquarters for distinctive artcrafts of its era. Jesus and Ganid were fascinated by its early history and ruins, spending three weeks there during their Mediterranean journey to allow Jesus time to conduct investigations of this historically significant location.

name: Susanna (daughter of Ezra of Alexandria)
originalDescription: Susanna discovered the empty tomb where Jesus had lain. (189:4.2-14) At Jesus’ 5th appearance, he greeted the assembled women believers, encouraging them to spread the good news of the kingdom and promising to be with them always. (190:3.1)
improvedDescription: Susanna, daughter of Ezra of Alexandria, was one of the five women who discovered Jesus' empty tomb on resurrection morning. She was among the women who encountered the risen Jesus and received his instruction to tell the apostles of his resurrection. She later witnessed Jesus' fifth morontia appearance to the assembled women believers at Joseph of Arimathea's house.

name: Susanna (daughter of Nazareth chazan)
originalDescription: Susanna, daughter of Nazareth chazan, was appointed as chief of the women's corps by Jesus on January 16, an astonishing decision that empowered ten women to minister in his kingdom. (150:1.1-2) At Jesus’ 16th appearance, he instructed his disciples to proclaim the gospel of the kingdom and live as God-revealing sons of God on earth. (193:0)
improvedDescription: Susanna, daughter of the former chazan of the Nazareth synagogue, was appointed by Jesus as chief of the women's corps on January 16. She was one of ten women commissioned to teach the gospel of the kingdom and minister to the sick, organizing their own administration and serving as their leader throughout Jesus' ministry.

name: Susatia
originalDescription: The Susatia, marvelous offspring of the Creator Son and Mother Spirit, reside in Salvington and are closely connected with the Spirit-fused mortals of Nebadon. (37:9.7) Susatia in personality registers represents the essence of the Local Universe. (30:1.45) (30:2.120) The Susatia, permanent citizens of Salvington, are closely associated with Spirit-fused mortals. (37:9.7)
improvedDescription: The Susatia are marvelous beings who reside as permanent citizens on Salvington, the headquarters of the local universe. They are the brilliant offspring of the Creator Son and Creative Spirit and are closely associated with the Spirit-fused mortals of Nebadon.

name: suspicion
originalDescription: Ignorance breeds suspicion, hindering intellectual cross-fertilization essential for brotherhood. (52:6.4) Ignorance breeds suspicion, which is incompatible with sympathy and love, hindering intellectual cross-fertilization and brotherhood among primitive inhabitants. (52:6.4) Suspicion is the inherent reaction of primitive men, but trust is a new human acquisition instilled by the planetary seraphim of the Adamic regime. (39:5.7) Judas indulged in hate and suspicion, ultimately leading him to betray his friends and his Master. (139:12.6,9) Suspicion, a mental poison, tremendously interferes with spiritual progress, defiling the earthly tabernacle of the marvelous gift from God. (110:1.5) Teach children to trust by guiding them to choose heroes and lifework wisely, and refraining from suggesting suspicion. (140:5.14) Stumble not into misunderstanding entanglements of suspicion, but love all men as brothers in the household of faith. (191:4.3)
improvedDescription: Suspicion is the inherent reaction of primitive beings that hinders intellectual cross-fertilization essential for brotherhood. This mental poison tremendously interferes with spiritual progress and is incompatible with sympathy and love, while trust represents a new human acquisition brought about by planetary seraphim.

name: Susquehanna River
originalDescription: The Susquehanna River carved through layers of volcanic deposits, revealing remnants of a tumultuous geological past 230,000,000 years ago. (59:4.16)
improvedDescription: The Susquehanna River carved through layers of volcanic deposits in the Appalachian Mountains, revealing successive geological layers over 13,000 feet thick from 230 million years ago.

name: sweat
improvedDescription: A bodily secretion produced through perspiration, mentioned in various contexts throughout the revelations.

name: swine
originalDescription: The swine became fetish animals, leading to taboos on eating their flesh. (88:1.5) Do not cast pearls before swine; present not that which is holy to dogs. (140:3.18) (186:2.2) Dogs drove thirty swine into the sea, believed to be carrying the evil spirits that tormented Amos before Jesus cured him. (151:6.6) The evil of eating pork, like the exposure of women during menstruation, stems from ancient taboos and beliefs. (84:4.8) (89:1.5) Giant pigs, over six feet tall, went extinct during the Miocene era when the first deer arrived and North America was dominated by ruminants. (61:3.5) The prodigal son desired husks of the swine. (169:1.7)
improvedDescription: Swine became fetish animals in ancient times, leading to taboos on eating their flesh. Jesus cast pearls before swine as an example of not presenting sacred things to those unable to appreciate them, and swine were involved in the legendary account of Jesus healing the lunatic Amos.

name: Switzerland
originalDescription: Andites in Switzerland merged with their brethren from Italy to form a great colony near Carthage. (80:7.11) During Jesus' trip to Switzerland, he engaged in a profound discussion about Buddhism with both father and son. (132:7.3) Pilate retired to Switzerland and committed suicide after his regretful condemnation of Jesus. (185:1.6) Despite varying beliefs about tree spirits among different tribes and races, the Swiss long mistrusted trees, fearing they contained tricky spirits. (85:2.4) The tribes dwelling in houses on piles over the lakes of Switzerland were part of the expanding fringes of African, Aegean, and Danubian migrations. (80:8.3)
improvedDescription: Switzerland was visited by Jesus during his Mediterranean journey, where he engaged in profound discussions about Buddhism. The region was inhabited by tribes dwelling in houses on piles over lakes and was later where Pontius Pilate retired and committed suicide after his condemnation of Jesus.

name: swords
originalDescription: The apostles armed with swords were prepared for the events leading up to the betrayal of Jesus in Jerusalem. (171:4.1) (172:0.3) (172:2.1) (172:5.2) Peter rushed forward with his sword, ready to protect Jesus from harm. (183:2.2) (183:3.7) The early priestly caste originated from a family of expert swordmakers. (69:3.9) Those who fight with swords perish with swords, but those who labor in the spirit achieve life everlasting in the kingdom with joy and peace. (181:2.11) (183:3.7)
improvedDescription: Swords were carried by some of Jesus' apostles, particularly Simon Peter, during the events leading to the Master's betrayal. Jesus taught that those who fight with swords perish with swords, while those who labor in the spirit achieve life everlasting.

name: sycamores
originalDescription: Zaccheus climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus, who then surprised him by deciding to abide at his house for the night. (171:6.1)
improvedDescription: Sycamore trees were among the flowering plants that appeared during significant plant evolution. Notably, Zaccheus climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus as he passed through Jericho, leading to Jesus' decision to abide at his house.

name: Sychar
originalDescription: Jesus’ 17th appearance in Sychar was a powerful message of salvation and brotherly love to the Samaritan believers near Jacob's well. (193:1) Jesus preached for 2 days in Sychar before moving to Mount Gerizim, where the dwellers believed in the gospel and requested baptism, which the apostles did not yet administer. (143:6.2) Nalda at Jacob's well, a Samaritan woman of Sychar, encountered Jesus and received the living water, ultimately finding redemption and proclaiming his divinity to the people. (143:5)
improvedDescription: Sychar was a Samaritan city near Jacob's well where Jesus made his 17th appearance to about seventy-five believers. Jesus and his apostles preached there for two days before establishing camp on Mount Gerizim, and it was where Jesus first encountered Nalda, the Samaritan woman at the well.

name: symbolism
originalDescription: Symbolism in religion can be positive or negative based on whether it replaces the original worshipful idea, but is distinct from direct idolatry. (85:3.5) The cult type of social organization persists by providing a symbolism for perpetuating inspiring ideals, fostering moral sentiments and religious loyalties, and ensuring cultural survival and realization. (87:7.1) Those with greater spiritual insight should be patient and tolerant of those who rely on symbolism to mobilize their feeble spiritual insight, avoiding disdain for the weak. (91:5.7)
improvedDescription: Symbolism in religion can be either beneficial or detrimental depending on whether it preserves or replaces the original worshipful idea. It provides a means for perpetuating moral sentiments and religious loyalties, and those with greater spiritual insight should be tolerant of those who require symbols to mobilize their spiritual understanding.

name: symmetry
originalDescription: All real beauty is symmetrical, embodying the integration of truth, goodness, and happiness in human experience. (2:7.11) In philosophy, leaning towards the spiritual level leads to idealism or even mysticism, while relying on metaphysics results in skepticism and confusion, highlighting the elliptical symmetry of all reality. (103:6.14) The unity of divine law and divine love reveals Father's infinite symmetry. (111:6.6) The presence of Holy Spirit, Spirit of Truth, and Adjuster in an evolutionary mortal signifies spiritual symmetry and enables the conscious realization of sonship with God. (34:5.7) Orvonton will have a high degree of symmetry due to the balanced union and perfect coordination of traits from the presiding Spirit, ensuring transcendent development in the eternal ages of the future. (34:2.6) The unique feature of Jesus' personality was its exquisite and balanced symmetry, admired by Thomas for its unmatched perfection and unity. (100:7.1) (139:8.7)
improvedDescription: A fundamental principle of divine reality characterized by perfect balance and harmonious integration. All real beauty embodies symmetry through the unified blending of truth, goodness, and spiritual elements in experience. Divine law and divine love reveal the Father's infinite symmetry, while the presence of spiritual endowments in mortals signifies spiritual symmetry that enables conscious realization of sonship with God.

name: sympathy
originalDescription: Teach believers to avoid false sympathy, as it hinders the development of strong characters and perpetuates self-pity and misery. (159:3.11) Jesus taught that one cannot develop strong characters out of self-pity. (159:3.1) Avoid seeking unearned recognition and undeserved sympathy, instead strive for honesty and fairness in preaching truth and proclaiming the gospel. (156:5.19) The gospel should never be preached through coercion, but instead through respect for the personalities and spiritual power within each individual. (159:3.2) Jesus rarely showed pity, preferring instead to offer genuine sympathy and sincere compassion that was practical, personal, and constructive, never allowing familiarity with suffering to breed indifference or increase self-pity in those he ministered to. (171:7.3) Avoid false sympathy and do not offer consolation to those who give up without a fight. (159:3.11) Overmuch sympathy, like any virtue carried to extremes, may degenerate into serious emotional instability or even fanaticism, as Jesus warned against during a discussion on the importance of possessing well-balanced characters. (149:4.3) Allowing free expression of evil in rebels' hearts uproots sympathy for evildoers in all citizens of Norlatiadek. (54:5.9-12) Ignorance breeds suspicion, incompatible with sympathy. (52:6.4) Jesus urged his listeners to embrace sympathy without sentimentality, piety without sanctimoniousness, and reverence free from fear and superstition, cautioning against the dangers of both recklessness and presumption. (149:4.4)
improvedDescription: A genuine emotional response and compassionate understanding toward others that should be practical, personal, and constructive. Jesus taught against false sympathy that breeds self-pity and weakness, instead advocating for sincere compassion that encourages courage and strength. True sympathy should be balanced and restrained, as excessive sympathy can degenerate into emotional instability or fanaticism.

name: synagogues
originalDescription: Jesus often spoke in synagogues at first, sharing his teachings during Sabbath services and evening assemblies, until opposition from religious leaders led to widespread closures. (146:4.1) Believers in Jesus were excommunicated from all synagogues in Jerusalem. (164:4.6,11) (164:5.4-5) (174:5.3) (190:3.3) (190:5.1) The synagogues, once open to Jesus, were closed to him by the religious leaders in Jerusalem, leading to widespread opposition against his teachings. (146:4.1) (154:1.2) (154:2.1) The Hebron synagogue, the one synagogue in Palestine that resisted the Sanhedrin's decree, was destroyed by fire. (154:2.1) Despite the Sanhedrin's decree closing all synagogues to Jesus and his followers, the synagogue at Hebron refused to acknowledge their authority, leading to its destruction by fire. (146:4.1) (154:2.1) Woe upon all those who seek chief seats in the synagogue while rejecting the revelation of God's love and truth. (166:1.4) (175:1.9) Trust in the spirit within you to guide your words when enemies bring you before the rulers of synagogues and high authorities. (165:3.7)
improvedDescription: Jewish houses of worship and community gathering where Jesus initially taught during Sabbath services and evening assemblies. Following opposition from Jerusalem religious leaders, the Sanhedrin issued a decree in A.D. 29 closing all synagogues in Palestine to Jesus and his followers, with only the Hebron synagogue refusing to comply before being destroyed by fire. Believers in Jesus were subsequently excommunicated from synagogues throughout Judea.

name: syncopation
improvedDescription: A musical technique or rhythmic pattern mentioned in The Urantia Book's discussions of celestial music and harmonious arrangements.

name: Syracuse
originalDescription: During Jesus' week at Syracuse, he rehabilitated Ezra, the backslidden Jew, helping him find God and ultimately leading to the establishment of the first Christian church in the city. (130:8.2)
improvedDescription: A Mediterranean port city where Jesus and his companions spent a full week during their journey to Rome. The notable event of their stay was Jesus helping Ezra, a backslidden Jewish tavern keeper, find God and return to faith, which later led to the establishment of the first Christian church in Syracuse through Ezra's partnership with a Greek proselyte.

name: Syria
originalDescription: The Syrians, worshiping their gods, also believed in Yahweh of the Hebrews as mentioned by their prophets. (96:1.13) The broad-headed Nodite-Andonites in Syria introduced pottery, agriculture, and advanced metalworking techniques to the region. (80:1.2) Syria was once the center of early metalworking and pottery industry, as well as an important hub for agriculture and domesticated animal trade. (80:1.2) Syria thrived in peace and prosperity during the time of Jesus under Roman rule. (121:1.9) King David laid heavy tribute on the neighboring tribes, including the Syrians. (97:9.14) Malach from Syria welcomed Jesus in Beirut. (156:3.1) Matthew preached in Syria, Cappadocia, Galatia, Bithynia, and Thrace, ultimately dying triumphant in the faith. (139:7.10) Norana from Syria displayed unwavering faith in Jesus as a healer, leading to her daughter's miraculous healing and demonstrating the power of gentile belief in the teachings of the kingdom of heaven. (156:1) Pilate, serving under the legatus of Syria, ordered to Rome after condemning Jesus to crucifixion. (185:0.1) (185:1.6) Rome strategically played off Seleucid Syria against Ptolemaic Egypt to maintain control over the vital Palestinian highway and caravan routes, allowing an independent Palestine to thrive. (121:2.8) The western Nodite headquarters were established in northeastern Syria on the shores of the Mediterranean. (73:1.5)
improvedDescription: A region in the eastern Mediterranean that served as an important center for early civilization, metalworking, pottery, and agriculture. The broad-headed Nodite-Andonite Syrians introduced advanced techniques and domesticated animals to surrounding areas. During Jesus' time, Syria enjoyed peace and prosperity under Roman rule and was the destination of Jesus' final journey outside Palestine, where he visited Antioch, Sidon, and other cities.

name: system
originalDescription: The personality as the meaning of the whole signifies the unification and coordination of all factors of reality within a system of interconnected individual members. (112:1.17,19) The arrangement of different morontia and spirit beings creates majestic melodies. (44:1.7)
improvedDescription: An organized arrangement of three or more interconnected parts that creates something greater than the sum of its components. Unlike simple relationships between two objects, systems involve positional values and cosmic organization where individual members are connected through their relationship to the whole. In human systems, personality serves as the unifying factor that coordinates all activities and imparts identity and creativity.

name: system centers
improvedDescription: Administrative or organizational centers within local systems that coordinate various functions and activities across the inhabited worlds of a system.

name: system co-ordinators
originalDescription: System Co-ordinators appear in Uversa personality registers as part of the Morontia Power Supervisors, a group inside the Universe Power Directors. (30:2.110) System co-ordinators harmonize differing power systems on morontia worlds to create a working unit for ascending mortals progressing from the physical to the spiritual. (48:2.15-16)
improvedDescription: Specialized beings belonging to the Morontia Power Supervisors who harmonize and blend the differing energy systems of various morontia worlds into working units. They enable ascending mortals to progress smoothly from physical to spiritual existence by coordinating the individualized energy systems of each successive transition sphere in a local system.

name: System Sovereigns
originalDescription: System Sovereigns, appointed in pairs or trios by the Constellation Father, lead the governments of individual systems for millennia, with transitions occurring only in the event of tragedy. (35:9.2) System Sovereigns are selected by a supreme council and appointed by the Constellation Father to rule in commissions of two or three on each system's headquarters, with changes in leadership occurring every dekamillennium unless a tragedy necessitates a sudden shift in personnel. (35:9.2-4) Chiefs serve for dekamillennium in commissions of two or three on the headquarters of System Sovereigns overseeing inhabited worlds. (35:9.2) (35:10.1) 709,841 primary Lanonandeks designated as System Sovereigns in Nebadon are assistants to the supreme councils of constellations and counselors in higher administrative work of the universe, entrusted with large executive responsibilities and unusual personal prerogatives. (20:1.10) (35:8.4) (45:2.1) Three System Sovereigns have rebelled in Nebadon. (35:9.5-6) (119:2.1-6) System Sovereigns, entrusted with wide powers of personal discretion, have exhibited disloyalty only three times in the history of Nebadon. (45:2.1) The Urantia government is advised by the returning governor general as a temporary member of the System Sovereign's cabinet of Planetary Princes. (114:3.4) The house of System Sovereigns presided over by the Most Highs functions directly with the superuniverse government once constellations are settled in light, leading to a shift in legislative functions. (55:9.2) System Sovereigns, not supervised by Trinity observers, exercise almost complete sovereignty over the local affairs of inhabited worlds. (35:9.5) Rarely do System Sovereigns grant permission for narration of another planet's affairs, making the presentation of Uranita's history a rare and extraordinary occurrence. (72:0.2) functions
improvedDescription: Primary Lanonandek Sons who serve as chief executives of local systems of inhabited worlds. They rule in commissions of two or three from system headquarters, exercising wide powers of personal discretion in directing system affairs. System Sovereigns are appointed by Constellation Fathers and serve for dekamillennium periods, supervising Planetary Princes and enforcing legislative mandates throughout their systems.

name: Taanach
originalDescription: From the summit of the hill of Simeon at Nazareth, Jesus gazed upon Taanach, where Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera. (126:1.2)
improvedDescription: An ancient city where Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera, visible from the hill of Simeon near Nazareth where Jesus would often gaze upon the surrounding landscape and recall historical events.

name: Tabamantia
originalDescription: Tabamantia delivered a profound acknowledgment to the chief of Urantia Adjusters, expressing admiration and respect for the magnificent group of celestial ministers who have volunteered to serve on this irregular sphere. (108:3.5-6) Tabamantia, an agondonter of finaliter status, triumphed over insuperable difficulties and persevered through isolation from an early rebellion, proving the power of evolutionary will. (50:7.2) Tabamantia, the veteran finaliter and sovereign supervisor of Nebadon decimal planets, recommended the granting of Material Sons to Urantia after inspecting the planet and its progress. (49:5.12) (73:0.3) (108:3.5)
improvedDescription: A veteran finaliter of agondonter status who serves as sovereign supervisor of all decimal or experimental worlds in the universe of Nebadon. He inspected Urantia and recommended that the planet be granted Material Sons, leading to the arrival of Adam and Eve.

name: tabernacles, feast of
improvedDescription: A Jewish religious festival, one of the major feasts observed in Judaism.

name: taboos
originalDescription: The seven commandments of Dalamatia and Eden, along with the ten injunctions of the Hebrews, were transformative taboos that replaced many pre-existing prohibitions and offered rewards for obedience. (89:1.4) Taboos served as the earliest form of societal regulation, evolving to become the ancestor of primitive self-control and foundational to ceremonial standards. (89:1.1) Sickness is believed to be a punishment for sin or taboo violation, a prevailing theory among various cultures throughout history. (90:3.8) Taboos on killing, believed to have been handed down from gods, were established by self-interest, sanctified by society as traditional mores, and consecrated by religion as moral law, resulting in the safety and sanctity of human life. (70:11.5) Taboos, burdensome yet effective in organizing society, serve as the foundation for the traditions and conventions that shape civilized man's established institutions. (69:0.3) (89:1.6) Consecrated land, marked with family insignia and protected by magic taboos, bestowed a “priest's title” to the owners, as seen with the Hebrews' respect for family landmarks and stone markers. (69:9.13) Establishing institutions of survival, such as industry, property, and war for gain, is rooted in the fear instinct and regulated by taboo, convention, and religious sanction. (69:1.3) Primitive society operated negatively through taboos, inherently restricting liberties and enforcing prohibitive laws. (70:11.2) Jesus delivered us from the slavery of taboo and taught us to see the world as fundamentally good. (196:2.9) The violation of taboos among early tribes was seen as both a crime and a sin, leading to punishment and the invention of hells for violators. (89:2.4) Taboos were man's earliest effort to avoid offending ghosts, evolving into ceremonial standards and primitive self-control. (89:1.1) Primitive religion upholds far-flung taboos essential for civilized society's evolution. (89:1.7) relating to taboos
improvedDescription: The earliest form of societal regulation, originating as man's effort to avoid offending spirit ghosts. Taboos became the source of ceremonial standards and the ancestor of primitive self-control, serving as lawmakers and institution builders that helped organize early society despite being burdensome and restrictive.

name: tact
originalDescription: Tact is essential for success in securing the support and cooperation of others. (160:1.9) Tact is the fulcrum of social leverage, enabling wise souls to avoid unnecessary misunderstandings and navigate troublesome interactions with tolerance and grace. (156:5.18) The master gains victory gracefully through tact and tolerance, avoiding unnecessary battles. (160:3.4) Jesus, had it not been for a tactical blunder, would have managed the crowd and disarmed even his violent enemies with good-naturedness. (150:9.3)
improvedDescription: The fulcrum of social leverage that enables individuals to win support and cooperation from others. Tact allows wise souls to avoid unnecessary social misunderstandings and navigate difficult situations gracefully, as demonstrated by Jesus in his dealings with others.

name: tactics
originalDescription: Jesus never resorted to ignoble tactics in facing his adversaries, always responding with enlightening and dignified answers. (149:4.5) (173:2.7) Jesus would not compromise or serve evil to gain worship, choosing instead to abide by the Father's will. (136:9.3) Women, being weaker, have become more tactful by trading upon their sex charms and outgeneraled even the most primitive of men at home. (84:3.5) The way you live your life is more important than the work you do. (39:4.13)
improvedDescription: Methods or strategies employed in dealing with adversaries or challenges. Jesus never resorted to ignoble tactics when facing his enemies, always responding with enlightening, dignified, and conclusive answers rather than unfair or deceptive methods.

name: tadpoles
originalDescription: Live loyally as a tadpole to become a growing and inspiring frog. (100:1.4) Frogs lay eggs in water, where their young first exist as tadpoles before undergoing metamorphosis. (59:5.6)
improvedDescription: The aquatic larval stage of frogs, representing a phase of development and growth. Used metaphorically to illustrate the importance of living loyally in one's current stage of development to achieve future growth and transformation.

name: talents
originalDescription: money | pounds skills | achievement
improvedDescription: Natural endowments, abilities, or resources entrusted to individuals for development and service. Featured prominently in Jesus' parable teaching about faithful stewardship, where servants are given different amounts of talents to invest and are expected to produce gains for their master.

name: Talmai
originalDescription: David sought to build himself up politically by marrying Saul's daughter, Nabal's widow, and Talmai's daughter, the king of Geshur. (97:9.10)
improvedDescription: The king of Geshur whose daughter David married as part of his political strategy to build himself up through advantageous marriages.

name: Tamar
originalDescription: Mary, the earth mother of Jesus, descended from a long line of remarkable women in the racial history of Urantia, including Annon, Tamar, Ruth, Bathsheba, Ansie, Cloa, Eve, Enta, and Ratta. (122:1.2)
improvedDescription: One of the remarkable women in the ancestral lineage of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who descended from a long line of notable women in Urantia's racial history.

name: Taoism
originalDescription: Taoism greatly influenced Buddhism in China, Shinto in Japan, and Christianity in Tibet, leading to its decline and transformation in various parts of the Orient over a millennium. (94:9.3) The earliest form of Taoism arose in China as a result of the teachings of Singlangton, Salem, and Indian philosophers, influencing a vastly different religion from modern Taoism. (94:5.2-6) Buddhism absorbed disintegrating Taoism's lingering ritualistic practices in China during the first millennium after Christ, creating a new, widely accepted synthetic religion. (94:11.1) Ganid’s abstract of Taoism emphasizes the pure, tranquil, and powerful nature of the Supreme Being, guiding humanity towards enlightenment and wisdom through humility and service. (131:8) Lao-tse envisioned God in Tao, alongside other great religious teachers like Gautama, Confucius, and Zoroaster, who arose in the sixth century before Christ. (92:5.12) A Mongolian merchant believer in Taoism spread the teachings of the universal Deity to his family and community. (130:2.2)
improvedDescription: An ancient Chinese religion and philosophy that arose from the teachings of Singlangton, Salem missionaries, and Indian philosophers, greatly different from modern Taoism. Taoism influenced Buddhism in China, Shinto in Japan, and Christianity in Tibet, and was one of the longest-persisting Chinese religions containing monotheistic truth, with Lao-tse envisioning God in Tao.

name: tapirs
originalDescription: Tapirs, along with a variety of other primitive mammals, roamed the forested mountain regions 45,000,000 years ago. (61:1.9) (61:7.15)
improvedDescription: Primitive mammals with proboscises that flourished in forested mountain regions 45,000,000 years ago alongside early horses, rhinoceroses, and other small mammals. Tapirs became extinct in North America during the last glaciation.

name: Tarentum
originalDescription: Jesus restrained bully attacking small lad at Tarentum, showing mercy over justice. (133:1.1) A man attacking his wife at the ship landing was gently approached by Jesus, who reminded him of the importance of mutual respect and equal partnership in marriage. (133:2)
improvedDescription: An Italian port city where Jesus, Gonod, and Ganid stayed during their Mediterranean journey, setting sail from there to Athens. At Tarentum, Jesus intervened to help an assaulted youth and counseled a man mistreating his wife, demonstrating mercy and wisdom in human relations.

name: Tarichea
originalDescription: Thomas the fisherman and Judas Iscariot met Jesus and the apostles at the fisher-boat landing at Tarichea. (138:5) (139:12.2) When Jesus first addressed the apostles, they did not suspect who he was due to their frequent encounters with the fish merchants of Tarichea. (192:1.9) Thomas, a former carpenter and stone mason turned fisherman in Tarichea on the west bank of the Sea of Galilee at Jordan outflow, was the analytical mind among the apostles. (139:8.2) Thomas resided at Tarichea, where, despite his lack of education, he was regarded as the leading citizen and possessed a keen, analytical mind. (139:8.2)
improvedDescription: A fishing village on the west bank of the Jordan River where it flows out of the Sea of Galilee, home to Thomas the apostle and where he and Judas Iscariot joined Jesus and the twelve. The village was known for its fish-drying establishments and merchants who bought fresh catches from local fishermen.

name: tariffs
improvedDescription: Trade duties or taxes imposed on imported goods, mentioned in the context of economic systems and taxation policies.

name: Tarim basin
originalDescription: The Tarim basin in Sinkiang was the eastern outpost of the Andite culture, where they built settlements and engaged in trade with the Chinese and Andonites. (79:1.2,8) The Tibetan centers were not as advanced as those of the Tarim basin, which was more advanced than Tibet. (79:6.5)
improvedDescription: A fertile valley in Sinkiang that served as the easternmost outpost of Andite culture for nearly fifteen thousand years. The Tarim basin was home to mixed Andite-Andonite settlements that engaged in trade with the Chinese to the east and Andonites to the north, rivaling Mesopotamia in its civilization before perishing when rainfall patterns shifted.

name: Tarshish
originalDescription: The young Philistine pointed out the ship landing of Jonah's voyage to Tarshish to Jesus, who taught him the importance of not escaping duty. (130:1.2)
improvedDescription: A distant port city mentioned in Hebrew traditions as the destination of Jonah's attempted voyage to escape his divine calling. Jesus used the story of Jonah's flight to Tarshish to teach a young Philistine about the importance of not running away from duty and service.

name: Tarsus
originalDescription: Mithraism was dominant religion in Tarsus during Paul’s adolescence (98:7.9)
improvedDescription: The birthplace of Paul the apostle, where Mithraism was the dominant religion during his adolescence, influencing the religious environment of his formative years.

name: Tasmania
originalDescription: Andonic migration to Tasmania spanned from Europe to Java. (64:1.6)
improvedDescription: An island reached by early human descendants of Andon and Fonta during their eastward migration 950,000 years ago, representing one of the farthest points of early human expansion from Europe through Java.

name: taste designers
originalDescription: Celestial artisans, taste designers improve morontia taste and sharpen evolving spirit senses to enhance beauty appreciation. (44:6.8)
improvedDescription: A division of celestial artisans who work to improve morontia taste and enhance the appreciation of beauty by sharpening the evolving spirit senses of ascending mortals.

name: tattoo
originalDescription: The tattoo, an ancient badge of tribal membership, symbolized the hardships and endurance required for initiation ceremonies. (70:7.10) Tattooing is an artistic evolution of earlier crude scarring practices. (89:8.3)
improvedDescription: The tattoo originated as an ancient badge of tribal membership, marking the hardships and endurance required for initiation ceremonies. Tattooing is an artistic evolution of earlier crude scarring practices.

name: tax collectors
originalDescription: Early rulers used secret clubs as a means for collecting taxes and debts, a practice that evolved from the tithe and temple offerings. (70:7.18) John taught his disciples to live by the spirit, not just the letter, of the law, instructing tax collectors to “extort no more than assigned” and soldiers to be content with their wages. (135:6.8) Jesus sold his harp and gave away the Greek Scriptures to avoid seizure by tax collectors and pay for his brother Jude's schooling. (126:5.5-6) (127:4.10) Matthew Levi was a publican who followed Jesus and dedicated his life to showing that God is no respecter of persons. (138:3.1,4) (181:2.14) Peter paid the temple tax for all three, fulfilling his promise to the temple-tax collector by catching a fish with a shekel in its mouth. (157:1) Zaccheus was a chief publican who much desired to see Jesus, and when the Master passed by the customs house in Jericho, he climbed up a sycamore tree to get a good view. (171:6.1)
improvedDescription: Officials responsible for collecting taxes and debts, historically using secret clubs for enforcement. Early forms of taxation included tithes and temple offerings. Jesus interacted with several tax collectors, including Matthew Levi who became an apostle, and Zaccheus, a chief publican who encountered Jesus in Jericho.

name: taxation
originalDescription: Taxation enslavement of the citizenry by the state. (70:12.15) The citizenry must control the levying of taxes. (71:8.6) Excessive taxation under national governments obstructs individual freedoms but will diminish under a world government. (134:6.10) The ideal state must support state power without handicapping industry, regulating social conduct to prevent unfairness and maintain peace in industry. (71:5.2) historically in continental nation of nearby inhabited planet inheritance tax in the future
improvedDescription: The system of levying taxes to support government operations, historically beginning with tithes and temple offerings. Excessive taxation can enslave citizenry and obstruct individual freedoms, while proper taxation under citizen control is essential for maintaining state functions without hampering industry.

name: taxpayers
originalDescription: The improvident expect to be fed by thrifty taxpayers, perpetuating a form of modern slavery. (69:9.5) Heavy taxpayers in this continental nation receive extra votes based on the amount of taxes paid, with a maximum of five additional votes permitted. (72:9.5)
improvedDescription: Citizens who pay taxes to support government functions. The improvident often depend on thrifty taxpayers for support, creating a form of modern dependency. In some governmental systems, heavy taxpayers receive additional voting privileges based on their tax contributions.

name: Teacher Sons
improvedDescription: Celestial beings who serve in educational and instructional capacities throughout the universe. These Sons are part of the divine plan for teaching and guiding ascending mortals.

name: teachers
originalDescription: The ascendant plan of mortal progression involves ascending by teaching those behind us in the scale of progression, continually giving out new truth and experience as acquired. (30:3.9) (30:4.23) (100:2.1) Beware of false teachers who will arise and lead many astray, for they will deceive even in times of great tribulation. (176:1.4) Teachers of the kingdom are to respect the personality of man, avoid coercion, and appeal directly to the divine spirit within each individual. (159:3) Jesus maintained his integrity by remaining a learner, unlike the proud professors at the university in Alexandria. (130:3.7) Teachers must be free beings, real leaders, in order to ensure that education prioritizes philosophy and the search for wisdom. (71:7.4) Perfectors of Wisdom are the ultimate source of wisdom for all teachers, guiding the application of universe knowledge and providing fountains of discretion and wellsprings of discrimination in all institutions of learning. (19:2.3)
improvedDescription: Individuals who instruct others in truth and wisdom, playing a crucial role in spiritual and educational development. True teachers maintain intellectual integrity by remaining learners themselves, and effective teaching involves giving out newly acquired truth and experience to those behind them in progression.

name: teachers of rest
originalDescription: Teachers of rest counsel seraphim on energy intake for efficient energy utilization and balance maintenance among active cherubim and passive sanobim. (44:5.10) Celestial artisans and energy manipulators are the teachers of rest, guiding us to replenish our spiritual energies through divine rest. (44:5.8)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans and energy manipulators who guide seraphim and other spiritual beings in the technique of divine rest. They counsel on efficient energy utilization and help maintain proper balance between active and passive spiritual forces.

name: teaching counselors
originalDescription: Assistant teachers are helpers and associates of teaching counselors, connected with educational enterprises and the sevenfold training scheme in the local universe, with a marvelous corps on Urantia fostering truth and righteousness. (39:2.7) Mota enhances philosophy like two eyes bring depth to sight, thanks to the guidance of many morontia counselor transition seraphim teaching counselors. (48:6.28) Michael's 4th bestowal was as a teaching counselor of the supreme seraphim in Nebadon. (39:1.15) The Teaching Counselors, secretaries to all teachers, from Melchizedeks to mortals, are invaluable assistants to the spiritual teaching corps of the local universe. (39:1.13-14)
improvedDescription: A specialized order of seraphim who serve as invaluable assistants to the spiritual teaching corps of the local universe. They function as secretaries and helpers to all orders of teachers, from Melchizedeks to mortals, and are particularly active in educational enterprises on mansion worlds and training spheres.

name: teamwork
improvedDescription: The collaborative effort of individuals working together toward common goals. Teamwork represents a fundamental principle of effective organization and achievement in both material and spiritual endeavors.

name: tears
originalDescription: The brothel keeper anointed Jesus’ feet with her tears of gratitude. (147:5.3) God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes, for the former things have passed away. (27:1.5)
improvedDescription: Expressions of deep emotion, often representing gratitude, sorrow, or joy. In the eternal realms, God will wipe away all tears, as the former things of pain and suffering will pass away.

name: Technical Advisers
originalDescription: Technical Advisers are devoted to interpreting laws for all beings and assisting in understanding how the Supremacy of Deity will react in various situations, including elucidating the technique of the Ultimate. (25:4.19) The Melchizedeks counsel Life Carriers and Universal Censors in the techniques and usages of spirit-world transactions. (25:4.18) The Technical Advisers play a crucial role in guiding various celestial beings and entities on the proper usages and techniques of spiritual transactions, but they do not interact directly with mortals. (25:4.18) Technical Advisers have never gone astray in their divine interpretation and expounding of law and experience, extending their service to the portals of Paradise without limit. (25:4.20) Technical Advisers are in Uversa personality register. (30:2.77) Living law libraries teach the applied laws of the universe to those journeying toward their Paradise goal. (25:4.12,15-16) (37:8.7) Technical Advisers maintain local rendezvous on mansion worlds and Jerusem circles. (46:5.21) (47:0.4) The number of Technical Advisers is slightly in excess of sixty-one trillion on Uversa in Orvonton, with about half a billion in Nebadon. (25:4.10) (37:8.7) Once enrolled, Technical Advisers may not transfer out, only the Ancients of Days have the authority to do so. (25:4.13) The original group of Technical Advisers was chosen from one million of the most orderly minds in the spirit world by the Infinite Spirit. (25:4.1) The Technical Advisers counsel achievement and prevent endless trouble and delays by instructing beings on the laws of the universe and the divine method of perfection. (25:4.15,17) Technical Advisers are recruited from supernaphim, seconaphim, tertiaphim, omniaphim, seraphim, mortal ascenders, and midwayers from various personality orders. (25:4.2) (27:0.1) residence in Jerusem circles
improvedDescription: Technical Advisers are celestial beings devoted to interpreting universal laws and guiding all creatures in proper spiritual techniques and procedures. Originally chosen by the Infinite Spirit from one million of the most orderly minds in the spirit world, they serve as living law libraries throughout the universes, preventing delays and facilitating progress by teaching the divine methods of perfection. They are recruited from various angelic orders and ascending mortals, maintain headquarters throughout the universe systems, and have never been known to go astray in their divine interpretations.

name: technicians
originalDescription: The 5th order transition minister seraphim educate and guide mortals on the seven mansion worlds in Satania. (39:6.1) Technicians serve as liaisons between survivors and Morontia Power Supervisors and Master Physical Controllers, helping ascenders adjust to the morontia spheres. (48:6.29) Technicians, space traversers, help morontia progressors adjust to new spheres of existence. (48:6.29)
improvedDescription: Technicians are the fifth order of transition minister seraphim who help ascending mortals adjust to life on the morontia spheres. They serve as liaisons with Morontia Power Supervisors and Master Physical Controllers, act as instructors about morontia energies, and function as emergency space traversers to assist pilgrims in their transition from material to spiritual existence.

name: teeth
originalDescription: Early mammals of the Cenozoic developed two sets of teeth, had multiple mammary glands, were covered in hair, and had large brains relative to body size, but no modern forms were present among them. (61:1.10) Human teeth are slowly evolving toward 28 as part of an ongoing adaptative process of evolution. (65:6.5) Magical charms concocted from human flesh, tiger claws, crocodile teeth, poison plant seeds, snake venom, human hair, bones of the dead, footprints dust, and bodily secretions were believed to hold great magical power, especially in love spells by the ancients. (88:5.2)
improvedDescription: Early mammals of the Cenozoic developed two sets of teeth along with other distinctive features like mammary glands and hair-covered bodies. Human teeth are slowly evolving from thirty-six in remote ancestors toward twenty-eight as part of an ongoing adaptive evolutionary process. Ancient peoples used teeth from various animals like crocodiles and tigers as magical charms, believing they held special powers.

name: Teherma the Persian
originalDescription: Teherma the Persian, not a fire worshipper, found enlightenment in Jesus' teachings at Capernaum. (141:6.1-3)
improvedDescription: Teherma the Persian was a businessman from Damascus who sought out Jesus after hearing of his teachings. Despite being mistakenly labeled a fire worshipper by Simon Zelotes, Teherma explained that fire was merely a symbol of the Pure and Holy One, and after speaking with Jesus, he became convinced of the kingdom teachings and joined the followers.

name: telepathy
originalDescription: Adam and Eve communicated through thought oscillations transmitted up to 50 miles, but lost the ability upon succumbing to evil. (74:6.6)
improvedDescription: Adam and Eve possessed the ability to communicate telepathically through thought oscillations transmitted via delicate gas chambers near their brain structures, with a range of about fifty miles. This power was instantly lost when their minds surrendered to the discord and disruption of evil.

name: telescopes
originalDescription: Spectral lines show red shift indicating the apparent recession of far-distant systems at incredible speeds. (12:4.14) New telescopes will reveal 375 million galaxies in outer space, unveiling the vast and expanding universe beyond our current knowledge. (12:2.2-3)
improvedDescription: Future telescopes will reveal approximately 375 million new galaxies in outer space, greatly expanding human understanding of the universe. Current spectroscopic observations show red-shifted spectral lines from distant systems, creating the appearance of incredible recession speeds, though this apparent motion results from various observational errors and time-space distortions rather than actual velocities.

name: temperament
originalDescription: Inherent temperament plays a significant role in shaping one's religious philosophy and experience. (100:1.6) (101:7.1) Despite variations in technique, man's inherent disposition towards wealth accumulation and generosity remains unchanged throughout history. (69:5.13)
improvedDescription: Inherent temperament significantly influences the formation of one's personal religious philosophy and spiritual experience. Along with social status, education, and other environmental factors, temperament helps determine the pattern of religious thought and practice, while also affecting one's disposition toward material accumulation and generosity throughout life.

name: temperance
originalDescription: The fruits of the Spirit - love, joy, peace, long-suffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, and temperance - illuminate the path of spirit-led mortals towards eternal life and the kingdom of God. (34:6.13) Jesus preached temperance and taught consistency through proportionate adjustment of life problems, warning against extremes in virtue that may lead to vice. (149:4.3)
improvedDescription: Temperance is one of the fruits of the Spirit that characterizes spirit-led mortals, along with love, joy, peace, and other divine qualities. Jesus preached temperance and taught consistency through proportionate adjustment of life problems, warning that even virtues carried to extremes may become vices and emphasizing the importance of well-balanced characters.

name: temperature
originalDescription: At the center of the earth, temperatures exceed those of the surface of the sun due to immense pressure and heat. (58:5.1) Gravity prevents the existence of absolute zero in the interstellar space, filled with energy currents and ultimatonic activities. (42:4.6) In the earth's atmosphere, temperature varies from 70 degrees below zero F in the stratosphere to 1200° F at higher altitudes. (58:2.6) The internal temperatures of suns, including your own, are much higher than commonly believed due to the intense X-ray bombardment, reaching an unbelievable 35,000,000 degrees in the central regions. (41:7) (41:9.1) Temperature, second only to gravity in energy and matter evolution, is the openly recognized transmutation of space potency into primordial force. (42:2.7)
improvedDescription: Temperature plays a crucial role in cosmic processes, from the earth's core which exceeds the sun's surface temperature due to immense pressure, to stellar interiors reaching 35 million degrees. In earth's atmosphere, temperature drops to 70 degrees below zero in the stratosphere before rising to 1200 degrees at auroral levels, while gravity prevents absolute zero from existing in interstellar space.

name: Temple of New Life
originalDescription: The Temple of New Life extends seven radial wings, housing resurrection halls for the assembly of the seven races of time, each with one hundred thousand personal chambers leading to awakening chambers for up to one million individuals, surrounded by personality assembly chambers for blended races, where real and conscious reassembly of personality occurs on mansonia number one. (47:3.5)
improvedDescription: The Temple of New Life on mansonia number one contains seven radial wings serving as resurrection halls for the seven races of time. Each wing houses one hundred thousand personal resurrection chambers leading to circular assembly halls that can accommodate one million individuals, surrounded by personality assembly chambers where the real and conscious reassembly of complete personality takes place during resurrection.

name: temple
originalDescription: All worlds of mortal ascent have exquisite temples of worship built by experienced architects of the spirit. (44:3.5) Upon the rock of spiritual reality, Jesus declared he would build the living temple of spiritual fellowship in the eternal realities of his Father's kingdom. (157:4.5,7) Take men into the temple before showing them its beauties, for first they must be reborn of the spirit to truly understand the profound teachings within. (141:6.4) The value of beauty leading up to worship was emphasized by Jesus in teaching his apostles about the importance of simplicity and natural surroundings in connecting with God. (167:6.5-6) In light and life, temples and open-air arenas of worship are creations of beauty and grandeur, devoted to the training of mind and expansion of soul. (55:5.5) temple of God temples in ancient times temples on Urantia in a nearby planet celestial temples
improvedDescription: Sacred structures for worship and spiritual fellowship found throughout the universes. Temples range from the exquisite morontia temples on mansion worlds to the stone temple of the Universal Father in the Garden of Eden. Jesus spoke of building a "living temple of spiritual fellowship" based on spiritual reality, and taught that human bodies themselves are "temples of God" where the divine spirit dwells.

name: temptation
originalDescription: One cannot overcome temptation through mere human will; true victory lies in developing a love for higher ideals and conduct through spiritual transformation.
