(156:5.5) Transform temptation by redirecting energies into higher channels and enter the spirit kingdom to enjoy comparative deliverance from the slave-bondage of the flesh, following the new way of mortal living revealed by Jesus. (34:7.6) (156:5.4) Adam's one hundred years on earth concluded with Serapatatia, influenced by temptation and flattery, convincing Eve to embark on a secret, ill-fated project. (75:3) Jesus’ great temptations
improvedDescription: A spiritual challenge that cannot be overcome through mere human will alone. True victory over temptation comes through developing genuine love for higher ideals and spiritual transformation, redirecting energies into more noble channels. Jesus faced his great temptation on Mount Hermon, where he encountered Satan and Caligastia in a universe trial that established his sovereignty.

name: ten
improvedDescription: The number representing completeness or a full measure in various spiritual and administrative contexts throughout The Urantia Book.

name: ten-cent piece
originalDescription: The temple courts were profaned by the extensive system of banking and commercial exchange, where money-changers charged high commissions for the exchange of a coin about the size of a ten-cent piece. (173:1.3)
improvedDescription: A reference coin used to describe the size of the half-shekel temple tax that Jewish worshippers were required to pay. Money-changers in the temple courts charged excessive commissions for exchanging various currencies for this orthodox Jewish coin, which was about the size of a ten-cent piece but twice as thick.

name: Ten Commandments
originalDescription: The two versions of the Ten Commandments reflect the evolving understanding of divine law and the changing concepts of divinity. (142:3.10-22) Jesus wrote the Ten Commandments in Greek on two boards, which were later destroyed by him. (126:4.9) (137:2.9) The taboos of Yahweh, promulgated by Moses, replaced thousands of pre-existent prohibitions and promised rewards for obedience. (89:1.4) (96:4.4) God's Ten Commandments, recorded by Ganid, bring great peace to those who love his law. (131:2.12)
improvedDescription: The divine laws given by Moses that evolved from earlier, more primitive commandments to reflect advancing concepts of God. Jesus noted how these ten negative commandments were later transformed in Isaiah's time into the positive law of love - to love God supremely and one's neighbor as oneself. Jesus once wrote the Ten Commandments in Greek on boards, which he later destroyed.

name: ten virgins
originalDescription: The parable of the ten virgins, from the Master's discourse on the destruction of Jerusalem and his second coming, recorded by Selta, caused great confusion among the apostles, except for John. (176:2.8)
improvedDescription: A parable from Jesus' discourse about the destruction of Jerusalem and his second coming that caused great confusion among the apostles. This parable was later incorporated into gospel records through the writings of Selta, a Jewish apocalyptist attached to Emperor Caligula's court.

name: tenderness
originalDescription: The evidence that man is indwelt by God is shown through manifestations of divine mercy and tenderness towards the weak and needy, illustrating the unique blending of human and Creator. (26:11.5) It is a great error to teach boys that it is unmanly to show tenderness; true courage and happiness come from being sensitive and responsive to human needs. (140:5.16) Indiscriminate kindness may breed social evils, Jesus preached wisdom, tolerance, and forgiveness. (140:8.13) The mature human looks upon all others with tenderness and tolerance, embracing the journey towards emotional and intellectual maturity. (160:1.6)
improvedDescription: A divine quality manifested through mercy and compassion toward the weak and needy, indicating that humans are indwelt by God's spirit. True courage includes the ability to show tenderness and emotional sensitivity to human needs, and mature individuals look upon others with feelings of tenderness and tolerance.

name: tension
improvedDescription: A state of strain or stress that arises in various spiritual, emotional, or social contexts as beings navigate challenges and growth.

name: Tenskwatawa
originalDescription: Tenskwatawa prophesied the 1808 eclipse, denouncing shamanism and white man's vices to challenge shamans against scientific progress. (90:2.9)
improvedDescription: A Shawnee prophet who predicted the 1806 solar eclipse and denounced both shamanism and the vices of white civilization. He exemplified the true teachers who periodically arise to challenge religious shamans and priests who oppose education and scientific progress.

name: tentmaker
originalDescription: Jesus worked as a tentmaker in Antioch, unknowingly preparing the way for the coming of Peter, Paul, and other Christian teachers in Rome. (132:0.10) (134:7.3) Paul was a devotee of the cult of renunciation and humiliation, influencing Christianity with teachings that looked down upon women and could lead to the end of the human race if universally obeyed. (89:3.6)
improvedDescription: An occupation Jesus practiced for three weeks while living in Antioch for over two months, during which he studied human nature and prepared the way for later Christian teachers. Paul, who was also a tentmaker, unknowingly followed in Jesus' footsteps and developed teachings about celibacy that were influenced by ancient continence cults.

name: Terah
originalDescription: The children of Terah, father of Abraham, fulfilled the expectations of the Melchizedek receivers with their intelligence, initiative, sagacity, and sincerity. (93:5.3-5)
improvedDescription: Father of Abraham who, along with his family, were halfhearted converts to the Salem religion and met the Melchizedek receivers' expectations for intelligence, initiative, sagacity, and sincerity.

name: terminal rest of time
originalDescription: Before achieving residential status on Paradise, ascendant souls undergo a terminal rest of time following their divine rest and final clearance of the Havona circuits. (26:7.4)
improvedDescription: A final rest period that ascendant souls undergo after attaining the Universal Father and receiving final clearance of the Havona circuits, preceding their achievement of residential status on Paradise.

name: terrestrial escape
originalDescription: The ministry of Thought Adjusters, guardian angels, and messenger hosts of the Infinite Spirit is essential to the liberation of all mortals from the bonds of flesh, enabling terrestrial escape to the morontia worlds of spiritual evolution and mind progress. (49:6) (55:2)
improvedDescription: The liberation of mortals from physical existence through natural death or translation, enabling their transition to the morontia worlds of spiritual evolution and mind progress.

name: tertiaphim
originalDescription: When a Michael Son is ready to embark on the adventure of universe organization, 1000 tertiaphim accompany him and retire with the appearance of native angels. (28:1.2-3) Michael, accompanied by a lone tertiaphim, departed his headquarters sphere and a new Lanonandek Son was appointed as acting System Sovereign. (119:2.4) The tertiaphim, as children of the Infinite Spirit, serve in liaison between Creator Sons and the Ancients of Days. (26:1.13) (28:1.1) Tertiaphim are dedicated to facilitating liaisons between Creator Sons and Ancients of Days. (26:1.13) The Tertiaphim may become Technical Advisers. (25:4.5) The tertiaphim retire from active service in the local universe when native angels appear. (28:1.3) Tertiaphim serve as Most High Assistants in the ministry of Paradise-origin beings. (37:4.2) Tertiaphim's appear registered in personality registers. (30:1.83) (30:2.85)
improvedDescription: High angels who are children of the Infinite Spirit, personalized on Paradise in groups of one thousand and given as companions to Creator Sons during universe organization, later serving as liaison ministers between Creator Sons and the Ancients of Days.

name: Tertiary Circuit Supervisors
originalDescription: Circuit supervisors direct spirit messages and personalities with efficiency, utilizing all forces in the universe, but they cannot annul material currents. (24:1.12) Circuit supervisors, employing high spirit personalities of circuit control, direct all spirit messages and personalities across the universe. (24:1.12) Tertiary Circuit Supervisors function on the headquarters world of every local universe, created in groups of seven hundred and are assigned by the Ancients of Days, serving eternally in their original assignment. (24:1.6,10-11) Satania was quarantined by the action of the Tertiary Circuit Supervisors. (53:7.3)
improvedDescription: Circuit supervisors created in groups of seven hundred who function on local universe headquarters, directing spirit messages and personalities across universe circuits and capable of isolating rebellious worlds from higher spiritual circuits.

name: testimonies
originalDescription: A Divine Counselor said: “I attest, as a Divine Counselor, to the eternal truth of the Father's love and spirit, revealed through the glorious personality of the Eternal Son.” (1:3.8) (1:7.9) (6:8.8) The unbiased testimonies secured by reflective seconaphim provide crucial insight for the angelic hosts at council sessions. (28:4.11)
improvedDescription: Authoritative declarations of truth, particularly those given by Divine Counselors based on their direct experience and knowledge of divine realities, serving as reliable witness to spiritual facts.

name: tests
originalDescription: Jesus passed the great test of refusing to use power selfishly, choosing to establish the kingdom of heaven in the hearts of mankind through natural, ordinary, difficult, and trying methods. (136:8.6) Jesus increasingly measured society and religion by the test of what it does for the human soul, bringing God to man and man to God. (126:2.5)
improvedDescription: Spiritual challenges and trials that measure character and faith, exemplified by Jesus' test of refusing to use divine power for selfish purposes and his standard of evaluating institutions by what they do for the human soul.

name: Thaddeus
originalDescription: James Alpheus, also called Thaddeus, embraced his role as one of the “least of all the apostles” with cheerful humility. (139:10.5)
improvedDescription: An alternative name for James Alpheus, one of Jesus' twelve apostles, who along with his brother Judas was known for his humble acceptance of being among "the least of all the apostles."

name: Thailand
improvedDescription: A Southeast Asian nation, historically known as Siam.

name: Thamna
originalDescription: Jesus passed through Thamna on his journey preaching the good news of the kingdom. (143:0.1)
improvedDescription: A village where citizens invited Jesus to preach during his ministry, located in the region between Jerusalem and Samaria.

name: thanksgiving
improvedDescription: The expression of gratitude and appreciation, particularly in religious contexts, representing a fundamental aspect of worship and spiritual relationship with the divine.

name: Thapsacus
originalDescription: Jesus passed through Thapsacus on his journey through Mesopotamia. (130:0.3)
improvedDescription: An ancient city in Mesopotamia through which Jesus and his traveling companions passed during their extensive journey through the Mediterranean and Near Eastern regions.

name: Thebes
originalDescription: Tutankhamen returned capital to Thebes, where the priests gained power and eventually seized the crown. (95:5.10)
improvedDescription: The ancient Egyptian capital city to which Tutankhamen returned the seat of government, where the priests subsequently gained significant power and eventually seized control of the throne.

name: theft
originalDescription: Among primitives, theft only occurred outside the group due to the belief that personal property held the essence of the owner's identity. (69:9.9) The commandments against theft are an integral part of loving and obeying God. (66:7.13) (93:4.11) (94:8.11) (131:2.12) (163:2.4) Stealing evolved as the great crime amid the advancement of civilization and the recognition of private property rights, intertwining with the historical view of women as property and the enforcement of marital fidelity through the evolving mores. (82:4.3-4)
improvedDescription: The act of stealing, which among primitive peoples was considered acceptable only against outsiders due to beliefs about personal property containing the owner's essence. As civilization advanced and private property rights developed, theft became recognized as a great crime, with commandments against stealing forming an integral part of religious and moral codes.

name: theology
originalDescription: God-consciousness is a universal reality that remains consistent, but the philosophical concept and theological definitions of God must evolve with each era as human knowledge advances. (5:5.12) Theology does not produce religion; religion develops values first, followed by interpretative beliefs. (103:1.4) The mission of theology is to define God and justify claims of religion, facilitating personal spiritual experience and living faith. (90:5.7) (103:9.6) The mission of theology is to facilitate self-consciousness of spiritual experience and validate experiential claims through living faith. (103:9.6) In theology, facts must not displace truth in the central teachings of the kingdom of God as derived from the life and teachings of Jesus of Nazareth. (143:6.3) The fallacy that any creed is uniquely The Truth impedes the progress and evolution of religion, as real growth requires the humility to learn from other faiths. (92:3.4) (92:7.3) Jesus's personal, living, and purely spiritual faith, untouched by theology's fixes and dogmas, was unshakable in the face of all trials. (196:0.5) Nalda dodged the issue of salvation with a discussion of theology and philosophy, despite her true thirst for living water. (143:5.5-7) The philosophy of religion, theology, interprets the values and personal experiences that validate religion, even if beliefs may contain elements of truth and error. (103:1.5) The paradox of human comprehension in theology, the psychology of religion, stems from the struggle to reconcile the concept of divine immanence with God's transcendence in religious experience. (5:5.6) Despite erroneous theology, religion can still be genuine and everlasting. (103:9.1-2) Theology is the study of one's religion, approached from inside, never becoming a science but intertwined with psychology and philosophy. (103:6.1-2) theologians
improvedDescription: The systematic study and interpretation of religious experience and divine truth, which serves to facilitate self-consciousness of spiritual experience and validate religious claims through living faith. While theology can help define and clarify religious concepts, it becomes lifeless when it masters religion rather than serving it, and must remain grounded in genuine spiritual experience rather than mere intellectual formulation.

name: theories
originalDescription: The highest religious doctrines are dead and powerless unless transformed through divine spirit and power, as declared by your teacher of old. (34:6.6) Experience always has advantage over theory. (102:6.7) Facts always support spiritual faith, while theories may challenge it. (195:7.1) You cannot confide in a postulate, just as you cannot pray to a chemical formula or worship a hypothesis. (102:7.3) Imprisoning divine truth in human formulations leads to post-mortem salvage of dead theories, while living truth thrives in spirit-discerned realities. (180:5.2)
improvedDescription: Intellectual formulations and hypothetical explanations that, while useful for understanding, remain powerless to transform human character unless animated by divine spirit. Experience always holds advantage over theory, and living spiritual truth cannot be imprisoned in theoretical formulations without losing its dynamic power.

name: thieves
originalDescription: A good Samaritan helped a man who was robbed and beaten. (164:1.3) Thieves killed the rich man with full barns, ending his earthly wealth and leaving him without treasures in heaven. (165:4.3)
improvedDescription: Criminals who engage in robbery and theft, exemplified in parables such as the Good Samaritan story where brigands attacked a traveler, and in the account of thieves who killed a rich man, demonstrating the temporal nature of material wealth versus spiritual treasures.

name: thieves crucified with Jesus
originalDescription: The two thieves, associates of Barabbas, were scourged before crucifixion and crucified with Jesus in place of their leader. (187:0.1-2) The soldiers took the bodies of the thieves to Gehenna after placing Jesus' body in the tomb. (188:1.5) Thieves crucified with Jesus cast reproach upon him, but one thief sought forgiveness and received salvation in Paradise. (187:3.3) (187:4.1) The two brigands, dispatched by Roman soldiers, caused a delay in taking Jesus down from the cross before his burial and resurrection. (188:0.1) “Lord, remember me when you come into your kingdom,” the repentant thief said, earning Jesus' approval and the promise of future salvation in Paradise. (187:4.1) One of the thieves crucified with Jesus had often heard him speak. (186:4.4-5) The penitent brigand, inspired by Jesus on the cross, renounced his prior belief in robbery as a patriotic protest. (187:4.5)
improvedDescription: Two brigands and associates of Barabbas who were crucified alongside Jesus, having been properly scourged before execution. While both initially mocked Jesus, one later repented and sought forgiveness, receiving Jesus's promise of salvation in Paradise, demonstrating the possibility of redemption even in the final moments of life.

name: thing, meaning, and value
improvedDescription: A conceptual framework representing the three fundamental aspects of reality that correspond to the domains of science, philosophy, and religion respectively, forming the basis for comprehensive understanding of existence.

name: third day
originalDescription: When the bestowal Sons, whether Creator or Magisterial, pass through death, they reappear on the third day, not always facing the tragic fate of Jesus of Nazareth on Urantia. (20:6.6) A newborn Graduate Guide appears on the third day after a servital's career is closed. (24:7.6) The prodigious number of Havona servitals, created constantly, appear in groups of one thousand on the third moment, with every fourth being more physical in nature. (25:1.3) Jesus would rise on the third day as he had forewarned his disciples. (171:4.2,8) (186:3.4) (188:1.8) (188:2.1,2) (189:5.1) (190:1.3,5) (190:5.3-4) (191:0.2) The apostles could not grasp the warnings of the impending setback, interpreting Jesus' mention of “rising on the third day” as a sign of imminent triumph in the kingdom. (171:4.8) The Jews believed that the soul would not linger past the third day, as decay was well advanced by the fourth day. (167:4.3)
improvedDescription: A significant time period in divine plans, most notably when bestowal Sons reappear after passing through death, and specifically when Jesus rose from the dead as he had repeatedly foretold his disciples. Among the Jews, the third day held special meaning as they believed the soul would not linger beyond this time, with decay being well advanced by the fourth day.

name: Third Person of Deity
improvedDescription: The Third Person of Deity is another designation for the Infinite Spirit, the third member of the Paradise Trinity who serves as the God of Action and the source of mind in creation.

name: Third Source and Center
improvedDescription: The Third Source and Center is another name for the Infinite Spirit, the third person of the Paradise Trinity who functions as the universal source of mind ministry and spiritual administration throughout creation.

name: Third Source personalities
originalDescription: The Infinite Spirit bestows a vast array of Third Source personalities, beings personal to Him but not in the Father's personality circuit, upon various groups in the grand universe. (9:8.6-12) Third Source personalities are contactable by beings in Father's personality circuit. (9:8.9) Spirit-fused mortals are not Third Source personalities but are included in the Father’s personality circuit, having fused with individualizations of the premind spirit of the Third Source and Center. (40:9.1) Supreme Power Centers are beings of high will freedom and action, endowed with Third-Source personality and exquisite intelligence. (29:2.9)
improvedDescription: Third Source personalities are beings personal to the Infinite Spirit but not included in the Father's personality circuit. These diverse personalities include power directors, Creative Spirits, and other spiritual beings who are contactable by those in the Father's personality circuit and possess minds with memory, reason, judgment, and creative imagination.

name: thirst
originalDescription: Jesus expressed his thirst while on the cross, prompting the captain of the guard to offer him wine on a javelin. (187:3.5) In the Divine Presence, there is no thirst, only the eternal satisfaction of spiritual enlightenment and moral strength. (34:6.8) (48:6.11) Believe in Jesus, never thirst spiritually, and receive eternal life. (153:2.7-9)
improvedDescription: Jesus expressed physical thirst while on the cross, prompting the Roman captain to offer him wine. In spiritual terms, those who believe in Jesus will never thirst spiritually, as the divine Spirit provides eternal satisfaction and serves as a well of living water springing up to everlasting life.

name: Thomas Didymus
originalDescription: personal life attitude after Jesus
improvedDescription: Thomas Didymus was the eighth apostle chosen by Philip, a former fisherman, carpenter, and stone mason from Tarichea. Known for his analytical mind and initial skepticism, he served as manager of the apostolic itinerary and became famous for doubting Jesus' resurrection until seeing him personally, after which he became a courageous and loyal supporter who eventually died for his faith in Malta.

name: Thor (Andite)
originalDescription: Thor, victorious commander in the final battle of Somme, led the white invaders to defeat the Cro-Magnoids in a five-hundred year struggle. (80:5.4)
improvedDescription: Thor was the victorious Andite commander who led the white invaders to final victory over the Cro-Magnoid blue race in the decisive battle of the Somme, ending a five-hundred-year struggle and becoming a hero to the northern white tribes.

name: Thor (god)
originalDescription: The master of lightning, Thor, emerged victorious in the decisive struggles between the white man and the blue man in the valley of the Somme. (80:5.4) (85:6.4)
improvedDescription: Thor was originally a ghost hero who became revered as a god by northern white tribes after his military victories, later evolving into a nature deity known as the master of lightning, illustrating how human heroes can be deified in religious systems.

name: thorns
originalDescription: A crown of thorns placed on Jesus' head symbolized his suffering, ignored by those consumed by hatred and prejudice. (185:6.2,4) Some seed fell among thorns, choked and unfruitful due to worldly cares and riches. (151:1.2) (151:2.2)
improvedDescription: A crown of thorns was placed on Jesus' head during his scourging as part of the mockery before his crucifixion, symbolizing his suffering. In Jesus' parable of the sower, thorns represent worldly cares and the deceitfulness of riches that choke spiritual growth and prevent fruitfulness.

name: Those High in Authority
originalDescription: The cream of governing ability, Those High in Authority, are Adjuster-fused mortals with superior administrative ability. (22:3.1) Those High in Authority, glorified ascendant mortals, are the everywhere-present and always-efficient executives of the Ancients of Days in the superuniverse government. (15:10.3-12) (22:3.3) Those High in Authority, as Trinitized Sons of Attainment, were embraced by Trinity after ascending through the regime, passing through Havona, and being mustered into the Corps of the Finality. (15:10.12) High Son Assistants serve as clerks for Those High in Authority, acting as personal aids to the high sons of the governments of the Ancients of Days. (22:10.1) Those High in Authority in Uversa personality register. (30:2.56) The Perfectors of Wisdom, Those High in Authority, and Divine Counselors represent the union of divine insight and experiential knowledge, achieving the maximum of administrative wisdom in the superuniverse. (19:2.4) (19:3.6-7) There are seventy billion skillful administrators of Those High in Authority commissioned in Orvonton, with seventy thousand being trinitized at each Trinity liaison. (22:3.2) Paper 25 was sponsored by One High in Authority from Uversa. (25:8.12) Those High in Authority portray the attitude of the higher government in superuniverse courts, fostering the execution of justice and rectification of misadaptations in evolutionary universes. (15:12.1) (22:3.4) The selection of candidates for Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number, is inherently and automatically determined by personal experience and spiritual values, not by arbitrary means. (22:4.4) The routine work of major sector affairs is carried out by Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants, with Those High in Authority overseeing service on major sectors. (18:4.4) Those High in Authority advance individuals with augmented trusts according to their character development, while ensuring that they are not overloaded to prevent disaster and disappointment. (28:6.15) Tertiary seconaphim are assigned to Those High in Authority among the ascendant trinitized associates of the superuniverse rulers. (28:3.1) (28:6.1) Those High in Authority, as Trinitized Sons of Attainment, are Adjuster-fused mortal beings of extraordinary executive genius who represent the attitude of the higher government in the superuniverse. (15:10.12) (15:12.1) (22:3.1) (28:6.1)
improvedDescription: Those High in Authority are Adjuster-fused mortals who demonstrated superior administrative ability during their ascendant careers and were embraced by the Paradise Trinity to become executives in superuniverse government. They serve as everywhere-present administrators for the Ancients of Days, representing the cream of governing ability from surviving mortals of space.

name: Those without Name and Number
originalDescription: The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Those without Name and Number, were embraced by the Trinity and assigned to the supernal service of the Ancients of Days, enlarging the executive branch of the superuniverse government. (15:10.12) Those without Name and Number have a transcendent concept of the Father's eternal purpose. (22:4.1) High Son Assistants serve as clerks for the superior group of the retrinitized trinitized sons of glorified ascendant beings in the Mortal Corps of the Finality and their eternal associates. (22:10.1) Those without Name and Number, members of highest mobile advisory body, assist in achieving the maximum of administrative wisdom by combining divine insight and personal experience from evolutionary progression. (19:2.4) (19:3.6-7) There are over 700 million Sons without Name and Number embraced by the Paradise Trinity in groups of seven thousand. (22:4.2) The high commission of Orvonton administrators sponsored, formulated, and put into English Paper 31 depicting the nature of Deity, the reality of Paradise, the organization and working of the central and superuniverses, the personalities of the grand universe, and the high destiny of evolutionary mortals. (31:10.22) Selection for the trinitization classes is inherently automatic, with personal experience and spiritual values determining the diverse characters of Those without Name and Number. (22:4.4) Those without Name and Number serve superuniverse government as Trinitized Sons of Attainment. (15:10.3-12) The routine work of major sector affairs is primarily handled by Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants, while Those without Name and Number play a significant role in the governance of the superuniverse. (18:4.4) Those without Name and Number, as the superior spiritual minds of survival races, are the supreme jurors of Orvonton, entrusted to render opinions and sit in judgment with a deep understanding of the ascendant career, ensuring justice is served in the highest realms of adjudication. (22:4.3) Those without Name and Number are the supreme jurors in superuniverse courts. (15:12.1) (22:4.3) The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Mighty Messengers and Those High in Authority, embrace Those without Name and Number, who worship beyond comprehension and are denominated “Those above Name and Number.” (15:10.12) (22:4.1) (28:6.1) Those without Name and Number in Uversa are registered in personality records. (30:2.57)
improvedDescription: Those without Name and Number are ascendant souls who developed exceptional worship ability and spiritual comprehension of the Universal Father's eternal purpose that transcends ordinary evolutionary understanding. As Trinitized Sons of Attainment, they serve as supreme jurors in superuniverse courts and constitute the highest type of spiritual mentality for rendering verdicts in cosmic adjudication.

name: Thoth
originalDescription: Thoth's spittle healed Set's eye, illustrating the widespread belief in spittle as a healing agent originating in Egypt. (95:2.6)
improvedDescription: Egyptian god who healed Set's eye with his spittle in the legendary battle of Horus with Set, illustrating the ancient belief in spittle as a healing agent that spread from Egypt to Arabia and Mesopotamia.

name: thought
originalDescription: Abrupt presentations of subconscious emanations in the mind are sometimes influenced by the Adjuster, while more often arising from natural psychic functions inherent in the evolving animal mind. (110:4.3) As a man thinks in his heart, so is he, trusting in Yahweh with all his heart, acknowledging him in all ways, and receiving divine guidance. (131:2.8) (131:5.2) Celestial beings excel in ideograph techniques to infinitely enhance thought preservation beyond concept picturization. (44:4.6) Attaining cosmologic levels of thought involves a hunger for harmony and beauty, a love of the beautiful, and a sensitivity to ethics that leads to the pursuit of divine reality values. (56:10.5-8) The Conjoint Actor unerringly draws all divine thoughts into the absolute circuit of universal mind. (9:6.2) Dreams illustrate the struggle between unexpressed desires and divine messages during unconscious sleep. (110:5.4) Few mortals are real thinkers, spiritually developing and disciplining their minds to favorably connect with their divine Adjusters. (110:7.6) The word God symbolizes the approach to supreme reality and recognition of divine value, with human likes and dislikes not determining good and evil. (100:3.2) The spread of civilization hinges on the effectiveness of language as the greatest thinking tool, requiring further development to express evolving thought. (81:6.16-17) Let the unrighteous man forsake his thoughts and return to the Lord, for he will abundantly pardon. (97:7.7) Feeling, not thought, was the guiding force in evolutionary development, blurring the lines between fear, avoidance, respect, and worship in the primitive mind. (85:7.2) Recognize and disregard the interference of preconceived opinions, settled ideas, and long-standing prejudices, in order to allow for the prompt recognition of every thought for what it truly is. (109:5.3) Religion must navigate the paradox of utilizing thought while recognizing the limitations of intellectual overdevelopment in attaining higher spiritual attainment. (102:3.1) Melody of thought—the thinking of spiritual thoughts can be so perfected as to burst forth in the melodies of Havona. (44:1.8) Thought, not feelings, lead us Godward as the divine spirit makes contact with mortal man in the realm of the highest and most spiritualized thinking. (101:1.3) God's thoughts are higher than man's, but deliverance from guilt comes from realizing one's highest ideals may not align with God's will. (103:4.3) (131:2.3) Life on the divine worlds of the central universe transcends human concepts, including the technique of Havona thought unlike ours on Urantia. (14:5.2)
improvedDescription: The mental activity that leads mortals Godward through the highest and most spiritualized thinking. While feelings guide evolutionary development, thoughts provide the realm where the divine spirit makes contact with human beings, enabling spiritual growth through the combined operations of the Thought Adjuster and the Spirit of Truth.

name: Thought Adjuster of Jesus
originalDescription: The Personalized Adjuster of Jesus, chief of his kind in Nebadon, guided him faithfully and wisely through his human life, leading him to the Father's perfect will. (109:6.4-6) (136:2.3,5) (168:2.6) The Thought Adjuster of Jesus, who previously served with Machiventa Melchizedek, guided him step by step during his human life and aided in the choosing of the path of the Father's perfect will. (109:6.4) (123:2.1) (136:2.2) The Thought Adjuster of Jesus collaborated in creation of Part IV, utilizing existing records from the life of Jesus on Urantia, including the Gospels of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. (121:8.1) (168:1.2) The Thought Adjuster arrived with Jesus on February 11, 2 B.C., marking his first personal moral decision. (123:2.1) (196:1.7) The advice of Gabriel was to let Jesus, as a Creator Son, have the power to resurrect himself. (189:0.1-3) At healing at sundown, Jesus passed the responsibility of this decision to his Father, resulting in a miraculous wave of healing for 683 individuals. (145:3.10-11) at Jesus’ baptism during Jesus life moments Thought Adjuster spoke
improvedDescription: The Personalized Adjuster who guided Jesus throughout his human life, having previously served with Machiventa Melchizedek. This extraordinary Monitor became chief of all Adjusters in Nebadon after Jesus' baptism, when it was personalized and returned to direct the celestial hosts during Jesus' remaining earthly ministry.

name: Thought Adjusters
originalDescription: Adjusters, serving all local universes, are organized as an independent unit administered directly from Divinington, functioning interchangeably throughout the grand universe. (108:3.1) Father fragments are fully cognizant of the universal reflectivity system and can avail themselves of its content. (17:3.9) The better adapted secondary midwayers are able to attain contact with Thought Adjusters of certain mortals, mobilizing spiritual civilization through reserve corps. (114:7.9) The circuits of Thought Adjusters allow for direct communication with the Universal Father. (5:3.2) (15:9.6) The divine presence is most fully discovered in communion with the indwelling Mystery Monitor, the Paradise Thought Adjuster. (5:2.3-4) During transit sleeps between mansion worlds, the Thought Adjuster remains with you, ensuring your personality stays intact throughout your journey of spiritual advancement. (47:4.4) The greatest days in Thought Adjusters' individual careers occur when the human subject breaks through into the third psychic circle, attains the first psychic circle, and ultimately fuses eternally. (110:6.22) Human subjects are often known by the numbers of their Adjusters until after fusion, when they receive real universe names bestowed by destiny guardians. (108:3.3) If the Adjusters were withdrawn, Urantia would regress to barbarism, as they play a crucial role in transforming and humanizing its inhabitants. (109:4.4) Master Spirit Number One, the direct representation of the Paradise Father, is the close associate of the chief of Mystery Monitors and speaks for the Universal Father in all associations of the Seven Master Spirits. (16:3.2) The mystery of divine indwelling is the profoundest of universe mysteries, revealing the eternal perfection of God. (1:4.1,3) (124:6.17) Adjusters and seraphim work in perfect harmony, though they do not directly communicate. (113:4.5) (113:5.1) Even if subjects fail to survive, Thought Adjusters are not examined, as their flawless technique is beyond criticism. (108:5.10) Though we have limited knowledge of Adjusters, it is clear they are organized independently and administered directly from Divinington, serving throughout the superuniverses with a serial organization that extends across races, dispensations, and worlds. (108:3) Thought Adjusters, being of the essence of the original Deity, proceed directly from the Universal Father and are fragmentized entities constituting the factual presence of the infinite God, undiluted and unmixed divinity. (107:1) Surviving individuals rejoin resurrected subjects after their identity transition is realized. (112:4.13) Upon death, your Thought Adjuster will proceed to the Father, while angels are not involved in your transition to a morontia form. (39:2.13) When mortals fail to achieve eternal survival, their Adjusters serve on Divinington until resurrection, never neglecting duty or service. (40:4.1) (40:9.2) (49:6.5) (112:4) (112:4.4-11) Solitary Messengers and Inspired Trinity Spirits can detect presence of divine Thought Adjusters through spiritual reactive phenomena. (23:1.9) (107:4.4) (108:3.9) To secure planetary release and begin the Paradise career, spiritual beings must humanize to receive Thought Adjusters after the morontia experience. (55:4.25,31) Thought Adjuster fusion imparts eternal actualities to personality which were previously only potential, culminating in the mystery of making God and man one, leading to an eternal embrace and eventual ascension to higher realms. About mortal fusion with Thought Adjusters see link on fusion (112:7) | fusion:Adjuster fusion arrival historically also known as... mission and function of Thought Adjusters mortal co-operation with Adjusters mortal communication with Adjusters nature of Adjusters types of Adjusters types of Adjusters, other classifications
improvedDescription: Divine spirit fragments of the Universal Father that indwell the minds of mortal beings, serving as guides toward spiritual perfection and eternal survival. These Mystery Monitors are organized as an independent unit administered from Divinington and function throughout all local universes, preparing mortals for eventual fusion and the Paradise ascent.

name: Thought Changers
originalDescription: Thought Changers guide individuals until age 20, then become Thought Adjusters. (107:0.7)
improvedDescription: The designation given to Mystery Monitors during the early phase of mortal indwelling, from their arrival until the human subject reaches comparative full growth at about twenty years of age on Urantia.

name: Thought Controllers
originalDescription: Thought Controllers are Adjusters called when subjects are older than forty. (107:0.7)
improvedDescription: The designation given to Mystery Monitors during the final phase of mortal indwelling, from the attainment of discretion at about forty years of age until deliverance from the flesh.

name: thought preservers
originalDescription: Thought preservers, celestial artisans, also preserve the noble ideas of great Urantia minds in the language of Uversa. (44:4.2-4)
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans dedicated to preserving the higher thoughts and noble ideas of the realms, treasuring gems of mentation on morontia worlds and recording the ideation of great minds in the language of Uversa.

name: thought recorders
originalDescription: Celestial artisans devoted to preserving and reproducing superior thoughts of the realms function in seven groups, including thought preservers, concept recorders, and ideograph recorders. (44:4) types
improvedDescription: Celestial artisans who preserve and reproduce the superior thoughts of the realms, functioning in seven specialized groups including thought preservers, concept recorders, and ideograph recorders.

name: thoughtless pagan
originalDescription: Jesus’ comment to Ganid regarding the thoughtless pagan emphasized the importance of being receptive to the teachings of God in order to truly understand and seek salvation. (132:7.1,2)
improvedDescription: A man encountered by Jesus, Gonod, and Ganid during their journey to the northern Italian lakes, whom Jesus chose not to engage because the man was not hungry for truth or ready to receive spiritual teaching.

name: Thrace
originalDescription: Jews in Thrace encompassed Matthew's death. (139:7.10) Thracians believed in immortality, unlike many other ancient tribes whose religious practices have evolved only minimally over time. (92:6.1)
improvedDescription: Ancient region where the apostle Matthew met his death at Lysimachia, conspired against by unbelieving Jews and Roman soldiers. The Thracians were notable for their belief in monotheism and immortality, representing advanced religious development.

name: three
improvedDescription: The number three, which has significance in various cosmic and spiritual contexts throughout The Urantia Book.

name: three-brained mortals
originalDescription: Three-brained mortals exhibit a higher level of spiritual, ethical, and worshipful attributes compared to two-brained and one-brained mortals. (49:5.13-18)
improvedDescription: Three-brained mortals exhibit a higher level of spiritual, ethical, and worshipful attributes compared to two-brained and one-brained mortals. Their third brain specializes in controlling physical activities, freeing the two superior brains for intellectual functions and spiritual activities.

name: three concentric circles
improvedDescription: A symbolic pattern of three circles arranged concentrically, appearing in various spiritual and cosmic contexts.

name: threefold fluctuation
originalDescription: The threefold fluctuation of light from Deity abode indicates complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents. (27:7.7)
improvedDescription: The threefold fluctuation of light from the Deity abode indicates complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents.

name: threshold
originalDescription: The threefold fluctuation of light from the Deity abode signifies complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents, engulfing all of Paradise in dominating tide of spiritual expression. (27:7.7)
improvedDescription: A point or level at which something begins or changes, often referring to spiritual or developmental transitions.

name: throwing stick
originalDescription: The Andonites crafted the throwing stick for successful hunting expeditions. (63:5.6)
improvedDescription: The Andonites crafted the throwing stick for successful hunting expeditions, representing an early tool-making advancement that worked in conjunction with their implement-using abilities.

name: thumbs
originalDescription: One million years ago, Mesopotamian dawn mammals emerged with primitive opposable thumbs, descendants of the North American lemur type of placental mammal. (62:2.1) The mid-mammals had perfectly opposable thumbs, just about as well adapted for diversified work as the present human thumb, and walked upright with feet almost as well suited for walking as the later human races. (62:3.2)
improvedDescription: One million years ago, Mesopotamian dawn mammals emerged with primitive opposable thumbs, descendants of the North American lemur type of placental mammal. The mid-mammals had perfectly opposable thumbs, just about as well adapted for diversified work as the present human thumb.

name: thunder
originalDescription: James and John, the sons of thunder, were transformed by Jesus into the apostles of love. (139:3.1,5) (139:4.6) (181:2.4) Thunder, the voice of an angry god, overawed early man and was feared and worshiped by primitive tribes and nature cults. (85:4.3)
improvedDescription: James and John, the sons of thunder, were transformed by Jesus into apostles of love. Thunder was regarded by early humans as the voice of an angry god and was feared and worshiped by primitive tribes and nature cults.

name: thunderstones
originalDescription: Bretons protect against lightning with ancient thunderstones, a tradition passed down from the original Andonite inhabitants of western Europe. (80:9.14)
improvedDescription: Bretons protect against lightning with ancient thunderstones, a tradition passed down from the original Andonite inhabitants of western Europe.

name: Thursday
originalDescription: Thursday, a once lucky day for weddings, was believed to bring the most favorable outcomes when celebrating under the full moon, with the custom of throwing grain to ensure fecundity. (83:4.5)
improvedDescription: Thursday was considered a lucky day for weddings and was believed to bring the most favorable outcomes when celebrating under the full moon, with the custom of throwing grain to ensure fecundity.

name: Ti Tao
originalDescription: Fifteen thousand years ago, Andites entered China via the pass of Ti Tao, eventually spreading throughout the upper Yellow River valley and progressing eastward to Honan. (79:7.1)
improvedDescription: A mountain pass through which Andites traveled about fifteen thousand years ago to enter China, eventually spreading throughout the upper Yellow River valley and progressing eastward to Honan.

name: Tiberias
originalDescription: Jesus succeeded Sepphoris, spending a week in Tiberias before moving on to other cities around the Sea of Galilee. (129:1.1-2) (135:12.1) At Tiberias, conferences of Herod and Sanhedrin were held to plot the arrest and trial of Jesus, despite Herod's initial refusal. (154:0.1) (154:3) James of Safed from Tiberias sought Jesus for his demon-possessed epileptic son. (158:4.2) Jesus told apostles to avoid Sepphoris and Tiberias in their future work. (137:5.1) Thomas, the leading citizen of Tarichea, was the son of excellent parents who lived at Tiberias. (139:8.2)
improvedDescription: A new city built by Herod Antipas that became the capital of Galilee, succeeding Sepphoris. Jesus spent a week there during his tour around the Sea of Galilee, and it later became a site where Herod and Sanhedrin representatives held conferences to plot Jesus's arrest, though Herod initially refused to take action against him.

name: Tiberius
originalDescription: Jesus was baptized in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, who was coemperor with Augustus beginning in A.D. 26. (136:2.8) Tiberius allowed Pontius Pilate to serve as procurator of Judea for a decade due to his competence in governing minor provinces, despite his moral cowardice and failure to understand the significance of Judaism. (185:1.1) Gonod carried greetings from the princes of India to Tiberius, who admired Jesus' kingly bearing and gracious manner. (132:0.1) Tiberius admired Jesus' bearing and wished for his own kingly and gracious manner. (132:0.1) Tiberius died while Pilate was on his way to Rome, never recovering from the regret of sacrificing Jesus and eventually being deposed. (185:1.6)
improvedDescription: Roman emperor who was coemperor with Augustus beginning in A.D. 11 and whose fifteenth year of actual rule was A.D. 26, the year of Jesus's baptism. He allowed Pontius Pilate to serve as procurator of Judea for ten years and admired Jesus's kingly bearing when meeting him through Gonod's introduction.

name: Tibet
originalDescription: Andonites in Tibet lived in crude stone huts and hillside grottoes, surrounded by forests and streams, abounding in game. (64:3.2) Andite migration to central Asia greatly influenced the ancestors of the Turanians and contributed valuable qualities to the Chinese stocks, with some of the early migrations extending into Tibet. (78:5.6) (79:1.1-2,8) Asoka established Buddhism in Tibet, spreading the religion throughout eastern Asia and making it the dominant faith in half the world. (94:9.1) Early Chinese settlements near Tibet were well established in control of eastern Asia. (78:1.7) Tibetans embrace elements of all world religions except the gospel of Jesus: sonship with God, brotherhood with man, and eternal citizenship in the universe. (94:10.3) Tibetans made little contact with the Chinese until recent times, despite the yellow race entering China over 300,000 years ago. (79:5.3) Migration from Tibet to the Yangtze valley added Andite blood to the river settlements, although not as extensively as in the north. (79:6.5) In Tibet, the shamanic priests and medicine men became wealthy by accumulating fees as offerings to spirits, often possessing most of the tribe's wealth, with half the male population belonging to this nonproducing class. (90:2.11) Religion in Tibet combines Melchizedek teachings with Buddhism, Hinduism, Taoism, and Christianity, resulting in an elaborate and diverse ceremonial practice. (94:10)
improvedDescription: A highland region where Andonites lived in crude stone huts and grottoes, and which served as an ancient gateway for Andite migrations into China. Tibet developed mixed religious practices combining elements of Buddhism, Hinduism, Taoism, and Christianity, though notably lacking the simple gospel teachings of sonship with God and brotherhood with man.

name: tidal friction
originalDescription: Tidal friction gradually stops planets' revolutions, leading to stable orbits and potential moon fragmentation. (57:6.2-3)
improvedDescription: A gravitational force that gradually slows planetary revolutions, eventually causing planets to always turn the same hemisphere toward their sun or larger body, as illustrated by Mercury and Earth's moon.

name: tidal wave
originalDescription: A tidal wave swept over Dalamatia, causing the planetary headquarters to sink beneath the sea, leading to the near-obliteration of its noble culture. (67:5.4)
improvedDescription: A massive wave that swept over Dalamatia one hundred and sixty-two years after the rebellion, causing the planetary headquarters to sink beneath the sea and obliterating nearly every vestige of its noble culture.

name: tigers
originalDescription: If unarmed man met a hungry tiger, society's prime gift to man is security, as nature confers no rights on man. (70:9.1)
improvedDescription: Large predatory cats used as an example of nature's indifference to human rights, illustrating that an unarmed person meeting a hungry tiger would demonstrate that nature confers no rights on humans, only life and a world to live in.

name: Tiglath
originalDescription: Tiglath was the lad that helped Jesus on Mount Hermon with supplies, and in return Jesus asked for guardian seraphim to be with him, and gave him a donkey as a present. (134:8.1,2,10)
improvedDescription: A young lad who assisted Jesus during his sojourn on Mount Hermon, carrying supplies and depositing food at a designated point 6,000 feet above sea level. Jesus requested guardian seraphim to be with Tiglath and gave him a donkey as a gift when departing.

name: Tigris River
originalDescription: The Tigris and Euphrates rivers were the cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations, giving life to the violet race and spreading their culture to surrounding lands. (78:1.3) The second garden adjoined the Tigris River, where Adam and his followers established a new center of culture and religion. (76:0.1) (76:1.1-3) (76:3.8) Sumerians lived at the mouth of the Tigris River, persisting for thousands of years to furnish the Nodite ancestry of the Sumerian peoples. (77:4.6) (78.8)
improvedDescription: One of two major rivers, along with the Euphrates, that formed the cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations in Mesopotamia. The second garden was located in the triangle between these rivers, and various ancient peoples including Sumerians and Adamites established important centers along its course.

name: time
originalDescription: Time is eternity, the absolute limit. (12:5.2) Time alone ripens fruit on tree, everything must await the coming of its time. (130:5.3) Animals lack self-consciousness of time, unlike man who progresses inward to perceive time as a whole cycle, moving beyond the linear sequence of events towards circular simultaneity. (130:7.5) (133:7.8) Faith in Jesus brings salvation from time, granting eternal life of unending progression in God-recognition and God-service. (101:6.14) Consulting the Imports of Time enables the forecasting of future events with the same reliability as other indicators used by the authorities of the universes of time. (28:6.11) Lucifer's futile attempt to short-circuit time threatened the freewill choice of all beings in Satania and stood as a monumental threat to steal personal liberty on an unprecedented scale. (54:2.3) (118:8.7,9) days and years function of time nature of time wisely using time independence of time
improvedDescription: A bestowal of Paradise that comes by virtue of motion and mind's inherent awareness of sequentiality. Time is the moving image of eternity, essential for finite creatures to coexist with the Infinite, and serves as both an opportunity for spiritual growth and a limitation that must eventually be transcended in the ascension career.

name: timidity
originalDescription: Andrew occasionally manifested dangerous timidity, but always maintained sincere justice and fairness in his leadership role among the messengers of the heavenly kingdom. (181:2.16) The God-knowing individual triumphs over obstacles with living faith, while many who are certain about God fear to assert their beliefs in the face of shallow objections. (102:7.6)
improvedDescription: A dangerous weakness occasionally manifested by Andrew, one of Jesus's apostles, despite his otherwise faithful leadership and commitment to justice and fairness. More broadly, the fear that God-knowing individuals may experience when asserting their beliefs in the face of shallow objections, despite their inner certainty about spiritual truths.

name: Timothy
originalDescription: Paul, joined by Silas and Timothy, preached in Corinth for eighteen months and encountered students of the “Jewish tutor of the son of an Indian merchant.” (133:3.3)
improvedDescription: A companion of Paul and Silas who joined them during their eighteen-month ministry in Corinth, where they encountered students who had been taught by a Jewish tutor.

name: tin
originalDescription: The Adamsonites mixed copper with tin to create bronze, revolutionizing metalworking. (81:3.5)
improvedDescription: A metal that was mixed with copper by the Adamsonites to create bronze, revolutionizing metalworking when one of their number discovered this combination at a highland copper mine located alongside a tin deposit in Turkestan.

name: tithing
originalDescription: sethites and Abraham Jesus' times light and life
improvedDescription: The practice of giving one-tenth of one's income or possessions, originating as an early form of taxation and later adopted by religious communities. The system was used by ancient Sethite priests, introduced to Abraham by Melchizedek, and continued through Jesus's time and into advanced civilizations as a method of public financing.

name: Titus (associate of Paul)
originalDescription: Fortune, once a youth, became the leader of the Christians in Crete and closely assisted Titus in uplifting believers. (130:6.5) Paul sent Titus to reorganize the churches in Crete despite the Cretans' questionable reputation. (130:5.1)
improvedDescription: A close associate of Paul who was sent to reorganize the churches in Crete and worked alongside Fortune, a former youth who became the leader of Christians in Crete, in uplifting the Cretan believers.

name: Titus (Emperor)
originalDescription: Titus, who destroyed Jerusalem in A.D. 70, brought about a terrible harvest of death and destruction upon the Jewish people. (121:8.7) (187:1.5)
improvedDescription: The Roman Emperor whose armies besieged and destroyed Jerusalem in A.D. 70, covering Golgotha with thousands of crosses and bringing terrible destruction upon the Jewish people forty years after Jesus's crucifixion.

name: Titus of Capernaum
originalDescription: Titus of Capernaum, a nobleman with a critically ill son, believed in Jesus and witnessed a miraculous healing. (146:5.1-3) Titus of Capernaum was among the nobleman's son trained for gospel service in Peter's school before being ordained by Jesus. (148:1.4)
improvedDescription: A prominent nobleman of Capernaum whose critically ill son was healed through Jesus's word while Jesus was in Cana. This experience led Titus and his entire household to believe with whole hearts, and his son later became a minister of the kingdom and was trained in Peter's school for gospel service.

name: Toda tribe
originalDescription: The Toda tribe of India regressed to primitive levels by praying to no one in particular, neglecting social, moral, and spiritual values in their rituals. (91:0.4) The semimagical recitations of the Toda tribe have evolved into a more intelligent and ethical form of prayer over time. (91:3.2)
improvedDescription: A tribe of India whose religious practices represent a regression to primitive levels, characterized by prayers addressed to no one in particular and rituals that contribute nothing to social, moral, or spiritual values, though their prayer forms are gradually evolving toward more intelligent and ethical communication.

name: Todan
originalDescription: Todan first heard the gospel at Rimmon and later brought the message to Mesopotamia and beyond. (146:1.4)
improvedDescription: An early convert who first heard the gospel message at Rimmon and later carried it into Mesopotamia and far beyond, being among the first to preach the good news to those dwelling beyond the Euphrates.

name: tokens
originalDescription: Civilized races cursed with belief in signs and tokens seek to avoid ill luck through superstitious remnants of an ancient ghost cult. (87:5.14)
improvedDescription: Superstitious signs and omens that civilized races still believe in as remnants of ancient ghost cults, used in attempts to avoid ill luck and trouble, representing humanity's slow abandonment of primitive methods of understanding the world.

name: tolerance
originalDescription: Understanding others leads to tolerance, which matures into love. (100:4.4) Differences in status are essential to the development of human tolerance and altruism. (64:6.34) Education evolves conciliators into interpretive teachers, increasing respect for others' opinions and avoiding clashes of mind. (25:3.12) Tolerance is essential for success in persuading and prevailing with others. (160:1.9) Tolerance is a fruit of the spirit, to be shown through forgiving and enduring peace towards others. (193:2.2) With winsome tact and tolerance, the Master gracefully gains victory without engaging in unnecessary battle or destruction. (160:3.4) Family is where tolerance is learned. (84:7.28) A mature person looks upon all others with tenderness and tolerance. (160:1.6) As you mature and gain experience, practice tolerance with difficult individuals to cultivate tact and wisdom. (156:5.18) religious tolerance
improvedDescription: The quality of understanding and accepting others despite their differences, which Jesus taught leads to friendship and ultimately to love. Tolerance develops through seeking to understand others' viewpoints and motivations, and it represents one of the fruits of the spirit that mature individuals demonstrate toward their fellows.

name: tom-toms
originalDescription: Rhythm was once universal in early man's efforts to influence spirits, along with the belief that sweating could eliminate sickness. (90:4.6)
improvedDescription: Universal primitive drums used rhythmically in early spiritual practices, believed to influence spirits and aid in healing ceremonies alongside other methods like sweating and vapor baths.

name: tombs
originalDescription: ancient times Jesus' times tombstone
improvedDescription: Burial places and monuments for the dead, ranging from simple stone cairns to elaborate sepulchres. Jesus criticized religious leaders who built elaborate tombs for prophets while planning to kill contemporary truth-bearers, and his own resurrection took place at the tomb of Lazarus in Bethany.

name: tomorrow
originalDescription: Tomorrow is wholly in the hands of the Father. (130:5.3) Thomas asked Jesus if they should continue having everything in common, and the Master replied, “Be not unduly anxious for the morrow, sufficient for the day is the trouble thereof.” (140:6.13)
improvedDescription: The future day that Jesus taught is wholly in the hands of the heavenly Father, counseling his followers not to be anxiously concerned about tomorrow since each day has sufficient trouble of its own.

name: tongue
originalDescription: Believers should pray for the extension of the kingdom of heaven, for the human tongue is a member which few men can tame. (146:2.13) The secret of greatness lies in a great man's ability to subdue his own tongue, revealing the true measure of planetary greatness. (28:6.20)
improvedDescription: The human tongue as a symbol of speech and communication, which Jesus taught is a member that few can tame but which the spirit can transform into a voice of tolerance and mercy. Control of one's tongue represents a true measure of greatness.

name: tools
originalDescription: The Andonites developed sharp stone tools for hunting and survival. (63:1.2-3) (63:5.6) Domesticated animals were living tools that enabled man to advance from his primitive state to higher levels of civilization. (81:2.10) Improved tools and efficient machines determine the survival of contending groups in advancing civilization. (81:6.20) The invention of weapon tools enabled man to become a hunter, gaining freedom from food slavery and advancing civilization through ingenuity and discovery. (68:5.4) Old men and cripples, due to modification consequent upon age and disease, were set to work making tools and weapons, later transitioning to building irrigation works. (69:3.4)
improvedDescription: Implements and devices created by humans to extend their capabilities, beginning with the Andonites' stone axes and clubs. Tools enabled the transition from primitive hunting to advanced civilization, with domesticated animals serving as living tools and mechanical devices determining the survival of advancing groups.

name: torture
originalDescription: In ancient initiation ceremonies, self-torture and painful cutting were used to harden and impress youths. (70:7.10) The olden ideas of self-discipline embraced physical torture, with priests teaching the virtue of suffering through castration, and Hebrews, Hindus, and Buddhists devoutly following this doctrine of humiliation. (89:3.4)
improvedDescription: Physical suffering inflicted as part of ancient initiation ceremonies or religious practices, including self-torture used to harden youth and physical humiliation taught by various religious traditions as a form of spiritual discipline.

name: Total Deity
improvedDescription: A conceptual designation referring to the complete and undifferentiated aspect of deity reality, representing the absolute unity of all divine attributes and functions before their differentiation into the various expressions of divinity.

name: totalitarianism
originalDescription: Materialism and mechanism may have been vanquished, but the devastating influence of twentieth-century secularism will continue to blight the spiritual experience of millions, born from the atheist science and totalitarian medieval Christian church. (195:8)
improvedDescription: A form of authoritarian government that seeks total control over all aspects of society, mentioned as one of the influences contributing to twentieth-century secularism that blights spiritual experience.

name: totems
originalDescription: Primitive tribes believed they were descended from animals of supposed ancestry, choosing them as their “totems”. (74:8.5) Totemism combines social and religious practices, with totems serving as both symbols of the group and their deity. (88:3.2)
improvedDescription: Sacred symbols representing animals or objects that primitive tribes believed were their ancestral origins, serving as both group identity markers and religious deities. Totemism combined social and religious practices, with totems eventually evolving into modern national symbols and flags.

name: tower of Babel
improvedDescription: A legendary tower mentioned in ancient traditions, associated with human attempts to reach heaven and subsequent divine intervention causing confusion of languages.

name: traditions
originalDescription: The stability of growth in Edenic culture acts upon civilization with the compelling force of tradition, bridging the gulf between God and man. (51:6.5) Custom establishes institutions of survival, including industry, property, war for gain, and societal regulation through taboo, convention, and religious sanction, rooted in fear, ignorance, and superstition. (69:1.3) You are not obligated to accept outworn religious traditions, for true spiritual freedom and liberty come from following the truth wherever the spirit may lead you. (155:6.5-7) Traditions can be oppressive and enslaving, as seen in the burdens placed on the people by leaders who say one thing but do another. (175:1.8) Do not allow reverence for traditions to blind you from understanding the true spiritual realities of the kingdom. (157:2.2) Philosophy struggles for emancipation from dogma, tradition, and the bondage of superstition, while science fights for deliverance from the slavery of mathematics and the relative blindness of materialism. (12:9.5) It is pitiful to behold giant intellects in cultural bondage, stagnating at the level of logical intellectuality, held securely within the cruel grasp of tradition and authority. (101:7.4) Traditions provide stability and co-operation, but they also hinder initiative and creative freedom, reminiscent of the world's ongoing struggle with the dominance of outdated customs. (66:6.2) Religious traditions are the imperfectly preserved and unreliable records of the experiences of God-knowing men, altered by primitive man's tendency towards mythmaking. (4:5.1) Ritual sanctifies customs, creates myths, and is the product of myths, evolving from social to economic to religious ceremonial practices that can be personal, group, or both. (90:5.2) Tradition is a safe refuge for fearful, halfhearted souls who shun the struggles of the spirit and uncertainties of the mind, seeking an easy path devoid of faith voyages and daring adventures. (155:5.11) (191:5.3) Traditions sentimentally link the past with the present, but can also stifle initiative and creative powers. (66:6.2) Jesus
improvedDescription: Cultural and religious practices passed down through generations that provide stability and continuity but can also stifle spiritual growth and creative freedom. While traditions can bridge the gap between past and present, they may become oppressive when they blind people to new spiritual truths and keep them enslaved to outdated customs and ceremonies.

name: traitors
improvedDescription: Individuals who betray trust or allegiance, violating loyalty to their commitments, causes, or communities.

name: tranosta
originalDescription: Tranosta is the transcendental energy system linked exclusively to absonite beings, operating from the upper level of Paradise and known as such on Uversa. (42:2.18)
improvedDescription: The transcendental energy system that operates on and from the upper level of Paradise, functioning exclusively in connection with absonite beings and known by this name on Uversa.

name: transcendence
originalDescription: The paradox of divine immanence and transcendence in religion challenges human comprehension, necessitating the function of theology to unify the concept of God's divine domination of the universe with the indwelling presence of God within each individual for intelligent worship and the hope of personality survival. (5:5.6) The mystery of God's transcendence should not lessen your faith, for He is personally present in a vast universe yet dwells within each of us. (12:7.12)
improvedDescription: The divine quality of God existing beyond and above the material universe while simultaneously dwelling within each individual. This paradox of divine transcendence and immanence challenges human comprehension but must be unified through faith to justify intelligent worship and validate the hope of personality survival.

name: transcendental Almighty
originalDescription: This is a manifestation of God the Ultimate to the universes of time which is postulated to occur when God the Ultimate begins to function as supercontrol of the universes. At that time He will exercise supercontrol of supertime and transcended space over the administrative functions of the Supreme Almighty. (118:2.4)
improvedDescription: A manifestation of God the Ultimate that will occur when He begins to function as supercontrol of the universes, exercising overcontrol of supertime and transcended space over the administrative functions of the Supreme Almighty.

name: transcendental energy
improvedDescription: The energy system that operates on and from the upper level of Paradise, functioning exclusively in connection with absonite beings and designated as tranosta on Uversa.

name: transcendental level
improvedDescription: The absonite level of reality characterized by beings and phenomena that transcend time and space, existing beyond finite limitations but below absolute reality.

name: Transcendental Recorders
originalDescription: Transcendental Recorders are personal gravity traversers, unlike local universe administrators. (23:3.2) Transcendental Recorders exist without discoverable form, unlike most beings in the seven superuniverses that possess tangible forms. (42:12.10)
improvedDescription: Personal gravity traversers who exist without discoverable form, unlike most beings in the seven superuniverses. They function as transcendental beings capable of instantaneous transit but are not available to superuniverse and local universe administrators.

name: Transcendentalers
originalDescription: Finaliters on Paradise strive to understand over one thousand groups of transcendental supercitizens, assisted by transcendental ministers, residing exclusively in the west of Paradise and playing a vital role in the superadministration of the master universe. (31:8.1-4) Absoniters, also known as Transcendentalers, exist beyond time and space, without beginnings or endings, eventuated rather than created, embodying the Ultimacy of Deity in transcendent acts throughout the master universe. (0:1.12) (55:4.7,19) The perfected mortal finaliters on Paradise strive to comprehend the nature and function of the transcendental supercitizens, aided by transcendental ministers, in the restricted area where the Transcendentalers exclusively reside. (31:8) Transcendentalers are solely focused on superadministering the master universe, beyond mortal ascent to Havona or the affairs of Havona and the superuniverses. (31:8.2) Transcendentalers are not involved in the superuniverses or Havona. (31:8.2) In Paradise classification, eventuated transcendental beings are loyal to the Paradise Trinity and obedient to the Ultimate, existing on four ultimate levels of personality activity and functioning on the seven levels of the absonite in twelve grand divisions. (30:1.92-93) In the next universe age, Transcendentalers may replace the finaliters as society's initiators of new supermaterial activities, possibly stepping in for the latter during the seventh epoch of evolutionary stability for outer space ministry. (55:4.7,19) Transcendentalers, ever loyal and eventuated, are neither creators nor creatures, existing on an absonite level beyond beginnings or endings, transcending time and space, and obedient to the Ultimate. (0:1.12) (30:1.92-93) (31:8.3) Upon reaching Paradise, mortal ascenders fraternize with upwards of three thousand different orders of Paradise Citizens, as well as with various groups of Transcendentalers and other unrevealed inhabitants. (27:2.3) The eventuation of transcendental beings on Paradise reveals their absonite nature as neither finite nor infinite, loyal to the Paradise Trinity, and functioning across various levels of personality activity and the absonite. (30:1.92-99) (31:8.3) (31:9.10) Solitary Messengers and Inspired Trinity Spirits maintain an organic relationship with Transcendentalers and the Master Architects. (31:9.13) The senior Architect of Master Universe, chief of Transcendentalers, administers the Trinity oath to mortal finaliters, marking their first serious contact with the governing corps of Paradise Transcendentalers. (31:8.4) (31:9.1) You can conceive of a Creator as a creature, yet struggle to comprehend the existence of the Transcendentalers, who create no beings, were never created, and are subject to God the Ultimate. (31:8.3) orders
improvedDescription: Eventuated beings of absonite attributes who exist beyond time and space, neither created nor creators, but simply eventuated. They reside exclusively in the west of Paradise and are concerned solely with the superadministration of the master universe, serving as transcendental supercitizens loyal to the Paradise Trinity and obedient to God the Ultimate.

name: transcription, celestial
improvedDescription: The process or method by which celestial beings record information and events.

name: transfiguration
improvedDescription: A significant transformative event in the life of Jesus, representing a moment of divine revelation and spiritual manifestation.

name: transformation
originalDescription: You are transformed by Spirit of Truth, endowed with power to perform the perfect will of God. (143:2.4) Cures effected by Jesus for Elman’s patients resemble miracles, but are transformations in expectant and faith-dominated persons under the influence of a positive personality. (148:2.2) The supreme desire to do the Father's will, coupled with the joy of faith realization, leads to loving spiritual ministry, intellectual communion, and social service being a byproduct of spiritual transformation. (178:1.11)
improvedDescription: The spiritual process by which mortals are changed through the Spirit of Truth, enabling them to perform God's will and transition from material to spiritual existence. This transformation occurs in expectant, faith-dominated persons and leads to loving service, intellectual communion, and social ministry as natural byproducts of inner spiritual renewal.

name: transformers
improvedDescription: Celestial beings or mechanisms involved in the transformation and manipulation of energy within the universe.

name: transit sleep
improvedDescription: A state of rest or suspended consciousness experienced during spiritual transportation or transition between celestial realms.

name: transit trio
originalDescription: The transit trio assist Havona pilgrims in their final journey to attain the Universal Father and enter eternity. (26:9.2)
improvedDescription: The transit trio are celestial beings who assist Havona pilgrims in their final journey to attain the Universal Father and enter eternity, announcing when the last venture of time is about to ensue.

name: transition
originalDescription: Transition is always accompanied by confusion, and there will be little tranquillity in the religious world until the great struggle between the three contending philosophies of religion is ended. (99:4.9) The difficulties of religious transition create challenges but also opportunities for growth and harmony in human fellowship. (99:4) Forewarn all believers to traverse the fringe of conflict during the transition from earthly to spiritual life, embracing responsibilities and obligations with the light burden of truth. (159:3.7) (160:1.6) Great danger of breakdown during societal transition requires careful guidance from those well-versed in history and experience of social evolution. (81:6.40-41) Religion should stabilize ideals during dangerous times of transition to ensure a smooth evolution of civilization. (99:1.3-4) (99:2.6) A nation's intellectual level determines whether a mechanical age will liberate or devastate, as science guided by wisdom can successfully navigate the transition difficulties caused by unemployment from laborsaving machinery. (81:6.22)
improvedDescription: The process of change from one state or level to another, particularly from material to spiritual existence. Transition is always accompanied by confusion and requires careful guidance, whether in religious, social, or individual contexts. It represents a critical period requiring wisdom and stability to successfully navigate from established methods to new and better procedures.

name: transition ministers
originalDescription: Almost one half of the transition ministers on Urantia joined the Lucifer rebellion, but Van and other noble beings remained loyal defenders of Michael and his universe government. (67:3.2) The 6th order seraphim transition ministers assist in guiding beings from earthly life to morontia existence on the mansion worlds. (48:6.1) Almost ten percent of the transition ministers were ensnared in the Lucifer rebellion, as symbolized by John's vision of the great red dragon. (53:7.7) Morontia world seraphim minister tirelessly to aid creature transition from material to spiritual, guiding souls on the seven mansion worlds towards perfection and unity with the divine. (39:6) (48:6) Transition ministers are the seraphim closest to humans, often igniting the will-to-service through personal friendship. (48:6.34) Seraphim of transitional ministry, through seven orders of assignment, guide ascending souls on the mansion worlds towards perfection attainment and spiritual progression. (39:6.1) (48:6.4-37) # seraphic evangels # racial interpreters # mind planners # morontia counselors # technicians # recorder-teachers # ministering reserves Transition ministers in Jerusem circles provide guidance for souls moving through various stages of spiritual growth. (46:5.22)
improvedDescription: The sixth order of seraphic servers who facilitate the transit of material and mortal creatures from temporal life in the flesh into the early stages of morontia existence on the seven mansion worlds. These angels serve in seven specialized orders including seraphic evangels, racial interpreters, mind planners, morontia counselors, technicians, recorder-teachers, and ministering reserves.

name: transition rest
improvedDescription: A period of rest or recuperation associated with transitional phases in spiritual progression or celestial transport.

name: transition world #1
improvedDescription: The first of the transition worlds, likely associated with the finaliters and their ongoing universe service.

name: transitional culture worlds
improvedDescription: Worlds that serve as cultural transition points, mentioned in connection with Jerusem.

name: translation
originalDescription: definition how it works examples
improvedDescription: The process by which mortals who achieve Adjuster fusion during their mortal lifetime are translated directly from material existence to morontia life without experiencing natural death.
