(26:5.3) in predictions Embracing uncertainty
improvedDescription: A fundamental aspect of mortal existence that serves divine purposes, teaching creatures to develop faith, courage, and trust while providing the essential element of adventure and growth in the cosmic journey.

name: Unclassified Centers
originalDescription: The unclassified centers are recorded in the Uversa personality register. (30:2.99) Supreme Power Centers act as balance wheels on spheres of extraordinary energy relationships, serving as universe balance wheels or energy governors in spheres of the most extraordinary energy relationships. (29:2.19)
improvedDescription: The seventh order of Supreme Power Centers that function in special local situations with extraordinary energy relationships, serving as universe balance wheels or energy governors on select spheres.

name: Uncle Joshua
originalDescription: The children referred to Jesus as Uncle Joshua, gathering eagerly at the repair shop for his captivating stories and laughter-filled presence. (128:6.11)
improvedDescription: The affectionate name children used for Jesus when he worked at the repair shop, where he would gather them around to tell captivating stories and share moments of laughter and wonder.

name: unclean spirits
improvedDescription: Malevolent spiritual entities associated with demon possession and mental affliction.

name: undershepherds
originalDescription: True undershepherds of Father's flock lead with worthiness and nourish the flock with good food, guiding them to green pastures and still waters. (165:2.6)
improvedDescription: True spiritual leaders who guide their followers with worthiness, providing good spiritual nourishment and leading them to peaceful, life-sustaining places.

name: understanding
improvedDescription: A concept related to comprehension, knowledge, sympathy, tolerance, and wisdom, particularly associated with mental and spiritual development.

name: unemployment
originalDescription: Civilizations must provide efficient methods of place finding and training in common labor to prevent long-term unemployment and maintain societal stability. (81:6.32) In the main continental nation of a nearby planet, labor is deemed honorable for all able-bodied citizens over eighteen, whether working at home, on farms, in recognized industries, on public works, or as compulsory laborers in mines. (72:5.11) Productivity improvements in land cultivation have liberated man power for more valuable tasks, despite job losses caused by the displacement of workers by machines in the transition to a mechanical age. (81:6.21-22)
improvedDescription: The condition of being without work, which civilizations must address through efficient job placement and training programs to prevent long-term economic instability and social problems.

name: unfortunates
originalDescription: The unfortunate few suffer so the majority can advance, but the favored majority should show kindness to those less fortunate who fail to achieve ideal partnerships that satisfy all biological urges under evolving social norms. (83:6.5) The church's well-intended ministry to the unfortunate has contributed to the unwise perpetuation of racially degenerate stocks, hindering progress. (99:3.5) Mortal man's search for God is driven by the divine trait of mercy towards the weak and needy, as evidenced by the Creator Son's relentless pursuit of the lowest will creatures and their joint preparation for eternal universe service. (26:11.5) The liberation of women, in enlightened societies, has resulted in disproportionate gains for the weaker sex in the progressive evolution of society. (84:5.9)
improvedDescription: Those who suffer misfortune or disadvantage, toward whom the favored majority should show kindness and consideration, as divine mercy naturally extends to the weak and needy.

name: unhappiness
originalDescription: Deficient unification engenders unhappiness, but a confluence of cosmic insight and emotional reactions can create an effective philosophy of living, leading to improved character and a unified personality. (140:4.8,9) The presence of the divine Spirit prevents the consuming thirst and hunger of mortal discontent, providing a well of satisfaction and spiritual illumination that transcends material joys and earthly satisfactions. (34:6.8)
improvedDescription: A state of discontent arising from deficient personality unification or lack of worthy goals, which can be overcome through spiritual insight and divine guidance.

name: uniformity
originalDescription: Each apostolic teacher offered their own unique perspective on the gospel, with Peter and James Zebedee holding the most influence. (148:1.2) Jesus prayed for unity among his followers, not uniformity, as sin stagnates while righteousness inspires individual creativity and communion with the divine spirits. (182:1.8) The security of a religious group is based on spiritual unity, not theological uniformity, allowing for individual expression of personal beliefs within the community. (103:5.12) The unity of true worship in the spirit of all men is forever true, while the uniformity of creeds remains unattainable. (92:7.4) (103:1.1) The religions of authority require uniformity in belief, but the religion of the spirit calls for unity of experience and insight, allowing for diversity of belief and intellectual views. (155:6.9) standardization
improvedDescription: A rigid standardization that differs from true spiritual unity, as Jesus prayed for unity among believers while allowing for diversity of personal interpretation and experience.

name: Unions of Days
originalDescription: There are exactly seven hundred thousand Unions of Days in existence, with no more being created. (18:6.2) (21:1.4) A Union of Days operates independently from the local universe government, only intervening when requested by local authorities. (18:6.5) Unions of Days are always in touch with each other and all required personalities, thanks to the Paradise circuits available to Trinity-origin beings. (18:6.4) Associate Inspectors are close associates of the Seven Supreme Executives, serving as personal representatives to local universes under their direct supervision. (24:4.2) The wisdom of the Union of Days reflects the united wisdom of the Paradise Trinity, guiding Immanuel's counsel for Michael to stay distant from rebellion's self-destructive path. (54:5.8) Faithfuls of Days, personal representatives of Immanuel, communicate within local universes under the jurisdiction of the Union of Days. (18:7.3) (33:5.3) (43:4.2) Immanuel, the Union of Days assigned to Nebadon, is the ambassador of the Paradise Trinity in this local universe. (33:5.1) Unions of Days appear in personality registers (30:1.13) (30:2.28) The mobilized Assigned Sentinels form the stabilization corps of the local universe under the shared supervision of the Father Melchizedek and the newly assigned Inspired Trinity Spirits serving the Union of Days. (55:10.3) A Union of Days is independent from the local universe government, acting as an observer and only intervening at the request of local authorities. (18:6.5) (33:5.3) Immanuel of Salvington, the 611,121st of the Supreme Trinity Personalities 6th order, is a being of sublime dignity who refuses worship and adoration, advising the Sovereign Son only when requested and never acknowledging subordination to his brother Michael. (18:0.7) (33:5.2) function
improvedDescription: Paradise Trinity personalities who serve as counseling ambassadors to local universes, with exactly seven hundred thousand in existence, acting as observers and advisers to Creator Sons.

name: Unions of Souls
originalDescription: The angels of Orvonton foster and promote teamwork as a crucial lesson in mastering the art of working with others in the universe, where lone service is rare and camaraderie is essential for higher ascension. (28:5.14) In synchrony with interpreters of ethics on Paradise, Unions of Souls reflect the ideals and status of ethical relationships, aiding in the wise resolution of universal problems arising from intelligent beings' associations. (28:5.13) Reflective associates of Perfectors of Wisdom, the Unions of Souls aid in promoting ethical relationships and providing valuable insights on the best solutions to relationship problems in the universe. (28:5.13-14)
improvedDescription: Reflective associates of the Perfectors of Wisdom who promote ethical relationships and teamwork, helping resolve problems arising from associations between intelligent beings throughout the universe.

name: United Kingdom
improvedDescription: A nation located in the British Isles, referenced in geographical contexts within The Urantia Book.

name: United Midwayers of Urantia
improvedDescription: A unified organization of both primary and secondary midway creatures serving on Urantia, combining the ancient primary midwayers with the secondary midwayers descended from the Adamic staff.

name: United States of North America
originalDescription: The United States of North America exemplifies the creative potential of race mixtures combining superior strata, leading to increased vigor and fertility. (82:6.5) Hero-venerating nationalism in the United States of North America stems from the development of ancestor worship in Eastern religions like Buddhism and Hinduism. (92:6.20) The American states surrendered sovereignty to the federal government, promoting peace and unity within the nation. (134:5.13-15) (134:6.5)
improvedDescription: The United States of North America exemplifies the creative potential of race mixtures combining superior strata, leading to increased vigor and fertility. (56:0.1-2) (56:9.11) (133:5.8) for mortals historically in the universe

name: Universal Absolute

originalDescription: Seven Universal Conciliators are created for each Havona Servital, one in each superuniverse, through a superuniverse technique of reflective response to transactions on Paradise. improvedDescription: The aspect of the I AM representing the union of the Father and Eternal Son that initiates the creative cycle, resulting in the appearance of the Conjoint Actor and the eternal universe of Havona, characterized by divine unity in all creative acts.

name: Universal Father
originalDescription: The seven sacred worlds of the Universal Father contain the inherent personality secrets of the universe, each reflecting a specialized manifestation of divinity, guarded and understood by select intelligences resident on those spheres. (13:0.3) (13:1-2) The absolutum is the original nonspiritual expression of the Universal Father, composed of a single form of materialization found only on Paradise. (11:2.9) All goodness takes origin in the Father, making it impossible for the Spirit to have more goodness; however, we can better comprehend this goodness through the acts of the Spirit. (8:2.7) Attaining the Universal Father is the passport to eternity, marking a significant milestone on the journey towards Paradise. (26:9.2) also known as | Deity; God:also known as attitude delegation of power how is described functions personality of God
improvedDescription: The First Person of Deity, the First Source and Center of all reality, creator and controller of the universe of universes. The Universal Father is the infinite and eternal God who bestows personality, indwells mortals through Thought Adjusters, and serves as the loving Father of all personalities throughout creation, being both transcendent in Paradise and immanent in the hearts of his children.

name: Universal Intelligence
improvedDescription: A designation referring to the cosmic intelligence that pervades creation, often associated with the mind ministry of the Infinite Spirit and the universal coordination of intellectual realities throughout the universes.

name: Universal Mind

name: Universal Mother
originalDescription: The Eternal Son is the Universal Mother, perfectly complementing the Universal Father in their universal family. improvedDescription: The Supreme Being in its nurturing aspect, serving as the divine Mother who sustains all universe creatures throughout their lives, complementing the Universal Father as divine parents of the cosmic family.

improvedDescription: The eternal self-relationship of Infinity expressing the I AM, revealed as the Universal Father in personality, as pure energy and spirit coherence in impersonal manifestation, and as the primacy of the First Source and Center.

name: Universe Sons

name: Universe Spirits

improvedDescription: The parable of the unjust judge teaches the importance of persistence in prayer, not to change God's mind, but to transform ourselves and enlarge our soul's capacity for spirit receptivity.

name: unknown

name: unwritten law

(94:2.6-7) During the compilation of the later scriptures of Hindu faith, the Brahmanas and the Upanishads, the Brahmans sought true reality by deanthropomorphizing the Indian concept of deity, but inadvertently depersonalized the idea of God into an all-encompassing Absolute. (94:2.6)
improvedDescription: Later Hindu scriptures compiled along with the Brahmanas, representing the Brahmanic priesthood's effort to find true reality by deanthropomorphizing the Indian concept of deity, but inadvertently depersonalizing God into a distant, metaphysical Absolute.

name: Ur

(78:8.9-10) The idea of God was best preserved at Ur of Chaldea during the dark days in Palestine, where divine righteousness and salvation by faith were preached. (93:5.4) Zoroaster's dream at Ur inspired him to remodel his people's religion based on the concepts he learned there. (95:6.2) Jesus
improvedDescription: An ancient Mesopotamian city that prospered during flood times and became a pottery industry center about seven thousand years ago. The idea of God was best preserved at Ur of Chaldea during Palestine's dark days, where divine righteousness and salvation by faith were preached, influencing Abraham's family and later religious teachers like Zoroaster.

improvedDescription: A mountain range that began forming during the Carboniferous age along with the Vosges and Black Forest mountains.

improvedDescription: A waterway that connected the Arctic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea during the Ice Age, forming part of a larger network of water passages when much of Europe was submerged.

name: uranium

improvedDescription: A heavy radioactive element that spontaneously disrupts due to the independence and free movement of its outermost group of electrons. The lead produced by uranium disintegration reveals the actual loss of energy in atomic breakup, indicating the existence of pre-electronic forms of matter.

name: Urantia

The number as a physical sphere is of an extraordinary magnitude and we are not offered (15:14.8) current status celestial government (114) history (57) (114) (121) | geologic ages; planetary epochs mortals of Urantia | mortals physical aspects (57) problems the future
improvedDescription: The name of our planet in the universe, designated as world number 606 in the Satania system of Norlatiadek constellation in the local universe of Nebadon. Urantia is an experimental decimal planet that has experienced unusual difficulties including planetary rebellion and Adamic default, but was honored by Michael's terminal bestowal as Jesus of Nazareth.

name: Urantia advisory council
improvedDescription: An administrative body that provides counsel and guidance regarding planetary affairs on Urantia.

name: Urantia Brotherhood of Urantia Midwayers
improvedDescription: An organization of the midway creatures serving on Urantia, functioning as intermediary beings between material and spiritual realms.

improvedDescription: A collection of 196 revelatory papers constituting the most recent presentation of truth to the mortals of Urantia, authored by various celestial personalities and presented as a composite revelation from many beings rather than a single universe personality.

name: Uranus
originalDescription: retrograde motion proves Uranus was originally satellite of Angona (57:5.13-14)
improvedDescription: One of three major planets originally from the Angona system that were captured by our solar system, with its retrograde motion proving it was once a satellite of the dark giant Angona before being gravitationally detached and incorporated into our solar family.

name: Uriah the Hittite
originalDescription: David sought to build himself up politically by strategically marrying multiple women, including Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah the Hittite, before committing a series of atrocities that culminated in Uriah's murder. (97:9.10,15)
improvedDescription: A Hittite soldier whose wife Bathsheba was taken by King David, leading to a series of political atrocities that culminated in Uriah's murder as David's corrupt political machine gained power in violation of Hebrew customs.

name: urine
originalDescription: Urine and blood, believed to be magical, were among the earliest medicines, soon supplemented by roots and salts, as fasting, dieting, and foul-smelling remedies were used to drive out disease spirits. (90:4.8)
improvedDescription: Along with blood, one of the earliest medicines used by primitive peoples who believed these human secretions possessed magical properties that could drive out disease spirits when combined with foul-smelling remedies.

name: Urmia
originalDescription: On the return from the Caspian region, Jesus gave up the direction of the caravan at Urmia and journeyed on to Capernaum, where he made his new home with the Zebedees. (134:2.5) Jesus rested in the old Persian city of Urmia on Lake Urmia on his way to the Caspian Sea. (134:3.1) school at Urmia
improvedDescription: An ancient Persian city on the western shores of Lake Urmia where Jesus stopped during his caravan journey to the Caspian Sea, delivering systematic lectures on the brotherhood of men at a unique school of religions founded by the wealthy merchant Cymboyton.

name: Usatia
originalDescription: Usatia, chief of Orvonton Census Directors, serves as a magnificent totaling personality for his subordinates stationed in the Orvonton universes. improvedDescription: The superuniverse chief of all Census Directors in Orvonton, serving as a magnificent totaling personality who coordinates reports from his subordinates stationed throughout the local universes rather than directly registering intelligent will.

name: usury
originalDescription: Never employ money-power to gain unfair advantage over your struggling fellows; honest wealth is entitled to interest, but cleanse your capital before claiming it. (132:5.19)
improvedDescription: The practice of charging excessive interest on loans, which Jesus condemned as an unfair use of money-power to exploit struggling individuals in financial distress.

name: Uversa
originalDescription: Uversa oversees one trillion inhabited or inhabitable worlds from its spiritual and administrative headquarters, surpassing all wonders of time-space creations. (15:7.11) Ancients of Days dwell in personal abodes at the point of spiritual polarity on their headquarters sphere. (18:3.6) The commission hails from Uversa, the capital of Orvonton, our superuniverse. (0:0.5) (15:7.10-11) A day on Uversa is equal to 30 Urantia days, while a year is equivalent to 3000 Urantia days. (15:8.1) Uversa language location inhabitants population government
improvedDescription: The spiritual and administrative headquarters of the seventh superuniverse Orvonton, governing approximately one trillion inhabited worlds and serving as the capital from which celestial administrators oversee vast cosmic territories with glory and perfection surpassing all time-space creations.

name: Uzziah
originalDescription: Uzziah, also known as Azariah, became king of Judah after his father King Amaziah was assassinated, but ultimately led the nation into a period of decline and tribute payments to the Assyrian kings. (97:9.22)
improvedDescription: Also known as Azariah, a sixteen-year-old who became king of Judah after his father Amaziah was assassinated by rural rebels, but during his reign the kingdom declined and was forced to pay tribute to the Assyrian kings.

name: vacations
originalDescription: The apostles and their disciples experienced emotional tension, leading Jesus to suggest 3-day vacations on Mount Sartaba for rest and relaxation. (143:3.1) Jesus enjoyed the annual Jewish vacation time of the feast of tabernacles with John. (134:9.4) In the main continental nation of a nearby inhabited planet, vacation is a vital part of the adult-education program and is funded through methods similar to old-age insurance, with travel being a common way to spend the one month off each year. (72:4.3) (72:5.9)
improvedDescription: Periods of rest and recreation, such as the three-day retreats Jesus organized for his apostles on Mount Sartaba to relieve emotional tension, and systematic vacation programs on advanced worlds where citizens take annual month-long breaks for travel and adult education.

name: vale of tears
originalDescription: Jesus hardly regarded this world as a vale of tears, but as the birth sphere of immortal spirits on the path to eternal achievement and divine validation through fusion with the Universal Father. (40:7.5) (149:5.5) (196:2.9)
improvedDescription: A traditional religious concept describing earthly life as sorrowful and filled with suffering. Jesus rejected this pessimistic view, instead regarding the world as a sphere where immortal spirits are born and begin their eternal journey to Paradise.

name: values
originalDescription: The Eternal Son, holding all spirit realities and values in his hand, exercises absolute spiritual sovereignty over the spiritual domains. (7:1.1) All spiritual values are duplicated and retained by the Reflective Spirits and their seconaphim offspring. (17:3.5) Religion is primarily a pursuit of values, making it easier to agree on values than beliefs or creeds. (103:1.4) Physical health, temperament, and social environment influence religious experience, but inner spiritual progress thrives when one maintains wholehearted devotion to supreme values. (100:1.6,8) Every meaningful value in every will creature is certain of survival, as nothing of survival values is ever lost, even in the wide universe. (109:3.2) (109:4.5) (109:6.1-2) The persistence of true values ensures that no worth-while experience is ever wasted, as every meaningful value in every will creature is certain of survival. (109:6) Primitive peoples displayed noble values through their touching affection for comrades, valiantly fighting to protect and save injured warriors in battles with inferior tribes, foreshadowing highly human traits of subsequent evolutionary development. (63:4.4) Van achieved the highest level of personality realization by combining intellectual determination and spiritual insight, showing that mind and spirit united can create superhuman values. (67:3.9) Jesus taught Angamon that true values must be sought in the spiritual world and divine levels, surpassing transient material standards, as the pursuit of moral insight and spiritual attainment is crucial for civilization's advancement. (132:1) Evolutionary religion may be psychologized, but only spiritual origin religion can truly conserve, exalt, and spiritualize values, revealing the all-loving Father to be worshiped and loved. (102:5.3) nature of values warnings
improvedDescription: Spiritual and moral realities that represent worth, meaning, and quality in existence. Values are primarily spiritual in nature, are retained by divine beings, and form the foundation of religious experience. All meaningful values in will creatures are certain of survival, regardless of the survival of the personality that discovers them.

name: Van (lake)
improvedDescription: A lake referenced in The Urantia Book, likely corresponding to the geographical Lake Van in eastern Turkey.

name: Van (Prince’s staff)
originalDescription: Caligastia times the rebellion after rebellion Adam and Eve arrival current status
improvedDescription: A loyal member of Caligastia's staff who served as chairman of the supreme council of coordination. Van remained steadfast during the Caligastia rebellion, leading the loyal forces for over 150,000 years until Adam and Eve's arrival, demonstrating exceptional spiritual insight and unwavering dedication to the universe government.

name: vanadium
originalDescription: The sea squirt oxygenates blood with vanadium, illustrating the adaptability of evolving organisms in their functions. (65:6.4)
improvedDescription: A metallic element used by sea squirts to oxygenate their blood, demonstrating how evolving organisms can adapt their biological functions using different materials to serve the same purpose as iron does in higher animals.

name: Vanites
originalDescription: The Vanites, descendants of Nodites and Amadonites who left Nod for Van, arose before the Bablot conflict. (77:4.10)
improvedDescription: A group descended from Nodites and Amadonites who forsook the leadership of Nod to follow Van and Amadon. They emerged as a distinct people prior to the Bablot conflict in the northern regions.

name: vanity
originalDescription: Vanity and fear, particularly ghost fear, were influential forces that drove primitive human beings into society, along with food hunger and sex love. (68:2.4,6) Vanity was an early cause of war, driven by the desire to exhibit tribal prowess and impose superior ways of life on inferior groups. (70:1.10) Early humans exhibited considerable vanity in addition to admiration for objects and other beings, marking a significant advance in emotional development towards worshipful feelings like awe, reverence, humility, and gratitude as the foundation of primitive religion. (62:5.4) Wearing extra clothing was an early badge of distinction, appealing to the pride of man in displaying property accumulations. (69:5.6) Vanity, pride, ambition, and honor hold men together and contribute to the formation of human associations, as they are futile without an audience to parade before. (68:2.10) Refrain from oppression, condemnation, and vanity to fulfill your purpose and bring restoration to the world. (147:8.4) Secrecy appeals to vanity, as it bestowed a sense of mystery and social aristocracy upon the members of these societies. (70:7.7) Vanity threatens to swamp present-day civilization, as unbridled self-gratification replaces legitimate social aims of self-maintenance. (68:2.11)
improvedDescription: An early emotional drive that contributed to human socialization and the formation of societies. While vanity helped bring people together and motivated achievements, excessive vanity threatens civilization by replacing legitimate social aims with selfish gratification.

name: variety
originalDescription: Variety is essential to the concept of beauty, encompassing the drama of unifying contrasts between Creator and creature, achieving the apex of cosmic art. (56:10.3) Variety is indispensable for the wide functioning of natural selection and the differential survival of superior strains. (64:6.31) Taking yourself too seriously decreases the likelihood of success; variety is restful, monotony wears and exhausts. (48:6.37)
improvedDescription: An essential element for beauty, natural selection, and balanced living. Variety enables the unification of contrasts that creates cosmic beauty, provides opportunities for evolutionary advancement, and prevents the exhaustion that comes from monotony.

name: Vedas
improvedDescription: Ancient Hindu sacred texts and scriptures that form part of the religious tradition of India.

name: Vedic priesthood
improvedDescription: The priestly class associated with the Vedic religious traditions of ancient India, connected to the development of Brahmanism.

name: vegetables
improvedDescription: Plant foods including herbs and vegetables that became part of human diet, first consumed by Adam and Eve on their way to Mesopotamia after leaving the first garden.

name: vegetarianism
originalDescription: Adam, Eve, and their first generation children practiced a vegetarian diet, abstaining from consuming animal flesh and opting for fruits, nuts, cereals, and vegetables instead. (74:6.3) (76:3.7) (76:4.4) The combination of a vegetable and meat diet marked a significant improvement in the health of ancient peoples. (81:1.8) Many Adamites were nonflesh eaters, but over time their descendants typically gravitated towards omnivorous diets, resulting in varied anatomic blends reflecting their herbivorous and carnivorous origins. (52:3.8-9) The practice of vegetarianism dates back to the times of the Caligastia 100, influencing the eating habits of many surrounding tribes and groups. (66:4.7)
improvedDescription: A nonflesh diet practiced by Adam, Eve, and their first generation children, who subsisted wholly on fruits, cereals, and nuts. This dietary practice originated with the Caligastia 100 and spread to influence many surrounding tribes, though most descendants eventually became omnivorous.

name: veils
originalDescription: Veils protected women from the evil eye, a widespread fear that influenced behaviors and beliefs. (87:5.4)
improvedDescription: Coverings worn by women to protect them from the evil eye, a widespread superstitious fear that led to the practice of veiling pretty women and eventually became adopted by many women who desired to be considered beautiful.

name: velocities
originalDescription: Transit personalities can utilize all three universally distributed lines of energy, each with a velocity of 186,280 miles per second, to attain varying speeds on journeys exceeding 559,000 miles per second, affected by mass, proximity of matter, and universe power circuits. (39:3.9) Adjuster transit time from Divinington to Urantia is 117 hours, 42 minutes, and 7 seconds, with practically no time intervening between dispatch and appearance in chosen subjects' minds. (108:1.9) Divine Counselor traveled 200,000 light years in 109 days, while our order of creation intercommunicates instantly across vast distances within the superuniverse of Orvonton. (19:7.5) (32:2.11) It is impossible to accurately determine, simultaneously, the exact location and velocity of a moving object; any attempt at measurement of either inevitably involves change in the other. (65:6.1) The seraphic transports achieve speeds reaching almost 559,000 miles per second by superimposing energy and power velocities, utilizing universal lines of energy. (39:3.9) Our astronomic estimates of velocities within our superuniverse and its associate realms are reliable, but become wholly unreliable when applied to the outer realms of outer space due to various factors of error and distortions. (12:4.14-15) top speed
improvedDescription: Various speeds achieved by different classes of beings in universe travel, ranging from 186,280 miles per second for basic energy lines to nearly 559,000 miles per second for seraphic transports, with instantaneous travel possible for certain gravity-traversing spirit personalities.

name: Veluntia
originalDescription: Veluntia's massive sun, one of the largest in Orvonton, has a density one one-thousandth that of Urantia's atmosphere. (41:4.6)
improvedDescription: A location with a massive sun that is one of the largest in the superuniverse of Orvonton, having a density only one one-thousandth that of Urantia's atmosphere.

name: venom
originalDescription: Magical charms were concocted from venom, human flesh, tiger claws, crocodile teeth, poison plant seeds, snake venom, human hair, bones of the dead, dust from footprints, and bodily secretions to ensure love charms. (88:5.2)
improvedDescription: Snake venom was one of many substances used by ancient peoples in creating magical charms, along with human flesh, tiger claws, crocodile teeth, and other materials believed to possess supernatural powers.

name: ventriloquism
originalDescription: Shrewd shamans employed ventriloquism to mystify tribesmen and perform supernatural feats. (90:1.4)
improvedDescription: A technique first used by shrewd shamans and priests to mystify and impress tribesmen, making them believe they were witnessing supernatural phenomena through this vocal deception.

name: Venus (god)
originalDescription: Roman religion, greatly influenced by extensive cultural importations from Greece, saw Aphrodite transplanted to Rome as Venus. (98:3.3)
improvedDescription: The Roman goddess of love, originally the Greek goddess Aphrodite who was transplanted to Rome as Venus when Roman religion was greatly influenced by extensive cultural importations from Greece.

name: Venus (planet)
originalDescription: Mortals living on Venus would be classified as superbreathers, according to the breathing order of intelligent creatures as explained in the paragraph. (49:2.13) The retrograde motion of Venus proves that it was originally a satellite of Angona. (57:5.13-14) Only three planets in your solar system are suitable for life, with Venus not being one of them. (15:6.15)
improvedDescription: A planet in the solar system with a thick atmosphere that would classify any potential inhabitants as superbreathers. Venus exhibits retrograde motion, proving it was originally a satellite of the visiting system Angona.

name: verdict
originalDescription: The eternal verdict is: “Well done, good and faithful servant”. (25:1.6)
improvedDescription: The eternal judgment pronounced by the Supreme Judge that declares "Well done, good and faithful servant" based not on material successes but on faithful performance of divine duty.

name: Vergil
originalDescription: Vergil's Stoic appeal to “nature and conscience” paved the way for Rome to embrace the laws of God through Christianity. (195:2.6)
improvedDescription: Vergil was a Roman poet whose Stoic appeal to nature and conscience helped prepare Rome intellectually to receive Christianity and the laws of God.

name: Veronica of Caesarea Philippi
originalDescription: Veronica of Caesarea Philippi was the woman that touched Jesus clothes when he was surrounded by the crowd, but in fact she was healed by her faith, not by touching the hem of Jesus' garment. (152:0.2-3) (171:7.8)
improvedDescription: Veronica of Caesarea Philippi was a woman who suffered from a hemorrhage for years and was healed when she touched the hem of Jesus' garment while he passed through a crowd. Jesus clarified that it was her faith, not the physical touch, that made her whole.

name: vertebrates
improvedDescription: Animals with backbones or spinal columns, representing a major classification of life forms.

name: Vesta
originalDescription: Roman Vesta was inspired by the Greek goddess Hestia, reflecting the cultural exchange between the two civilizations. (98:3.3)
improvedDescription: Vesta was the Roman goddess of the home and hearth, derived from the Greek goddess Hestia as part of the cultural exchange between Greece and Rome.

name: veto
originalDescription: The Universal Father, with divine selflessness, relinquishes authority, delegates power but always remains primal, holding the all-powerful veto scepter over the destiny of creation. (3:6.1) In a settled system, the system conclave, subject to the veto of the Constellation Fathers, becomes supreme in governing the self-governing inhabited worlds. (55:8.1) The Most High observer wields veto power over the planetary government's administration, making the description of Urantia's government complex and unique. (114:5.1)
improvedDescription: A power of rejection or prohibition. The Universal Father wields an all-powerful veto over creation's destiny, while in settled systems the Constellation Fathers hold veto power over system conclaves, and on Urantia the Most High observer has veto authority over planetary government.

name: Vevona
originalDescription: Vevona, as chief of the souls of peace, proclaimed Jesus' birth as a planetary helper seraphim. (39:5.5)
improvedDescription: Vevona is chief of the souls of peace, a type of seraphim who remained on Urantia from Adam's day and later heralded Jesus' birth to the worlds as leader of the angelic host.

name: Vicegerington
originalDescription: Vicegerington is the secret sphere where beings originate from the Father and the Son, a Paradise home for glorified beings of diverse ancestry. This planet is the “bosom of the Father and the Son” and is the secret sphere of certain unrevealed beings who take origin by the acts of the Father and the Son. (13:1.11) The Corps of Conjoint Trinitized Finaliters on Vicegerington include Havona natives in their ranks. (31:1.5) The creature-trinitized beings held in reserve on Vicegerington embody unrevealed ideas and experiences of a future universe age, under the guardianship of the Architects of the Master Universe. (22:7.10) (23:4.3) Vicegerington is the secret home of beings originating in the Father and Eternal Son, where glorified beings of complex ancestry gather from throughout the seven superuniverses. (13:1.11) Solitary Messengers accompany creature-trinitized sons to Vicegerington and never leave, under the sole supervision of the Architects of the Master Universe. (23:4.3) The secret of trinitization is the key to the authority of Vicegerington. (13:1.12) (22:7.1)
improvedDescription: Vicegerington is one of the sacred spheres of Paradise, known as the "bosom of the Father and the Son," where beings who originate from acts of the Father and Son reside. It is the secret sphere containing the mysteries of trinitization and serves as home to glorified beings of complex ancestry and creature-trinitized sons held in reserve for future universe ages.

name: vices
originalDescription: Jesus had little to say about social vices, preferring to teach true virtue instead of focusing on moral delinquency. (140:8.21) The Master warned against extremes, as any virtue pushed too far can morph into a vice. (149:4.3)
improvedDescription: Moral failings or bad habits. Jesus taught that virtues carried to extremes can become vices, and he focused on teaching positive virtue rather than condemning social vices or moral delinquency.

name: vicissitudes
improvedDescription: The natural changes, variations, and ups and downs that occur in life and experience.

name: victory
originalDescription: The race for perfection toward the goal of eternity is an adventure of divinity attainment, where certain victory crowns the efforts of all who run the race of faith. (32:5.8) Jesus taught victory through sacrifice of pride and selfishness, promoting mercy, forgiveness, and spiritual deliverance from anger and revenge. (141:3.8) Faith maintains divine trust in ultimate victory amidst cruel fate and indifferent natural forces. (101:3.10) Victory on earth is achieved through the unwavering faith that there is a higher power within us capable of overcoming any obstacle. (4:4.9)
improvedDescription: Ultimate triumph achieved through faith and divine partnership. True victory comes through sacrifice of pride and selfishness, maintaining trust in divine power despite apparent obstacles, and recognizing that the indwelling spirit can overcome human limitations.

name: viewpoints
improvedDescription: Perspectives or ways of looking at and understanding various subjects or situations.

name: Vilton
originalDescription: Vilton, a local system recorder, was tertiary Lanonandek #374 and a member of the original Lanaforge group. (45:3.6)
improvedDescription: Vilton is a local system recorder, tertiary Lanonandek number 374 and secretary of the Lanonandek ministry of Satania, who was a member of the original Lanaforge group.

name: vine and branches
originalDescription: The parable of the vine and branches teaches that believers must abide in Jesus, bearing much fruit of the spirit to experience the supreme joy of yielding a spiritual harvest. (180:2) (193:2.2)
improvedDescription: The parable of the vine and branches teaches that believers must abide in Jesus to bear much spiritual fruit and experience the supreme joy of spiritual harvest. Jesus declares himself the true vine with believers as the living branches who can only be fruitful through maintaining their spiritual connection with him.

name: vinegar
originalDescription: Jesus' final request was for vinegar to quench his thirst. (187:5.3)
improvedDescription: Jesus' final mortal request was for vinegar, a sour wine commonly used in those days, to quench his thirst during crucifixion.

name: vineyard
originalDescription: The parable of laborers in the vineyard teaches that the kingdom of heaven is characterized by fairness, generosity, and mercy. (163:3.5-7)
improvedDescription: The parable of laborers in the vineyard teaches that the kingdom of heaven is characterized by divine fairness, generosity, and mercy, where God's grace is given equally regardless of when one enters his service.

name: violence
originalDescription: Violence is the law of nature, war the natural state of evolving man. (70:1.1) Society secures freedom from violence through police regulation. (81:5.5) Violence has been outlawed as a procedure in adjusting personal or industrial differences, with new techniques developed for the correction of economic abuses. (72:5.3) True religion opposes violence as a technique of social evolution, promoting nonviolence and peaceful evolution instead of violent revolution or supporting established political and economic orders. (99:0.2) (99:2.5) Unregulated competition breeds violence and determines individual's place in industry. (71:5.1)
improvedDescription: Violence is described as the natural law of evolving man and the automatic reaction of primitive humanity. While society secures freedom from violence through police regulation and legal systems, true religion opposes violence as a technique of social evolution, promoting nonviolence and peaceful development instead.

name: violet race
improvedDescription: A racial designation associated with the Adamites and their descendants.

name: vipers
originalDescription: John the Baptist accurately referred to religious leaders as the offspring of vipers, condemning their actions. (175:1.21)
improvedDescription: John the Baptist accurately referred to hypocritical religious leaders as the offspring of vipers, condemning their evil actions and character.

name: virgin birth doctrine
originalDescription: Christianity intertwined the miraculous birth of Mithras with the legends of Jesus' birth. (98:7.7) Jesus of Nazareth, the Jewish baby, was conceived and born just like all other babies, except for the mystery of the incarnation of the divine Son of Paradise within him. (119:7.5,6) (122:8.2) (128:0.1) Leaders of various races, including Buddha, Mohammed, and Genghis Khan, were believed to be born of virgins, with their return eagerly awaited by their followers. (92:5.6) The sun god fathered virgin-born saviors who were destined to deliver their people after being rescued on sacred rivers. (85:5.3)
improvedDescription: The belief that divine or heroic figures are born of virgins, which became intertwined with Christian legends about Jesus' birth through influence from mystery religions like Mithraism. However, Jesus was conceived and born naturally like all other babies, with the mystery being the incarnation of the divine Son of Paradise within him.

name: virgins
originalDescription: In ancient times, a girl's desirability as a wife among primitives was increased by bearing a child before marriage, showing the shift in societal values towards chastity and fidelity. (82:3.13) Men have long regarded women as peculiar, even abnormal, believing women did not have souls and denying them names, leading to the custom for priests to have initial intercourse with virgins due to the fear of the first sex relation with a woman. (84:4.5) Virgins were dedicated to tending sacred temple fires, symbolizing the purifying spirit of their venerated deities. (85:4.4) (89:8.1) Primitives restricted bathing practices of virgins due to beliefs about childbirth and spirits. (84:1.3-4) Religious leaders conceived as being born of virgins often have careers marked by miraculous events, with their return eagerly anticipated by their followers. (92:5.6) Virgin daughters were valued as commercial assets by fathers, leading to the enforcement of chastity norms and the emergence of professional prostitute classes. (82:4.5)
improvedDescription: In ancient cultures, virgins held special significance in religious practices, often serving as temple attendants tending sacred fires. Societal attitudes toward virginity evolved from early times when bearing children before marriage increased a woman's desirability, to later periods where chastity became highly valued and commercially important in marriage arrangements.

name: virtue
originalDescription: Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle taught that virtue is knowledge, with cardinal virtues including wisdom, courage, temperance, and justice, while Xenophanes and Anaxagoras emphasized monotheism and a First Cause. (98:2.6) The Master warned against the dangers of extremism, emphasizing the necessity of balance and proportion in one's virtues. (149:4.3) Virtue is the consistent choosing of good over evil, influenced by moral nature, ignorance, and a sense of proportion in the attainment of cosmic achievement. (16:7.6-7) Virtue is volitional, and righteousness is not automatic in freewill creatures, as a Creator Son is eternally pledged to uphold, protect, defend, and retrieve his personal creation if ever challenged. (21:3.14) Morality is the indigenous virtue of human personality. (16:7.1) | morality Ambition is dangerous until fully socialized, virtue is not truly acquired until acts make one worthy. (48:7.19) The exercise of virtue requires a sense of proportion in distinguishing between good and evil amidst influences like ignorance, immaturity, and delusion. (16:7.7) Virtue is the consistent choosing of good over evil in the day-by-day life of mortal man, a demonstration of conformity with the cosmos and progression in cosmic achievement. (16:7.6) Wholeheartedly doing the will of the Father in heaven is the epitome of supreme virtue. (16:7.10)
improvedDescription: Virtue is the consistent choosing of good over evil through the daily exercise of moral will, representing righteousness and conformity with cosmic purpose. It requires a sense of proportion in distinguishing between good and evil, and is influenced by moral nature, wisdom, and spiritual insight. Supreme virtue is wholeheartedly choosing to do the Father's will in heaven.

name: viruses
originalDescription: Most disease-causing bacteria and their auxiliary viruses are renegade parasitic fungi, showing a retrograde movement in evolution. (65:2.3)
improvedDescription: Most disease-causing bacteria and their auxiliary viruses are renegade parasitic fungi that represent a retrograde movement in evolution.

name: Vishnu
originalDescription: Hinduism's survival is due to its integral role in Indian society, with its adaptability and tolerance extending to other religions like Buddhism and Christianity, claiming both Buddha and Christ as incarnations of Vishnu. (94:4.9) Vishnu is a member of the Hindu trinitarian concept, alongside Brahma and Siva. (104:1.5) The worship of animals, symbolizing forgotten gods, was influenced by a misinterpretation of the golden rule, as seen with Vishnu often shown with a horse's head. (85:3.4) Vishnu, a periodically incarnating Hindu deity, is believed to become real and living in the imaginations of Indians, alongside the lord of life and death, Siva. (94:4.5)
improvedDescription: Vishnu is one of the three primary deities in the Hindu trinitarian concept, alongside Brahma and Siva. He is extremely popular due to the belief that he periodically incarnates in human form, making him real and living in the imaginations of Indians. Hinduism claims both Buddha and Christ as incarnations of Vishnu, demonstrating its tolerant adaptability to other religions.

name: vision (sight)
originalDescription: With exclusive spirit vision, celestial beings cannot perceive material buildings, but can discern the reality of both spirit and material beings. (44:0.16-18) Through energy transformers, mortals can momentarily glimpse celestial beings like angels, who are of spirit nature and origin, appreciating human efforts and loving mankind. (38:2.1) (45:1.2) (47:1.2) (50:2.7) (158:1.8) (189:4.11) (191:3.2) After being delivered from the limited vision of your material eyes, you will have an enormous range of morontia vision to see your spiritual associates of lower orders. (9:8.13) (24:6.2) (39:4.18) (46:5.10) With a range of material and spiritual vision, beings can perceive both the physical and metaphysical worlds simultaneously. (44:0.16-18) The extended vision of mortal beings in the era of light and life allows them to see the semimaterial progeny created by the Seven Master Spirits, previously invisible to short-range sight. (29:1.1) (55:4.8) (55:6.3) Although imperceptible to human senses, morontia temples are real and visible to morontial beings. (44:3.9)
improvedDescription: Vision ranges from material sight limited to physical objects to extended morontia and spirit vision capable of perceiving both material and spiritual realities. Celestial beings with exclusive spirit vision cannot see material buildings but can discern spirit counterparts, while ascending mortals gain progressively expanded vision ranges as they advance through morontia and spirit levels.

name: visions
originalDescription: Heed the indwelling Adjuster's call to your soul, allowing it to constantly bring forth visions of eternal purpose amidst the uphill struggle of life's challenges. (111:7.2-3) Regarding visions as divine communications, one should postulate a superconscious level of ascending intellectual activity as the zone of immediate contact with the indwelling spirit entity. (100:5.6) The shamanesses threw themselves into trances to communicate with the ghosts of the dead and become powerful prophets and mediums through their cataleptic fits. (90:1.3) Avoid cultivating a trancelike state of visionary consciousness as a religious practice to prevent reality avoidance and potential danger. (100:5.8) Human beings sometimes glimpse seraphic transporters, which have been influential in shaping the traditional idea of angels possessing wings. (39:5.12) Jesus brought to God the greatest of all offerings: the consecration of his own will to doing the divine will, always interpreting religion in terms of the Father's will and living a life of prayerful consecration, not relying on visions or extraordinary religious practices. (196:0.10) Kirmeth of Bagdad, the trance prophet, arrived at Bethsaida and caused a disturbance with his peculiar visions and dreams before Jesus allowed him to preach freely, ultimately leading only a few unstable souls back to Bagdad. (148:8.3) particular visions dangers
improvedDescription: Visions should be carefully evaluated as potential divine communications, with genuine spiritual visions distinguished from mystic trances, dreams, or emotional experiences. True prophetic vision occurs at a superconscious level of contact with the indwelling spirit entity, while cultivating trancelike states as religious practice can become dangerous reality avoidance.

name: visiting
originalDescription: In order to maintain wholehearted devotion to preaching the gospel, evangelists must avoid overmuch social visiting and other trivialities that could lead to a loss of time. (163:4.5)
improvedDescription: Evangelists should avoid excessive social visiting and trivial activities that could detract from wholehearted devotion to preaching the gospel and lead to loss of time.

name: vitamins
originalDescription: Sunlight plays a crucial role in providing essential vitamins through the protection of the ozone layer. (58:2.2)
improvedDescription: Essential vitamins are produced through the health-giving ultraviolet rays from sunlight that reach Earth's surface, protected by the ozone layer which filters out dangerous radiation while allowing beneficial rays to pass through.

name: vocabulary
originalDescription: True character development is not solely achieved through expanding one's vocabulary, but rather through a genuine and deepening understanding of values and ideals as well as personal growth in one's religious experience. (100:1.3)
improvedDescription: True character development is not achieved merely through expanding vocabulary, but rather through genuine understanding of values and ideals, along with personal growth in religious experience and spiritual progress.

name: vocation builders
originalDescription: Divine builders and celestial artisans design and assemble the abodes of spirit and morontia workers, enhancing both workshops and worker vocations. (44:3.3)
improvedDescription: Vocation builders are celestial artisans who design and assemble the abodes and workshops of spirit and morontia workers, contributing to both better working environments and the vocational enhancement of the workers themselves.

name: voices
originalDescription: Jesus' ultimate offering to God was the consecration of his own will to divine service, consistently interpreting religion in terms of the Father's will, demonstrating a life of prayerful devotion rather than relying on religious voices, visions, or practices. (196:0.10) Jesus' musical voice was both fascinating and authoritative, captivating all who heard it. (125:5.1) (127:1.2) (154:6.5) (162:6.2) (183:3.5) (190:2.3)
improvedDescription: Jesus possessed a musical, fascinating, and authoritative voice that captivated all who heard it. In his religious life, he achieved communion with God through intelligent prayer and sincere worship rather than relying on supernatural voices, visions, or extraordinary religious practices.

name: Voices of Angelic Hosts
originalDescription: The primary seconaphim, representing the voices of the angelic hosts, provide insightful counsel to the Ancients of Days. (28:4.11)
improvedDescription: The primary seconaphim, representing the voices of the angelic hosts, provide insightful counsel to the Ancients of Days.

name: Voices of Conjoint Actor
originalDescription: The primary seconaphim serve as the personal representatives of the Infinite Spirit on the superuniverse headquarters. (28:4.4)
improvedDescription: The primary seconaphim who serve as the personal representatives of the Infinite Spirit on the superuniverse headquarters, being the nearest equivalent to a specialized personalization of the Third Source and Center.

name: Voices of Creator Sons
originalDescription: Spirit-fused mortals and Son-fused ascenders, as children of Reflective Spirits, embody the intricate connection between the local and superuniverse levels of God the Sevenfold. (40:10.2) The primary seconaphim of the Creator Sons possess the remarkable gift of reflecting the minds of these Master Sons, allowing the Ancients of Days to perceive their voice unfailingly. (28:4.6-10)
improvedDescription: Primary seconaphim who possess the remarkable gift of reflecting the minds of the Creator Sons, allowing the Ancients of Days to perceive their voice unfailingly and thus know the attitude of Michael regarding any matter under consideration.

name: Voices of Mercy
originalDescription: The mission of Voices of Mercy is to ensure that unerring justice is delivered with generous mercy, transitioning from justice guides in lower realms to higher spheres and on Salvington. (39:4.5) Superior seraphim foster holier emotions of men and angels through the Voice of Mercy. (39:2.5)
improvedDescription: Superior seraphim who ensure that unerring justice is delivered with generous mercy, often appearing before referee trios and serving as mercy ministers who foster higher impulses and holier emotions of men and angels.

name: Voices of Seven Master Spirits
originalDescription: The primary seconaphim represent the collective natures and reactions of the Seven Master Spirits. (28:4.5)
improvedDescription: Primary seconaphim who represent the collective natures and reactions of the Seven Master Spirits before the Ancients of Days, providing collective rather than individual representation.

name: voices of the Garden
originalDescription: The personal seraphim of Adam and Eve, known as the “Voices of the Garden,” serve as planetary helpers in the evolution of human races. (39:5.3) Solonia reproved Adam and Eve for disobedience in the Garden, highlighting the confusion of Urantians in attributing supernatural events to the Universal Father. (51:3.5) (73:7.5) (74:8.15) (75:4.2,8) (75:8.8) (76:6.5)
improvedDescription: The personal seraphim of Adam and Eve, known as the voices of the Garden, who serve as planetary helpers in the evolution of human races and accompany Planetary Adams on their world missions.

name: Voices of Wisdom
originalDescription: The seconaphim in perpetual liaison with living libraries of Paradise transmit the essence of wisdom to the Perfectors of Wisdom in specialized reflective service. (28:5.7-10)
improvedDescription: Seconaphim in perpetual liaison with the living libraries of Paradise who transmit the essence of wisdom to the Perfectors of Wisdom, serving as living concentrations of the coordinated wisdom of the universe of universes.

name: volcanoes
originalDescription: Volcanic action early equalized internal-heat pressure and crustal contraction, facilitating the cooling of the earth's crust and marking the beginning of the stabilization epoch 850 million years ago. (57:8.2,11,16) Volcanic activity on Urantia peaked during the age of volcanoes from 1.5 to 1 billion years ago. (57:7) (59:3.2,6) (59:5.20) (60:1.3) (60:3.1) (60:3.6) (61:4.1) 330 million years ago, the eruption of the volcanoes of eastern Kentucky was one of the greatest single volcanic activities the world has ever known. (59:2.5) Mount Royal, at Montreal, is the eroded neck of a volcano from a time when great volcanic activity occurred in the St. Lawrence region, 230 million years ago. (59:4.16) 80 million years ago, extensive volcanoes at the end of continental drift caused great disturbances in Earth's crust, initiating profound changes along the Pacific shores of Asia and giving rise to numerous isolated volcanic cones worldwide. (60:3.11,15) When lava cools, it forms granite with a density less than three times that of water, contrasted with the earth's core being twelve times denser than water. (58:5.5) Lava flows diversified earth's crust and insulated the planet, facilitating control of terrestrial energy. (57:8.18) Lava flows brought heavy metals from deep within the earth to the surface through ancient volcanoes. (58:5.2) Mount Horeb volcano's eruptions awed the Bedouins, leading them to fear Yahweh as the supreme god of the Hebrew Semites, in the belief that their God was “mighty, terrible, a devouring fire, fearful, and all-powerful.” (96:1.3,11) (96:4.5) Volcanoes became fetishes, not mountains, in early man's belief system. (88:1.1)
improvedDescription: Geological formations that played a crucial role in Earth's early development, equalizing internal heat pressure and crustal contraction while bringing heavy metals to the surface through lava flows and contributing to the planet's stabilization.

name: Volga
originalDescription: The earlier and purer strains of Andites migrated to northwestern Europe via the northern route through the grazing lands of the Volga, while later invasions arrived on horseback in three great waves. (80:4.1-2)
improvedDescription: A river region through which the earlier and purer strains of Andites migrated to northwestern Europe via the northern route through the grazing lands.

name: volunteers
originalDescription: In preparing the Garden of Eden, only volunteer laborers were utilized, without the need for hired workers, despite the challenges of the troubled world. (73:4.4) Seraphim always serve as destiny guardians, volunteering their services based on human needs and the status of the angelic pair. (113:2.3,7)
improvedDescription: Individuals who offer their services freely without compensation, including the volunteer laborers who prepared the Garden of Eden and seraphim who volunteer for assignments as destiny guardians based on human needs and angelic qualifications.

name: Volvox
originalDescription: Early single-celled animal types, like Volvox, evolved into communities such as Hydra and jellyfish, leading to the development of various other marine creatures that survived the evolutionary struggle. (65:2.5)
improvedDescription: Early single-celled animal types that evolved into communities, serving as a foundational step in the development of more complex marine creatures like Hydra and jellyfish.

name: voodoo doctors
originalDescription: Magic was practiced through the use of wands, incantations, and unclothed rituals, with women outnumbering men among primitive magicians, where “medicine” meant mystery, not treatment, as the voodoo doctors of the twentieth century are typical of magicians of old. (88:6.1)
improvedDescription: Twentieth-century practitioners of magic who are typical of ancient magicians, representing the continuation of primitive magical practices involving mystery rather than medical treatment.

name: Vorondadek Sons
originalDescription: The Most High observer of Urantia, a Vorondadek Son, asserted his authority and began emergency administration with a full staff of his choosing. (109:7.7) (114:3.5) (114:4) In the government of Norlatiadek, twelve Sons of the Vorondadek order administer and assist with administrative work during times of stress. (35:6.5) (43:5.2-15) Twelve Vorondadek Sons sit en banc as a high court of review for special cases concerning planetary status, with their broader focus on legislative functions in constellation governments. (35:5.7) Vorondadek Sons, created by Creator Son and Mother Spirit, are known as Constellation Fathers leading the governments in each local universe. (20:1.2-10) (35:5.1) The Vorondadek Sons, although cited for error on occasion, have never fallen into rebellion. (35:5.4) (43:3.1) The Vorondadek Sons serve as historians of local universes, overseeing high court cases and legislative functions within constellation governments. (35:5.7) The Vorondadek Sons are in charge of the 2nd Salvington circuit, where ascending mortals undergo their highest level of education in universe legislation. (35:7) In emergencies, the Vorondadek Sons exercise absolute authority over the planet's celestial beings on isolated worlds in spiritual darkness. (35:5.6) Vorondadek Sons exhibit unique characteristics in personality registers. (30:1.36) (30:2.43) The government of Norlatiadek includes twelve Sons of the Vorondadek order, with number 617,318 serving as the current Most High ruler. (43:5) known as Most Highs when commissioned as Constellation Fathers, they have a significant role in guiding the architectural headquarters spheres of constellations, presided over by three Vorondadek Sons, known as the Most Highs. (15:2.4) (20:1.10) (35:5.1) (35:6.1) (43:3.1-2) Vorondadek Sons observe quarantined planets, exercising absolute authority in emergencies and sometimes serving as regents even on non-rebellious worlds. (35:5.6) (43:5.8) Despite their inability to reproduce, the number of Vorondadek Sons in Nebadon remains fixed at one million. (35:5.2) The recorded number of Vorondadek Sons in Nebadon is one million, with no ability to increase their ranks. (35:5.2) Personalized Adjusters confer with Vorondadek rulers on the headquarters worlds of the constellations. (109:7.6) The Vorondadek Sons are reliable, efficient rulers with stability of purpose and divinity of judgment. (35:5.3) Vorondadek Sons reside in the fourth circle of Jerusem, where they are joined by other visiting observer Sons, Most High Constellation Fathers, Perfectors of Wisdom, Divine Counselors, and Universal Censors. (46:5.15) Vorondadek Sons serve as ambassadors and consuls, trusted with full sovereign powers in critical universe situations. (35:5.5) The work of the Vorondadek Sons pertains to legislative functions within the constellation governments, making them historians of the local universes. (35:5.7) The Vorondadek Sons, having wrote Paper 36, understand the limits of the Life Carriers' involvement in the evolution of moral creatures. (36:3.8)
improvedDescription: Local universe Sons of God created by the Creator Son and Creative Spirit, known as Constellation Fathers when commissioned as Most Highs to rule constellation governments. They serve as reliable administrators, historians of local universes, and observers on quarantined worlds, with exactly one million existing in Nebadon.

name: Vorondadek worlds
originalDescription: The Vorondadek planets, part of the second group of seven worlds surrounding Salvington, provide ascending mortals with unparalleled education on universe legislation under the guidance of senior Vorondadeks. (35:7) Ascenders educated in universe legislation on Vorondadek worlds achieve the acme of their training in the enactment of general legislation under the guidance of senior Vorondadeks. (35:7.1-2)
improvedDescription: Seven planets in the circuit of seventy primary spheres surrounding Salvington, each with six satellites, where ascending mortals receive the highest level of education in universe legislation under the guidance of senior Vorondadeks.

name: Vosges mountains
originalDescription: The Vosges mountains, along with the Black Forest and Ural mountains, began during this epoch, with remnants of older mountains scattered across Great Britain and Europe. (59:5.12)
improvedDescription: Mountain range that began forming during the Devonian epoch, along with the Black Forest and Ural mountains, as part of the geological development of Europe.

name: voting
improvedDescription: Democratic process referenced in the context of political sovereignty and governance among nation-states.

name: vows
originalDescription: The tragic and pathetic Hebrew narrative of Jephthah and his daughter illustrates the heart-tearing contention between ancient religious customs and advancing civilization, as men and women are addicted to making foolish vows. (89:6.3) Historically, vows evolved from self-denial pledges into contracts with gods, offering a form of exchange for self-torture and mortification. (89:3.5) Moses enabled his people to escape worst results of human sacrifices by establishing a system of ransom in place of their rash vows. (89:7.1)
improvedDescription: Sacred pledges made to gods that evolved from self-denial practices into contractual arrangements, often involving self-torture or sacrifice, with Moses later establishing a ransom system to help people escape the worst consequences of rash promises.

name: wakes for dead
originalDescription: Ancient ghost cult customs continue to influence wakes for the dead with noisy rituals and foul-smelling potions. (87:6.11)
improvedDescription: Funeral rituals continuing ancient ghost cult customs, featuring noisy ceremonies and foul-smelling concoctions believed to drive away disturbing spirits from the deceased.

name: Wales
originalDescription: The division of political sovereignty among nation-states leads to warfare, as seen in England, Scotland, and Wales before they reposed sovereignty in the United Kingdom. (134:6.3)
improvedDescription: A nation that, along with England and Scotland, engaged in constant warfare until they surrendered their individual sovereignties to form the United Kingdom, illustrating how political unification can end conflict.

name: walls
originalDescription: The protection afforded by ancient city walls against ferocious beasts and surprise attacks enabled progress in human dwellings and animal subjugation. (66:5.8)
improvedDescription: Protective barriers built around ancient cities to defend against ferocious beasts and surprise attacks, enabling human progress in dwelling construction and animal domestication.

name: walnuts
originalDescription: Walnuts were among the many present-day trees that first appeared during a period of great plant-life evolution, second in importance only to the appearance of man himself. (60:3.19)
improvedDescription: Walnuts were among the many present-day trees that first appeared during a period of great plant-life evolution, second in importance only to the appearance of man himself.

name: wampum belts
originalDescription: Writing progressed up through the stages of the “message stick,” knotted cords, picture writing, hieroglyphics, and wampum belts, to the early symbolic alphabets. (69:4.7)
improvedDescription: Wampum belts were one of the developmental stages in the evolution of writing systems, progressing from message sticks and knotted cords through picture writing and hieroglyphics to early symbolic alphabets.

name: wands
originalDescription: Magic practiced with wands was a common ritual among unclothed practitioners, with women outnumbering men in the ancient art. (88:6.1)
improvedDescription: Wands were ritual implements used by primitive magicians in their practice of magic, typically employed by unclothed practitioners, with women outnumbering men among ancient magicians.

name: war
originalDescription: War has always been a kingmaker, teaching mankind that neither peace nor conflict can be governed by committee. (70:5.5) benefits of early war causes of war detriments associated with war evolution of war
improvedDescription: War is the natural state and heritage of evolving man, serving as a kingmaker throughout history. While war has had evolutionary value in promoting organization, efficiency, and cultural exchange, it becomes increasingly destructive as civilization advances and must eventually be abandoned in favor of peaceful solutions to international conflicts.

name: warnings
originalDescription: Adam and Eve were warned of dire consequences for departure from the divine plan long before they left Jerusem. (75:7.4) Misunderstood warnings as unjust interference with personal freedoms led Lucifer and Caligastia astray. (66:8.2) A sojourner on the seventh mansion world has access to the Satania prison worlds, serving as a solemn warning against rebellion. (45:1.11) Warning only intensifies evil determination once love is dead. (179:4.8) Despite numerous warnings from the Master, divine guidance often proves ineffective in dealing with embittered human nature. (139:12.11)
improvedDescription: Warnings from divine sources, while well-intentioned, often prove ineffective when dealing with embittered human nature or those whose love has died. Adam and Eve were warned of consequences for departing from the divine plan, and Lucifer and Caligastia received loving warnings about their pride, but these were misunderstood as interference with personal liberty.

name: washing
originalDescription: Jesus washed the apostles' feet, teaching them the meaning of true greatness and service. (179:1.2,8) (179:3) Jesus washed only for cleanliness, disregarding the Pharisees' ceremonial practices. (166:1.2) (167:1.3) (179:3.1)
improvedDescription: Jesus washed the apostles' feet as a demonstration of true service and greatness, and practiced washing only for cleanliness rather than observing the ceremonial hand-washing rituals favored by the Pharisees.

name: wasps’ nests
originalDescription: Early people wrote on wasps' nests, under the direction of Fad, who formulated the first alphabet and introduced a writing system. (66:5.9)
improvedDescription: Wasps' nests provided crude paperlike writing material for early peoples under Fad's educational system, who also used tree barks, clay tablets, stone slabs, and hammered hide parchment for recording information.

name: watchful householder
originalDescription: The parable of the watchful householder teaches vigilance for the sudden and unexpected departure of the Son of Man. (165:5.6)
improvedDescription: The parable of the watchful householder teaches vigilance for the sudden and unexpected departure of the Son of Man, comparing spiritual preparedness to a homeowner guarding against thieves.

name: watchful servant
originalDescription: The parable of the watchful servant teaches us to be vigilant and prepared for the trials that lie ahead. (165:5.5)
improvedDescription: The parable of the watchful servant teaches disciples to remain vigilant and prepared for trials ahead, like servants keeping their lamps burning while waiting for their master's return from a feast.

name: watchtower
originalDescription: Building a watchtower without counting the cost could lead to unfinished work and mockery from neighbors. (171:2.3)
improvedDescription: A watchtower represents the importance of counting the cost before undertaking discipleship, as one would calculate expenses before building such a structure to avoid mockery from neighbors for leaving it unfinished.

name: water
originalDescription: Water is present on all evolutionary planets if not too small, shaping their physical aspects along with other determining factors. (41:10.3) The energy within one drop of water is equivalent to 100 horsepower for 2 years due to the vast number of atoms it contains. (41:7.13) Understanding the water molecule should have prevented the theory of materialism, as knowledge of the unique properties of water transcends the predictable additive consequences of its components, revealing a deeper truth beyond mere analysis. (12:9.3-4) Man achieves divine union through reciprocal communion and conformity to the divine will, not merely as a drop of water merging with the ocean. (1:7.2) worship Jesus superstitions regarding water water in heavens
improvedDescription: Water is a fundamental substance present on all evolutionary planets that are not too small, playing a crucial role in shaping their physical aspects. The energy contained within one drop of water equals 100 horsepower for two years due to the vast number of atoms it contains. Understanding water's unique properties should have prevented materialistic philosophy, as the union of hydrogen and oxygen creates something qualitatively different from the predictable sum of its parts.

name: water gate
originalDescription: In Jerusalem, on the last day of the feast, hundreds of priests and Levites officiated in a sacred ceremony involving myrtle, willow, and palm branches, as well as a golden pitcher of symbolic water, entering the temple through the water gate. (162:4.4)
improvedDescription: A gate in the Jerusalem temple through which the sacred procession entered after collecting symbolic water from the pool of Siloam during the Feast of Tabernacles. On the last day of the feast, priests carrying golden pitchers of water would enter through the water gate to perform the ritual pouring at the altar.

name: water hogs
originalDescription: During the last glaciation in North America, the horse, tapir, llama, and saber-toothed tiger became extinct, being replaced by sloths, armadillos, and water hogs that migrated from South America. (61:7.15)
improvedDescription: Large mammals that migrated from South America to North America during the last glaciation, replacing extinct species such as horses, tapirs, llamas, and saber-toothed tigers. They arrived along with sloths and armadillos as part of the faunal exchange between the continents.

name: water of life
originalDescription: In his discourse on the water of life, Jesus offered to quench the spiritual thirst of those who believed in him, promising the outpouring of the living Spirit of Truth upon all flesh. (162:6) The divine spirit is truly the water of life. (153:3.2) Why hesitate when you can freely partake of the water of life and enter joyfully into your spiritual inheritance? (165:3.8) Salvation of sonship is given only to those who hunger for truth and thirst for righteousness—for God. (193:2.2) Jesus was the living water, bringing refreshment springing up to eternal life for all who believe. (121:5.12) (143:5.2-3) (182:1.11) In the world to come, believers will partake of the water of life, joining together in the kingdom of God for eternal salvation. (166:3.5) The Passover emblem of the water of life was served by Jesus to his companions during the first feast celebrated without the paschal lamb. (127:6.7) The presence of the divine Spirit, the water of life, prevents mortal discontent and unspiritualized hunger, springing up in believers as a well of satisfaction leading to everlasting life. (34:6.8)
improvedDescription: A spiritual metaphor used by Jesus to describe the living Spirit of Truth that quenches spiritual thirst and brings eternal life. Jesus declared himself to be the living water that springs up to everlasting life in believers, representing divine sustenance that prevents spiritual discontent and provides lasting satisfaction.

name: Waters of Merom
originalDescription: Jesus journeyed from Beersheba to Dan, passing through Waters of Merom, in his final tour through Palestine. (134:7.5) (157:3.2) (158:7.1)
improvedDescription: A body of water in northern Palestine near Dan, passed by Jesus and the apostles during their travels through Galilee. The location served as a geographical landmark on their journeys between Caesarea-Philippi and other destinations in the region.

name: watersnake
originalDescription: A famous sentence by Jesus was: “If your child asks for a watersnake, would you give it to him just because he foolishly requests it?” (144:2.4)
improvedDescription: A serpentine creature mentioned by Jesus in a parable about prayer and divine wisdom. He asked rhetorically whether a father would give his child a watersnake if the child foolishly requested one, illustrating how the heavenly Father gives appropriate gifts according to divine wisdom rather than unwise petitions.

name: wave phenomena
improvedDescription: Physical manifestations involving wave motion and energy transmission, likely referring to various forms of electromagnetic radiation and other wave-based energy patterns in the universe.

name: wax board bond
originalDescription: Debts recorded on wax board bonds ensured the unfaithful steward's welcome into the houses of all who do business with his master. (169:2.4)
improvedDescription: A type of debt record written on wax tablets used for commercial transactions in ancient times. In Jesus' parable of the unfaithful steward, these bonds documented the amounts owed by the master's debtors and could be altered to reduce the recorded debts.

name: way
originalDescription: Isaiah the second proclaimed a high concept of God surpassing that of all prophets and religious teachers before him, declaring, “As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways.” (97:7.5) The bestowal Son establishes a new and living way for the spiritual uplift of mortal races, embodying Paradise truth for the freedom of mankind. (52:5.4-5) Commit your way to the Lord and trust in Him, for He will bring it to pass. (131:2.10) The Universal Father knows all our ways, from the number of stars to our sorrows, and His understanding is a comfort to all humanity. (3:3.2) Jesus realized that God's way, though not easy, was the path he must walk. (136:9.4) The prophets and priests cried out for deliverance, torn between the promise of righteousness and the looming threat of evil - a Great Choice for the Jews enduring alien rulers. (97:8.3) Jesus chose to always follow the Father's way, despite the strong desire to win his people and the world over to his new spiritual kingdom.
